Optimum is a board game published by the christin youth organisation Svenska Kyrkans Unga in the Evangelical Lutheran Church of Sweden. The game is about sustainable development and environmental impact. The game is card driven, and by certain cards the players can produce, investing in human rights, reduce the greenhouse effect or acquire political influence. In the game you have to design your in-game society as optimally as possible so that you can win the game.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Herzogenaurach,a city in Germany.
The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from the Saarland, a region/federal state in Germany.

On a desolate fringe-world, miners discover powerful technology buried in the ruins of an ancient alien civilization. Soon the wasteland erupts in conflict as desperate factions vie for control of the region.  Mercenaries from far-away lands converge on the wastes: tech-wizards, warriors in robotic armor, zog-riders and archaeologists, all eager to be hired and join in the impending struggle.  Battle is coming.  Rally your forces and outgun the opposition!

Fringe Wars is a fast-paced board game where 2-5 players lead small, competing factions hell-bent on controlling the wasteland and procuring the alien artifacts it holds.  Players use hero cards to recruit and deploy troops, and fight for control of key locations.  Hero cards can be used in combinations to make actions stronger, and deciding how to use each card is vital to winning.  Players attack each other and the vicious beasts that live in the wasteland to take control of territories.  The player who is able to obtain the most alien artifacts through the heated battle is the winner.

boardgame: Ninja: Legend of the Scorpion Clan – Himeji expansion
description:
This free Print & Play expansion is a big new challenge for the ninja and his fellow, the intruder.
If you found the original map, a piece of cake, try this one.
The map is based on one of the finest castles, which you can visit in Japan, notably in Himeji. For the purists, I must admit that some walls have been a little squeezed. This was necessary to fit the miniatures and improve game play.

Based on the 1930's movie. A simple children's race game. Collect evidence, until you have the arch criminal enmeshed in the evidence chain.

With wooden impliments, 24 cards, 2 dices etc.

boardgame: Wally Woo and Dasher Diesel Have a Race!
description:
Children's game that appeared in the Robin Annual.

Join in the race with counters and a dice! Start either on blue Wally Woo the wooden train, or red Dasher Diesel. The number you throw with the dice is the number of "squares" you move forward. If you land on a Blue, have another turn — if you land on a Red, miss one turn. You can go either way in the middle, to avoid a crash! The first person to reach the other end of the track, wins!

(from Deer Valley Game Company website:)

The Peninsular Campaign is an operational level game using the block unit system to re-create the limited intelligance of the actual campaign. Players maneuver their forces without adequate knowledge of their opponents strengths or even location.

The game board covers the actual and potentional topography of McClellan's offensive operations in the Spring of 1862; from the rugged hills covering Richmond's southern approach and the swamplands covering its northeast approach to the protective confines of the C.S.S. Virginia's base port of Norfolk. A point to point movement system is employed on a 20 x 24 playing map.

Limited command abilities and critical supply wagons are key to managing your armies and achieving victory.

Statistical sports game featuring stock car racing.

As usual with these games, dice are rolled and the result cross-referenced against charts and/or tables.

Munchkin Game Changers collects the cards from four out-of-print Munchkin boosters (Munchkin Fairy Dust, Munchkin Monster Enhancers, Munchkin Reloaded!, and Munchkinomicon), along with the cards from Munchkin Fairy Dust Dice and four cards previously available only directly from Steve Jackson Games. (The total card count is 68.) This set also contains two unique Munchkin Level Counters – one for Fairy Dust and the other for Munchkinomicon – as well as two 19mm sparkly pink dice. The four individual cards not previously distributed to outside markets are:


     "Bull-Headed" (from the 2011 Exclusive Warehouse 23 Munchkin Booster)
     "Fairy Dust-Up" (from Munchkin Promo Cards)
     "Pumped-Up" (from the 2011 Exclusive Warehouse 23 Munchkin Booster)
     "Reload!" (from the 2010 Exclusive Warehouse 23 Munchkin Booster)


Munchkin Game Changers was available exclusively from the Barnes & Noble bookstore chain until November 2014, when it is announced to enter the normal distribution market.

Designer's blurb:
Megadungeon! is a monster-slaying, treasure-looting dungeon delve suitable for the whole family.  Each player selects one of three characters, warrior, sorcerer, or thief. Each player, on their turn, draws a random dungeon tile, places it and moves their character into it.  Thus, you are able to explore a completely different dungeon every time you play.

This game has been nearly a year in the making, with nearly ten months of playtesting and revision, and the end result is, in my own biased opinion, a fantastic, family-friendly boardgame reminiscent of great old games like Tactical Studies Rules (TSR)'s Dungeon! and SPI's DeathMaze: Heroic Adventure in the Corridors of Doom.

Torn Armor is a fantasy squad-based tactical miniatures board game for 2-4 players. Based in the mythical World of Torn, it presents the lore, races, people, heroes, and history to players through the use of game-play scenarios. The core set focuses on the conflicts between two factions:

The Sisk Empire: an expansionist society, believing that their advanced culture and religious ideology are something from which everyone can benefit. Unfortunately their war-machine is thinly stretched, over-taxed, and on the brink of collapse. They seek wealth, raw materials, technology and slaves.

The Maychians: anthropomorphic creatures that live in the so-called "New Continents" in the northern hemisphere. Their land is an undiscovered country, full of untapped resources and ancient ruins. The Maychians possess primitive technology, but are fervent in their desire to defend their homeland and resist the Sisk invasion.

Included in the core set are 39 highly-detailed miniatures, 12 unit stat cards, a full color rule book, full color scenario book, 4 double-sided color battle posters, 8 Magical Item cards, 8 Spell cards and 25 dice of 5 different colors.

The battle for Torn World begins as the Sisk explorers land on the beaches of the "New Continents" with the intent to discover and claim ancient magical relics from a forgotten age. But the Maychians have spotted the intruders and are ready to stop them at any cost! Which faction will prevail? Only you can decide!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Mythic Battles: Expansion 1 – The Bloody Dawn of Legends
description:
Mythic Battles: Expansion 1 includes new and powerful warriors for the armies of both Hades (Minos and The Damned of Tartarus) and Athena (The Matriarchs, Arachne and Arachne's Babies) so that players can reinforce their armies while facing off against one another in six new epic scenarios.

Mythic Battles: Expansion 1 also introduces a new type of cards: Heroes, exceptional beings with otherworldly powers that are not units, but rather cards to add to your deck to get bonuses.

Hockey Night in Canada Challenge is a head-to-head stand alone game and an expansion to Big League Hockey Manager.

The object of the HNIC Challenge stand alone game is to win the championship by winning four matches.  Each "coach" selects a team of one goalie, two defensemen, and three forwards, plus two extra players.  Each match is played in three periods.  Dice rolls determine the score in each period by both teams.  Challenge cards can be played in each period to influence the score or affect the entire match.  If the score is tied after three periods, overtime is played until their is a winner.  Once a team has won four matches (of a possible seven), they win the championship!

The expansion pack option for Big League Hockey Manager allows General Managers (GM's) to collect a Challenge each time they win a match.  Challenge cards can be traded or sold before the "trade deadline".  Once a GM was won five matches, the first and second place GM's are determined. The HNIC Challenge stand-alone rules are used to play the championship final between those two teams.

(Note: The standard rules for HNIC Challenge are best played with two players.  The expansion pack rules can be played with two to four players.)

From the publisher's web site:
Get ready for a quick, exciting best-of-seven series that's pits you and a rival coach in a challenge for the championship. This game is easy to learn and fun to play. Your teams face off as you roll the dice and strategically play your Hockey Night in Canada Challenge cards. Add quarter and semi finals and up to eight people can play. Each playoff round takes less than ten minutes and is perfect for hockey fans ten years old and up. This is also two games in one: Hockey Night in Canada Challenge AND an expansion pack for BLM's award winning General Manager game.

Objective: gain as many quiz-cards as possible giving correct answers; become 'Quizkönig' and gets the crown.
 - acting by turns -
Active player draws a card asking clockwise - correct answer? hand card over.. - if not, try yourself finding correct answer; if unsuccessful - ask next player; round ends after each player had an active task. Open questions are collected by the quizmaster.
Next round: contraclockwise
Next round: random
new session

Remarks:
content ~ 500 different questions;
product code on box differs from game code printed lid inside: 611 5 001 3;
some questions are niddles; if the quizmaster collects more cards than anyone else he may name the team 'dullards'

Triplets Passover game
It's a card game for the entire family. Players gain knowledge of various concepts related to the Passover story in a fun, dynamic setting

A set of three cards constitutes a triplet
The three concepts represented in the three cards may be connected to a proverbial saying from Jewish sources (the Torah or the Passover Haggadah). Alternately, three figures can make up a triplet if they share a familial connection.

Objective: gain most victory points giving correct answer as fast as possible
 - acting simultaneously -
active player turns the spinner; spinner (mounted on a disc) ends up pointing at a  letter & a theme (similar to http://boardgamegeek.com/boardgame/12043/stadt-land-fluss); get the name first collecting a victory point;
remarks:
several discs available

Educational card game issued by the German Goverment for political education (Bundeszentrale politische Bildung):
A card is drawn an read. There is some trvia like "How many cows live in Germany? Everyone writes an estimate on a paper. The papers are revealed and read aloud, Now everyone choses one number. The number closest to the real number scores points.

Objective: be the first reaching target space (space 60 before WWII/space 50 after WWII) & learn traffic rules
 - moving by turns -
simple 'Roll & Move' game; ending on a marked space follow instructions printed on lid inside.

remarks: 
pewter figures - 
changing designs over the decades due to product lifecycle - from very first edition 1926 to last edition 1955 with different boards & boxes/meeples
remarks on the board (for the children) are made in rhyme form

Box Description: 100% Pasturized fun. In this moo-tastic matching game all you have to do is pick the pairs from the herd as fast as you can!

Spread the cards on the table face up.  This is the HERD. Then, all players should "One, Two, Deja Moo!" and the game begins.  Now all players search the cards in the HERD, looking for matching pairs of cows.  There are no turns.  Once all cards are gone from the HERD, each players counts the cards they have collected, the player with the most cards wins.

A set of three engraved red dice, 1 white die, 3 red tokens, and a red power card giving the red player a special one time use game ability("Discard at the end of any productive season. Each opponent may give to you a good (gold, wood or stone) of his choice to gain 2 Soldiers.").  The dice and tokens feature thematic designs and are to replace the standard dice and wooden tokens that are included in the base game.

The dice and tokens feature thematic designs and are to replace the standard dice and wooden tokens that are included in the base game.

A set of three engraved green dice, 1 white die, 3 green tokens, and a green power card giving the green player a special one time use game ability ("Discard at the beginning of a productive season (after dice were rolled and all modifieres and rerolls applied), your opponents cannot influence Advisors with value 13 or higher during current season.").  The dice and tokens feature thematic designs and are to replace the standard dice and wooden tokens that are included in the base game.

A set of three engraved blue dice, 1 white die, 3 blue tokens, and a blue power card giving the blue player a special one time use game ability("Discard at the beginning of a productive season to reroll all your dice once.").  The dice and tokens feature thematic designs and are to replace the standard dice and wooden tokens that are included in the base game.

A set of three engraved black dice, 1 white die, 3 black tokens, and a black power card giving the black player a special one time use game ability("Discard after you lose a battle. You get the prizes for the victory (you still get the penalties for the defeat and are considered a "loser" for the Fortress, +1 VP to the most powerful defender, etc.).").  The dice and tokens feature thematic designs and are to replace the standard dice and wooden tokens that are included in the base game.

Tactical miniatures combat in the late medieval/early renaissance period, with a focus on Italy. Units are maneuvered, with combat decided by dice, playing cards and special fate cards. Campaign rules for Condottiere companies are included, as are armies from outside Italy. Designed for 28mm miniatures but conversion to other scales is easy. Single or multi-basing. Troops have 20mm frontage, 20mm depth for infantry and 50mm depth for cavalry. Units are 16 infantry, 10 cavalry or 2 cannons and 8 crew.

A 2 or 4 Player strategy game.

Players’ attempt to score points by exchanging the positions of coloured pyramids on a grid in order to form specified shapes of one colour. The choices of colours that can be moved have limitations, and the first to score each shape get most points.

(An ICEHOUSE adaptation of Sid Sacksons’ TRANSFORMATION – expanded and with a Variant, “TRANSFORMARTIAN EXXTRA”, to increase the  options available. Plus how to play with 3 or 6 Players!).

Game from The Beezer Book, 1976.

The object of the game is to reach the apple store first.
Any number of players can play. All you need are a dice and some counters. Throw the dice before the game. The one who throws the lowest number is Farmer.
Each player gets two throws at the start except Farmer. He throws last and only gets one throw.
If Farmer lands on a square with a player on it that person is caught and is out of the game. If a player lands on Farmer’s square, Farmer loses one turn.
The penalties in square D and square I apply to Farmer only. The penalties in square 2 and square 14 do not apply to Farmer. Players must throw the exact number to finish on 35.

An express game of urban planning for 2 to 5 players.

In Steamplanners, players build linear cities, bidding on contracts for factories, offices, train stations, churches and palaces to create thriving urban landscapes. A player may have their plan for civic glory thwarted however by the railroads of other players.

To Win:
There are a variety of urban building tiles in the game worth victory points:


     Train Stations
     Palaces
     Churches
     Factories
     Corner Office Towers (left and right orientations)
     Central Office Towers


Players must acquire needed Building tiles through contracts (auctions). To win the game a player must use the Building tiles to build the city worth the most victory points.

Each city may only have one Train Station and no more than one Palace. A city may, however, have one or more Churches, as well as one or more Factories and Central Office Towers. To score bonus victory points, a completed city requires both a left and right Corner Office Towers. Central Office Towers need not be present in a completed city, but they help keep your citizens from leaving on a train through the nearest railroad.

Additionally there are two other types of tiles:


     Parks
     Railroads


These are not worth victory points, but instead may help or hinder an opponent.

The game ends when one stack of Building tiles is exhausted.

This game is an express re-theme of Wettstreit der Baumeister.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Rise of the Runelords – Character Add-On Deck
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Take the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game to the next level with the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Rise of the Runelords - Character Add-On Deck. Inside this 110-card expansion deck you'll find four new classes for your Pathfinder Adventure Card Game characters – Amiri the barbarian, Lini the druid, Sajan the monk, and Seelah the paladin — along with all the additional cards you need to expand your adventures to accommodate five or six players. New monsters, weapons, and equipment, as well as additional copies of some of the game's most useful spells, blessings, allies, and more give you everything you need the face even the deadliest threats.

Errata: Early printings had the "Role card" of each character listed as belonging to "B"  (for Base Set). This has been fixed in later printings to instead say "C" (for Character Add-On Deck).

description:
Game description from the publisher:

Visual Panic is a hellish observation game! Find objects or words in the stated color quicker than the other players. Concentration and speed will be your most precious weapons to win the Visual Panic challenges!

Runes and Bones have been sources of power and divination for Tribal Orc Spirit Talkers for countless generations. As the wisdom of the few remaining spirit talkers fell to the blood lust that consumes the race, the Runes and Bones once used to guide the strategy of the warriors now serve only to entertain them. What once was an honored system of divination, now serves as a campfire game for Orcish warriors everywhere.

From the Creator:
Runes and Bones was created in the summer of 2006 as I was preparing for a 10 day trip to meet up with my Orcish horde for Ragnarok XXI.  Ragnorok is the most popular annual event for Dagorhir Battle Games Association.  It is a week of camping, characterization, and warfare.  I wanted something that felt tribal and could accommodate many players, thus, Runes and Bones was born.  I have created many sets since then for other camping trips and cons.

Gameplay:
When playing Runes and Bones you go around the circle and bid bones in exchange for runes from the bag, which you then play to attempt to win the pot.  Runes have a variety of characteristics which determine which runes they beat and vice-versa.  You are also able to barter runes and/or bones from other players on your turn.  If, on your turn, the rune you played last turn is still the winning rune, you win the pot.

Rare subject for a game in Britain, where Basketball has never reached a popularity seen in America.

Players assemble the plastic basket, which stands 12 inches tall. Then players take turns to catapult the ball with the intention of scoring baskets. First to score a pre-agreed number wins.

"Two sitting down round games for everyone" as the box says. But I have a mint box and contents, all the cards are still joined at the perforations, but the rules only relate to Musi-Winks. So Musi-Matics will not be described here.

MUSI-WINKS
A Round Game for Everyone
In each packet are,
TEN HAPPY SAM FACES, TEN MOURNFUL MICK FACES and THREE WINKER WILLY left and right EYES. They are perforated so as to be torn into a total of 86 small cards.
As the name implies the game is best played to music and a piano, gramophone or other music should be available.

INSTRUCTIONS TO THE M.C.
Shuffle the 86 small cards and put the pack into an elastic band. Ask the players to sit or stand in a circle and when the music starts the pack is passed round. At intervals the music is stopped and on each occasion 3 (more or less according to the time available) players commencing with the one holding the pack take the top card. When the music recommences the pack is passed on by the last player to draw a card and play continues until all the cards are used up. Twelve players would each have on the average about 7 cards.
Each player then proceeds to make up complete faces, observing the following rules:
1. A Winker Willy Eye (left or right, but only the appropriate one) can be used to make a complete face with Happy Sam and Mournful Mick Cards, mixed if necessary although it is advantageous not to do so.
2. If a Winker Willy. Eye is not used then Happy Sam faces must be made up entirely of Happy Sam Cards; likewise for Mournful Mick faces.
3. Any player holding a Winker Willy Eye may exchange it with any other player for three other cards.
4. Only ONE Winker Willy Eye can be used in making up a face.

SCORING—
Complete Faces—
A face made up of 1 WINKER WILLY EYE and 3 HAPPY SAM CARDS or 3 MOURNFUL MICK CARDS ... 10 points
A face made up of 1 WINKER WILLY EYE and mixed HAPPY SAM and MOURNFUL MICK CARDS ... ... 8 points
A complete HAPPY SAM FACE ... . 5 points
A complete MOURNFUL MICK FACE . 5 points
Features—
Odd cards are valued as follows—
A complete MOUTH made up of HAPPY SAM OR MOURNFUL MICK CARDS or both .... 2 points
AN EYE ... 1 point

The winner is the player scoring the highest number of points.

boardgame: The Underground Game & What's the Letter?
description:
A box of two party games together.

The Underground Game:
Any number of players above 2 can play although 4 to 12 players provides the greatest fun. It is ideal for all ages from 11 years upwards and will be of particular appeal to those who enjoy crossword puzzles.
Each player is handed a card on which there are 32 clues to the names of stations on the London Underground. A time limit should be set in which the players must find as many answers as possible. When the time limit has expired, players may take turns to call out their answers which the M.C. will confirm if correct. Players score 1 point for each correct station.
For those not familiar with the London Underground, maps are supplied. These should be passed round to give each player an equal chance to use one.

What's the Letter?
Each player is handed a card and must answer the questions using one or two letters from the alphabet as applicable. The M.C. should announce a time limit of say 5 minutes for this game. Players are given one point for each correct answer with extra points if they have more than one acceptable answer to a question.

Agentin salaiset paperit (Agent's secret papers) is a Finnish board game very similar to New York Chase, or Scotland Yard, but just simpler. There is an Agent whose starting location is known, moves are secret, and his task is to escape from the city. There are also the Chasers whose task is to get the Agent in sight so that finally there is no corner for him to hide behind.

The rules are in Finnish but the map only has a few Finnish word so the game itself is almost language independent.

boardgame: Munchkin 5, 6 e 7: Tutti i Mostri Fatti a Fette
description:
It is an Italian expansion for Munchkin. It consists of Munchkin 5: De-Ranged, Munchkin 6: Demented Dungeons, Munchkin 7: Cheat With Both Hands, Munchkin Marked for Death, Munchkin Monster Enhacers plus some promos.

From publisher website:

Ammettetelo... Avete sempre desiderato combinare tutti i set di Munchkin per giocarli assieme. Più demenziali e folli le cose diventano, meglio è. Ora potete decisamente imboccare il tunnel della pazzia senza ritorno e giocare a Super Star Munchkin Cthulhu Zombi dei Caraibi FU di Nuovo!

Questa espansione non solo vi darà una vera marea di carte in più, ma è costruita apposta per mescolare tutto. E laddove unSuper Munchkin non bastava, ora avrete un Ultra Munchkin. Se i Mezzosangue erano scarsi, i Terzosangue vi daranno quel che volevate. E se non vi basta essere un Baro!, ora potrete essere Bari con Entrambe le Mani! Una miriade di Potenziamenti per Mostri e Oggetti… e un nuovo tipo di carta, il Potenziamento del Potenziamento! Per la prima volta la nuova Classe: il Ranger! Ora potrete addomesticare i Mostri e usarli come Destrieri! E ora, oltre alle carte Caverna già viste in Munchkin Cthulhu, potrete aggiungere addirittura le nuovissime e gigantesche carte Dungeon con questo set! Più nuove carte Portale!

description:
A standard party quiz game really but an added feature is how time is used up. All 84 question slips are laid face down on the table and shuffled around.  A time for the quiz to last is set by the host, say 20 minutes for example.

At the word "Go!", each team captain comes to the table at the same time and each selects a question slip. This is then taken back to his team, who may consider what the right answer may be. When ready, the team captain goes back to the table and hands their answer to the host. If it is right, the captain takes another slip and returns to his team. Note this is done in real time, there are no turns as such. If the answer is wrong, the captain must wait half a minute before being allowed to take a new question to his team.

When the time is up, the team with the most correct answers wins.

A variation is provided for individuals to play but it is pretty tame, just being a regular quiz game.

Game in The New Book of Games, circa 1950.

The player who wins the toss begins the game, by placing the “Ball” on the circle of his colour in the centre of the field. He then throws the dice and moves the ball along the line opposite the number thrown. The same player continues to throw until the ball is lost to an opponent by being kicked on to a circle of the opposing colour.
When the ball is kicked out of play it is placed on the goal-keeper’s circle, and he takes the resultant goal kick.
After a goal has been scored, the ball is kicked off by the side which has lost the goal, and the game restarted as in rule 1.
A fixed time for playing should be settled before the game begins, and it is won by the side which scores the largest number of goals within that time.

Game found in the New Book of Games, circa 1950. A somewhat identical Australian game titled "Across the World by Air" that differs in a few destinations is listed elsewhere on BGG.

Two, three or four players can take part. If only two or three are playing, they will select their startin points as they like from the four provided and noted in the left hand bottom corner of the board; if four are playing, all four will be used.

Each player plays from his starting point in the direction of Melbourne along the route indicated by the colour noted in the left-hand bottom corner of the board; thus, a player starting from London travels along the white line via St.John’s, Quito, Buenos Ayres (Note: this is not an error, this is how it is spelt in the rules), Cape Town, Lisbon, Cairo, Calcutta and Borneo, to Melbourne, which is the end point of the race for all players.

The first player to throw a six begins the race, moving along his route as many bars as he had thrown points. If a throw takes him to an obstacle, he must move back, miss a number of throws, or wait for his next move till a six is thrown, according to the directions on the board in each case. If a lucky throw lands him on to “Following Winds”, he moves forward as indicated.

The first player to reach Melbourne is the winner.

Game found in The New Book of Games, circa 1950. Two boards are provided, one for the batting side and one for the bowling side.

Opening batsmen, before beginning play, are allotted a colour, either red or white. When a batsman is out, the next batsman in takes the colour just vacated.
One counter is used for batting and one for bowling. These are placed on the squares marked “Start”.

To start play, the bowler throws the dice and moves his counter forward the number of spaces indicated by the throw. Should he fall on a red square, the “ball” is considered to be playable and the batsman is enabled to make a scoring stroke. He does this by throwing the dice and moving forward the number of squares on the batting chart thus indicated. The number of runs shown on the square is credited to the batsman on whose colour the counter is lying after the move is completed.

If the bowling counter falls on a wicket-taking square, the batsman who is out is the one whose coloured square is occupied by the batting counter. After taking a wicket the bowler bowls again before the batsman can make a stroke. Should the bowling counter fall on a square marked “extras”, these runs are credited to the batting side, but the batsmen make no stroke, and therefore the bowler must play again.

Batsmen cannot be out when lying on the square marked “Start”. It is a simple matter to work out the averages of all the different batsmen, and if bowling averages are also wanted, six throws of the dice would constitute an “over”.

Each of the two players controls a team of 11 batsmen, two of whom must always be batting at the same time. When 10 wickets have fallen the innings is considered closed, so that one batsman is left “ not out.”

Game found in The New Book of Games, circa 1950.

For this game a large board must be drawn like the one in the image. The football is a counter, which at the beginning of the game is placed on square 14 for the kick-off. The two players, or sides — “Rovers” and “United” — throw the dice alternately. Rovers add the value of their throw to the number of the square on which the ball lies, and move it on to the square indicated; thus, if the ball is on 17 and they throw a 4, the ball is moved to 21. United subtract the value of their throw from the number where the ball previously was: thus if it is on 8 and they throw 3, the ball goes to 5. A goal is scored when the ball enters square 1 or square 27.

After a goal has been scored, the game is restarted, and the side which has lost the goal kicks off. If a throw leads to a number over 27 or less than 1, it is a goal lick, and the other side places the ball on the square in front of goal, and throws. If 1 is thrown while the ball is in a touch-line square, the ball is out of play and not moved, the other side taking a throw immediately.

A time limit, say five or ten minutes, must be fixed for the game before starting. When elapsed, the side with the most goals, wins.

boardgame: Disney Gummi Bears: The Secret of Gummi Glen Game
description: The Pantry shelf is empty and it's your task to fill it with some freshly brewed gummiberry juice.  As you travel through Gummi Glen collecting needed ingredients, you'll visit scenic "Old Rumbler" Falls and the most delicious gummiberry patch in all the kingdom.  You choose the route, but you must make all the stops, turning on the water in the pump room, racing through the quicktunnel and, of course, having the brew stirred in Grammi Gummi's kitchen.  Be the first to reach the pantry after completing your journey and you will win the game.

Cheap and cheerful game featuring a plastic board with a 'gun' in two corners. Each player takes 3 balls and 2 ships and sets up his ships in the notches for FLAG SHIP and 2nd SHIP as shown on the box. Then each player takes 3 torpedoes (ball-bearings) for his gun.

Each player places a ball inside his torpedo as shown on the box. He moves the torpedo to the right or left to aim the shot in the desired direction and then presses the trigger to release the ball.

Each player, in turn, gets to shoot three times, trying to knock down the ships of his opponent. Knocking over a FLAG SHIP scores 30 points, knocking over the 2nd SHIP scores 10 points. The player with the highest score wins. A perfect score is 40.

Party game that features some nice nostalgic British advertisements.

The host puts the 18 advert cards around the room and each player gets a card to record their answers. When the host thinks that enough time has gone by, he calls 'Time'. Everyone hands their answer sheet in and the one with the most correct answers wins.

Products featured mix well-known with obscure (to modern minds anyway).

Objective:
gain most victory point finding matching terms

Designer's comment:
Choose Your Own Adventure gamebooks are wonderful things. They contain little paragraphs scattered across the pages in a seemingly chaotic order, each one ending with a set of choices and numbers which take you flicking through the book to the next paragraph in your personal journey through the book.

Which got me thinking. What happens if you make those numbers actually mean something? What if they replaced the paragraphs themselves? And what if you made them random? What would a solitaire game like that be like?

This.

3 Dice

A CYOA solitaire game using just 3 dice and no gamebook

User comment:
This simple solitaire adventure game with dice was a submission for the RPG Solitaire Challenge.

The game is played by rolling three differently coloured six-sided dice.  Using a single die 3 characteristics are rolled and recorded to represent your abilities.  To begin playing the three dice are rolled and each die is referenced against a three column table to determine a location, a monster and a reward.

If a monster exists a die is rolled to determine combat results.  If you win the encounter or the room is empty you total the room dice and record the total as your Experience Reward.  When 50 points have been gained the adventure is over and abilities are reset to their starting values.  Experience can be used to increase an ability by 1 point.

"Preparations"

Next to start the first game, you have to carefully remove the figurines carved in cartons, which are then inserted in the transparent base. Each participant chooses prpopria figurine.
All figurines are placed on the starting.

"Goal of the game"
The aim of game is to get there the first to finish with your cards.

"Rules of the Game"

The youngest player starts the game, and pulls dice first. The figurines are moved so many square, how many points are made with dice: If a player has pulled a "five", cause to your figurine of five steps. If a player arrives with his figurine on a square occupied by the figure of a player, they can be reached both on the square; Then we go on a square can stay two or more figures, without one should "go away" or "eliminate" other.
But that's not all, because when they leave Mickey Mouse, Donald Duck and company, there is always something! There are, for example, the red square, coming on as you always have surprises.
When a figure at the end of its move, it stops on one of these "red square", you will immediately carry out the provisions it has prescribed.
The eight red square have the following meaning:

Square 4: you forgot to pay "entry in the race!" Get back to square one! when it reaches your next turn, you will have to start over.

Square 9: What a disgrace! You have a flat tire! To replace the wheel must remain stationary for a turn to play!

Square 13: Due to the great nervous tension, you went off the track and forced to follow the path of emergency marked with orange. To square 22 can finally get back on track and continue running normally.

Square 17: As every driver intelligent and well informed, you are able to skillfully exploit the wake of the other players. So roll the dice again.

Square 24: are you afraid of this journey full of twists and you're forced to slow down. You may proceed only if the dice with one or two shots

Square 32: too fast! You drifter and lost valuable time. You can re-rolling the dice only when you passed by at least one competitor. If you are already in last place, then you can proceed to the game normally.

Square 35: other drawbacks! Always proceeding at full throttle, you have used too much fuel and your tank is now empty. To refuel you are forced to stop in the pits for a turn.

"End of the game"
The player who first comes to Goal with his figurine won the game. It is not necessary to come and stop right on the destination square, if the score of the dice is higher than necessary, unnecessary steps are not counted.
The game continues until the last competitor has crossed the finish line.

boardgame: România (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride: Europe)
description: To play, you either need to have the original Ticket to Ride or Ticket to Ride Europe. The Romania map can be played with either rule set and components, but is preferable to play with the Ticket to Ride: Switzerland rules.

This expansion requires the Asteroid Junkies! game to play, but includes all parts needed to play with five or six players!
BONUS: A new set of rules provides different variants for more options when playing Asteroid Junkies.

A game for very young children. It appeared in the Playhour Annual in 1973. It 'stars' a character called Dozey Dormouse.

Use counters and a die. Move according to your roll. If you land on one of the seven Dozey illustrations, miss a turn "for a doze".

The first player to reach space 53, wins.

In Bad Neighbors, the goal is to kill all of your neighbors by inflicting various monsters on them, including zombies, vampires, aliens, and demons. Unfortunately, the neighbors are often prepared with weapons (including shotguns, holy water, machetes, stakes, and more). The game's art is humorous, parodying pop-horror icons from TV and movies (e.g. Buffy, Star Trek, Dawn of the Dead, etc).

Game-play: On your turn, you can attack any other player with a monster card. If the defending player has a matching weapon, they equip that weapon by placing it under a living family member card and remain safe. For instance, if you attacked with "hell mutt" (a demon) and they equip "holy water" (an anti-demon weapon), they're safe.  Otherwise, the defender must choose one of their family members to perish. Flip that card over, revealing a gravestone.

If you can't or don't attack, you may play an action card or discard to refresh your hand. At the end of your turn, you refill your hand to 4 cards.

There are two ways to win: 1) kill all your neighbors; 2) equip your family with all 4 kinds (suits) of weapon.

In turn, a player moves one's pawn to the next unoccupied interesection ar corner. When a player reaches The Attorney, The Kids, The Shrink, The Dating Scene, or The Court that player draws an Experience CArd to see if that player will be awarded a Milestone. The player who collects a Milestone from each area and reachs the "You're a Winner" area is the victor.

Hunt for the Ragnarok is a hidden movement board game for 2-5 players in which one player takes the role of the last heir of a fallen galactic empire fleeing for his life and title aboard the freighter Ragnarok while the rebellious nobles houses pursue.
The desperate and damaged Ragnarok must cross the vast reaches of space - avoiding other inhabited worlds and using stealth and deception to avoid capture. The other players, acting as ships of the rebellious nobles, search for the Ragnarok - each seeking to be the first to find the heir and strengthen their own claim to the title before one of the others can do the same.

The Ragnarok has limited resources and moves slowly. It must rely on deception, misdirection and stealth to avoid the ships of the opposing players.
The Hunters ships have more options for movement, but find tracking the Ragnarok to be the challenge. As they move, they scan space around them and receive data back from the Ragnarok player in the form of cards.
Each Hunter only receives the data derived from their own ship sensors. Since they are working primarily for their own victory and not the collective victory of the Hunters.
Hunters also have access to a once per game power which is derived from a list initially communally available, but each power is removed from the game as it is used.

The Ragnarok wins the game if he can cross a large portion of the board and reach his destination planet. 
The Hunter who finds Ragnarok wins the game and claims title to the throne of the empire.

The game includes a variable board set-up, specific scenario set-ups, advanced and optional rules and play formats.

Hunt for the Ragnarok finished second in the 2012 KublaCon Game Design Contest.

Intelli Wars is a Strategic level Science-Fiction themed space wargame where you manage fleets of spaceships and spend available resources to attack and conquer your opponent’s star systems by deciding when to commit your forces, where they must go and with what support while repairing, refitting and bringing in reinforcements.

It is a 2 player, competitive, turn based, card driven, board-wargame. This is a science-fiction war setting between two space empires. The map is a blank hex grid to allow for various scenarios and thus increase replay value. Players are in the role of fleet commanders in charge of a theater of war with a God’s eye point of view. The scope is that of a campaign operation; events are just part of the wider war.

A unit represents a fleet of spaceships and while the time scale is abstract a turn is meant to represent anywhere from a few weeks to a couple of months. To win you must achieve the scenario's victory point level.

The game in its current format is print & play and files are provided in this page for downloading components and rules in electronic format for printing and assembling.

You can join this race with counters and dice! Choose to be either Sam Steam train or Digby Diesel. Throw the dice and move your counter along the track the number of spaces shown on the dice. From publisher website:

"From covert actions against insurgent groups and daring sabotage missions to precision strikes against fortified positions and reconnaissance deep behind enemy lines, the special forces units deployed by many nations are the spearhead of modern combat operations. "Classified", the latest companion volume for Force on Force, allows wargamers to recreate any and all of them. With detailed background information, extensive orders of battle for the world's preeminent special forces units, and a range of scenarios, "Classified" gives Force on Force players a detailed and realistic experience of modern special operations missions across the globe."

boardgame: Triumph of Nations: Wargaming Rules for Napoleonic Tabletop Gaming
description:
From the publisher website:

"In 1812, after a period of considerable victories, Napoleon found himself fighting on two fronts, suffering setback after setback. With his forces in Spain being pushed back, Napoleon launched an invasion of his former ally, Russia, leading the half-million-strong Grande Armee to Moscow and a brutal defeat at the hands of Russian forces and the bitter winter. On the defensive, Napoleon was defeated and exiled, but returned to lead his loyal armies against a coalition of European nations in a final campaign that culminated at Waterloo. "Triumph of Nations", a companion for "Field of Glory Napoleonic", provides players with all the army lists and details they need to recreate the battles of the later Empire, from the disastrous invasion of Russia to the glorious Hundred Days."

boardgame: Happy Families Thrive on Cadburys Bournville Cocoa
description:
Cadbury advert/game from The Champion Annual for Boys in 1950. At this time, Cadbury often used the back of children's annuals to advertise and they nearly always liked to make it a game as well.

HOW TO PLAY: All you need to play this game is a dice, together with a dice cup (an egg-cup will do) and some coloured counters or buttons, one for each player. The game can be played by any number of players, and the first player to reach 80 and get into ‘bed’ is the winner. Don't forget that you must obey any instructions that you land on. With your last throw you must get the exact number to reach 80: if you throw too high a number you must wait for another turn.

Cadburys, the makers of Bournville Cocoa, hope you will have a lot of fun with this game, and that Mummy will always give you plenty of lovely chocolatey Bournville Cocoa to help you to grow up healthy and strong.

Objective: Using the detailed street map of Bremen each player is given five companies to be established in the city as close to the centre as possible.

With the help of dice and counters, each players chooses where to place, turn or remove company tiles.
Once a company is established on the map, the corresponding four 'places worth seeing' are revealed, so familiarizing players with the sights of the city.

Contents: 80 cards, 8 counters, 3 dice, rules and city information.

Trivia game full of questions with obvious answers. Example:

Q. In what straits is the Rock of Gibralter situated?
A. Straits of Gibralter

Play until you are all fed up and the one with the most right answers, wins.

In the madcap and chaotic card game Meerkat Slammers, the meerkat tribe has lost its leader and descended into anarchy as each competing faction is determined to prove it's the best! Not all meerkats fight fair, though, and players can employ a number of dirty tricks in order to get one up on his rivals.

At the start of the game, each player is dealt 15 cards in five piles, one card in the first pile, two in the second, and so on. Turn the top card of each pile face up, then place the rest of the deck face down in the middle of the playing area. Someone flips over the topmost card of the deck to create the discard pile, then the chaos begins. Every meerkat clan card features a fighting score. You may place any of your face-up cards on the discard pile so long as its fighting score is one below or one above the number of the topmost card on the discard pile. Once you play a card, you can turn over the topmost card of the pile from which it came. In addition, you can always have five individual piles, so if one pile empties, move any face-up card from an existing pile to create a new one.

Event cards can be played at any time. Slam them down next to the discard pile and shout the name of the card. Play stops so that the event instructions can be carried out, after which a new round begins.

The objective is to get rid of all your cards. This alone, however, is not enough to claim victory as many potential clan leaders have come undone at the last moment. As soon as a player is rid of his last card, any player may slam his palm down on the discard pile to claim the victory.

boardgame: The Castles of Burgundy: 3rd Expansion – German Board Game Championship Board 2013
description: Player Board created by Stefan Feld exclusively for the regional qualifying round of the German board game championship 2013.

Hello Sunshine! is a charmingly simple game that will have you and your child laughing and playing together!  Play hide and seek with Sunshine – an adorable plush toy – while teaching your child positional words such as: In, On Top, Below, Next To, and more.

Game Play:
1.  Grown-up selects a card and hides the plush Sunshine according to the card.
2.  Grown-up shows child the card and says, "Can you find Sunshine ______ ?"  (example, UNDER the table)
3.  When child finds Sunshine, everyone celebrates!
4.  Try another card.

This expansion includes four new cards. Two of them are so called color changer. These are two new travel cards. 
Additionally two new adventure cards (with the color changer symbol) - Leopoldville & Addis Abeba are included.

These expansion is included in the Abacus Katalog-Edition 13 and was printed on high quality paper. This guarantees a perfect fit to the base game.

boardgame: Thunderstone Advance: Towers of Ruin – Modules
description:
This 16 page booklet was given out by Pegasus Spiele at the 2012 Essen SPIEL fair.

It contains several variants and scenarios for Thunderstone Advance.

In Wrong Chemistry, players want to collect as many points as they can by forming molecules based on the cards they draw. The expansion Wrong Chemistry: Expand Your Lab adds to the action of the base game, first by allowing for play with up to five players, and second by adding new element cards that expand upon the tactics of the base game by giving you new ways to earn points and by using both sides of the hex tiles.

In addition, new scientists have been added to the game; each player chooses one at the start of play and can use the power of this scientist up to three times during the game. Two new cards – "The Element of Surprise" and "Transmutium" – can be played in combination with the scientists to let you play during your rivals' turns.

Finally, five new cards – each worth 5 points – are placed face up on the table, waiting to be claimed by any player who can form these difficult combinations. As soon as the final card is claimed, the game ends – but the game can end in other ways as well as you'll discover during the game!

From the web page:

The radical dice-rolling, word-building race!

Roll the vowel dice and combine the letters with the consonant cards in your hand—build the highest-scoring word and you win the round! 2–8 players. Grades 3+

The ancient city-states of the Mediterranean constantly fought with one another for supremacy. Can you be the first civilization to achieve greatness?

Civscape is a take-that civilization card game, with all its rules on the cards. Play it while waiting for friends to show up (the rules allow others to be dealt in as they arrive) or play it to wrap up an evening of heavier gaming. Civilization games are usually strategic and deep; this is a tactical game of back-and-forth as you grow your civ and others knock it back.

In the Civscape card game, you will add Buildings to the Skyline of your city, discover new Technologies that are added to your City’s Timeline, establish new systems of Government, carefully balance your Resources and race against your rivals to build Wonders and Statues.

Each city-state is constantly pushing the limits of what it can sustain: a city grows larger than its technology supports, fields more military than it has resources for, and comes up with more plans than its current system of government can implement. Even as you expand your own city, you can cause trouble for other city states, subjecting them to calamities, disbanding their military, forcing them to choose new leaders, or seizing the crown of the Emperor.

You will play one of nine cities: Athens, Carthage, Corinth, Ephesus, Pella, Rome, Sparta, Syracuse or Thebes. Each city has 10 other corresponding cards, with a unique Wonder, Statue (great leader), other Buildings, Technology, Government, and Events. Prior to gameplay, each player selects their City and its cards, which are mixed together into the overall draw deck. Choose sets of rivals like the Peloponnesian War (Athens vs. Sparta and Thebes), the Punic War (Rome vs. Carthage), the League of Corinth (Pella & Corinth vs. Ephesus), the Sicilian War (Syracuse vs. Carthage) or one of the other suggested scenarios. With thousands of combinations, every game is different!

Download the free print-and-play PDF with all 108 cards.

description:
The airship marked a turning point in society. Now the skies were open to exploration, commerce, and illegal activity. The clouds became hiding places for Speakeasies, black market dealers, and much more unsavory activities. As a result, the Sky Patrol was created to help keep the peace. In an attempt to avoid being arrested during a recent “job”, you have accidentally wrecked your airship along with a few unlucky others. Fix your airship as quickly as possible to avoid certain incarceration!

On your turn, you roll dice trying to match them to a type of component (using simple icons). Once you know what it looks like, you search for a token that has the same icons, trying to recover a piece of your airship. Once recovered, airship components grant special abilities like making your turns easier or even griefing opponents.

Not all the tokens are useful. Most of the time, the player will select a token that is of no use to them and place it back in the pile. Memorization skills are necessary to ensure a player doesn't needless pick up the same token again and to remember where opponents have placed tokens the player needs. But with the special abilities that can be earned, the tokens can be shifted multiple times per game.

The first player to collect all of their tokens to complete their airship wins the game.

Up until now a squid and its friends - lots of little fish - have been playing happily together in the sea. However, a racy little shark has suddenly appeared and is circling this group of playmates. Luckily the squid is able to spread lots of ink ! Now we need your help ! Come and play together and help save the squid and its friends by covering the shark in so much ink, that it can't see properly. Because a shark that dissapears in a 'soup of ink' cannot be dangerous anymore ! The excitement begins: Is the shark going to eat up 9 little fishes first ? Or will the squid manage to squirt enough ink to save his friends first?

A game for 2 players, simulating two ninjas dueling to the death in a bamboo forest.  Players use attack cards, action cards and withdrawal cards as they fight and withdraw until there is only one ninja left standing... or none.

The deck consists of 25 unique cards. Attacks are played in a rock-paper-scissors fashion, with Melee attacks countering Ranged, Ranged attacks countering Traps, and Traps countering Melee attacks.  Attacks continue until either one player cannot counter (they die) or an appropriate withdraw card has been played.  Play continues until at least one player is dead.

-Seize them! ...said the King.
My chambers were robbed!! 
Those bandits took my treasures and they are escaping through the castle!

In Rogue you own one of the four thieves groups: Cult of Masks, Sentinels, Fox Guild, Narrow Path Clan and your purpose is to become the Master Rogue by leading your group to have the more Victory Points at the end of the game. To do that you must collect the most Treasure tokens. Each player owns 11 cards, 4 Master Thieves, 6 Gambits and 1 Black Market card (score card which gives you points depending on the treasure you have collected).

Each player chooses 1 Master Thief and 3 Gambits to begin the game and places the chosen cards face down on the table in an accessible (by all the players) area. All these cards form a mini deck of 16 cards. You never shuffle the deck. Now you cut the deck 3 times (one time each player) and the last player takes the deck, deals the cards on the table (4 rows x 4 cards each row, from the top left to the bottom right) and plays first. In each round, the player can do one of the following actions:Reveal a hidden card or to Exchange one of the cards in his/her hand, with a hidden card on the table. In that case the player who chooses the 2nd action, must place his new card faced up but note that this action costs you -1 Victory Point at the end of the game.

The Gambit cards have 2 different colored dice from the four colors you will find in the game (red, blue, green, yellow). When a Gambit card is revealed and in the same column (vertically) there is another Gambit, a Dispute takes place between the two. The players who own these Gambits, roll the corresponding dice and the one with the most hits takes a Treasure token. The Treasure tokens are faced down aside and when a dispute is commencing, you take one such token in random and place it on the Gambit cards, revealing the Treasure (the cards have been designed specifically for this purpose). In case of a tied dispute, the Treasure is lost (the King's guardians snatch it as your thieves flea to take cover). In case the 2 Gambits belong to the same thieves group, no dispute takes place as they work together to get the treasure. The player who owns them, takes a Treasure token immediately!

Each colored die represents a basic element: blue for Aqua, red for Ignis, green for Terra, yellow for Aura. The Master Thieve cards have those elements. The Master Thieves give additional skills to the Gambits when a Dispute takes place. For example when you roll a Red and a Green die for your Gambit and there is a faced up Master Thief on the table (your or your rivals') which represents Terra (green die), you add +1 Hit to the final result! Yet nothing is over. The Black Market cards have 4 different combinations of the Treasures, different for each player which grant him Victory Points, depending on the combination. But don't be so sure you can win the game since the Master Thieves can steal the Treasures from each other with a very tricky mechanic you will discover in the game. Beware.. If your Master Thief loses his Treasures, then you must place him sideways to deactivate his ability to steal, so you must place a new Master Thief to get revenge! Prepare yourself for the faster course of your life as no one knows of his victory, until the last card on the table is revealed!

A race begins...

 A Thieves' Race!

Red October was published in Combat! #5. It is a highly abstracted political conflict game about the situation in Russia from February-October 1917.

The four players represent the Provisional Government, the General Staff, the Bolsheviks, and the Anarchists. They all can place political factors in the boxes in the various areas on the board and attack other player's factors (using a standard wargame CRT). Some players have other options: the Provisional Government can announce an offensive against the Central Powers, the General Staff can try for a coup in a given area, and the Bolsheviks can try to seize power, which may result in their getting control of some areas (and ends the game regardless).

Each player has a different set of moderate and decisive victory conditions. It is possible for more than one player to achieve a victory condition. Negotiations are encouraged by the rules.

boardgame: Shingen vs Kenshin: Blood bath at Kawanakajima
description:
One of the first two games for the Series: SS (Sho-Kikaku), depicting the 4th battle of Kawanakajima during the Japanese Sengoku period. Takeda Shingen and Uesugi Kenshin were great warlords in the Sengoku period. Kawanakajima was their main battle ground in Shinano Province.

Players control either the Takeda or Uesugi clan army.
Each unit represent 500 soldiers and named by the commander.
Each turn, players alternately activate one unit for movement and combat. Combat result table is a differential of strength between attacker and defender.

The winner is determined by the destruction of the opponent army.

"Viy" is a card game based on the story of Nikolay Gogol. The game is in development.

The players will take a role of one of special orthodox monks fighting the evil spirits. Their task is to send the witch to the hell. It requires joint efforts to serve three nights for the repose of the "dead" witch and to do not allow her to return to the living world.

At the same time the monks have to constantly clean the temple from arriving evil spirits, sending them back to the underworld. During the night the monk can be affected by one or more demons. If the brothers don't conduct an exorcism in a proper time, affected monk moves to the side of evil forces. Such player gets a special assignment. His sole victory depends on this exercise.

It's a game made for making laugh the others. Each player will answear to the message sent by the narrator, writing the most original and funny thing. The 2 fastest players will take microfones to gain points in the improvisation points-track. Each player will then read his sentence and the others will vote with 1, 2 or 3 fingers (1 = not so fun, 2 = funny, 3 = hilarious). A player need a certain number of votes to gain points! Also, there are jokers that double the votes of a certain player. Master LOL is an unusual challenge for humorists! In one word, the game is made for laugh!

From back cover:

"Void is an Exciting fast paced miniature tabletop battle system for 2 or more players. The game is set in a universe where the homeworlds of Viridia, Ironglass and Prime battle for colonies in the farthest reaches of the galaxy. VASA, the policing boby of panhumanic space, tries to keep in check the ambitions of the imperialistic homeworlds. However with the emergence of the Koralon what remains to be seen is whether humanity can put aside its petty rivalry or whether mankind will become another victim of the aliens."

Void is played in turns, split in phases. In the third phase players take turns activating a squad and completing one order for the squad. Once all squads have completed their orders a new turn begins.

Victory conditions vary and are choosen before play starts. examples include; Annihilation of opponent, Most Survivors, Most Damage, Objectives, Scenarios or Time Limit.

Players build armies before game play starts based on a agreed upon point limit from army lists provided in rules.
500 points a couple hours
1000-1500 a afternoon
3000+ a full day

Reimplemented By Void 1.1.

boardgame: War Plan Red: North American Conflict
Monopoly: Phineas and Ferb Collector's Edition
description:
Phineas and Ferb want to make every day of their summer vacation spectacular. Jump into their decidedly quirky world-kiddie MythBusters meets sitcom-sibling hijinks meets classic spy spoof-which has become the top cable animated series among 6 to 14 year olds.

Phineas and Ferb want to make every day of their summer vacation spectacular! Jump into their decidedly quirky world-kiddie MythBusters meets sitcom-sibling hijinks meets classic spy spoof-which has become the top cable animated series among 6-to14-year-olds. Start your 104 days of summer right and join Phineas, Ferb, and the whole gang as you round Danville hot spots looking for the ultimate adventure: a chance to own it all! Can the Candace token finally stop Phineas and Ferb from their schemes? There's only one way to find out! In MONOPOLY: Phineas and Ferb Collector's Edition, you can buy, sell, and trade your way from the Googolplex Mall to the Hard D Har Toy store. Team up with the O.W.C.A to purchase Agent P's secret locations then scrounge up Dr. Doofenshmirtz's childhood homes to finally defeat him once and for all! Let the battle for summer glory begin!

En galen värld is a party game. One of the players reads a question, like "Why did Americans scalp Mexicans in the 16th century?" or "Why did an American billionaire fill an airplane with ping pong balls?" The other players then writes down an answer. The player how read the question then have to decide how of the other player that is closest to the answer. That player gets one point. The player with most points at the end of the game is the winner.

In this card game players try to be the first to form a sequence of cards reading "OPEN SESAME" in the one colour whilst taking care that the "40 Thieves" don't get the phrase first.

If playing for points, the first player to 1001 wins.

The power is yours...if you dare to take it.

The Council calls to the heroes of the realm in search of a new ruler to guide the kingdom to further prosperity. The Council is in need of a champion, and your past deeds have caught the eye of its members. Show them why you deserve the right to sit amongst the most powerful rulers in the land. This will be no small task as other powerful heroes will be answering the Council's call and building a reputation of their own. Some will create strong alliances, some will harness the earth and elements to awe and inspire the kingdom, while others will carve a bloody wound in the kingdom's enemies. How will you decide to gain the favor of...The Council?

Each turn in The Council, a player moves to a new location, facing random encounters along the way. While at locations, players can attempt to complete quests or recruit assistance for future encounters. When four public quests have been completed, the game ends. Victory points are awarded, and the player with the most VPs becomes the Council's selected champion.

description:
"In this game you pit your wits against chance, disease, moneylenders and an unjust world. Even survival is a success, but which of you will make most PROGRESS up the social ladder."

Each player takes the part of one family, consisting of 3 characters. Each family starts out as a labouring family, but by hard work, saving and a lot of luck, may afford to get one member of their family a better job. By advancing each member of the family, the players try to progress to the top of the social ladder.

A family game for 2-6 players, produced by Traidcraft, a UK charitable organisation.

boardgame: Hodge Podge, It's Not Just Trivia
description:
It's not over until the game picks the winner.  This game incorporates several mini-games that are played as you make your way to the End Game.  Success in the mini-games help increase the possibility of winning the End Game ans thus be selected the winner.

Trivia, memory, dice rolling luck, and use of resources are combined to create a fun and challenging game.  Not being good at trivia won't be a major stumbling block in this game, unlike other trivia games.  And not having a good memory won't mean you are doomed to lose the game either.

Every game plays differently and there are several aspects to this game that adds excitement and fun.  No matter how badly the game is going for you, you will still feel you have a chance right up until the winner is determined.

boardgame: Famous Flagships: The World's Smallest Yacht Racing Game
description:
The splash of salt spray across your forearm as you unfurl the spinnaker, the groan of the mast as it catches the wind, the thrill as you round the buoy and cut across the waves. Do you have what it takes to be a famous racing skipper? Find out today with Famous Flagships - the World's Smallest Yacht Racing Game!


Famous Flagships is a fun, fast-playing yacht racing game the captures the excitement of historic yachting regattas like the America’s Cup. The game pits a challenger and defender against each other amidst shifting winds and ever-changing fortunes. Both yachts fill their sails, displacing wind to either side and gaining speed as they steer the course. The result is a tight, tactical card game, with victory going to the first yacht to reach 50 knots. Famous Flagships is the sixth game in Famous Games Company’s line of World’s Smallest Sports Games and the first to introduce the line's new 11-card format.
family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada
Series: The World's Smallest Sports Games (Famous Games Company)

description:
A friendly sea creature that blows bubbles for you to catch. Grab a bubble catcher and let the fun begin! The catcher will count the bubbles. Listen for the "pop" as each bubble is counted. The one who catches the most bubbles will be the winner!

Includes:
Dolphin
Electronic Catcher
Indoor play mat
Bubble solution
Rules

This crazy chicken eats everything!
When you place your hand inside you'll hear wacky sounds of the many things this freaky fowl has swallowed! Remove the strange items the chicken has eaten without taking out any of the three organs!

Includes:
Chicken
7 Green Items
3 Chicken Organs
Rules

DARK WORLDS is a science-fiction strategy board game of space exploration, discovery, technological development and conflict, for two to four players. Each player assumes the role of a ruler of a space-faring civilisation. Rulers are tasked with spreading the influence of their civilisation throughout the universe. This requires the control of space sectors, and the discovery of unknown worlds (planets), with the aim of colonising them and harvesting their resources.

Planets come in a variety of types. Some, due to their harsh environments, support only limited populations. Others, such as lush, earth-like terran worlds, can support vast populations and have abundant resources. The greater the population that a colonised world supports, the more precious resources (and victory points) it will yield for the ruler that controls it.

Gathered resources pay for establishing colonies, and for technological research, such as hyper - drive engines and weapons for spacecraft, and improved planetary infrastructure. Each technology developed will yield particular advantages for a civilisation, in addition to providing victory points for winning the game. Resources may be spent to hire mercenary fighters, serving your battle for control of space sectors.

Some worlds also yield rare alien artefacts, which confer victory points for the player that finds them.

Ultimately, the ruler who controls the most developed worlds, controls the greatest number of space sectors, researches the most technologies, and discovers the most valuable artefacts will be victorious!

description:
The game looks like the real Monopoly, but totally focused on the animal theme. In dog-opoly you buy for example different breeds where you can place a doghouse or dog bone later in the game.

Watch out that you do not end up in the shelter or to pay a fine because you have fleas!
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

boardgame: Krimi total: Das Geheimnis der Burg Wolfsklamm
description:
Translation - "The secret of Castle Wolf's Glen"

The 10th case of the Krimi total series takes place in dark ages. Kunigunde von Kauzenstein was murdered at Castle Wolf's Glen.  
8 or 9 players slip into the roles of aristocrats and attendants, one of them is the murder.
His goal is not to be convicted, while all other try to find out the truth.

boardgame: The Great Heartland Hauling Co.: Badlands
description:
An expansion for The Great Heartland Hauling Co., Badlands introduces two new location cards that provide no initial products but have high demands for three products. These two special locations are placed on the outskirts of the playing area and are only playable with five players using the included fifth set of player pieces.

Includes:

     2 Badlands location cards (Serenity, Markham)

     1 player aide card


This is a promotional expansion initially distributed to Kickstarter backers of the main game and later distributed by Dice Hate Me Games at conventions and similar venues.

boardgame: Halt die Strasse gesperrt  das neuzeitliche Verkehrsspiel
description:
Objective: be the first entering space '100' - learn traffic signs
 - moving by turns -
simple 'Roll & move' game; ending on a illustrated space follow instructions printed on lid inside

Objective: be the first moving all your meeples 'home' - Pachisi-variant with a turnable wheel
 - moving by turns -

Move your 4 meeples clockwise around the board to your "home" section. All players move around the same board, so they may capture each others meeples. Captured meeples are returned to their player's starting point. With a '6' you may roll the die again, using this number turning the wheel forward or backwards.
remarks:
published late 1930's/early 40's

Place the 22 picnic cards. Each player in turn spins and moves his (or her) playing piece the number of spaces spun.

When a player lands on hot dog, he draws a picnic card and places it, color side up, in front of him.

When a player lands on a skunk, he must go back two spaces unless he has a picnic card. He discards one or more picnic cards to the bottom of the card pile and move forward one space for each card discarded. He may discard as many cards as he chooses. HINT: It may be a good idea to save some picnic cards for another turn.

The winner is the first player to reach Finish. A player does not need to spin the exact number to win.

With her stunning looks and wits, Dakota could have lived a comfortable life.  Instead, she chose a life of crime.  She did time for everything from robbery to car theft.  Her last sentence would have kept her inside long enough to go from the jail to the old folks home, but then the zombies came.

Dakota is done running and hiding.  She's here to claim her liberty and gain respect by spilling zombie blood anywhere she chooses to call home.  She speaks freely about her past to any survivor she meets and improvises according to their reaction.  But is she really the evil version she claims to be?

Dakota is an optional promotional survivor available to those that raise an extra amount on their pledge for the Zombicide Season Two Kickstarter campaign.  A zombie version (Zombivor) will also be included.

boardgame: Zombicide: Box of Zombies – Set #5: Zombie Dogz
description:
This box of Zombie Dogz includes 20 miniatures and the cards to add to the zombie deck of any existing Zombicide base game.

Zombie dogs are extremely fast and dangerous, getting three activations per turn.

Cards are numbered 55-66.

Heart Family trick-taking using double-six dominoes played by 3-4 players. There are eight suits (0-6 and "doubles") and each domino belongs to two suits. The highest is always the double, followed by high number to low number (for example, the suit of 4s has the order 4-4, 4-6, 4-5, 4-3, 4-2, 4-1, 4-0). The suit of doubles has the order from 6-6 down to 0-0. Leader can decide which suit and other player must follow the suit.

There are 11 points in the game. Seven dominoes of the suit of 3s worth 1 point and 0-0 worth 4 points. The goal is to avoid them and keep your score low. There is "shoot the moon" option and you make all other player score 22 points if you are successful.

Expansion decks 1,2, & 3 for Ultimate Soldier: 
Contains 3 Expansion Decks:
Revolution: 6 Soldiers, 5 Weapons, 1 Sniper
Civil War: 6 Soldiers, 5 Weapons, 1 Sniper
Wild West: 6 Soldiers, 5 Weapons, 1 Sniper

Use Expansion soldiers and weapons to play the game Ultimate soldier with mercenaries from different eras.

How to use the expansion sets.

Remove 6 soldiers and 4 weapons and a sniper from the base game. Replace them with the 6 soldiers, 5 weapons, and sniper from one from one of the expansion sets and play the game with the same rules. Power weapons for the expansion soldier are listed on their cards.

Nyans is a game where people are playing in teams. One team member will consider one image out of six that looks nearly the same. Then the other team members will then have to guess which one the other one is thinking on, by him or her with words, gestures and/or pictures describing it, before the one minute timer runs out. If the team answers right they will go forward on the point track. First to the end of the point track is the winner.

Fred used to be a mathematical genius with intuition and an aggressive instinct.  Trading was second nature for him, and while he regretted some of his decisions, he enjoyed his job a lot.  Still, he felt something missing, a thirst his huge bonuses could not quench.

Then the zombies came, changed his world, and brought him the answer.  A predator at heart, Fred needed real action to feel alive.  He was the first on the trading floor to understand what was going on and what he needed to do.  Fred was the only one to get out alive.  His real life had just begun.

Fred is an optional promotional survivor available to those that raise an extra amount on their pledge for the Zombicide Season Two Kickstarter campaign. A zombie version (Zombivor) will also be included.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Blood of Gondor
description:
'Game description from the publisher:

"We have this honour: ever we bear the brunt of the chief hatred of the Dark Lord, for that hatred comes down out of the depths of time and over the deeps of the Sea."
    –Denethor, The Return of the King

After Lord Boromir valiantly leads the war host of Gondor to reclaim the city of Osgiliath, he asks the Rangers of Ithilien to pursue their retreating foes. He also asks several of Middle-earth's greatest heroes to join the hunt.

The Blood of Gondor is the fifth Adventure Pack in the "Against the Shadow" cycle for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game. It immerses players in the dangers and the thrills of the hunt. They control Middle-earth's heroes in an all-new scenario in which they seek to stop their foes from retreating and regrouping. However, the forest roads of Ithilien are full of perils, and the Orcs and Uruks of Mordor lurk in wait along the way. Will the hunters become the hunted? The Blood of Gondor introduces scenario-specific rules for "hidden" cards and the dark menace of the Black Númenorean.

Not only do the sixty cards in The Blood of Gondor introduce a new scenario, but they also offer players a new hero and three copies each of several new player cards that reinforce the "Against the Shadow" cycle's focus on the Gondor trait and cards that enhance the viability of decks built from a single sphere.

Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
A free racing game, distributed in Game Workshop's 1997 Games Day conference handbook.

Includes cardboard tokens which may be used in lieu of their miniatures.  Poke and prod your horde of cave squigs to the finishing line faster than your opponents, or even send your pack to chomp down another team.

As a nice bonus touch, the additional rooms provided fit in nicely with Warhammer Quest pieces.

boardgame: Conquest of Planet Earth: Santarri Mutant Men Supplement
description:
The new cards in the Santarri Mutant Men Supplement are designed to be shuffled right into the existing COPE card decks and will give your Aliens powerful new Events and Space Stuff.  But beware, the National Guard are out in full force to stop you and they have brought along a very well-armed and very ornery redneck as a local guide!

This pack was included as a bonus for those who purchased Conquest of Planet Earth: Apocalypse at GenCon and PAX in 2012, but now seems to be exclusively available from the Flying Frog website.
