description:
Salvage Team is a tile-based board game about looting starship wreckages. The players control a group of salvagers who loot valuables off wrecks before massive recyclers reduce them to metal bricks.

The players build a group of salvagers and define their team leader. As long as the group travels together they can use their leader's abilities and properties. Alone, however, they can cover more ground, but are more vulnerable to threats and other salvagers.

At the end of each round, each player's Recyclers remove one tile from the game board. The goal of Salvage Team is to get as much loot off the wreck before the last tiles are eaten. The game can be played in a single match or a longer campaign.

Statistical sport game. Amazing theme taken, a very early British Football League season - and he is Swedish!

As usual with these games, dice are thrown and cross-checked on various charts and tables. You can easily play the whole season in an evening (there were only 11 teams in those days).

Statistical game by one of the genre's much acclaimed designer.

Based on a professional ten pin bowling league that was existed at the time in America (maybe it still exists, I don't know).

As usual with these games, dice are thrown and the result cross-checked against various charts and tables. Each match is resolved quickly so a season can be replayed in a relatively short time.

Statistical Sport game by a Swedish designer. Fast replay tennis matches, so you can play a tournament such as Wimbledon in short time (not every round obviously but certainly from the quarter-final matches onwards.

Roll dice and cross-check result against various charts and tables to get a result of a match

Objective: be the first entering space '90' - learn traffic signs
- moving by turns -
simple 'Roll & move' game; special: two (red & blue) separated routes, you may choose one of them; use two dice; 
ending on a marked (red or blue) space follow instructions printed on lid inside.
remarks:
a stoplight is included, which must be operated with a battery & touched during described situations

Players of LIFER asume the role of prisoners in Rawdeal Prison, equipped only with a handful of cigarettes they use as currency to acquire their stash of inventory.

To begin, up to four players are dealt 6 cigarette cards of various values. These are then traded in turn for cards with the corresponding values in the Inventory deck. Players begin to amass equipment such as, Bolt Cutters, Files, Picks and Blueprints to set them en route to escape.

There are 3 separate routes out of jail, and prisoners must collect the correct combination of inventory cards to complete their escape. The first to collect the correct combination is the winner.

But all is not simple, the Inventory deck is peppered with Trick Cards, such as the Warden and the Thug, which allow players to poach cards from their opponents. There are also detention cards such as Solitary and Infirmary which alters players progress through the game.

A fast strategy and tactics dice game for 2–4 players. The game comprises 36 dice than can be placed in a 6×6 grid on a playing board. Each player considers the outer row of six dice which he is facing (his 'home row') and will try that either a) all six dice show the same number, or b) the dice be placed in ascending (1-2-…-6) or descending (6-5-…-1) order. For this, 35 dice are placed in the playing area leaving an empty space, and the 36th die is used as action die: the active player rolls it, and the rolled value can be split into several sub-values (for example: 6 = 3+1+1+1) to perform one or more of several possible actions, such as turning a die, swap files, etc.
DICE provides several options to build the starting dice grid, as well as advanced rules for a longer and more challenging game experience (additional dice moves and a more challenging winning condition). 
DICE is available in several colored sets: Natur (36 white dice); Regenbogen (6× 6 dice in each of the colors red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and violet); Devil Dice (black dice with red pips, and the pips are randomly placed on the die faces, making it impossible to predict which value will show after turning a die); and also two- or three-color dice sets.
More colors means a more complicated game and more possible tactical variations, as additional winning conditions can be established (for example, a home row with alternating black and white dice, if a two color DICE set is used).
According to the rules leaflet included with the game, there also exists a set of 121 dice called MARATHON DICE.

The game recreates the atmosphere of a Motogp Race based on real statistical data.

It's possible to make a single race or the whole MOTOGP championship of 2011,2102,2013 and 2015 season.

The game it's specially designed for solitaire play.

Pilot cards, circuit cards, race sheet, miniatures and a generic circuit board need to develop every race.

The game needs also the use of serveral dice (1d10 and more d6 dice).

Game lenght 45 minutes - 1 hour per race.

THE GAME IS FREE TO PRINT AND PLAY
IN ITALIAN / ENGLISH

description: This expansion set includes 2 sets of three different dogs.  Their accompanying cards are placed in the equipment deck, and when found, the holder of the card becomes that dog's handler.  Actions can be spent to command the dog to move, search, and attack.  Dogs give their handler extra melee dice when in the same zone.

Game description from the publisher:

In the party game Words of Art, players use the letter cards in their hand to build words – the catch is, the words have to relate to the text on the Description Card, or with the picture on the Art Card.

Luckily each of your cards shows two letters on it, and you choose which letter to use on each card. Each letter has a point value, plus you'll get bonus points for every card used, so creativity is rewarded!

Game description from the publisher:

In the fast-playing, five-suited card game Stop It! players battle to lay down all of their cards. There are no set turns. Whoever has the next card in numerical and color sequence is the one who plays. With careful planning, you can run the table – but don't get cocky. Opponents can stop gameplay by claiming a "Stop It!" card and start a new run of their own.

4 players have similar puzzle with different colors and they start to race to build a puzzle on the middle of the table.
If the puzzle is ready the player who has the most puzzle-pieces on his color placed on the middle is the winner.

Food for thought is a nutrition themed board game. The emphasis is on eating a balanced diet exemplified by 6 food attributes of Fat, Carbohydrates, Fiber, Vitamins, Minerals and Calories. It is part chance (dice) and part strategy (players decide whether or not to 'eat' the food card they land on) based. The theme in general is also to introduce younger players to the above mentioned food attributes and make them more informed about nutrition.

The players start off with all food attributes on the 'good' row of the 'nutritional grid'. Depending on the food choices they make, their counters move up or down the grid until one of the food attribute counters reaches the "lose a life" row, at which point they lose a life. Players can also un-balance other players nutrition grids via the "Payback time", "switch identity" and "Chance" events. Interspersed with the food cards are "exercise", "catch some rays", "water" and "regular doctor's visit"c cards that teach the importance of these attributes.

The goal of the game is to stay alive (by not having any counter reach the "lose a life" row on the nutritional grid) by 'eating' the 'right' (balanced) foods. Another goal also is to make your opponents die by making them eat the 'wrong' foods as a result of a "chance", "payback time" or "switch identity" event. The player who is the last to stay alive wins the game.

Please visit http://foodforthoughtgame.com for a complete description of the game as well as a picture of the actual game board.

A Game of Valence

The object of the game is to create the most particles. Players take turns dropping a proton on an empty cell. When a proton is dropped, the surrounding cells are examined in order to determine if an electron is to be created there or not. An electron is created in a surrounding cell if there is a majority of one color's protons surrounding that cell. In that case, the newly created electron takes on that color and orbits the surrounding protons. If the number of surrounding protons of either color is the same, then an electron is not created in that cell, or if the cell already contains an electron, that electron vanishes.

The score indicators display the total number of particles owned by each player. The game ends when no remaining proton placements are available. The player with the most particles wins. Ties are not possible.

Mad scientists need some corpse parts to build their creature, Igor will help them reach their goal by digging into the graveyard. There are also other villagers and a vampire living near the towers where lurk mad scientists. The weather is changing fast in this village...

Each player controls a mad scientist and his servant Igor, they begin the game in their own tower where they lead some exepriments about life and death. 
Igor will have to bring, arms, legs and chests to the tower while the mad scientist will have to look for a fresh head by killing a villager. When a mad scientist has all parts in his possession and the weather is stormy, he reanimates the monster and claims victory.

Foul Play is a party game for groups from 6 - 12. It is a fast paced who done it full of bluffing, lying, deceit, and fun. It only takes minutes to learn and you can play all night.

A narrator guides the game as players take roles in a murder mystery to try to find out who the murder is.

In Crosswalk players try to get the highest score after three rounds by moving their pawns (pedestrians) back and forth across the crosswalk.

A four player game

The crosswalk contains ten spaces marked from 1-10 moving east to west.

You will need 40 cards in four colors (Red-Blue-Green-Yellow) and numbered from 1-10. Use four cubes, meeples, or pawns in four colors ( Red-Blue-Green-Yellow ) these are pedestrians and are placed on the number 1 slot on the crosswalk at the start of game.

The Deal

Shuffle the deck.

With 2-4 players you deal five cards one at a time to each player face down, the rest of the deck is placed face down as stock. As the game progresses players may be dealt less cards.

The Play
Players then look over their hand, any cards not belonging to their suit are discarded face down next to the stock making a discard pile.

Any players not having at least one card in their suit must discard their whole hand face down to the discard pile and re-dealt five cards.

Each player chooses a card from his hand and places it face down before him. After all cards are down, they are turned over simultaneously.

This is called a match and the winner of the match is determined by who has the higher rank.

The player with the highest ranking card moves his pawn in his suit color to the corresponding number on the crosswalk.

The other players then compare their rank values with the lead players rank, the difference between them, is where the other players move their pawns to.

Example: The lead card is Red-7 the other players cards are Green-6, Blue-4, Yellow-4. Now the difference between Green-6 and Red-7 is 1, so the green pawn is moved to the #1 spot. The difference between Blue-4 and Yellow-4 to Red-7 is 3 so the Blue and Yellow pawn are moved to the #3 spot.

Players then play all remaining cards in their hands in the same manner, any players left with cards after one of the other players have none, must discard their remaining hand to the discard pile face down.

Now you return to The Deal, when the stock is empty the discard pile is made into the new stock.

The game stops when the stock and discard pile are empty then you score the round.

The Score
The game is played in 3 rounds.

The numbers on the crosswalk are also the scoring track, so (1=10) (2=20) (3=30) (4=40) (5=50) (6=60) (7=70) (8=80) (9=90) (10=100)

The number on the crosswalk that your pawn is on after the game stops is your score for that round.

The player with the highest score after 3 rounds is declared the winner.

A two player game

All the rules are the same as in a four player game except as follows.

At the start of a two player game, players decide which two of the four color suits they are going to play and which two corresponding pawns are theirs to move and to score with. Use the higher score out of the two pawns at the end of each round.

A three player game

From the deck decide which suit of the four color suits to discard, also discard the pawn of that corresponding suit color, these cards and pawn are not to be used in the game. You should now have a deck of 30 cards in three suits and three pawns to play with. From this point on, all the rules are the same as in a four player game.

Headstrong and resourceful, Lea was one of the rare teenagers who hung out with the popular kids and made the honor role.  Had her fiery temper not caused so much mischief among teachers and classmates alike, she could have reached the very top.  Society and its dumb rules didn't fit her taste, and that kept her from having it all.  The zombies changed all that.

Life has gotten more challenging for Lea.  She has outsmarted and outgunned most opponents so far, but lately she finds she's afraid to be left alone.  She is learning to swallow her pride and trust the other survivors.  Zombies are teaching her how life can be easier when you have friends you can rely on.

Lea is an optional promotional survivor available to those that raise an extra amount on their pledge for the Zombicide Season Two Kickstarter campaign. A zombie version (Zombivor) will also be included.

Nudge out the other players to snag the best position on the table to show off your award winning desserts.  In this flicking, flipping dexterity game you will flick your tokens on the table to surround and capture opponents’ pieces while you try to defend your own desserts.  Keep an eye out for dessert eating creeps, don’t get raw egg on your desserts and, if you can, claim the trophy to protect your desserts for a time.

Similar to Uno with a special card for this edition: Pinkititis - if you come down with a case of Pinkititis, there's only one way to cure it: discard it and cover it up with all the green cards in your hand! You may choose the next color to resume play. If you do not have any green cards, use the card as a wild and select the next color to resume play. The point value of the Pinkititis card is 50 points

Álmok is a game about deep buried memories in the mind. It is a journey between dreams and nightmares. It is an experience based on the unconscious perceptions of the oniric mind.

In Álmok, the players are in deep sleep, trying to collect parts of their dreams to scape an eternal nightmare. Use your memory, find your way to the wakening, tap in sleeping powers of the unconscious mind, be the first to get free from your dreams.

Álmok uses the classic Memory Game mechanic with some twists to make the game more dynamic and enjoyable. In your turn, you turn cards face up and check if they are a group, like the classic game, but here, the groups must have 3 cards, but you can combine then using their color or their number, much like a rummy game. Each group you form, give you points and also a special dream ability that you can use later to gain even more points or save for bonus points in the end of the game.

The player who reaches a certain amount of points first (based on the number of players), is the winner.

Cartas Assombradas (Haunted Cards in English) is a game where the players are supernatural investigators in a journey inside an old haunted manor. In each round, the players try to explore more and more of the house to prove their courage and gain points. The bravest (or luckiest) player that was not catch by the house's ghosts and explored more of the house, win more points. When dawn came, the player with more points is the winner.

Using a Push Your Luck mechanic, in your turn, each player take a card from the stack. If it is a house hallway, it means that the player explored that hallway and receives its points. If it is a ghost, he can lose some points, or even get out of the round. There are also equipments to help players in this adventure. Before he plays, a player can choose to leave the house in that round and gain the points he collect so far. If a ghost catches him, he loses all points.

The game ends when dawn came, being dawn a card that is shuffled in the deck before the third round.

Kose Bucak Turkiye is a word game hosting Turkey which minimum 4 people can play in 2 teams. The players proceed on the game board and they draw cards in regional steps, and explain the card to their team member(s) by talking. And they use the cards that the other member guessed; in their “targets” areas on the game board.

This is a special premium-painted version of Koshka and her Grand'Ma walker that come in the Operation Zverograd expansion.  Because it includes its own reference cards, this is playable with or without the Zverograd expansion.  It is available for purchase by Elite members of Paolo Parente's Dust website.

From the website description:

We are very proud to introduce our first Elite Exclusive item: Koshka and her own walker Grand'Ma. They are both presented here in their “Night Ops” version, used during many special operations in all parts of the world where the SSU is present. Koshka is the most active and successful SMERSH operative ever. Many of the SSU bold operations have been made possible thanks to her and the skills she has piloting her very own war machine.

As you can see this “Elite Version” comes with a special diorama base that can also serve as a playing base for Grand'Ma (it takes one space on the field). The walker can also be placed on a regular grey round base that we have magnetized: you can swap Grand'Ma from one base to another with ease.
We also magnetized Koshka: she can be played independently, the rock under her feet is the same size as a regular base. And thanks to the magnet she can be placed back on the large base with her robot with instantly. In an instant Koshka and Grand'Ma can go from a Collector Item to being fully playable. And of course, as usual, everything is already painted and you can enjoy it as soon as you open the box.

This particular pose of the KV47, on which Grand'Ma is based on, has been specifically created by a good friend of Dust Studio: Italian modeler Livio Emanueli. He has taken the time to help Vincent create this unique model for you during his last visit in our production facility in China.

Of course these two models come with their very own unit cards, but these are stat and skill wise identical to the regular Dust Tactics version.

description:
Objective: Using the detailed street map of Hannover each player is given five companies to be established in the city as close to the centre as possible.

With the help of dice and counters, each players chooses where to place, turn or remove company tiles.
Once a company is established on the map, the corresponding four 'places worth seeing' are revealed, so familiarizing players with the sights of the city.

Contents: 80 cards, 8 counters, 3 dice, rules and city information.

Leprechaun Gold is a hidden treasure game.

Players start with an old Journal written by Prince Domnall in 1017 in Ireland. His curiosity leads him on an adventure. As a player you follow his tracks from the journal and use the map to find clues that will lead you to the hidden Leprechaun Gold.

Game requires set up from a 3rd party.

Game set up.
The game includes an instruction sheet, it guides the set up with common household items.

Players start at a designated location and read the first 2 entries in Prince Philip's journal. Then follow his clues to step one on the map and the first clue location set up before the beginning of the game.

At the first location players must figure out 2 riddles and write down 3 characters that will be used later in the game.

There are 6 stages to stop at each with a riddle to solve and more characters that help put together the final clue.

There are several variations of riddles and characters so the game can be played over and over with a different path  each time.

A remake of the original Ultima 1 computer game by Richard Garriott but in Print & Play boardgame format. For [1] player/solitaire gaming.

The game will consist of all [4] map board continents with each map containing four quadrants printed on 11x17 sheets, which allows you to play on one board at a time or put them all together for one big gameboard.

You can play as one of four character types: THIEF, MAGE, FIGHTER or CLERIC who adventures across the lands of Sosaria, ie gameboards, to accomplish goals, gather gold, gather gems, weapons, armor, spells and scrolls, travelling aids, and equipment.  Adventure into Dark Dungeons, Castles, and Towns for Supplies, Equipment and information. Speak with Kings to gather information about the location of all four gems, Fight wandering monsters and evil individuals, open chest to find riches, plus level up and increase your characters abilities and win the game by discovering secret information, finding lost treasures, and defeating the evil overlord "Big Boss" Mondain.

All the fun of the original Ultima 1 but with more challenges, more monsters, more riches, and more fame... plus in a Print & Play board game format.

I've taken some liberty with the game since a board game can't do everything a computer can do but should keep the original play as close to the PC game as possible.

For those of you who have played through the original PC game, you will find that this board game will be similar experience with you traveling, searching, and fighting your way across Sosaria.  Just remember that you are now the computer and will be tracking all your stats and information.

I will also try to add as many random elements to the game so each time you play it will be the same Ultima 1 but with a little different experience.

boardgame: Star Wars: The Card Game – The Battle of Hoth
description:
Game description from the publisher:

"Attack pattern delta. Go now!"
    –Luke Skywalker, Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back

In The Battle of Hoth, the fifth Force Pack in "The Hoth Cycle" for Star Wars: The Card Game, massive and heavily armored Imperial AT-ATs march forward in inexorable waves while Rebel snowspeeder pilots employ daring group tactics to slow their advance. Meanwhile, the desperate fight on the desolate ice planet of Hoth is destined to have tremendous repercussions throughout the galaxy. Will the Empire's AT-ATs and stormtrooper legions surround, siege, and destroy the last remnants of the Rebellion? Or will the valiant sacrifices of the Rebel Alliance's rearguard hold the lines long enough for their allies to find safety?

Inspired by the early scenes from The Empire Strikes Back, the five new objective sets in The Battle of Hoth allow players to recreate the movie's events or to imagine alternate outcomes. This Force Pack could witness the end of the Rebel Alliance, or it could open an opportunity for its noble spies and smugglers to conduct critical espionage while the Empire's eyes are turned toward Hoth. As Imperial walkers continue their march and Rogue squadron's snowspeeders race across the icy battlefield, the fate of the galaxy is on the line!

Contains objective sets 59-63.

52 cards with mathematical operation or answers.
Players try to play from the hand operation-cards like "4+5" or "18-9" to get a correct answer card from the table like "9".
If the deck is exhausted the player with the most answer cards is the winner.
3 more game variants included.

Game description from the publisher:

Who will write the future? Creation and Control pits a new wave of enigmatic Shapers against the latest and greatest of Haas-Bioroid's developments in artificial intelligence, efficiency, and security. The first deluxe expansion for Android: Netrunner The Card Game, Creation and Control introduces 165 new cards (three copies each of fifty-five individual cards) that will instigate dramatic new strategies and high-stakes battles for the control of valuable files and the futures outlined within them.

Creation and Control focuses on the struggles between the executives at Haas-Bioroid and those Shapers who are driven to tinker with their programs and hardware by an almost-religious compulsion, but fans of every Android: Netrunner faction will find plenty of great uses for their influence, as well as twenty-seven neutral cards (three copies each of nine individual cards) that can sharpen the focus of any deck.

family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Objective: Using the detailed street map of Leipzig each player is given five companies (each divided in 4 pieces/tiles) to be established in the city as close to the centre as possible.

With the help of dice and counters, each players chooses where to place, turn or remove company tiles.
Once a company is established on the map, the corresponding four 'places worth seeing' are revealed, so familiarizing players with the sights of the city.
remarks:
game approach is identical with the 'cityprogame' family; game production was financed by advertisements of locally based companies

boardgame: Die Legenden von Andor: Das letzte Lagerfeuer
description:
This card is part of the Spielbox magazine issue 01/2013.

It is a single card expansion for Legends of Andor to be used with Legends 2-5 to slightly reduce the difficulty of the legends.

RULES:
At the beginning of the game a dice roll decides at which point the card comes into play. According to the dice value the card will be  placed besides the legend bar. The card is activated if the narrator reaches this point of the legend bar.

The card allows all players to give and receive willpower and strength with the other players. Any number of points can be moved among the players. The heroes do not need to be in the same zone. A hero must always have at least 1 willpower and 1 strength left.

If other events happen at this point of the legend bar, this card always happens last.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
publishers: Festival Internacional de Juegos de Córdoba

description: A bingo-style game, Speed Place Value uses a novel randomizer element.  Each of three domed shakers contains four marbles - red, yellow, green and blue.  Each also has four divots labeled with place values like "ones", "millions" and "hundredths".  The color-coordinated Bingo cards have numbers on them where the four relevant digits are colored in a matching set of colors.  At the start of each round, someone shakes the shaker until the four balls settled into the four divots.  Players then evaluate the nine numbers on their game boards for digits that match the place value/marble color pairs, placing a chip on a match.  The first player to place three chips in a row wins.

All out video game war with a board game.

Take control of the 3 video game theaters or earn 16 bits to win.

Press Start is a 3-6 player game with a co-op expansion to make it a 4-10 player game.

There are 6 theaters of video gaming. Each player rolls for the chance to pick where they start. Highest point total on the dice chooses first.

Each player lays their game cartridge and a piece of wire in the theater and on the tile where they want to build. Each player lays down 2 cartridges and 2 lengths of wire to start the game.

Commodities that are associated with the tiles that players touch are gathered. To gain control of a theater or area a player must build up to having 2 working consoles or cabinets. Players upgrade by trading commodities and action cards or by buying a pause cards. Players can by parts or earn them by battling others and taking their items.

A player must gain control of 3 theaters or gain 16 bits to win.

On each players turn they roll the dice, dice differ depending on what theater they are in. A player can only play in one theater per turn. They may have occupancy in as many theaters as they want, but they must choose which theater to use on their turn.

A player can make as many moves as they like on each turn until they run out of commodities, action, parts or pause cards.

Press Start Pocket is a 2 player game deck of 56 cards.

Each deck (Series 1, Series 2, Series 3) is a stand-alone 2 player game. Two decks can be combined for a 3-4 player game, and 3 decks can be combined for a 5-6 player game. Combining decks also gives expanded capabilities for warriors.


Battle to win control of the pixel world.

Players start with a video game warrior and battle their opponent to take control of the video game universe.

There is no hiding, to win you have to battle.

Overview:

Both players get 7 cards and the rest of the cards are placed in 2 piles face down in the middle of the table.

Pile 1 is a warrior card pile

Pile 2 is an action card pile

The first player draws and action card then either attacks, plays a gather card to earn points toward another warrior or discards.

During a battle the players follow the attacks and counters rule according to the reference card.

To win a player must win 3 battles or earn 4 soldiers all alive at once.

Integrates with:


    Press Start Pocket: Series 2 
    Press Start Pocket: Series 3

Objective: Using the detailed street map of Berlin each player is given five companies to be established in the city as close to the centre as possible.

With the help of dice and counters, each players chooses where to place, turn or remove company tiles.
Once a company is established on the map, the corresponding four 'places worth seeing' are revealed, so familiarizing players with the sights of the city.

Contents: 80 cards, 8 counters, 3 dice, rules and city information.

boardgame: もぐもぐハント (Mogu Mogu Hunt)
description:
'Let's hunt for some yummy earthworms!'

Mogu Mogu Hunt is a fast paced game, where Moles (Mogu's) are trying to bring back some supper.  Players are Moles who are digging into the earth to see what delicious snacks they can find.  They take turns in placing food (visible & hidden) at the end of the tunnels.  Then players take turns in placing path tiles in front of their moles or another players mole, forming tunnels layer by layer into the earth.  Once all the tiles are placed, the paths are resolved.  Each mole follows the tunnel paths downwards, turn at each intersection, and if they reach food they pick that up and score points.   But be careful, sometimes you might end up with some poison mushrooms (-1 point).  The mole who gathers the most delicious food (scores the most points) is the winner.

This is the first game designed by dGames, and was released at the Tokyo Game Market in Nov 2012.

Objective: 
A   improve your ability to create words out of letters -
if wanted in a competitional modus
B   improve your ability using basic arithmetic operations -
if wanted in a competitional modus
use the phone checking result

Each player is confronted with the same task; the faster you find the correct answer the earlier you may use the phone reviewing your result - correct answers give a bonus points;
question sheets are available showing different levels of difficulty

comparable (Lesetelefon only) with Dial N Spell

In order to assist in the construction of their space stations, each race has formed a team of ambassadors to visit other space stations in need. These ambassadors are willing to offer their services and strengthen the bonds of the newly-formed alliance.

These ambassadors are introduced in the game with the addition of a fourth action that allows the players to invite them to their station and make them part of their Council. Each ambassador comes with a different ability, based on his race's strengths and capabilities. However, there is limited space in each player's Council so they have to choose wisely who they are going to invite.

Among the Stars: The Ambassadors is a boxed expansion for Among the Stars featuring more than 150 new cards. Apart from the Ambassador cards, it includes more than twenty different new Locations, new Objectives, a new Alien race, new Conflict sets (to replace the original one, rather than mix with).
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Objective: identify as many as possible of the cards shown to you
 - acting by turns -
the pile of (72) cards is shuffled; a certain amount (depending on difficulty level) of cards is faced up and quickly turned down again. Active player must mark (with red piles) as many symbols on the board as thought being shown. Each matching symbol gives a victory point, a non matching ones a malus.
next player's turn..

In Warlords of Rhaemar your Warlords face off against other mighty Warlords. Choose your equipment wisely and you may just come out ahead.

This is a card based equipment strategy game. You draw or draft your Warlords and then draw from the 4 equipment decks. Each round you choose a Warlord to play and then equip them with mighty artifacts or lash out at your foes with horrible curses. Surviving warlords are scored at the end of the round and a new round takes place until every player has been starting player. After the final round, the player with the most victory points is the Ultimate Warlord!

Objective: be the first finding the slide 'Pünktchen und Anton' after having collected a complete set (6) of slides (persons) who are present in the identically named movie/novel from Erich Kästner
 - acting/moving by turns -
all slides (25) are shuffled and placed face down on the board;
each player moves according dice roll; ending on a space with a slide player may keep it if it is a missing piece. If not, slide must be placed back, also on another free space. Throwing a double player may choose: move and roll again - or jump to any corner - or jump into Pünktchen's home (because later in the game you will find dumped slides at this place, too; handicap: get out with a double only.

In Scoralet, each player receives a small tray with a 5x5 grid of felted spaces.  Between the players on the table are placed square wooden tiles with letters on them.  Each player in turn calls out a letter and the other players pick this letter from the central pile and place it in one of the spaces on their tray.  Their goal is to spell out as many 3, 4, or 5 letter words as possible, left-to-right or top-to-bottom.

Play continues until all 25 spaces are filled.  5 letter words score 15 points, 4 letter words score 10, and three letter words score 5.  2 letter words do not score.  Smaller words within larger words also may score their own points, so long as the larger word is not simply a plural of the smaller.  In that case, only the larger plural word scores.  Proper names of people or places do not count unless agreed upon prior to the game's start.  Highest score wins.

The distribution of the letter tiles breaks down for each player in the following manner:

a-3, b-2, c-1, d-2, e-3, f-1, g-1, h-1, i-2, j-1, k-1, l-2, m-1, n-2, o-3, p-2, q-1, r-2, s-2, t-2, u-2, v-1, w-1, x-1, y-1, z-1.

This totals 42 tiles for each player.  Prior to start, the pile must be made of this many tiles for each player, and no more.  84 tiles for two, 126 tiles for three, 168 tiles for four players.

Print ads from 1936 referred to this game as the "successor to Anagrams."  Two editions were sold, a two-player edition for 50 cents and a four-player edition for 1 dollar.

Zombie Shuffle is a game for 2 to 4 players that lasts about 5 to 10 minutes by Blaise Sewell and Feels Right Design. Just like in any zombie story the object of the game is to survive the zombie apocalypse. Before you begin, you set up the Zombie Cards in this nifty "Z" shape and pick your Survivor Card to represent you in the game.

Everyone is dealt 5 cards that are a random set of weapons, some of which offer a utility function, but ALL of them can be used to Attack Zombies and other Survivors.
Zombie Shuffle cycles between a Night Phase, where you must defeat incoming zombies, and a Day Phase that offers the opportunity to prepare for the next zombie attack. The game begins when the first zombie in the "Z" is flipped. Survivors must Attack the zombie with ANY 2 through 10 card corresponding with that zombie's suit.

The winner keeps the Zombie card to track their number of kills. If a player does not lay down a card corresponding with that zombie's suit then they are considered Attacked and either lose equipped armor or become a zombie!Before the next zombie card is flipped, Survivors can use the Zombie Cure, Equip Armor (which protects from Attacks), Steal another Survivor's Weapons/Supplies or may instead decide to Attack another Survivor.

Just like Attacking a Zombie, the Attack Card must correspond with the Survivor you're Attacking. If a Survivor is a Zombie and loses one of these battles then they are Dead for the remainder of the game. The other Survivor gets to keep their card, again to track their number of kills, and scavenge the dead player's cards.

The Day Phase ends when  everyone has had a chance to play a Utility or Attack Card and everyone has drawn enough cards to have 5 cards in their hand again.

The winner is the Survivor with the most kills at the end of the game. This has been a quick rundown of the game play and there are a few more awesome quirks to Zombie Shuffle not listed above. (For example, each Survivor has a unique Melee Weapon that can give them a Power-Up when equipped.

boardgame: Zombicide: Box of Zombies – Set #2: Toxic Crowd
description:
This box includes 24 toxic zombies and 6 additional spawn cards to include them into any Zombicide base game.

Sculpts of the zombies are from the Zombicde: Toxic City Mall, although three exclusive sculpts are included.

Zombie sculpt breakdown
15 Toxic Walkers (5 of a mix of 2 Box of Zombies only sculpts)
6 Toxic Runners
3 Toxic Fatties (2 of the Box of Zombies only sculpt)

Spawn cards (133-138) come with a higher density of Toxic zombies than Toxic City Mall's deck.

boardgame: The Game of Going To The Klondike
description: Ca. 1898 early McLoughlin Bros. game involving the lust for gold in the great Northwest in the 1890s. Beautiful graphics of prospectors braving the wild untamed frontier in search of riches enhance this very scarce and desirable game for game collectors or gold rush fanatics. Interior of game shows the great Northern Lights over a series of dangerous mountain passes and rugged terrain. SIZE: 18-3/4" l. x 10-1/4" w.

Victus recreates the the ancient world’s most deadly sport, Gladitorial Combat! This is a complete stand alone game that puts two or more players in control of history’s greatest fighting machines using Dynamic Movement, Abilities Pooling and pure Cunning! The rules allow for play of one off games to settle old scores, bracketed tournaments or full immersion in the Ancient world using the Victus Campaign system. In the campaign, Gladiators battle to win their freedom or to gather glory as they advance in both wealth and skill. The Fortuna tables are used to expand a Gladiator's fate beyond the arena.  The rulebook comes with it’s own board and paper models to allow for quick start to play; however, since gameplay takes place on a hex board, scale is not limited.

Players use a system to record their actions each turn and perform them in the order of a contested Initiative, which is determined by a roll off with modifiers for gear based on weight. Although Actions are recorded, maneuvering takes place in each turn as players perform counter strikes and have chances to maneuver in response to their opponent. Guard and Facing play a role in this.  Gladiators use their Fortitudo (used for striking), Agilito (used for defense and movement), and Virtus (used for stamina and as a resource pool) to perform their Actions each turn through the phases. As wounds are dealt, Gladiators become weaker.

Weapons have different effects against different armor and carry their own advantages and disadvantages in relation to the fighting styles. For example the Thracian's Sica is able to reach behind a Myrmillo's shield more effectively.  Striking takes place with models either targeting specific areas of the body or attacking untargeted areas with both players rolling in Attack/Defense. Armor for specific areas hit are taken into account and then if the attack passes through the armor the player rolls on a Wounding Table which can lead to anything from a Stun to a Fatal Wound. Fatal Wounds are worked up to, Stuns count as Modifiers to Abilities and subsequent rolls on the Wound Table. Gameplay invloves combatants striking, counter striking and working each other over in the process.

As this action is taking place, a Gladiator can attempt to ask for Missus, i.e. Mercy (if the match allows!) and depending on his performance and roll on the Missus table he could prolong his fighting career before being seriously wounded.

Shi**y Shark Movie, a game parody of shark movie and creature feature tropes (especially the bad ones), is a card and dice resource management game where players move their ships' pawns around a 5x5 constantly changing ocean layout of cards with varying backs representing swimmers, sharks, creatures, and open water items which could be almost anything...good or bad.
To succeed, players will outfit custom ships and crew, needing certain ones to adopt particular strategies (for example, a ship must have a lifeguard crew member in order to save swimmers).
Whoever finishes the game with the most saved swimmers and killed/captured sharks is the winner.

Objective: be the first reaching target space (113)
 - moving by turns -
simple 'Roll & Move' game; ending on a marked space follow instructions...

This 1930s German game is a spin-and-move tour around Europe. There is a spinner in each corner of the board, and the four cute cars provided are in nicely painted ceramic: a very unusual feature in an era of typically metal or wooden pieces.

Each player starts and finishes in Berlin, and may travel either clockwise or counterclockwise. Players must follow directions in the rules if they land on one of Europe's 12 major cities, some good and some bad. In surely unintentional pre-WWII irony, the trip from Berlin goes no further east than Stalingrad, where the directions say the driver "decides to stay a few days in the city of Tractor Works", and loses two turns.

Hive: The Pillbug is a new pair of tiles that can be added to Hive. The pillbug can move one space at a time OR it can move an adjacent unstacked piece (friend or enemy) two spaces: up onto the Pillbug itself, then down into another empty space adjacent to itself.

Exceptions:

     The Pillbug CANNOT move the piece the other player just moved.
     The Pillbug CANNOT move any piece in a stack of pieces.
     The Pillbug CANNOT move a piece if it splits the hive (violating the One Hive Rule).
     The Pillbug CANNOT move a piece through a too-narrow gap of stacked pieces (violating the Freedom to Move Rule).


Furthermore:

     Any piece just moved by the Pillbug CANNOT move OR be moved OR use its special ability on the next player’s turn.  
     IN SUMMARY, when playing with the Pillbug, apply this simple rule of thumb: any piece physically moved is rendered immobile on the next player’s turn: it CANNOT move, OR be moved OR use its special ability. A Pillbug that has used its ability has NOT been physically moved, therefore it CAN be physically moved by the opposing Pillbug on the next player’s turn. 



     The Mosquito can mimic either the movement OR special ability of the Pillbug, EVEN WHEN the Pillbug it is touching has been rendered immobile by the opposing Pillbug (as described above).
     A Pillbug under a Beetle (or Mosquito acting as a Beetle) CANNOT move OR use its ability, like any piece trapped under a stack.

People think of stuntmen as modern daredevils, unsung heroes paid to put themselves in danger and escape the most difficult situations to make someone else look good.  In fact, stuntmen are careful and patient individuals in perfect physical condition.  They plan their stunts like their lives depend on the preparation, because they do.  Rick knows exactly how far he can push himself and what to do when something goes wrong.

Unfortunately, Rick was in the hospital after a rough stunt when the zombies came and got most of his friends.  His ribs still hurt, but that won't last.  Then it will be time for payback.

Rick has been wary of other people for a long time - they can break so easily under pressure.  He acts like a moody loner for now but is slowly beginning to trust some of his teammates.

Rick is an optional promotional survivor available to those that raise an extra amount on their pledge for the Zombicide Season Two Kickstarter campaign. A zombie version (Zombivor) will also be included.

A classic combination of our two favorite things - being dads and playing games!

DadOpoly is a new and unique version of the classic that follows a dad's journey through fatherhood.  From a house on Single Street to a hotel on College Court, DadOpoly is all fun and games (as long as you are not on Diaper Duty).  The images are original, the cards are funny and the properties really hit home, making this a family friendly game that is loads of fun for all to play.

Here comes the new era of the space develop. For the foundation of the greatest empire, You must build establishments connected with combo among a lot of others, and use their functions to produce victory points and to keep the other nations in check.

Overview
Players play the game with the cards representing various establishments and political systems. Players take their turns in order. During their turns, they should RESEARCH to draw cards or BUILD to play their cards. The cost for the build is paid by selling cards from their hands, so players should choose which card to build and which card to sell prudently. And players can use the functions of the  cards in their tableau to draw extra cards, to get victory points, to steal cards from the other players, or to keep them in check by getting some advantages triggered by them. In the simple play, making the combos with various cards and maximizing the effects will be a key for the game.

In a simple explanation, Stardust Imperium is a game which is hybrid of "Race for the galaxy" and "Magic the gathering". I'm a huge fan of them, but I had to make a game which can substitute them because those games are little bit hard for my friends. Consequently, I made a game in which players use abilities of their cards so they directly interact with each other like "Magic the gathering" in a frame in which players build their nations like "Race for the galaxy". It took the merits of those games, but it was easy, simple and short. I'm satisfied with the basis of this game and while I'm continuing balancing tests, I decided to open this game in 3 languages so that more people enjoy this game and give me feedbacks. Please enjoy this game freely under non commercial use.


宇宙開拓の時代が開きました。貴方は最も偉大な帝国を作るため、様々な機関達の中で互いにコンボで連携される機関達を選択して建設し、機能を活用する事で勝利点を生産する一方、他国を牽制しなければなりません。

スターダスト・インペリウムは簡単に説明すると、レイス・フォー・ザ・ギャラクシーとマジックザ・ザ・ギャザリングを合わせたゲームと言えます。私はその二つのゲームの大ファンですが、これらは友達とするには少し難しいので、これらの代わりになるようなゲームを作らなければならなかったてす。結果的に、私はプレイヤー達がレイス・フォー・ザ・ギャラクシーのように自分の国を作るフレイムの中で、マジックザ・ザ・ギャザリングのように各カードの能力を活用して直接的に相互作用するゲームを作りました。こうして二つのゲームの取り柄を生かしていながら、安くて、簡単で、それにして早く終わりました。私はこのゲームの基礎に満足しますし、カードのバランス調整の為のテストを続けると同時に、もっと多くの人々にこのゲームを楽しんでいただき、そしてご意見を承るため、このゲームを三ヶ国語で公開する事にしました。商業目的でない限り、自由に楽しみ下さい。

概要
プレイヤー達は各種の機関と政治制度を表すカードを持ってゲームを進行します。プレイヤー達は順番通りターンを進め、自分のターンには研究を通じてカードを引くか建設を通じて手札からカードを出します。建設の為の費用は手札からカードを売却する事によって支払いますので、どんなカードを建設し、どんなカードを売却するか慎重に決めるべきです。そしてプレイヤー達は自分の場のカードの機能を使って追加のカードを引いたり、勝利点を得たり、他のプレイヤーからカードを奪ったり、他のプレイヤー達の行動によって利益を得る事で他のプレイヤー達を牽制したりする事が出来ます。簡単な流れの中で多様なカードの機能を連携して最大の効果を得る事がゲームを切り開く鍵と成るでしょう。

우주 개척의 시대가 열렸습니다. 당신은 가장 위대한 제국을 만들기 위해 수많은 기관들 중 서로 콤보로 연계되는 기관들을 선택하여 건설하고 기능을 활용하여 승점을 생산하는 한편으로 다른 나라를 견제해야 합니다.

스타더스트 임페리엄을 간단히 설명하자면, 레이스 포 더 갤럭시와 매직 더 개더링을 합친 게임이라고 할 수 있습니다.  저는 두 게임의 열렬한 팬인데, 이 게임들은 친구들과 하기에는 다소 어려워서 이것들을 대체할만한 게임을 만들어야 했던 것입니다. 결과적으로, 저는 플레이어가 레이스 포 더 갤럭시처럼 자신의 나라를 만들어가는 프레임 안에서 매직 더 개더링처럼 각 카드의 능력을 활용하여 직접적으로 상호작용하는 게임을 만들었습니다. 이렇게 두 게임의 장점을 살린 한편 쉽고 간단하며 빠르게 끝났습니다. 저는 이 게임의 기초에 만족하고, 카드의 밸런스 조절을 위한 테스트를 계속하는 동시에 더 많은 사람이 이 게임을 즐기고 의견을 주기를 바라는 마음에서 이 게임을 3개 국어로 공개하기로 결정했습니다.  상업적으로 이용하지 않는 한도 내에서 자유롭게 즐겨주시길 바랍니다.

개요
플레이어들은 각종 기관과 정치 제도를 나타내는 카드를 가지고 게임을 진행합니다. 플레이어들은 순서대로 턴을 진행하며, 자신의 턴에는 연구를 통해 카드를 뽑거나 건설을 통해 손에서 카드를 내려놓습니다. 건설을 위한 비용은 손에서 카드를 판매함으로써 지불하므로, 어떤 카드를 건설하고 어떤 카드를 판매할 것인지 신중하게 결정해야 합니다. 그리고 플레이어들은 개인공간의 카드의 기능을 이용하여 추가로 카드를 뽑거나, 승점을 얻거나, 다른 플레이어에게서 카드를 빼앗거나, 다른 플레이어들의 행동에 따라 이득을 얻음으로써 다른 플레이어들을 견제할 수 있습니다. 간단한 진행 속에서 다양한 카드의 기능을 연계하여 최대한의 효과를 거두는 것이 게임을 풀어가는 열쇠가 될 것입니다.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Team Work is a card game based on the "Tragedy of a team project" in an university. Each player plays a colored number card at his/her choice in clockwise order. Same colored cards are in the same project. So each participant gets the average point of the project.  If you find a honor student, get a free ride. If there is no free rider, be a honor student!

Simple memory game for very young children. It consists of a color die and 16 large green wooden tiles in different shapes. These either show a flower or a beetle on one side. 
The tiles are laid out face down. Players take turns rolling the die and flipping over a tile. If it shows a flower in the color matching the die, the player gets to keep it. If not, the tile is flipped back. The player with most flowers at the end of the game wins. 
As tiles come in four different shapes (because the flowers have a different number of petals) and each color/shape combination exists only once, there is a deduction element - if there is only one tile left with a given shape, it must be the beetle.

In the days when the world was still young, a race appeared full of splendor, dignity and beauty. They lived in harmony with their world and the power of their artifacts flowed through everyone and everything. Thus it happened that they isolated the mysterious essence of time itself and put it in twelve mighty crystals to keep it there. In the center of a hovering island, they created the enormous Citadel of Time where they used the pulsing power of the crystals to find out what was, what is and what will be to change the way of the universe.

For milleniums, this sign was anticipated and dreaded. Darkness fell over the island as two of the four suns eclipsed. The colossuses would come to posses the astral body of their creator. The Citadel of Time would fall...

In Kolossus, each player starts at one corner of the world with five warriors, two guardians, and one mighty colossus, with players pairing into alliances. The creatures have different strengths and abilities to move across the map, and using their abilities wisely is the key to success. With the use of mana, players can enhance their creatures' abilities to make them harder to beat or to hit stronger.

The aim of the players is to reach the Citadel of Time and control it as long as possible because the alliance controlling the Citadel receives a time crystal for every turn it does so. To win, an alliance needs to have the most time crystals at the end of the game or to expel all creatures from one side from the island.

Schattenmonster is a memory game. In the basic version, players take turns flipping open pairs of tiles - matching pairs can be kept, others are flipped back. Pairs consists of a monster picture on a colored background and its shadow on a grey background. 
In the advanced rules, flipping open certain monsters without finding the matching tile immediately leads to special actions, such as everyone passing the collected pairs to the left, to the right, or re-shuffling the remaining tiles. The first two effects can be used tactically as well. 
The player with most pairs is the winner.

Victorian England is a great place to live—if you’re wealthy, and if you’re not a werewolf. Unfortunately your own family estate has been tapped out, and you’ve been bitten by a wolf with a gypsy curse. Can you restore your family fortune without slaughtering the local peasantry? Or will you be killed by a vengeful mob or hanged by Scotland Yard?

Wolf Man's Curse! is a competitive card game for 3-8 players. Each seeks to gain money (Coin cards) while avoiding angry villagers (Mob cards), dodging the police (Scotland Yard cards), minimizing victims (Innocents cards). They also want to avoid being targeted by a Silver Bullet card.

Each hand represents one month of three full-moon nights. The game continues until the Fiancée card is scored. If a wolf man has both the Fiancée and a Silver Bullet, he dies. Next, the wolf man with the most Mob points is lynched. After that, the wolf man with the most Scotland Yard points is hanged—unless he has slain no Innocents. Among the surviving wolf men, the one with the most Coin wins the game!

"Kuro-maku" (literal translation is "black curtain") means "wirepuller" in Japanese. This game was designed under the influence of Illuminati.

The game of conflict for domination of Japan in modern age. Companies and organizations in the game is fictitious, however they have a name reminiscent of a real organization.

Each player take one character card. Each card represents a person that have ambitions to domination of Japan. They have basic abilities, special ability and victory condition. So, each player performs a wirepuller behind black curtain of Japanese society.

Organization cards is another type of card in play. These cards are represent a corporation, political party, government agencies or other group. In the game play, players control many organization and appoint to one of five section of their syndicate. Money section is use for income, other four sections is use for attack to other organization.

In each turn, players first receive income and then take two actions from attack, recover, re-organize, exchange or pass.

The game is end who achieve personal victory condition or time limit. In later case, winner determined by most powerful attack ability in each syndicate.

It was a plan, as foolproof as it could be for escaping the most secured prison in this country. And it worked – until you decided to rest in an abandoned barn without knowing it was the meeting place for all the nearby villains in their ultimate quest. The battle between you and them was short but fierce. It was a battle with no survivors...only legends.

In Roundhouse Kick, each player is an escaped prisoner who must fight several monsters in an abandoned barn. In each round all players roll the dice and try to kill as many monsters as they can because the monsters are worth points. After the players attack, though, the monsters fight back. The game ends once all the monsters or all the players are dead.

Objective: be the first moving all your (4) meeples into your target zone.
 - moving by turns -
active player moves forward according die roll; ending on a space with clear text follow instructions printed on the board. Opponent's meeples may be sent back to starting position with a matching number.
remarks:
Illustrations are from the movie 'Winnetou 1' (Rialto/Jadran Farbfilm)

By answering environmental questions players move through a spiral of environmental challenges towards the centre of the board causing as little damage to the environment as possible.  The player who causes the least damage wins the game.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Game comes with 2 tinplate cannons, card central keep, 2 walls, 4 towers, 10t 'flats' of 25mm scale infantry of the line and 15 pieces of ammunition. The rules are inside the lid.

This Game is played on the floor or table, one player undertaking the defence and the other, (or others), the attack.
Stand the Fort up. The towers will hinge into position. Then there are numerous instructions to complete the fort and that depended on how much you paid for the game. If you didn't have all the items mentioned in the instructions, then you had to make do. Chad Valley obviously made several editions of this game. Finally, the 15 discs are laid out as instructed. These represent trenches.

The defender places his Cannon, (provided in the betier editions), at one side of the Fort, and the attackers then send one man to the first Disc (or Trench), that is,thee the two furthest from the Fort; the defender then endeavours to shoot this man, and should he miss, the attacker may then move forward to the next Trench, or bring np another man as he considers best, when the defender again fires, and so on in turn, the object of the attacker, (or storming party), being to occupy the three Trenches nearest the Fort, each with one man, and if successful in this the attackers are then said to “charge” and the Fort is taken. If, however, the defence kill sufficient numbers of the attackers to prevent the three front Trenches being occupied, he has repulsed the assault and wins the game.

Soldiers having reached the front trench cannot be fired
at. Should the defender exhaust his supply of ammunition he must miss one shot while a fresh supply is obtained.

Coquina Expansion add 6 new cards and 5 wooden pink coquina building stones.
Normally, building stones in forts are removed using white, gray and black attack die results. Since the dice do not have a pink face, there's no way to remove coquina stones during the first attack phase! They can only be removed using the red targeting dice results during the second phase.

Tablecloth board game.
Roll and move from start to finish. There are different foods on the way. Healthy vegetables helps You to move faster, some not-so-healty move You backwards. Player who finishes first is the winner.

Contents:
- tablecloth
- 4 game-pieces
- dice

The game is played on a 8 x 8 grid. Players place hedgerows to mark enclosed areas. These are then marked for a player by a play-do figure which the players can form when they need them. Each square is worth one point. 
Bonus points are awarded for claiming golden coins found on the board.

Sei stark. Sag Nein! is aimed at teaching kids how to react to dangerous situations with strangers. It consists of cards in three colors:


    Red cards show a picture and ask a multiple choice question about how to behave in the corresponding situation. The player should give the right answer and also explain why she thinks it's right.
    Green cards demand physical tasks of the players and are aimed at strengthening the physical self-conscience.
    Blue cards train observing skills: The players see a picture and are asked questions about it.


Players take turns drawing a card; a moderator poses the questions. Each correct answer or fulfilled task is rewarded by a point. After a pre-determined time, the player with most points wins.

The game consists of a wooden board with 8 by 8 hollows (or 6 x 6 in the smaller edition for two players). These are randomly filled with marbles: Orange for carrots, green for salad and yellow for dandelions. Each player also owns a hare (a marble in a unique color) on the field.
Players take turns hopping two fields forward and one to the side, picking up ("eating") the marble on the field they land on. The food marbles are worth 3, 2, and 1 point respectively; and landing on an opponents hare eliminates the opponent (and is worth 4 points). When no more hare can move, the players count their points; the player with most points is the winner.

Yet another new take on the Prestel Art Game. 
Players spin a spinner and move around the game board. Depending on what field you are on, you will have to: 
- draw a picture yourself
- look at a card with a painting, then turn it over and answer some questions about it from memory
- do a pantomime. 
For each task that was fulfilled successfully, the player gets a jigsaw puzzle piece (there are some fields on which these can be lost as well). The first player to complete his/her puzzle is the winner.

Though this shares a name with The Prestel Art Game, this is a separate and different game.

Widget is a fast, easy-to-play, card game that asks players to make crazy, silly, funny, weird, unusual or otherwise great sentences out of the words they have in their hands.

The person who creates the "best" sentence, as judged by the other players, wins.  Special cards that allow players to switch cards around along with other special rules add an element of strategy to the game, making it easy to learn but different and exciting every time you play.

The words and the illustrations have been carefully selected such that it makes Widget a family-friendly game that also works as a good party game for adults.

UNAUTHORIZED PRODUCTION

    
    
         The information that BGG has on this item indicates that San Guo Sha is an unauthorized reproduction of BANG! with just a few differences, published in China and distributed in several coutries of the world.

    


This expansion is a collector's edition. It adds 9 new character cards to the game. 7 of these characters were already present in the base version of the game but have gotten new artwork and new abilities. These new character cards does not replace the old ones, since the abilities are different. 2 new characters are also introduced. The cards were released monthly in some issues of Zhuo You Zhi magazine as a free gift.

family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

description:
The Mind’s Lie is a game about how our minds consistently look at situations in biased and misleading ways.  The goal in this game is to spot these errors and convince the other players that you are correct.

The Mind’s Lie is normally a game for 3-6 players that can be played in 30 minutes or less.  Players take turns reading scenarios and then attempt to identify the biases inherent in each situation.  Players then vote in secret and, after the reveal, try to convince each other to change their positions.  The player who is most successful at convincing other players to vote as the player votes wins the game.

Over time, it is expected that players will gain the ability to identify and mitigate the effects of common cognitive biases in everyday life, thereby improving their critical thinking and decision making skills.

Far from Home is a SCIFI board game where two space crews vie for survival. Only the destruction of the enemy capital ship will ensure the future of your crew.  Out thinking your enemy and the successful allocation of your resources are your only means of success. The timely deployment and upgrading of your ships may provide a necessary advantage but watch out, your enemy will do whatever it takes to survive. You do have an ace up your sleeve, the powerful Capital Laser. But its success is dependent on a roll of the die and your opponent’s resolve to shield it. Do you have what it takes to survive far from home?

Far from Home is a Print and Play SCIFI wargame for an audience looking to scratch  the SCIFI wargame itch in under an hour.
The game itself prints on two 8 1/2 x 11 sheets of paper: 1 sheet with Map and the other sheet with ships-double sided, required tokens, and a tech tree.

Pennies are used for action points.

The rule book is 5 pages long.

Rules for games using miniatures. The author is identified only as O'Neil. There are 32 pages of rules with a card stock insert of charts and tables.

The rules cover the gray area of gaming between fantasy role playing games and the large tactical Ancients army game. Players can use the rules to recreate gladitorial combat between man vs. man (or man vs. fantasy race) combat or they can use them to recreate massive battles such the Trojan Wars or the battles in Middle Earth.

The Game Sequence:
1. Charges and spells are declared.
2. Counter charges and counter spells.
3. Reaction tests to charges and spells.
4. Normal movement.
5. Missile fire (ranged combat).
6. Reaction tests to missile fire.
7. Melee (hand to hand) combat.
8. Reation tests to melee combat.
Steps 1-8 are repeated until one side is victorious.

A system of rules for modern aerial combat using aircraft miniatures or using paper & pencil on large sheets of paper (alternately, grease pencils on clear plastic sheets).

There are 20+ pages of rules and 4 different 2-sided data charts (2 copies of each).

Aircraft information for modern era (pre-1980) aircraft provides performance (speeds, maneuvering) and armament data (number of guns, range, damage). Players recreate historical or hypothetical aerial battles using NATO, Warsaw Pact or other air forces.

Shorter scenarios use primarily 1-2 aircraft per player.

Larger numbers of players would allow different types of aircraft, such as bombers and escorts being attacked by defense fighters. These smallest of these actions (scenarios) can last 120+ turns and involve an area of 200" x 300" (19.7 ft x 25 ft.).

1 Turn=10 seconds
1 Unit=2 miles

For miniatures play, the editor recommends a large floor area with 1 unit=10 cm (or 4-5 inches).

These rules are for use with Modern micro armor miniatures in 1/285th and 1/300th scale. The rules are suitable for use with HO scale models if adjustments are made to the sizes of the artillery and bomb grids.

Ground scale is 1"=50 yds (1:1800) and each turn represents about 1 minute. 6, 12 and 20 sided dice, rulers and playing pieces are required for play. Sighting distances in unobstructed areas range from 40" to 80", so the playing area should take this into account. Players provide their own terrain as required by their scenario instuctions.

The rules concisely cover movement, sighting, vehicles, infantry, artillery, chemical weapons, smoke, minefields, aircraft, helicopters and antiaircraft artillery.

Using the information in the military organization section, players can deploy a military force based on a country's actual military practices.

This game was created by the Wycliffe Bible Translators, an organization with the goal of translating the Bible into every language on earth, to give potential recruits an idea of the hazards, obstacles and rewards of working for Wycliffe.

Though there is no publication date, the game appears to be from the 1970s or '80s.  It is primarily a roll-and-move game in which players must start on an inner (or Home Country) track and amass items, knowledge, funds and supporters that will allow them to proceed to an outer (or Tribal Work) track, where they must overcome various trials and be the first to finish their translation.  While the mechanic seems to be mainly roll-and-move, there are a few decisions that may add a small element of strategy.

From the game box: "Taste the problems & joys of a Bible Translator--without getting your feet muddy.  First, getting training and support at home.  Then language work and pioneering abroad.  Real-life incidents to help or hinder.  The first to complete St. Mark's Gospel wins the game."

boardgame: Bolt Action: Armies of Great Britain
description: With this Bolt Action supplement players can now build armies for British and Commonwealth troops such as the Australians, New Zealanders, Canadians, Polish Airborne, and Gurkha's.  From the early campaigns in Europe to the deserts of North Africa, and the jungles of the South Pacific and the Far East, British Commonwealth forces faced the Axis threat.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Educational game for kids to learn the clock, based on the popular Maya the bee theme.

Each player plays Maya the bee who wants to visit her friend Willy (a picture in the center of the spiral board). The board is also a large clock, with fingers starting at 10 o'clock. 
Players start on the outside of the board, moving in a spiral towards the middle, using a simple roll and move system. There are fields in different colors, some having special effects: 
On a Philip field, players receive a protective card that they might need when someone gets on a clock field. On a clock field, players draw a clock card and have to set the new time. The clock fingers push the Maya pieces back on the spiral, unless countered by a Philip card.

The first player to reach Willy is the winner.

Description taken from official preorder page:

The Pixel Lincoln Bonuses Pack includes a Pixel Lincoln button, Pixel Sentinels, Cheat Codes, the Celebrity Custom Cards pack, the Lincoln Coin Card Pack, and a shiny penny*


    Penny may not actually be shiny.


Contains all the promotional extras created during the KS campaign.

This is a set of trivia questions about Birmingham, Michigan and surrounding area intended for play with Trivial Pursuit board, pieces and rules. Birmingham is a northern suburb of Detroit. Trivial Birmingham has six categories.

Business (Blue) - Local retailers and other merchants, past and present.
Civics (Orange) - Area government, education and community services.
Geography (Green) - Streets and other landmarks of Birmingham and surrounding Oakland County area.
History (Yellow) - People, places and events that shaped Birmingham.
Leisure (Brown) - Entertainment, the arts, sports, restaurants and other leisure time trivia.
Michigania (Pink) - Facts from all categories, about Detroit and the entire State of Michigan.

Rules for Napoleonic miniatures war games. The rules are primarily for use with 25mm figures, though the ground scale can be adjusted for different size figures. Each figure and commander is assigned a points value, so players can create armies with a set number of points.

Players form up their miniature units based on their points values and use them to recreate actual or hypothetical Napoleonic battles. The terrain is varied and is supplied by the gamers. The rules concisely cover movement, combat and morale effects. Battle casualities are determined with dice rolls and the use of a rotating casualty disc "tool" (which the players have to assemble from 2 pieces of card stock and a pin.

boardgame: Naval Wargame Rules for the Napoleonic Wars 1793-1815
description:
(Excerpt from the Introduction): These rules have been formulated with the period 1793-1815 (French Revolutionary and Napoleonic Wars) in mind, although such was the progress of Naval Warfare at the time, they could, with the preparation of suitable Ship Definitions, be used for any period during the eighteenth and first half of the nineteenth centuries.

Ground scale is 1:2400. Ship scale is 1:1200, although the rules may be modified for other scales. Measurements are in mm. Time scale is 1 move=1 minute.

There are data for a number of different types of ships ranging from frigates to the vaunted ships-of-the-line. Players use scale models to recreate historical or hypothetical naval engagements. The concise rules cover wind direction, movement, and gunnery. Dice rolls are used to generate random amounts of damage and gunfire accuracy.

These rules were published by Navwar in the United Kingdom.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A set of rules for naval war gaming in the Ancient era published by the London Wargames Section. The author, Ed Smith, is also the designer of the Avalon Hill game, Trireme, which covers the same topic.

Equipment Needed:
A board
Miniature ships
Movement cards
Average dice (a d6 numbered 2,3,3,4,4,5)
Normal dice
A deck of playing cards (for optioonal rules)

A homemade board (about poster board (22"x28")) consists of 2.5 cm x 2 cm rectangles; each row is offset from the adjacent rows.

Turn Sequence:
1. Wind change (optional rule)
2. Hole plugging
3. Write Manoeuvers (this is a British game design)
4. Movement
5. Oar and crew transfers
6. Archery
7. Oar raking results
8. Ramming results
9. Grappling and backwatering
10. Boarding action results
11. Morale

The player who returns home with the most vessels (including prizes) is the winner in general. Players may agree on other victory conditions, perhaps within the framework of a campaign.

description:
Donelson Breakout covers the Confederate attempt to escape from Grant's siege of Fort Donelson. It uses the Age of Rifles system, for a fast, low to moderate complexity, brigade-level game of ACW battles. Interactive Sequence of play, orders, and a modern, fire-based combat system (no odds optimising!). To win, you have to use your army like a 19th century commander. Maintaining morale is the key. Running away will inspire defeatism in your troops just as heavy losses, but no side will emerge unscratched when encountering the enemy. Among all this you have to contend with multiple subordinates on a battlefield where no information can be sent faster than a rider can go. How you are able to orchestrate your leaders to enable effective concentration of force will decide the battle.

The system combines a grand tactical perspective (battle strategy, not micromanagement) with a focus on a historically sound use of your units. Four pages of basic rules aim at an easy entry into the world of pre-industiral command and combined arms with four types of orders and assault combat, cavalry charges, and artillery bombardment. Nine pages of full rules add concepts such as additional order types, written orders (although markers are available for those who do not wish to write), reaction charges, and enfilading fire. Finally, 2 pages of Advanced and Optional rules add hidden movement, division level doctrine, and others.

Scale:
400m per hex
1 hour per turn
brigade level units
Individual army and division commanders
