description:
A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Nancy, a city in France.

Card game published as a fund-raiser for a charity that cares for greyhounds after their racing career ends.

This is a game best played with 4 players plus one caller [the caller can be one of the players).
One pack of 52 cards are dealt out with 13 cards to each of the 4 players. The caller also has a deck of cards which is dealt by turning up one card at a time and names the card for all to hear, i.e. Suzie 1, Phoenix 6, Smart Dresser etc.

The player with the card called out places the card from his/her hand face upwards on the table. The caller continues to call out the names of the cards until a player lays down the last card in his/her hand and calls out Super Six.

As the caller calls out the card dealt the card is placed on the table so that a cross-check can be taken for the winning hand.

The cards are a little odd. One would expect only to have greyhound cards but not so, there are Mascot, Trophy, Kennel, and four individual cards that have no category to fit them in. I think the original game must have been more complicated, taking advantage of all these cards but as publication date drew near, the charity probably felt worried about this and cut out all rules except as described above, reducing the game to little more than Lotto.

boardgame: High School Musical 3 Prom Night Game
description:
Card game that uses similar mechanics to Uno and Whot but adds a slight twist with the Action Card choice.

The cards feature three areas, one of which must be matched to be able to play a card. The choices are numbers, suits or Action symbols. The cards have numbers and suits because you could use the deck to play traditional card games. If you cannot play a legal card, then you draw another card from the deck.

The Action Symbols allow the player to make a special action. Most of them you get to choose who is affected rather than the traditional way of the effect being forced on the next player to your left.

The game ends when one player has used all his cards, of course after he/she has remembered to shout out "High School Musical" when they are down to just one card.

Teramyyd: Earthsphere is a turn-based fantasy adventure board game set in the world of Teramyyd, a rich and fully-realized setting with a nuanced and detailed history and mythos.

In the game, you play a skyship captain, adventuring across the land to secure the resources you need to complete quests, defeat creatures, plunder the ship of your fellow captains, or to defeat the fearsome Kobolos dragon. Where lies your destiny? The choice is yours!

Promotional game issued by Singapore Airlines.

Four games are described that can be played with the cards.

Game 1 is Rugrats Match-Up and is played like Pairs in Britain but like Memory in the rest of Europe.
Game 2 is Look for the Rugrats! Each player rolls the die (that has to be assembled) to see which Rugrat they are for that round. All the cards are laid out face-down. The seemingly same card backs actually hide an image of one of the Rugrats, and you have to use the special viewer to see it. So it is a race to find your Rugrat as each player searches at the same time. First to find their Rugrat claims the card and then the die is used again to determine which character you are for round 2, and so on. The player who wins the most cards, wins.
Game 3 is Rugrats Hideout. Players draw one card each which they can look at but must keep secret. Players take turns to ask 3 questions to try to find clues as to which card your opponent holds. If you guess the most correct answers, you win.
Game 4 is Rugrats Showdown. This is played like Slap Jack.

In Ride the Rail, two players take the role of a fledgeling railway partnership that is desperate to help the people of Middleton by building a railroad line to the towns of Eastport and Westgate.  The players are in frantic race against the Rail Baron, who has been hired by the rich and powerful aristocrats of Middleton to build a railway first.  The players must navigate difficult terrain, manage precious resources, and build trading posts to trade goods and hire workers.  Meanwhile, the Rail Baron will use his cronies and seemingly limitless resources to try his best to derail the players’ plans.  Will this new partnership succeed or will the Rail Baron emerge victorious?

Ride the Rail is a cooperative, tile-laying, resource management game for 2-players, ages 8+.  In Ride the Rail, players try to connect a central hex to two other hexes (Eastport nad Westgate) by placing hexes, drawing tiles, and managing resources.  Rail Baron cards provide randomness and obstacles the players must overcome.

The game is played in turns, which are divided into three phases:

Rail Baron Phase: In the Rail Baron Phase, players flip one card over from the Rail Baron card deck and follow the directions.

Player Phase: During the Player Phase, players can place new Rail Hexes, change existing Rail Hexes; build Bridges and Tunnels, move Trains, explore Rail Hexes, place Workers on Resource Tiles, remove Workers from Resource Tiles, build Trading Posts, trade with the Bank, and redistribute Resources between players.

Resource Phase: Players are awarded Resource Tokens according to the placement of Workers on Resource Tiles.
Start the next turn and repeat until the game ends

Game ends when the players’ Trains reach Eastport and Westgate.  If the players have more resources when the game ends than the Rail Baron, they win.

Main idea:
An educational game aimed at teaching/practising the relation between sounds and their written representation in the form of letters. Many primary school children have a hard time differentiating similar sounds, such as German ö - eu - ei. The game is meant to help them by making them listen closely.

Game play:
Basic game: Each player gets 6 picture cards and puts them in front of him/herself, face up. The remaining picture cards form a face-down stack at the center of the table. The stack with the 24 face-down letter cards is given to the game master, oldest player, or is passed from one player to the next each turn.
The game master (or one of the players) looks at the top letter card and reads the sound. Each player can now discard one of their picture cards if it matches the sound from the letter card. For example, if the letter card is "SCH", the picture cards showing a key, ("Schlüssel"), pig ("Schwein") or frog ("Frosch") can be discarded. A player who doesn't have a matching card has to draw one from the draw deck. If the drawn card matches, he/she may discard it immediately.
The first player to get rid of all his/her cards wins the game.

The "expert" variant: Each player gets 8 cards and may discard as many matching cards as they can when it is their turn (rather than just one picture card per sound card).

Download: 7 Wonders - Empires

With this fan-expansion, there are three possible game modes you can play, and one variant that can be added to any game. It is my hope that these modes will open up new options and styles of play for you and your friends. From a different, more comprehensive 2-player mode to a strategic, deep, 4+ player team game, these new boards should keep you coming back to 7 Wonders for more.

Version 2, released 5/31/14, includes re-balanced Wonders, 4 additional Leaders, and 7 additional Cities cards.

Disclaimer: Please note that this is no longer available at Print and Play Productions, and the designer will not be seeking a new printing company. Repos gave permission for this fan expansion to be made, but since the expansion contains copyrighted materials (icons, logos), print on demand companies do not have the right to print it. It is free for personal use and personal printing.

Wealthy individuals on the Earth funded a project and have assembled the tools and mining teams capable of a 1 year expedition to a Near-Earth asteroid and have placed you in charge of its operation.  The mission is not without risk however.  In an effort to achieve the greatest resource achievement and make more room for hauling resources back to the Earth, the teams have been supplied with limited water and must learn to “live off the land” by locating sources of water to sustain themselves all the while avoiding the numerous other dangers of mining an asteroid such as outgassing and related geological effects. You and your team will be pitted against other mining teams with the same capabilities and it will be up to you to outperform the other teams to achieve the largest, most diverse haul and achieve victory.  Good Luck!

Aidler-14 pits players in a unique mining mission where the ultimate goal is to acquire the greatest amount of resources, making the most efficient use of available mining tools and techniques that are available through the use of the mining assistance cards.  Abilities range from advanced movements on the asteroid surface to being able to double and even triple your teams mining production for the turn.  Your mining team has 3 workers which you deploy to either explore and uncover new resources to be mined, mine resources that have already been discovered, or process raw mined materials into processed goods that will save weight and space on the Earth return vehicle for the trip home.  The game consists of 12 rounds with each round being broken up into 6 distinct stages.

Worker placement, dice rolling, and timing are the key mechanics to this game as choice of available cards and locations on the board are chosen in succession beginning with the starting player for the round.  Placing more than one worker for mining will increase the odds that more resources are produced, but at the same time increases the risk of losing those workers to the damage sustained mechanic of the game that simulates the many dangers associated with this type of mission.  Management of water resources cannot be overstated as the teams will have to seek out sources on the surface to replenish water utilized to keep your team alive as well as operate the equipment on the surface.

Combining the most unique and exciting elements of outstanding games such as The Settlers of Catan, Agricola, and Stone Age with a science based approach to the prospect of mining for resources in space makes Aidler-14 stand out compared to the many other space mining-type games that are on the market and makes for endless replayability and a lot of fun.

A space-themed mathematics trivia game for ages 11 and up.

Aim of the game:
Collecting 16 galaxy tiles (4 each in yellow, red, green and blue) to put them on the corresponding player board. (The resulting picture shows a supernova.)

Game play:
Each player rolls the die once. The player with the highest roll starts and moves his/her pawn according to the die roll. When passing the orange start space, players get a galaxy tile of their choice and put it on their player board. Players then take turns, trying to collect galaxy tiles by answering the mathematical questions from the blue and black question cards. Which colour tile you get depends on which space your pawn lands. Galaxy tiles can also be collected by answering questions about space on the "black card" and "joker card" spaces.
The player who first manages to collect a complete set of galaxy tiles wins the game.

the card is a 2 strengh zombie with a special power.

English:

Vietnam is a collector NAC-based Tactical level board-wargame, set in this bloody conflict. It recreates the battles and challenges that came under american detachments. The huge amount of tokens shows the different units and weaponry used: Viet Cong, Green Beret, heliborne troops, infantry, mortars, anti-aircraft, missile launchers, Chinook, Cobra, Huey and Cayuse helicopters.

Two, three or four players take command of American and South Vietnamese troops, and the army of North Vietnam.
Once initial troops placed on the board, each player takes turns moving the mobility factor of each unit, applying modifiers depending on the passing zone (rivers, streams, jungle paths, roads and rice fields).
Infantry units can engage in combat with adjacent opponents, existing differences between combat values ​​depending on their experience and equipment (guerrillas, viet cong, infantry and Green Berets are an example).
Artillery units can launch ranged attacks against other tabs or objectives.
The weather factor also plays a big role.
The firepower and mobility offered by different types of helicopters are the great strength of the American contingent, as opposed to the number of North Vietnamese infantry and difficult terrain mobility, may be grouped, giving the oportunity to cheating with empty groups that must be discovered approaching them.
Several Fire Support Base, which at siege are recreated in an extended area on the board, providing another level, much more exciting, including mines and barbed wire to open a gap.
At the end of the round 14, players earn points for every target that their units still maintain (bases, hills and villages). The winner is the most points of victory has joined.

Similar: Vietnam


Spanish:

Vietnam es un wargame de coleccionista, basado en el juego de la desaparecida NAC, y ambientado en el conflicto que le da nombre. En él se recrean los combates y dificultades a las que se vieron sometidos los destacamentos estadounidenses. La enorme cantidad de fichas reflejan las diferente unidades y maquinaria usada: viet cong, boinas verdes, tropa helitransportada, infantería, morteros, antiaéreos, lanzamisiles, helicopteros Chinook, Cobra, Cayuse y Huey.

Dos, tres o cuatro jugadores toman el mando de las tropas americanas y survietnamitas, o el ejercito de Vietnam del norte.
Una vez colocadas las tropas iniciales sobre el tablero, cada jugador se turna moviendo el factor de movimiento de cada ficha, aplicando modificadores dependiendo del tipo de zona por el que pase (ríos, riachuelos, jungla, caminos, rutas y arrozales).
Las unidades de infantería pueden entablar combate con los adversarios adyacentes, existiendo diferencias entre los valores de combate dependiendo de su experiencia y equipo (guerrilleros, viet cong, infantería y boinas verdes son un ejemplo).
Las unidades de artillería pueden lanzar ataques a distancia contra otras fichas u objetivos.
El factor climatológico también juega un gran papel.
La capacidad de fuego y movilidad que ofrecen los distintos tipos de helicópteros son la gran baza del contingente americano, en oposición a la cantidad de infantería norvietnamita y su movilidad por terreno dificultoso,pudiendo agruparse, engañando al contrario con grupos vacíos que avanzan y deben ser descubiertos acercándose a ellos.
Existen varias Base de Apoyo al Fuego, que al asaltarse se recrean en una zona ampliada en el tablero, ofreciendo otra escala, mucho más emocionante, incluyendo minas y alambradas por las que debe abrirse una brecha.
Al finalizar el turno 14, los jugadores suman puntos por cada objetivo en el que aún mantengan sus unidades (bases, colinas y poblados). El ganador es el que mayor cantidad de puntos de victoria haya sumado.

Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements of the city of Dijon, in France.

Small World's Designer Edition is a spare-no-expense version of Small World the board game; this version of the game is true to Philippe Keyaerts' original wooden prototype.

The Designer Edition will feature: 
 - 307 deluxe wooden tokens covering all races from the base game and its Cursed!, Grand Dames and Be Not Afraid expansions + the Lost Tribes
 - each race comes in its own individual, lavishly illustrated box
 - the banners, special powers and markers that complement the deluxe tokens will all be heat-printed on wood, matching the tokens neatly
 - the victory coins will all be distinctive metal coins specially forged for Small World
 - the maps will be super-sized copies of Small World's 2-5 player maps, and include a new 6 player map specially designed by Philippe Keyaerts
 - the Dragon, the Catapult, the 2 Heroes will all be represented by custom resin miniatures
 - the new "Pocket Encyclopedia" from the Kickstarter campaign

The copy purchased by Kickstarter backers will also include a deluxe wooden version of the 3 new Races & Special Powers in the Kickstarter Backers Bonus Pack as well as an engraved plaque.

The Designer Edition is a true collector's edition. There was a single production run made, with only a limited number of copies for the retail channel.

Sovereign is a relatively simple deck-building war game with a great deal of player interaction. Each player takes on the role of a leader, hidden behind his castle walls. The goal is to amass an army and batter down your opponent's walls.

All cards have two possible uses, most of which can either be a Simple Action, which is played then discarded, or a Unit, which remains in play until it is defeated in battle. Players generate gold to purchase more Contract cards and Influence to place their Units on the table.

There are two main paths to victory: one may defeat his opponent on the field of battle, or create a great Monument. Monuments are built in three tiers, each giving a permanent bonus (depending on the Monument). The third tier ends the game in a victory.

Pirates in Pocket is a fan-made PNP card games for free. Up to four players can compete for the King of Pirates on high seas.

Each player may send his/her ship to plunder ports & merchant ships, or attack other player's hideout. You may also send the captain or first mate to ports to do some secret missions like recruiting crews, gambling, collecting rumors & treasure hunting...No one knows what you have sent (ship or crew?)to each place until they are revealed. So it's critical to guess where your opponent's ship is going.

There are events in different places each turn. You may collect rumors in taverns to get hints about the coming events but not all rumors are correct.

You may use loots to hire more crews, bid parts to upgrade your ship, bribe guards, gamble, or even buy fames. Beware, as your fame go higher & higher, there is a big chance that you will be hunted by the Royal Navy or challenged by the notorous Ghost Pirate.

There are three kinds of combats in this game : Ship to ship combat, melee combat & duels between captains. Each captain has his/her own special ability which may get bonus in certain combat.

The publisher released several packs of cards in their Top Hat Trivia series.

Each version has cards with six questions on each one. The form of the questions is that 3 possible answers are read out so even the youngest family member can have a blind guess with a 33% chance of success.

The starting player takes a card and rolls the die. The result indicates which question to be asked. He asks the question to the player on his left. If the correct answer is given, the answering player takes the card and now it is his turn to ask a question to the player on his left, and so on. If a question should be answered incorrectly, the player who gave the wrong answer takes the card, rolls the die and asks the player to his left a question. (which could be the same one of course).

The winner is the first player to secure four cards.

Simple quiz game but does have the virtue of an interesting array of question styles.

There are 90 cards and each one will be in one out of six types;

Code Crackers - Work out the secret code.
Cryptic Clues - As in a cryptic crossword.
Anagrams - Unravel to reveal the correct word.
Extreme Close-Ups - Guess the onject from an unusual angle.
Sudoku - Solve the number puzzle.
Logic Words - Guess word from an array of letters

The answers are on the back of the cards. You or your team get 1 point for every correct answer. The most points after a pre-agreed time has expired. wins.

boardgame: Fortune and Glory: Rise of the Crimson Hand
description:
The Order of the Crimson Hand has infiltrated every level of society and performs cult rituals, binding dark creatures from another world to do their bidding! The stakes have never been higher, and the heroes must keep their wits about them if they are to prevent the rise of the Crimson Hand.

Fortune and Glory: Rise of the Crimson Hand is packed with new material for Fortune and Glory: The Cliffhanger Game with new cards for every deck; new dangers to face – including a new game mechanism for "deadly" tests; new villains to fight, each with a highly detailed figure; epic city events, unique to each major city, that can be triggered; devious Crimson Hand Acolyte figures that are dispatched to race the heroes for artifacts; and a powerful new vile organization to contend with!

Adventure awaits as these new treasure hunters set out to find fortune and glory!

Operational level wargame based on Japanese anime series Space Battleship Yamato 2199. The game covering war between Empire of Gamilas and Comet Empire.

Players control star fleet of either the Empire of Gamilas or Comet Empire.
The main engine based on Red Dragon Rising: The Coming War With China. Players manage movement, combat and call for reinforcements to home star.

Winner determined by victory points based on control of star zones or several three death conditions.

Espionage cards it's a dynamic and very fun game. Using your brain you have to discover the three variables of the confidential file, you have a lot of tools to your disposition. Use your private agenda...and you'll be the winner

The game is simple:
Every player recibes a deck of 15 black cards called "personal agenda"

Then you take the deck called "Confidential file" with 15 yellow cards and split it in 3 parts : 5 cards of locations (London, Athens, Paris, Tokyo and Panama), 5 cards of Spies (Orange Spy, Blue spy, Red spy, Green spy and White Spy) and 5 cards of devices (Jetpack, Airplane, Lethal Gas,Satellite and Helicopter). Shuffle every deck and take , without looking, 1 card per deck and face it down in the middle of the table. then merge the remaining cards into one deck, shuffle and deal evenly among the players.

Now its time to play!: 
The objetive of the game is to discover the face down cards using logic and deduction.
First of all you know that the "confidential file" cards can't be in the center of the table so you discard those card from the "personal agenda" deck in your hand.

Now a player begins its turn by asking other players about some of their cards. For example: I ask to the player on my left for "the BLUE SPY in LONDON", or "In AIRPLANE to PANAMA". Every question must involve 2 elements of the 3 different variables (cities, spies and devices) it may include 2 of the same element, for example 2 agents, 2 cities or 2 devices.
Once the question was askes, the other player checks his/her cards of "confidential archive" and if he/she has 1 or two of those cards, he must show 1 to the player who asked. By knowing the card, you can discard what he/she shown from the personal agenda. otherwise he says "I'll pass" and another player must be asked.

The game continues like this until someone thinks that they know the cards facing down and makes and acussation! if he guesses right he wins if not the player to the left makes and acussation and so until someone wins!

A set collection and negotiation game centered around the early days of the Erie Canal.  Players move along the canal collecting cards depicting the various cities along the waterway.  Players use a rondel to move, draw cards, and trade cards with other players. Specific numbers of City cards are then traded in to allow placement of a warehouses in one of the two good types depicted on a specific city.

A fluctuating economic chart, based on the number of total goods on the board, determines the current price of a warehouse in each good.  The player with the most total value in goods at the end of the game wins.

A sports trivia game with questions involving many sportive themes, like, Soccer World Cups, Olympic Games and etc. The game mechanism includes a little arena where the game pieces are throwed by the players.
The players impulse the game pieces in the "arena" and the final position of those pieces determine what theme question has to be answered. 
The arena has 3 tipes of holes, like a pool table, the kind of role (by proximity) determines a kind of question (True or false, direct answer, or multiple choice question).
So, the colored pieces determines theme and the hole determines a kind of question to be answered by the players.

A general trivia game, based on the brazilian Grow's ultrasucceces Master. This game has 12.000 questions about 24 diferent themes and areas. The objective is move throght the board colecting points in all areas of knowledge answering the questions according to the board cases.

boardgame: The Lost Dutchman: Pixel Prospector Player Board
description: An additional player board for The Lost Dutchman. The Pixel Prospector has a cheat code enabled and he will use it to his advantage as he searches the Superstition Mountains for The Lost Dutchman's Gold Mine!

From the publisher's website:

World War 2 air combat game depicting the nighttime air raids of British bombers hitting German cities.

As the head of the British Bomber Command, you plan the attacks on Germany in order to undermine the morale of the civilian population. Or as a General of the German Air Force (Luftwaffe), you defend using your ace squadrons and organizing an effective civil defense.

The gameboard enables you to set up countless variations of these historic events. You play as many nights as you wish - each night takes 30-45 minutes to resolve.

Immerse yourself in the strategic and tactical thinking needed to survive in those dark times.

Gameplay allows for easy entry into the action with some advanced rules for the hearty wargamer. The game rules include weather conditions, full moon/new moon bonuses and penalties, clouds and thunderclouds, fog, flak, searchlights, balloon barriers, target markers, and much more.

The British player secretly plots the course of the bomber while the Mosquito acts as escort or decoy. The German player tries to figure out where the bomber is going, making sure to efficiently use the fuel to get as many hits on the bomber as possible.

THIS IS THE SECOND EDITION WHICH INCLUDES 9 EXPANSIONS


     Ju88 Night Fighter
     Railroad Flak (x2)
     Accoustic Mirror 
     Quad Flak
     Skilled Gun Crew
     The Walls Have Ears
     3-5 Player Variant
     British 3.7in QF Anti-Aircraft Gun
     British Searchlight


The box has a large "Includes 9 Expansions" logo on the cover.

Note that these are the same expansions that are provided in Duel in the Dark: The Complete Edition – Expansion.

description:
The Black Sheep is a bonus monster card for Dungeon Fighter, distributed as the "goodie" in Plato Worldwide #2.


Blacksheep [sic] hates winners: inflicts its damage to the Hero with the highest number of life points.

This game is intended to teach children table manners in a fun and interactive setting.

By rolling the dice, players will advance their pawns around the board. By landing on each space, a new scenario is proposed and the parent can use the opportunity to instruct their child on table manners.

By answering correctly, the child would receive a card representing the space that was landed upon. The game ends when the child has all the cards in their hand.

Preppin is a 2-4 player card game for ages 8+. The goal of the game is simple, keep your family alive and be the lone survivors of the cataclysmic disasters.

Each player is the head of a household trying to survive an onslaught of disasters. Every round you must decide how many of your family members will go out searching for supplies, and how many will stay sheltered. Family members who are sheltered, are safe from disasters. Those that go out, might not come back. Next you draw 1 card from any area pile of your choice for each unsheltered family member and place them into your hand. After you've drawn your cards, you take care of your upkeep by discarding 1 food card and 1 water card. If you can't, then you lose a family member to starvation or dehydration.

Once each player has taken a turn, the last player chooses a disaster card from the disaster deck of their choice. The red ones are the worst.  The disasters effect everyone at the same time. In order to survive the effects, you need to use the surplus cards you've collected in your hand. For example: The Oil Crisis Disaster card is drawn. It takes 1 shelter and 1 food away from everyone, or else you lose 1 family member. In my hand I have 2 Waters, 1 Food, a Generator and a Gas Mask. I will discard my food and generator to survive the disaster.

After everyone has dealt with the disaster, the next round begins starting with the player to the left of the person who just drew the disaster. The game ends when there is only one player left, crowning them the most prepared.

description:
Ross was leading a peaceful life as a warehouse manager when the zombies appeared. Ross, however, is more than he appears. He left the army twenty years ago with scars and PTSD but believed he had moved on. The invasion triggered something in him, something dirty he had thought buried forever. His soldier's instincts came roaring back, along with old wounds. His tactical awareness and skill with weapons, however, are priceless to any survivor team. If he would just put down his bowling ball...

Ross is a promotional character available as an option for backers of the Season 2 Kickstarter campaign.  The figure includes English, French, and Spanish language versions of the weapon card "Ross' Bowling Ball".

A selection of games and puzzles aimed at the very young, using characters from the Chuggington TV series. Altogether there are three different games and 4 puzzles to keep a young mind busy.

Naturally, there is a pre-school Top Trumps game with 12 cards. Each train is rated for Speed, Friendship, Adventure and Wisdom and each is illustrated in colour. This will be a short game which is right for the age group - they won't have time to get bored.

Secondly, you can play Pairs with the cards because there are 12 cards giving a description of each of the twelve trains. So the player won't find an exact match but will be able to find, say, two Chatsworth train cards.

Thirdly, there is a simple which is played a little like Rummy but much easier. You have a hand of five cards and you draw a card (from the stack or the top of the discard pile). If you get a matching pair, you may lay it down and discard one of your other cards on to the discard pile. The first person to get rid of all their cards, wins.

ZinZig is a wine-themed trivia game designed to both test knowledge and educate players. It requires the addition of two or three bottles of wine for gameplay purposes. Players take turns to roll and move on spaces around the circular board, receiving a wine-related trivia question each turn. A successfully answered question is rewarded with one of the four resources (barrel, grape, bottle, and cork). Some spaces allow for special actions along with the trivia question (including swapping, trading, or stealing resources). A player who has collected all four resources may enter the center of the board, "production," no longer rolling to move but instead progressing through four final stages (crushing, fermenting, aging, and bottling). The first player to complete the production stages is the winner.

Issue game in Command Magazine Japan #109

Designed by Kita-Kitsune.

This is a mini size card-driven game covering the Eastern Front of WW2 from September '41 to June '42.
The map uses point to point system from Odessa to Rostov.
This game uses improved card-driven system in which players start with the determined deck but can purchase additional card by using specific event card.

Victory is determined by VPs which can be gained via control of important cities.

Speedy Bee is an observation and speed ​​game.

The bees are looking for pollen. The first player who have six pollens wins the game.

The game includes three designed dices with four small balls representing the primary colors (blue, yellow, red) and black.

The board has six pollen balls, three with the color of the dices and three representing the mix between the primary colors (green, purple and orange). And the black color is used for the queen of the bees at the center of the board.

Each turn, one player rolls the dices and then all players have to catch the correct pollen balls depending on the color dices and according to the color mixing rules.

If you get the correct ball, you win a pollen token. If you are  wrong, you have to give one back.

"Kalory: A game for all fun loving, weight conscious people!"

Kalory is a roll-and-move, weight-loss-themed Monopoly derivative which instructs players on methods of losing weight.

After rolling dice, players move their playing pieces around the board, gaining or losing Kalory chips (which represent weight). They also perform actions on cards that they draw, depending on which space they land on. The first player to lose all of their Kalory chips is the winner.

Players may select different game lengths: a lightweight game, a middleweight game, or a heavyweight game. The game length corresponds to an increasing number of starting Kalory chips.
The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Sharp Solar, a company located in Germany

Card game for diehard Hannah Montana fans because you will need to know her songs very well.

Shuffle the deck and place one card face-up next to it. This is the start of the discard pile. Shuffle the Points cards (they vary from 1 to 10 points).

The main cards have a symbol on them. That is important. The symbols are;

Music note: name the title of a Hannah Montana song which includes the word on the card.
Flower: sing 2 lines from the chorus of a Hannah Montana song, which includes the words on the card.
Butterfly: say 3 things about the character on the card.
Star: this is the secret Joker card! Draw a point card without having to carry out an assignment. All other players are required to draw an action card!

The Play. If the player has an action card, of which the colour or symbol corresponds with the top card of the discard pile, he/she can discard it.
If the symbol is identical, then he/she can execute the assignment (see above) on the card he/she is discarding. The other players will judge whether the assignment is successful. If the assignment is successful, then the player can draw 1 points card. If both the symbol and the colour are the same, then the player can draw two points cards! If the assignment was unsuccessful, then the player does not get to draw a points card.
If the symbol is different, but the colour is the same, then he/she does not get to carry out an assignment.
If the player does not have a suitable action card (other symbol and other colour), then he/she has to draw another action card.

The player who has collected the most points when the Points cards have run out is the winner, the Secret Star of the Music Game!

Before the game begins each player customizes several of the cards with names of family members, friends, pets etc. in Mad Libs Criss Cross Game. Then each player picks nine cards from the card tray and puts them onto their card holder (3 Nouns, 3 Verbs and 3 Misc.). The first player lays down a sentence made of cards and counts up the total score which is printed on the individual cards. The next player lays down a sentence crossing over or adding to the previous sentence and adds up the score. When all of the cards are used or no one else can make a sentence the game is over. Object of the Game: The player with the most points wins.

"The funny game of druggists" published by Julius Süss, Leipzig, Germany in 1920. It is the typical children's roll and move board game with interesting theme - children's games in pharmacy store. 
The goal of the game - to pass the pharmacy store first (pass the 110 stops).

Game board measures - 29 x 44 cm.

Designed by Jay and Susie Hernishin for their daughter, Fairy Mischief is a light game designed to help younger gamers transition into more advanced gaming with the goal of making more girl analog gamers!

Players take on the roles of fairies competing in displays of magic. The fairies will duel one another over colorful gem pieces that are randomly drawn from a satchel (watch out for the powerful black stones that grant special abilities). When one fairy has won a gem of each of the five colors, they win!

The basic game uses the classic game of War (players win duels by flipping over the top three cards of their deck and comparing who has the highest cards in a best-of-three fashion) combined with other elements to introduce the players to "gamery" skills (collection, special powers, and randomness).

There are also more advanced ways to play, so the game can grow with or adapt to kids. New concepts include (push-your-luck, hand ranks, informed guessing, discard pile awareness, etc.)

description:
Textra is word game, that consist of a plastic container, 206 letter tiles and 4 letter trays - clearly inspired by Scrabble, but the main gimmick here is the plastic container with clever mechanism to separate vowels and consonants. In the container you have trail where you put your letter tiles to make words. And when the trail is full (something like 15 tiles), the first used letters drop in to two departments one for vowels and other for consonants.

When you start the game, the first player puts one vowel and one consonant in any order in to the trail and then every player gets six letter tiles to make words. You can choose the distribution of vowels and consonants at the start of the game and always when you refill your tile tray. At the players turn you try to make a word using as many already laid letters in a trail as the first letters of your new word. Rest of your word consists of letters you have in your tray. You just put the letters from your tray to the trail to make a new word. Then you multiply newly added letters with letters you used from the previous ones and that makes your points. You fill your hand and then it is the next players turn.

Example: "AUTO" becomes "autoMATIC" (4*5=20 pts.), then "ticK" (3*1), then "tickLE" (4*2) etc.

In a four player game you play to the end of the round, when someone gets total of 60 points. Winner is the player who got the most points. In three player game you play until 80 points and with two players until 100 points. When playing alone you just decide number of rounds and try to make as many points as as you can.

You can play this game as long as you like, because the used letters are dropping back to containers departments, which is the same place where you take the new letter tiles. There is 100 vowel tiles and 100 consonant tiles and six wild tiles in the game. There's quite a few letters missing, that are used in Finnish language. Letter distribution of the game is:  vowels: (40*A, 10*E, 20*I 15*O, 15*U) and consonants: (5*H, 5*J, 15*K, 15*L, 5*M, 10*N, 5*P, 5*R, 10*S, 20*T, 5*V). You can play the game in any language, where you can make enough words with these letters. Because of "the clever" mechanism, the vowel tiles are bit smaller than the consonant tiles.

This is a very light (but difficult-to-master) dexterity game with collectable figurines made of a rubbery material. The game board is magnetic, and so is the bottom of every figure.

There are several modes of play, but all of them involve flicking your figurine(s), trying to get them to land on certain spots of the game board.

The figurines are mostly sold in single, blind-purchase booster packs (although there are some other packages available).

Objective: be the first ending a round trip in Berlin
 - moving by turns -
simple 'Roll & Move' game: choose one of the coloured routes around the world; ending on a marked space follow instructions...

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from the winning French soccer team 2000

Game description from the publisher:

Munchkin Zombies Decay d6 features six custom six-sided acrylic dice previously available only in a limited release. Three of them are swirled pearlescent red with pus-yellow pips; three of them are swirled pearlescent ivory with blood-red pips, and all six feature a Munchkin head in place of the one. You want them so you can kill the monsters and take their stuff!

Munchkin Zombies Decay d6 also includes four exclusive new cards for Munchkin Zombies!

Educational board game for kindergartens about vegetables.

There is track on topside of the board and various vegetables growing on the game board under the track.
Player rolls the dice in his turn and moves accordingly on the track. If child lands on square where the name of vegetable is written he/she must recognize this vegetable from the board. Then he/she rolls again and the dice show one of the six categories player must answer the question about this vegetable.
If the answer is incorrect the player moves backward 3 squares.

Player who finishes first is the winner.

The game commences from the Sydney side of the Bridge marked Home and continues over the proposed scenic railway to the North Shore, and back along, the main roadway of the Bridge following the numbers from No. 1 to 100.
Each player throws the dice in turn it being necessary to throw a 6 to commence the journey.
The first player to return to Home wins the game.

This is a Kickstarter exclusive promo for all backers getting at least 1 copy of Dragon's Hoard.

According to the designer of the base game, this card "allows a player to stay safe from actions like wizards, knights, and angry mobs for an entire round, and aids in the acquisition of a highly valuable treasure".

(Source: Kickstarter page for Dragon's Hoard.)

Contains a complete Red Block company for AT-43 the ready assembled and painted miniatures game.
• 1 unit of 12 Krasnye Soldaty: regular infantry containing three grenade launchers, two medics and an officer
• 1 unit of 8 Krasnye Soldaty: regular infantry containing one grenade launcher, two mechanics and an officer
• 6 Dragonov Kommandos: elite infantry with one sniper rifle, an electronic warfare specialist, a medic and an officer
• Odin O-1 and Manon O-2: the Red Blok’s two greatest heroes
• 1 Urod: Odin O-1’s and Manon O-2’s combat strider

The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Quickfunds Dubai, a company located in Germany.
The classic Monopoly with constructions and other elements from Key-Systems, a company located in Germany

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Board Game (Second Edition) – A Feast for Crows
description: A Feast for Crows is the second expansion for A Game of Thrones: The Board Game (Second Edition). This four-player scenario includes setup instructions and adds house cards for House Arryn. Also introduced is a deck of Objective cards and four Special Objective cards, one for each House, that alter the way players score Victory Points.

boardgame: Triassic Terror: Limited Edition Predator Tiles
description:
From Kickstarter Update #2

"These two tiles show the T-Rex and the Raptors at their most vicious, and are used for creating a more violent game where these two predators are more devastating:

1. The new Raptor Tile allows each Raptor, after it moves, to eat 2 Dinos and scatter 3 Dinos.

2. The new T-Rex Tile allows: 1) if T-Rex moves 1 area it eats 6 Dinos ; or 2) and if it moves 2 areas it eats 4 Dinos.

This makes each of the new tiles a bit stronger and perhaps more desirable to choose than before, but doesn’t imbalance the game play."

There's commotion in the village! The legendary Galipote, who, years ago, terrorized walkers in the west of France, has been seen at night, hidden behind the old oak tree : giant eyes, large jaws and big claws, of either green, yellow or red color.

At the beginning of each round, each player start with a hand of 7 Galipote cards.

During his turn, a player can places one of his card as Testimonial, on one of the 6 available sides, matching half-symbols and colors. If there is a Legend card available, he can places up to 2 cards according to the Legend. If he places a card with fur on the side, other players vote for the new Legend card. If he complete the Figure of Galipotes in one side (a symbol), he can score the cards for him.

The first player to get rid of all his cards, there is one turn until the end the game.

He scores as positive points all the remaining cards in players hand. Other players scores as negatives points all their cards in hand. All players scores as positive points the number of different colors of the Figure of Galipote, for each symbol.

Objective: be the first ending a round trip in Gotha, Thuringia; learn more about Thuringia.
 - moving by turns -
simple 'Roll & Move' game; ending on a marked space follow instructions
remarks:
published before or during WWII

A Decktet game for two players, in which you are a merchant attempting to claim as many trade routes throughout the kingdom as you can.

Ruta is a game of card drafting and network building, inspired by the popular game Ticket to Ride. A 5x3 grid of cards is used to represent the people and places with which players are trying to establish trade routes. Players take turns drawing cards and playing those cards to claim routes (orthogonal connections between adjacent cards on the map). Tokens are used to mark each player's routes.

Points are awarded for each route that is claimed, and players earn bonus points for their longest continuous route. When one player is out of tokens, the game ends, and the player with the most points wins.

This game requires a Decktet extended deck and 20 tokens in 2 colors (10 of each color). Suit chips may be used.

Requiem is a miniatures expansion of the game "Empire of the Dead", a whole host of new characters, and ingenious steampunk machines, from Victorian fiction.

The game is set in an age where a steampunk victorian empire rules the world. An empire fueled by an extraordinary discovery. The mineral that has become known as infernium. But all is not well, dark shadows from our deepest nightmares stalk the smog shrouded streets of London. An ancient evil is rising from defeat.

Players choose from 4 starter factions which they can expand and add to from a range of special character models. Each game is played over several turns, each turn consisting of four distinct phases (maintenance, initiative, activation and combat).

The expansion, Requiem, adds 70 new characters to the original game and introduces new factions like the Clickers (a race of automata) or the Supernatural Branch (new character types, weapons and machines used by Scotland Yards Occult Police force) and also the rules covering the new Victorian Machines.

It’s an age of kings and kingdoms, heirs and assassinations. You and your opponents are vying for control over the four royal bloodlines. To emerge the victor, you must carefully manage a mismatched court of nobles to create the most prestigious kingdom the world has ever seen.

Bloodlines is a micro game using 20 playing cards and 5 cubes. Each player drafts a hand of cards, each of which has a special ability and a point value that contributes to your influence in that suit's bloodline. Once all cards have been played, the prestige of any lines you control is based on presence of cards of that type in your opponent’s kingdoms.

Each card you play has an associated special ability, and that card remains in your Kingdom as influence in that bloodline. The card abilities are:


     Ace (1 Influence): The Assassin may eliminate any unprotected card from the game, including any heirs placed on the card. 
     10 (2 Influence): The Castle protects all of the played cards in your kingdom from the effects of the Assassin and the Jack.
     Jack (3 Influence): The Jack may swap any two unprotected played cards of equal Influence value. You may swap for a lower valued card if swapping from your own kingdom. 
     Queen (4 Influence): The Queen may produce an heir on any card you have played, including herself and cards in Castles. The heir increases this cards influence in that bloodline by 1.
     King (5 Influence): The King has no ability, but he may be played directly into an empty Castle already in your Kingdom.


Full Rules available in the files section below

Simple board game for children about water safety.

Players roll the dice and move on the board. There are many special squares on the track to move forward or backward, miss the turn or answer the simple questions how to act safely near the water. Who finishes first is the winner.

Dating is hard in the 14th century, and as a matchmaker, business is slim. The plague has most folks too afraid to leave their homes, let alone go out looking for romance. Literacy is very low, so the letters you receive from clients are oftten difficult to make out. To make matters worse, you're not the only match-maker in town.

To come out on top of your rival matchmakers you will have to go with what little information you can decipher and find matches for your clients. At times it will be in your best interest you to work with your opponents, at others it will benefit you to set devious traps for them.

Love is in the air! But so is the plague...

In Black Plague Blind Date, players take turns choosing a card from their hands to play face down as a "blind date". They then must truthfully describe their card with 1-3 colored "info cubes" (blue = male, pink = female, black = infected, white = healthy, green = high class, brown = low class). Cards change hands during the exchange phase, then each player plays a second card face up next to any blind date. Blind dates are revealed and reputation points are scored based upon the best/worst dates. Matching two healthy townsfolk or two plague-ridden citizens will earn you points, but don't accidentally cross the two and spread the plague! The game is played over 4 "seasons" (rounds) with each season affecting scoring in a significant way. It's a quick-playing game of deduction and bluffing for 3-4 players.

Full rules are available under the Files section of this entry.


    Black Plague Blind Date won the February 2013 Boardroomers Game Design Contest in the 3+ Players category. Entrants in the contest were limited to using no more than 20 standard playing cards and 10 cubes.

The Questor Expansion adds components for a fifth player, as well as a compass token to Questor.  The fifth player will play as the Psionic Prisoner, whose goal and ability are just as unique as the other characters':  The Psionic Prisoner merely must escape the dungeon through one of the four exits.  The challenge is that the way the Psionic Prisoner deals with monsters is by retreating from them, and when he is in the game, he starts in the middle space, with monsters guaranteed in the surrounding four spaces.  So, he can't move until another character liberates him.  Fortunately, the Psionic Prisoner has the option to rotate any revealed room 90°, rather than move or dig.

Because the Psionic prisoner is one more character the Healer must heal, this expansion includes a white disc.  The compass token included with the expansion is a fancy-looking way to track one of the losing conditions of the cooperative / solitaire game.

From the Website:

"Station Run is a board game version of the computer game Elite, released by Ian Bell and David Braben in the mid 80s.

You and some other players need to get from one end of the game board to the other without your ship being destroyed. Other players might do it, or some of the random people who ply the spacelanes might get to you first.

Station Run is a free-to-download game. No need to whip out the credit card for this one, and no need to try and remember your paypal password."

From the free PDF rules:

"Station Run is a game for one to four players. Each player is the pilot of a ship which has just hyperspaced into the system. They must get to the nearby planet’s space station in order to sell cargo, refuel or whatever else they might want to do. The player can take on a number of roles. Traders are ferrying cargo between systems to make profit, which they can use to improve their ship or buy another one. Bounty Hunters are seeking out wrongdoers and bringing them to justice. Pirates are only interested in plundering the traders and stealing their cargo. Miners are looking for asteroids to mine for valuable minerals.

The aim of the game is to get from one end of the game board to the other in one piece. Random encounters may occur, as other spacefarers and asteroids make their way around the planet’s
orbitspace. The game is designed to be open-ended and as freeform as possible (just like the computer game)."

ALL ABOARD THE YELLOW SUBMARINE!

Travel 80,000 leagues under the sea to the unearthly paradise known as Pepperland in this colorful Yellow Submarine edition of MONOPOLY. All you need is love to help The Beatles battle the Blue Meanies as you buy, sell and trade your way through the Sea of Science, the Headlands, Nowhere Land, the Sea of Holes and more.

Ad hoc, ad loc, and quid pro quo! So little time! Advance to Go!

Monopoly, the classic game of property trading with a Beatles theme.

Game includes 6 collectible pewter tokens; Yellow Submarine, Dreadful Flying Glove, Jeremy Hillary Boob, Love Sign, Sgt. Pepper Drum, Old Fred

Solar will attempt to usher in a new style of card games with its almost unheard of game mechanics. The game will start with both players choosing who picks the first character, that person starts on defense, the other play, on offense. Then, they both take turns choosing characters until they both have 12 card decks to play with. Each character shall feature its own art work and class, as well as one, two, or three attacks to use while on offense,and one or two defense moves while on defense. At the beginning of each turn, both players draw 3 characters from the top of the deck, and put one on the battlefield, and the other two on the bottom of his/her deck. Then, the player on offense may use his characters attack skills on his/her opponent's characters. If the hit lands successfully, the character is defeated. The game is over,and the winner decided, when one player has lost all of his characters and is labeled the loser.

“Mijn eerste verkeersspel” (=My first road game) is a dice game for kids. The players have to walk from home to a particular purpose (e.g. school) on the other side of the road. The rules for pedestrian traffic have to be followed. Cars on the road can be moved by die roll. The game ends when all players have arrived at the goal. The main issue is finishing, although the player who finishes first wins. The game can be played with different rule variations.

A game about fishery. You go out by dice rolls with your diver and take chances fishing sea creatures for points - harder game is tricker to get and rare, easy game worth less points. Once everyone has gone around once and is back, the one with the most points wins.

-----

Um jogo sobre pescaria, onde cada jogador movimenta seus peões pelo tabuleiro, conforme os pontos obtidos nos dados. O objetivo é chegar à zonas de pesca, onde tentarão conquistar troféus e pontos em cada pescaria. Criaturas marinhas pescadas em profundidades maiores valem mais pontos. Vence o jogador que possuir mais pontos no final da partida.

 - moving by turns -
simple 'Roll & Move' game; endimg on a red space follow instructions glued on lid inside;

probably published befor WWII

A two-player, print and play game designed for 2013 Two-Player Print-and-Play Design Contest.

Players take turns moving a pawn around London using tokens as action points.  The Jekyll player is trying to discover the parts of a cure, to rid themselves of the Hyde personality.  Hyde is trying to take full control of the pawn, by limiting the Jekyll players ability to use the tokens.

Most of the players' actions turn into a tug of war with the other player.  Moving the pawn can place it in an advantageous location for the other player, and playing cards gives the other player a chance to draw them into their hand.  Players must try to accomplish their goals without aiding their opponent.
family: Books: Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde

description:
The third expansion to Cards Against Humanity features 100 new cards – 25 black question/statement cards and 75 white answer cards – to be used with the base game, in addition to 12 blank cards.

In this card game, players take turns being the Card Czar, which means they read a black question card or a fill-in-the-blank statement card. The other players then select a white answer card from the ten in hand that they think best or most humorously fits the question/statement. The Card Czar then shuffles the answers and chooses the one he likes best, with that answer's owner receiving the black card as his "awesome point" for winning the round.

Once a player has at least one awesome point, he can wager an awesome point when giving a white answer card in order to throw in a second answer card, theoretically giving him a better chance to be chosen. The winner of the round receives the original black card as well as any that are wagered in that round.

Play continues until a pre-determined score is reached, or until everyone is tired from laughing so hard.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
From the Publisher's website:

At their most desperate hour, the proud trollkin kriels set out to awaken the legendary mountain kings-ancient monsters of unimaginable size and power. Wurmwood, however, has its own designs on the slumbering beasts and plots to turn them on civilization. Meanwhile, the Skorne Empire seeks retribution against an ancient foe, and Everblight works to perfect his most terrifying dragonspawn yet.

HORDES: Gargantuans brings you the next thrilling chapter of the HORDES saga. Bulk up your game with:

• Powerful new gargantuans-giant warbeasts that tower over armies and bring all-new strategies to the table.
• New warlocks for each faction, including the first-ever warlock units.
• New units and solos to expand HORDES armies.
• New narrative fiction picking up directly after the exciting events of HORDES: Domination.
• A painting and modeling guide to help you prepare your gargantuans for combat.
• Theme Force lists for each new warlock, which allow you to create armies based on specialized forces found in the HORDES world.

The object of Speed is for the player to move his/her ship, plane, car and train along the designated routes from start to finish. The first player completing the routes for all four vehicles wins. Two to four players may play. There are 16 player pieces. One each of ship, plane, car and train in four colors (red, green, black and blue)

Movement is driven by card play. Each player selects four cards from the 40 card deck to start the game. Each player must maintain four cards in hand at all times. There are four "suites" (in red and black) corresponding to each of the four types of vehicles. The first player plays a card in the suite of his/her choice, announces the card and plays it face up. The player then draws from the deck to replenish the hand. The other players follow, and the player who played the highest value card in the suite lead gets to move the associated piece that number of spaces forward along the corresponding route. If a player does not have a card of the suite lead, they must play another card from another suite. The used cards are placed in a discard pile and shuffled if the draw pile is depleted.

The first player to reach the finish (Picnic Grounds) by any count wins the game. Two to four persons may play. Players take turns spinning and moving. If a player lands on a red colored space, the player must take a card and follow the printed instructions. There are 25 reward/penalty cards in a complete game. If the cards are exhausted during play they are reshuffled.

The rain has stopped and the sun is out with a shining smile, but the beautiful rainbow is nowhere to be seen! It's all because those playful colors of the rainbow got distracted by the fun facilities in the park. They are running around and having a good time all over the park. Let's go find those colors and make the rainbow appear!

Contents: 54 game cards, including 49 Element cards and 5 pass cards.

This is a memory game where all of the cards are double-sided. Players take turns turning over cards to score matches. Players can collect pass cards and re-arrange the position of the game cards surrounding that card.

The game ends when the number of Element cards left on the playing surface is less than or equal to the number of the colors used to play the game. For example, if 5 colors were used to play the game, the game ends when there are 5 or fewer Element cards left on the playing surface.

At the end of the game, each player should add up the values of the cards he/she has collected: 1 point for each Element card, and 2 points for each Pass card. The highest scoring player wins the game.

Pogs are small cardboard disks with a variety of designs on them; they were popular in the early 90's. In the original game of Pogs, players would gamble Pogs from their own collection in an attempt to expand their collection further. Players would stack Pogs face down in a stack and throw a 'Kini' (a hard plastic disk, sometimes referred to as a 'Slammer') from above in an attempt to turn the Pogs over and claim them for their collection.

Pog word builder combines the classic action of Pog with the challenging gameplay of Scrabble. Use 1 letter and 2 letter Pogs to build the biggest words possible. Pog's are obtained in the same way as the original game, by by trying to turn over a stack of Pogs containing unknown letters. The player who builds the most words wins.

As the emphasis of Pog word builder is on the number of words, rather than the length or the quality of words, the game is easily accessible to young players.

Zippers is a fast-paced communication quiz game. On the game cards are two sentences each, each one describing a different word. But the two sentences are, like a zipper, intertwined and at first make no sense. But if you look closely, you can possibly find out the answer. If you think you have the word, hit the reply card as soon as possible and receive the points.

Memory Matching Mayhem

Players capture dinosaur cards by turning over a card in the battle card deck. In order to capture the dinosaur, the drawn battle card must be equal to or of a greater value than the now overturned dinosaur card.

When and if all the dinosaurs are successfully captured, the player with the greatest combined point value of all captured dinosaurs win.

Board game for local Estonian children game room.
Roll and move on the game board. Some special squares. Who finishes first is the winner.

Timeout is a trivia game with time pressure. Each player start with the same amount of time, and the faster you answer the faster you get to stop your clock. When running out of time you are out of the game.

When it's your turn, a trivia question is read out loud to you by an opponent and your time begins ticking. If you answer correctly, you get to stop the electronic clock, that's counting down. If you do not answer correctly (or don't answer at all) the clock counts down 20 seconds and the stops. 
The questions are divided into categories. Which category you get a question from depends on a die roll. Sometime you get to choose the category for yourself or for an opponent.
If a player fails to answer correctly, the next player has the chance to answer and thereby be rewarded with 10 bonus seconds. When out of time, a player is eliminated from the game.

Killer: Vanilla is a fast-paced card game with its tongue firmly in cheek. Players deal out death and destruction in many forms while trying to avoid the onslaught coming back at them. (This game is not recommended for children or those of a sensitive nature: bless 'em.) Kill off the weakest players to improve your hand as you balance defense and gear against a varied offense. Silent scoring heightens the tension... can you take another hit? Are Doctor De'Ath or the Booby Trap in someone's hand? Be the last man standing or take ‘em all with you!

Each turn a player may: make an Attack (Hand to Hand, Ranged, Area, Special) against any other player; play an Equipment card (aid survival); or change any or all cards in hand. Defense cards (Parry, Evade, Flee, Safe House, other) protect against Attacks, while Follow Ups boost damage. Global cards generally apply to all players. If a player gets a killing blow, his hand limit increases and he takes his target's cards. After the first kill, the scoring goes silent, leaving players with only an impression of how close to elimination they are.

Killer:Vanilla is a take-that card game which suits a 'beer & pretzels' mentality more than serious gaming. Play time is about 5 minutes per player, when you are used to the cards. The built-in expansion, The I In Team, adds some more complex solo cards as well as specific cards and rules for Team games.

description:
Helen was a federal agent, proud to uphold the law and protect the people.  Her inquisitive nature and logical mind lead her into investigations, and she became a terror to the nation's organized crime syndicates.  She finally managed to anger the wrong people, though and she was under investigation for trumped up corruption charges when the zombies came.  With the streets full of flesh-eating monsters, the game had clearly changed, but Helen found that her skills and experience still applied.  She finds it very refreshing not waiting for a warrant before breaking into wherever she needs to go.

Helen is an optional promotional survivor available to those that raise an extra amount on their pledge for the Zombicide Season Two Kickstarter campaign. A zombie version (Zombivor) will also be included.

boardgame: Emperors and Eagles: Wargaming Rules for Napoleonic Tabletop Gaming
description:
Publisher Blurb:

In 1789, the French Revolution acted as the spark that ignited the volatile powder keg of Europe, pitching the continent into a conflagration of war and bloodshed that would last for decades. Amidst this chaos rose Napoleon Bonaparte, under whose influence France would become a dominant power, controlling, directly or indirectly, the majority of Europe. This companion for Field of Glory Napoleonic gives players all the information they need to recreate the French Revolutionary wars and the rise of Napoleon through to the campaigns of the Grande Armée and the early Empire. Emperors and Eagles also includes full army lists for the Peninsular Wars, where Britain and its Portuguese and Spanish allies slowly, but surely, turned the tide of the war and pushed Napoleon's forces back to their own borders.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Suzerain combines elements of Deckbuilding with more traditional Trick Taking games. Throughout the game players will be playing to tricks in order to gain cards from the middle. After a trick the card gained, and the one used to gain it, will go into the player's discard pile. When a player plays their last card in hand their discard pile becomes their new hand and play continues.

Scoring is based upon sets of ranked cards as well as the Pawns and Courts which interact with certain ranked cards for bonus points.

This is a game for the Decktet; a deck with 6 suits and many double-suited cards. For more information on the decktet visit decktet.com or check out the BGG listing for Decktet.

In the game of Dragon Caster, you take on the role of a powerful sorcerer who fights other Casters for glory and conquest. Your casting numbers represent either the dragons you can summon to battle, or the type of mana that your dragons may obtain. This mana can be used to grant your dragons a variety of special powers. In order to gain victory, you must destroy the area containing your opponent's Caster.

boardgame: The Battle of Red Cliffs: Command Expansion #1
description:
Command Expansion #1 comes with nine cards, numbered 1 to 9.

During setup, each player randomly takes one Command card. Command cards give player a unique ability for the entire game or once per round.

Examples:

(1) Scavenger. When refilling your hand from the draw pile, you may claim one of them from the top of a discard pile instead. The card may not be a wild card from the Trash pile.

(2) Rapid Advance. You can create 1-3 sets per turn.

boardgame: The Battle of Red Cliffs: Command Expansion #2
description:
Command Expansion #2 comes with nine cards, numbered 10 to 18.

During setup, each player randomly takes one Command card. Command cards give player a unique ability for the entire game or once per round.

Examples:

(10) Summon East Winds. Once per round, you may create a set from any one card in the discard pile. This must be performed on your own turn.

(11) Hard of Heart. After you draw 1 card at the beginning of the turn, you may discard 2 Beauty cards and draw three new cards or discard 3 Beauty cards and draw five new cards.

boardgame: The Battle of Red Cliffs: Allegiance Expansion

The Allegiance Expansion comes with nine cards in three allegiance colors.

During setup, each player takes an Allegiance card of his/her team. When the round ends, Allegiance cards count as a card in the color set it matches.
