boardgame: The Battle of Red Cliffs: 2013 Essen Promo – Earth Element
description: Earth Element is an event card. When it is drawn, active draws a card then place it on the side face up. When the round ends, all players may use it as an additional score card to score. Remove from the game after scoring.
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

description:
This game was inspired from the game villages created by Jaron Frost.

Players play cards in groups of 3 cards to create levels, and may add more later on.  Each card has a coin values. The first player who reaches 100 coins wins the game. Each turn players may attack a level of a player with one of their units to steal one of their cards. Each unit have a special ability that may affect their values during the game.

Cards are in French and English
family: Video Game Theme: Super Mario Bros.

description:
As the eastern bloc crumbles in 1989 Gorbatjov is ousted in a reactionary coup led by the Soviet military. In desperate attempt to turn back time and unite the eastern bloc the long prepared offensive into western Europe is launched.  A critical element for success is to be able to interdict the American reinforcements over the Atlantic to its NATO allies. That job belongs to the submarines of the arctic fleet in Murmansk. But they need air support and support from surface vessels as well and Murmansk is too far away from the North Atlantic seas to provide that support. In order to seize air fields and naval bases on the Norwegian west coast as fast as possible an invasion of Finland and Sweden is launched at the same time  as the Norwegian border is crossed on the Nordkapp to outflank the Norwegian easily defensible terrain along the northern coast.  Neutrality will not protect Sweden this time. But maybe an army of 600 000 trained men and 600 tanks will?
This game deals with the southern part  of the Soviet operation against Sweden, the invasion of Skåne. The well trained paratroopers of the VDV and the equally well trained Baltic marines land to face the bulk of the Swedish armoured brigades supported by infantry and artillery in terrain ideally suited for armoured units.  Will the Swedish built  turret less tank Strv 103 meet the expectations, though twenty years in service now, or will it be up to the British built Centurions, with more than thirty years in service,  to destroy the invaders? 
With X pages of rules, XXX counters X maps and  13 scenarios the game is designed to help understanding of the challenges of the Swedes as well as those of the Warpac. While gameplay is designed to be fast and easy with command and control issues being in the centre of the design the optional rules allow for deeper study into logistics and recovery. Units are platoons and each game turn equals one hour . Hexes are 500 meters across.

Hit fast! Hit hard! centres on the 7th armoured brigade, PB7 Malmöbrigaden, and its hypothetical but well planned and trained actions around Ystad on the Scanian south coast.  Equipped with Strv 103C, the upgraded version of the famous “S” tank was the cutting edge of the XI. Division responsible for repelling or at least fixing an invasion  aimed at Ystad or Trelleborg. While PB7 might be superior in fire power to the air assault and naval infantry units it initially will encounter these are the elite units of the Soviet union. If the soviets seize a working harbour mechanised units with T-72M1 tanks are sure to pour in fast.  You will also see  parts of two different Swedish types of infantry brigades as well as territorial defence units  and the 12th independent mechanised battalion with Strv 102R, partly upgraded Centurions, and motorised rifle companies in trucks rather than Pbv 302 infantry fighting vehicles.

"One against All" in English is a word game in which a player, in turn, plays against all others.

One player has 6 minutes to choose three secret words (maximum 10 letters per word), and gives a definition for these three words. The player draws half of the letter-cards for his opponents and gives information how many number of times this letter is present in the words etc. Player keeos other half of the cards.

When all the cards are drawn, the opponents have 6 minutes to find the words. For this, they can ask questions related to words, the player can only answer yes or no. Opponents can also ask if the letters kept by him during the draw are part of words.
The player receives tokens for each question or request letters, and receives a bonus for word not found.
The game is played over several rounds. The final winner is the one who has the most points.

PERFIDIOUS is a two player war game, set on a small map where two factions battle for control of the other’s Home Hex.  All the armies in the game have their true strength hidden and battles are won based on the claims of the players (but the players can lie to win).

Players must use deceit to outwit their opponent and only by hiding your strengths and weaknesses will they succeed. But be careful; your opponent can call your bluffs and in doing so, may unravel your plans completely!

The base rules are simple and easy to learn but the game can be modified by a collection of optional rules to be mixed and matched at the start of the game by the players.

This game is also completely free to play and can be printed with any standard printer using 8½x11 paper.  Some components require cutting so scissors are required for the initial building of the game.

This game is released under the Attribution-NonCommercial 3.0 Unported

In CiRKiTRY, players have been invited to a local electronics competition. Your goal is to create a electronic circuit that uses all 5 of the following components: a Battery, a Resistor, an Inductor, a Capacitor, and an LED bulb. However, to make things more difficult, everyone will be competing on the same circuit board.

Players take turns uncovering circuit board tiles and placing their electronic components along the conductive paths. But be careful! The Voltage on the Volt-a-Meter fluctuates as components are played. If the Voltage gets raised too high, you might cause a destructive power surge.

Use Solder pieces to create pathways and make new connections. Not all 5 of the electronic components need to be your own, and you can attempt to piggyback off of your opponents' placement.

CiRKiTRY does not require prior knowledge of electronic engineering to play.

—description from the designer

They came for our resources…
They came to annihilate us…
They came for our… cows?!

Welcome to Mars vs Earth! In this alternate-reality, 1950’s era setting – we are not alone! Aliens from the planet Mars have secretly descended upon Earth. As a member of an elite multi-national squad, you must defend our home against the vicious, little green men as they try to abduct our wives, mutilate our cows and boil our oceans!

To complicate matters (as if inside-out cows weren't bad enough), the aliens can replicate our DNA and clone human beings. They know our agency is the last line of defense and they have infiltrated our ranks. That trusted partner of yours may actually be an alien spy! Hey, I don’t remember you holding your hand over your heart during the National Anthem – when was the last time your DNA was screened?

About the game:

Mars vs. Earth is a simple card game, playable by 3 to 10 players in 30 to 60 minutes, that employs a modified "hidden loyalty" system.  Mars vs. Earth introduces new mechanics, such as scaled martian powers and an accusation/trial system, that sit atop a wacky theme to increase play value and enjoyment.

Think your neighbor is secretly working against the agency?  Keep him from leading missions, or better yet make a formal accusation and bring him to trial for his evil deeds!  Of course you need to be careful - if you accidentally oust a true human from the agency they will become a lowly citizen with far fewer powers to help combat the martian threats!

The game is over when either the humans have foiled enough of the martians' plans or the martians have destroyed every last human resource, be it military defenses, tech, people or food.  In either case, Mars vs. Earth is guaranteed to create some of the greatest play experiences imaginable!

KiCKeT is a football simulation game based on tactical movement of miniatures. The game is played with the feet: miniatures movement, passes and shots on goal, goalkeeper defence are all performed with one foot.

The game is about to get published and several versions of the box are envisaged. The boxes only differ in terms of amount of components, i.e. the number miniatures and the addition or not of various accessories.

“Standard" components:
- 2 or 4 wooden miniature field players, 20 cm high
- 2 wooden miniature goalkeepers, 20 cm high
- 2 goals of adjustable size
- 1 small fake-leather ball, soft inflated (no risk of damage…)
- markers for field and goal area corners

The game is playable on many surfaces: grass, carpet, parquet floor (with the “socks” accessory), cement, even sand !

The number of miniatures should be adapted to the field size, e.g. 2x3 m with 2 field players and a goalkeeper, 4x6 m with 4 field players and a goalkeeper.

As for human players, they can of course play 1-on-1, but playing 2-on-2 works perfectly, and with more players also assuming more miniatures (and space) are available. An atmosphere of table football played with the feet, or of giant, say, 8-players football table.

Basic rules:
- players take turns, and have 2 actions per turn
- possible actions are: move a miniature, or play the ball; the ball can be played directly with the foot, or a miniature can be pushed such that it moves the ball
- to be allowed to kick the ball, it must first be under control: a miniature controls the ball if one foot cannot be inserted between the miniature and the ball
- a shot on goal can be taken at any time, if the ball is under control; the intention to shoot must be announced
- the opponent can defend his goal with one foot; his other foot must stay planted behind the goal
- any contact with another miniature is a foul, which ends the turn

Other simple and logical rules take care of particular circumstances, in order to keep the flow of the game going.

Defeat your Pharaoh foe by being the first to place five pyramids in a row.

Game includes 24 plastic pyramids - 12 green and 12 blue.  Players take turns placing a pyramid on the table.  Pyramids must touch when they are placed.  First player to get 5 in a row wins!

Simple auction card game. Players have a money cards from 10 to 150 dollar bids, and two special cards "Sheikh" and "Forgery". They bid on paintings, the paintings bring victory points (4 to 15) and have a frame of color, if you collect three pictures of the same frame, yout get another 5 points. Forgery of "fake"

Gameplay is a blind auction. After considerm players scoring points for the picture and points beyond.

Theme of the game: the auction of paintings XIX - XX centuries.

"Kupi Slona" - translates "Do You Want to Buy an Elephant?". It is a version of a climbing game (a famous game mechanics).

See: Cheat

Join the galactic conflict by taking control of one of five factions in this customizable card game. Pick your strategy. You can choose to the Empire, those forces still united in uneasy alliance, or side with the rogue element, Warlord Darkon, and his stolen Imperial Technology. Become a member of the Bogathan Empire, hell-bent on making up for those centuries of denial and finally eradicating all the inferior life forms. Maybe you’ll join with the Merchant Coterie, banding up with other freighter captains, merchants, and mercenaries, while trying to fulfill your Black Market deals. Become a part of the Void, sentient battle computers that want nothing more than to continue to repair and expand, even at the expense of everything else in the galaxy.

Shattered Empires is an expandable card game where each player has their own customized deck of cards built around a chosen faction. Each deck contains a Base, which represents the player, then all the components necessary to build a fleet of ships and wage war in an effort to destroy all opposing bases.

Players take turns allocating their resources, building ships, acquiring more resources, then using those ships and resources to attack their opponents.

boardgame: Happy Feet Two: Slippery Slopes Game
description: In Slippery Slopes, players attempt to be the first to move his or her penguin to the end space. After rolling a die, a player moves his or her token to the next space matching the color or design on the die. When landing on a Happy Feet space, the token slides down a snow hill and lands in a new space.

A Sci-Fi twist where Allied forces battle to destroy a time-travel port that guarantees the survival of the enemy's supreme human race.

Time Soldiers is a turn-based game for 2 players (Axis & Allies) or two teams of up to 3 players each. Played on a board divided into 3 separate areas for temporal jumps (Giza, Egypt 2560 BC, Holy Roman Empire 1248 AD, and Central Europe 1945 AD), and 1 area where soldier reinforcements can be acquired by each team. Located in the center of the board is a time-travel port that transports soldiers through both time and space to engage in battles.

The primary object of the game for the Axis Powers is to finish their two-stage plot to rid the world of the human race in 1945, then time-travel to the future in 1946 to start a supreme human race. The primary object of the game for the Allies is to locate the time-travel port, battle Axis forces to gain control of it, and shut it down before the Axis Powers finish their two-stage plot. If either stage of the plot is finished, the dynamics of the game changes drastically with battles in both time and space.

Players control and move soldiers of different ranks and powers in an attempt to battle and control element cards used in the Axis Powers’ two-stage plot, with movement and battle results determined by dice rolls. There are multiple successful strategies that could be used to win, and both sides have an equal likelihood of winning.

Created by award-winning video game designer and artist Ray Shingler, utilizes lessons learned from a 15 year career in video game and virtual world user interaction.

A children's game from the Rupert Fun book, in 1988.

A parent should first write some short messages, each on a separate piece of paper and give one to each player. It is important that each message should be different and yet have the same number of letters in it, say fifteen.

The idea of the game is to see which player can first cross out all the letters of his message. To do this the players take turns to shut their eyes and put the point of a pencil on the checkered squares of the game. If the pencil lands on a picture, the player must say what the object is. For example, “D” for Dog. If “D” appears on the player’s paper it can be crossed off the message. Sometimes a player will be lucky enough to land on one of the squares which contain an alphabet letter (that too can be crossed off). Should the pencil land on a blank square, the player ends his turn. The games goes on in this way until one player has all the letters of his message crossed out.

Here are some examples of ready¬made 15-letter messages which the parent can use to start the game.
PLEASE COME TO TEA.
DO YOU LIKE MY TOYS?
MEET YOU TOMORROW.
I HAVE SOME SWEETS.

A Spanish-language card game that is a variant of Crazy Eights, based on the Dungeons & Dragons cartoon.

Each player is dealt four cards (or seven cards if there are only two players) with the remaining deck placed with the top card face-up beside it (if a five is revealed then it should be shuffled back into the deck and the next top card revealed instead). Players take turns placing one of their cards face-up on the revealed deck, matching either the number or colour of the previous card (e.g.: 3 matches 3 and red matches red). Fives are wild and the player placing it decides its colour and number. If a player has no cards that will match the current card or wild cards then he must draw another from the unrevealed deck until a legal play can be made, but if there are no remaining cards then he passes instead. The player to discard all his cards is the winner.

The deck's red suite is The Last Illusion episode, the green suite is The Traitor episode, the yellow suite is Day Of The Dungeon Master episode and the blue suite is The Treasure Of Tardos episode.

There are 32 playing cards plus a card explaining the rules.

The box's artwork is based on the box's artwork from Quest for the Dungeonmaster.

The goal of the game is to create a pattern of three pawns (in a row or with an "L" shape) as well as the symmetrical pattern along the diagonal of the board.

Pawn can be set at the square dictated by the dice, or moved one step to an adjacent square.

Our ancestors invented a way to reduce the craving for playing football during the cold winter months. It was the year 1900. The game was coined “Gombfoci”, a rather self-explanatory term, which describes a game played with buttons from coats upon a kitchen table. It spread like wildfire and soon came to be well known in such far off lands as Spain, USA or Brazil. These people changed the rules and by this created a new game called “12 Touches”, which later became an even greater success than its predecessor. (Geraldo Cardoso Décourt’s, in 1928).

This game simulates football, the board it is a small football field and both players control a team of disks (or rings), and move them by flicking or pressing their edges, in order to hit a smaller disc (the ball) with them. The players need to score   by moving the ball into the goal. 
A match takes 20 minutes divided in 2 halves of 10 minutes each.

The game is turn-based, only the sector ball rule will change this. A turn normally consists of moving one of your buttons. The buttons have to slide on the table, but not by pushing or pulling. They can be moved by flicking or by pressing by the finger. The buttons cannot be lifted up and put elsewhere, only after goals and before goal-kicks, free kicks, penalties or corner kicks.

Sectorball actually means a valid pass in the football game. If you move a button and the ball stops close enough (maximum one button size) to another button controlled by you, then you get another turn.

The game is also suitable for solo play. The player then controls both sides. In this can be simulating whole world championships.

Players can play single matches, tournament based matches, league or swiss system.

We are moving the two games in one box. Same board game, two approaches. East meets West on the same "soccer pitch": Sector Ball Table Football vs. 12 Touches Brazilian.

OPFOR Insurgency II is the new, solitaire version of the old OPFOR Insurgency series from Warcoach Games.  The new version is not only solitaire but incorporates all the best features of the previous Insurgency versions.

In this version, you lay down a grid of cards representing various terrain in your command area.  Underneath are secretly placed a variety of cards that could be decoys or real insurgency units of various sizes.  One card is a HVT (High Value Target). You draw a random hand and have 3 turns to find and destroy the HVT using those resources.  Your resources could include intel units to help you locate the HVT, combat units to destroy it or any insurgents, support units to add strength to your combat units, or local national units to help in both intel and combat.

Based on the best-selling Veto (second edition) CCG – the first and only Polish collectible card game – Veto: The Boardgame is set in the 17th century, the time of noblemen, winged hussars, court intrigues, and elections of the king. In this political game, which also includes voting mechanisms and resource management, players are trying to place their candidate on the Polish throne.

Description from publisher:

Even as the war continues some “older” vehicles have managed to stay on top. Or have not been destroyed yet. It's not the case for the very efficient “Nordwind”, officially know as Sd.Kfz 234/1. Its missions have remained the same for a few years now: recon and engage light armored targets. This vehicle does these two jobs superbly.

With the “Nordwind” Dust Studio is proud to introduce its new line: DUSTIFIED KITS. As usual with our Premium line these are carefully assembled and painted, and everything you see is there: the weathering, the pilot, etc. These new vehicles will add an amazing historical touch to your armies.

The Premium Edition models are not just “pre-painted”. What we offer is the exact replica of Vincent Fontaine’s work, Dust Studio's Master Painter.

boardgame: Cards Against Humanity: PAX East 2013 Promo Packs
description: Cards Against Humanity PAX East 2013 Promo Packs A, B, and C were released as promotional expansions for Cards Against Humanity at the Penny Arcade Expo East 2013 convention. Each pack contains 8 white cards and 2 black cards. The themes of these packs center around video games, sci-fi, tabletop board games, and the convention itself.

It is a survival horror dungeon crawl adventure. Each player takes on a role as a cliche movie trope and tries to survive and evening filled with Zombies, Vampires and Werewolves. Players must collect items, deal with their quirks and try not to turn into the monsters that they are running away from. Of course, if they do they will gladly turn and try to kill their former partners… gotta love contagious disease.

This is an action point based game where players move their characters toward the exit. Players must decide if they should search rooms for items, flee, or fight the monsters. Monsters move and attack based on specialized dice. As players take damage they become more likely to turn into a monster.

Players earn victory points by killing monsters, revealing rooms, and achieving objectives on their quirk cards. Players who have died still can earn victory points by killing other players.

Have you ever played the old bar game, "Bullsh!t"?

Mash PotatoS essentially is exactly that, but with potatoes instead of numbers.

A group of players sit around in a table with the deck in the middle. When it comes to their turn they draw a card and place it in the "bowl" claiming out loud what color the potato is. It then goes onto the next turn if no one yells "Mash!" accusing the played card's claim a lie.

If the accuser is right he would win; if not he would lose his next turn.

No matter what color the potato actually is, the same color can not be claimed twice in a row.

boardgame: Flash Point: Fire Rescue – Dangerous Waters
description:
Flash Point: Fire Rescue - Dangerous Waters takes the firefighting experience to – and below – the seas.

This expansion for Flash Point: Fire Rescue includes two double-sided half boards (similar to those in the 2nd Story expansion) that represent a ship on one side and a part of a submarine on the other. The interior of the ship features an engine room, cabins and heat-conducting steel walls on a 6x8 game board, while the submarine is an elongated 3x16 game board that features victims who can't just leap out of a window to safety – and who might be in danger even within the sick bay.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Probably made to cash in on decimalisation of the currency in Britasin.

Each player gets a board, with spaces for coins that will complete your image of Mr. Money. There is no choice or skill. You roll the Deci-Dice (sic) and your roll result tells you which coin you may place on your card. Play continues until one player has a completed card and that player wins.

In this game, you have captured by the village and thrust into a pit, which is the home of a dragon. You must fight the dragon to be able to get out. You are fortunate to have been left with some items, but this dungeon crawl will be hard.

To start the game, you make your character, and then the dungeon.
On your turn, you move, you fight, you see.
Will you find vast treasure, and weapons of great strength? Or will you find monsters of hideous nature? Can you survive?

If you are playing 2 player:

You have been captured by the barbaric goblins, and thrust into a pit, known as the arena. In order to get out, you must kill the other player. No rules, no limits, just a brutal blood bath. Oh, and did I mention there are still other monsters in here? Have fun...

All mechanics are the same, except you must fight the other player. And if you leave an area, it gets reset next time you enter it, so there is all ways something new.

The game features of Toby Twirl and his chums. Toby Twirl was a Pig with human hands and feet and he appeared in books by Sheila Hodgetts between 1946 and 1958. His image was illustrated by Edward Jeffrey. This game is from one of hthe Toby Twirl Adventures books.

Rules
An exciting game for a number of players. Choose one player as Leader, the rest being Runners. Use a dice for scoring and counters or buttons for players. The Leader has first throw; a six entitles anyone to an extra throw. Don’t wait for a six to commence. If a Runner catches up to, or passes, the Leader, he or she wins and takes the place of the Leader next round. If the Leader reaches home without being caught he or she is the winner and the next player in turn is the Leader for the following round.

A two player strategy game of space combat. Players take turns acting out their orders from the previous turn and then recording secret orders for their next move. Players can gain variable amounts of knowledge about their opponent's next move using their Interdictors to intercept enemy communications. Some notable elements of the game are secret orders, interception of orders, and facing-dependent piece movement.

In a two players game, the aim is to find the card hidden by your apponent according to its resemblance to your own cards in a minimum of turns.
Each player take a identical deck of card.
Each smiley on card offers 3 features: Head, eyes and mouth in 3 colors.
the first player choose a card from his deck. The second player other choose a card from his deck  and show it to the 1st player. Then, 1st player with one of the three special cards show the second player the scale of resemblance between the chosen card and the hidden one: Thumb up, flat hand or thumb down
- Wingo Dingo Trio: test your reflexes.
Depending of the result, the 2nd player place the played cards on the row in front of the special card.
Then he propose a new card, and so on.
At the end, once the card has been discovered, players count cards placed in special cards rows and score the number of turn it took to discover the oponent card.

Doubloons is a high seas pirate themed game that takes place around the Doubloon Islands.  You and up to three other people will take on the roll of one of six captains, whose goal is to claim the treasures of the surrounding islands as your own and become the wealthiest pirate in the seas.

Doubloons will be playable with 2-4 people and is intended for players of all ages.  The game board will be made up of 25 modular tiles, randomly placed at the start of each game to ensure that no two games are played exactly alike.  Players will take their ship and travel from island to island with the goal of obtaining more loot than the other players.  There will be multiple decks of cards to give you the ability to attack other players for their loot or defend against the attacks of others as well as provide for some additional effects.  Each player will also have a unique special ability that they may use during the game to give it an additional tactical challenge.

The Usual Suspects is a deduction game playable with common components.  Players attempt to deduce which of twelve spies are innocent - with no knowledge, initially, if any spies are in fact innocent.  Players record their suspicions during the game, and score them only when the game ends, such that everyone remains involved until the game is complete.

This game was released to the public in the Re: Geek of the Week #69 - Joe Huber thread.

Please note that this is a different game from the much better known and professionally published game Unusual Suspects.

boardgame: Zombicide: Special Guest Box – Kevin Walker
description:
This expansion includes two survivors and their Zombivor counterparts both designed by Kevin Walker.

Kevin Walker went freelance in 1987 and spent years illustrating for British comic 2000 AD and Games Workshop. There followed years of comics, both writing and illustrating, with a wide variety of media, before becoming a father and realizing it was time to get some work done. Illustrations for the Magic: The Gathering trading card game, several computer game projects and comic work followed, working for 2000AD, DC Comics, Dark Horse and Marvel. Working on Thunderbolts actually attracted some attention. He's now the regular penciller and inker on Avengers Arena for Marvel Comics.

In recent years he has worked on cover art for titles such as Young Bond, Artemis Fowl, Changeling and Vampirates. He is currently working mostly for Marvel Comics from his home in Yorkshire, England.

The two survivors include:

Uncle Honk

Little is known about Uncle Honk, as he likes to call himself. Most people think that he used to work in a circus. Some mutter that, more likely, he was a patient in a psychiatric hospital. What everyone knows for sure, however, is Uncle Honk is a brute with a taste for the circus world and zombicide. If he likes you, you can rely on him and all his formidable might. He’ll expect the same in return, of course. Uncle Honk likes to taunt zombies every once in a while, to get in a “funny” during a fight. With his blood-chilling laugh, Uncle Honk is both your best friend and your worst nightmare.

Butch

After graduation, Butch held down a deadend job for a while before embracing the biker life. For him, it wasn’t really about the one percenter life, but more about getting some freedom of choice, a little room to breath in a constricted world. Better to die young with stories to tell than as a senile fool in a gloomy hospital, right? Living on the edge taught Butch a useful trick or two that let him survive when the zombies invaded. Absolute freedom is now at hand, and despite all the slaughter, life is good....

boardgame: Zombicide: Special Guest Box – Adrian Smith
description:
This expansion includes two survivors and their Zombivor counterparts both designed by Adrian Smith.

Adrian Smith (born 1969 in England, United Kingdom) is a illustrator/concept designer living in Scotland. Best known for his work with Games Workshop and numerous similar companies, Adrian has also done concept design work for computer games companies such as THQ and EA. Other credits include a short comic series in France written by Pat Mills and book illustrations alongside renowned artists from around the world. Recently Adrian has been self publishing, the first project being his art book "Illuminations", and plans are in motion for a series of comics early in 2013. In addition to these personal projects, he still finds time for freelance work with various companies."

The two survivors include:

Padre Johnson

Padre Johnson led his small flock during the early days of the apocalypse, until they were finally, inevitably overrun.  The Padre was the only survivor, miraculously emerging unscathed from the ruins of his church.  Now he spreads the good Word amongst the survivors, albeit slightly unhinged from his narrow escape from death.  Still he's handy with a shotgun and a quick prayer, and is always a welcome sight.

Bones

Bones is (or was) the lead guitarist of a heavy metal band famous the world over... but lately things have been getting a little stale.  World tours, sell out stadiums, groupies, even the drugs and booze just weren't that fun anymore.  In his darkest and most sober moments, Bones knew he hadn't really done anything creative for the best part of a decade - just rehashing the same old thing over and over....

The zombie apocalypse changed all that of course, and ironically Bones is now incredibly inspired to create songs about death, blood and liberation!  Unfortunately, electricity is hard to come by so he's perfecting his strictly acoustic metal for now.

boardgame: Zombicide: Special Guest Box – Karl Kopinski
description:
This expansion includes two survivors and their Zombivor counterparts both designed by Karl Kopinski.

Karl Kopinski was born in Nottingham in 1971, drawing became an obsession from an early age. After studying fine art at university he picked up his first freelance work with Games Workshop’s publishing house Black Library, which quickly led to a position in the main GW art department where he worked for 7 years, looking for new challenges he returned to freelance work around 5 years ago. He is currently still based in Nottingham and now works in a wide variety of fields and media including book illustration, concept design for the games industry, military history paintings and portraiture, and of course the legendary Magic: the Gathering cards!!

The two survivors include:

Angry Mary

Mary used to lead a peaceful life in retail, with a husband (cop), two kids and a house with a white picket fence. Then the zombies came and took it all.  Returning home to an (almost) empty house one day, she narrowly escaped becoming zombie chow by dispatching the untidy interlopers of her lovely home. Something snapped, and the wild energy of her misspent youth came roaring back. Mary emptied her husband’s gun safe and turned into an urban warrior. She does seem to have a special grudge against zombies though and never passes up a chance to kill them.

Red Cap Ben

As a young boy, Ben was strongly influenced by the Cold War and the fashion for survival that abounded from the 60’s to the 80’s. He went from job to job, knowing the inevitable would happen and preparing for it. He didn’t really expect zombies to show up, though, and still suspects it might be a government conspiracy. Nowadays, Ben is best known as “the red cap”, a hardcore survivalist with numerous successes on what seemed impossible missions. As grumpy as he acts, Ben likes the nickname.

Boozle is a party game perfect for up to eight players. Boozles are party animals. They come in many colors and each have their own style of drinking. To win the game all you have to do is collect five Boozles of the same color by drawing them and stealing them from other players. Outmaneuver your friends and force them to drink while you steal their precious Boozles!

Each player has the same five actions available to them: Snatch, Nope, Drink, Draw and Repeat.  At the beginning of each turn all players simultaneously choose two of their actions and play them in secret.  Players then go around the table revealing and resolving their actions until everyone has used both of their actions.  At this point the actions go back into your hand and the turn starts over.


    Poozles (purple) are classy fellows. They drink like sirs.
    Roozles (red) are angry drunks. If something wasn't destroyed, the night wasn't a success. 
    Yoozles (yellow) are the life of the party. Free rounds and high-fives for everyone.
    Groozles (green) are a bit much. They always take things one step too far. 
    Bloozles (blue) are drama queens. If they aren't making a scene, they're crying in the corner.

Shattered Olympus is a tactical mythological card game for 2-4 players. Two factions fight with unique heroes on a battlefield und can play cards from a central card pool. With crystal as resource, they can built up their fighting forces with units, equipment, spells and locations. To win the game, one faction has to defeat the opponent heroes. The card deck is customizable to provide a huge amount of variety. This game was designed to minimize the impact of luck and therefore doesn’t include dies or coin flipping mechanics.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
From the publisher: "The wise ones of a small tribal village have gathered everyone around the campfire. 'We are searching for the most able tribes person in the village. They will be chosen as our tribal champion!' The tribes people work hard to impress them. They recount stories they were told as youngsters, hunt bison, gather berries and defend the village against rival tribes. Soon they will find out which one of them will achieve the most glory and earn the respect of the wise ones."

Triba Vivo is a simple card game with an Esperanto theme, as componets are written in this language. Players try to get the majority of the cards of one of four types in order to get victory points. The point values for each type range from 0 up to 3, and this is defined randomly at the begining of each round. The game lasts as much rounds as needed until one player gets 12 victory points, who then wins the game.

Each round has two phases. First, each player gets 5 cards. Then 1 card is drew from the deck and put face up at the end of each point of a five-point Esperanto-Star depicted on the gameboard. Next players alternately select 1 card from their hands and put it in one of the 5 points of the Star (up to a maximum equal to the number of players plus one), not covering completely the card below. The player also has to put one of his/her playing pieces in anyone of the numbered rondels along each arm of the Esperanto-Star. At the end of this phase all cards and pieces will have been set upon the game board.

Now comes the second phase. Beginning with the star arm marked with a small white star, each star arm is resolved clockwise in the following manner. The player who has a piece of his/her color in the highest rank, as defined by the numbers on the rondels (lowest numbers are highest) is the first to take one of any of the cards on that star arm. Then the second highest player selects one of the remaining cards, and so on, until each player took one card. There will be one card left behind on the star point. Remaining star arms are resolved in the same way. Victory points are then scored according to the rules described above. If none achieves 12 points, a new round begins.

From http://www.illgottengames.net/pocket-tactics.

Pocket-Tactics is a 3d print-and-play strategy boardgame in which fantasy forces battle for control of a hex tile-based map. The game is fast-paced (most games lasting between 10-30 minutes), designed for ease of printing, and the entire game is small enough to fit in your pocket. The system is dynamic though resolution is quick and there aren't a ton of rules to track or memorize, making it ideal for advanced and casual gamers alike.

We are the citizens of the Weate State. We deserve to be happy, we deserve to move up in this world, and this company. If someone else is being successful, that only makes your success less. You must defeat the other citizens of the Weate State that are trying to be better than you. You will have to liquidate their assets, buy them out, and engage in a little bit of friendly “combat-atition”.

Weate State is a living card game currently in development by Right After Then.

Players take the role of a character in the Weate State.  Everyone starts with a random deck of cards and a character card.  Throughout the game players have to make choices as to what items they want to use, and what they want to deposit into the bank to use as money.  Many ways exist to take down opponents, but in the end only one will stand with their deck, bank, and and health in intact.

description:
Top-A-Top Kids is a new version of Kuźnia Gier's Top-A-Top party game that's designed especially for children.

Game play remains much the same as in the original game as the players take turns placing their cards in the middle of the table and reacting appropriately to the funny pictures on the cards played, e.g., by making special gestures or saying something. Having good reflexes and the ability to make associations is crucial as your reaction has to be quick and appropriate, or else you lose a turn and take all the cards on the table into your pile. The first player to get rid of all of her cards wins.

You can use these 71 cards to randomly determine which bank chips to use for each game of Puzzle Strike. You can also use them to choose random characters. Contains 51 puzzle chip cards (covering all puzzle chips in the base game, Shadows expansion, and promo chips) as well as all 20 characters (10 from base game, 10 from the Shadows) expansion.
(from the Sirlin Games webpage)

Chuhan War is a 2-3 player wargame that simulates the battle between Chu kingdom and Han kingdom during 206 B.C. to 202 B.C. It is a strategic and simple historical game. There are 14 turns and 4 scenarios. Players move their units to attack enemy’s units and occupy the provinces to gain supply points. Each player can draw other kingdoms over to his side during the event phase. It is necessary to spend supply points to recruit units.

A player wins by eliminating the opposing king.

Command Line is a table top game where players take command of vast robot armies and battle across a system of planets. The board is made up of many different planets currently inhabited by biological lifeforms. Each players starts with their own planet, some units, and some generators. As generators produce more units and currency, you will expand to different plants and destroy the inhabitants. Currency can be used to buy upgrades, more generators, and tech cards. Tech cards allow you to modify the moves you take, some will even give you the power to destroy entire planets.

This is a game of pure strategy; there are no dice rolls, random card draws, or any form of luck. Combat is resolved with a calculation of attack and defensive power, and these powers can be improved throughout the game. By the end of this war, armies will spread across the system, devastate their opponents forces, and even destroy planets.

The game Silhouette consists of 48 tiles with silhouette images on one side. These are placed on the table face down. The active player flips over two tiles and tries to make a compound word out of the two images. If successful, the player keeps the pair and goes again; if not, the tiles are returned and the next player now goes.

Once all tiles have been claimed (or no other pairings are possible), the player with most tiles wins.

boardgame: For the Crown (Second Edition): Expansion #1 – Shock & Awe
description:
Tensions mount in the war for the crown, and both sides are turning to new tactics and strange allies. Battlefields are haunted by ghosts, buffeted by invisible forces and wracked by lightning. Armies march under the auspice of statues, and secure formations are breached by attacks from odd directions. Which side will best master these new devices and capture the enemy sovereign?

For our popular deck-building game, For the Crown 2nd edition, comes the first expansion, Shock & Awe, introducing 13 new cards and 10 new unit types to pit against your opponent. Bolster your economy with the Caravan, duplicate key assets with the Changeling, and rule the battlefield with the mighty Amazon. Who has the wit and will to lead these new forces to victory, and who will succumb to Shock and Awe?

Note, the For the Crown Expansion Kit 1: Shock & Awe is not a ‘stand alone’ game. You must own a copy of For the Crown 2nd edition to use this expansion kit. This expansion kit cannot be used with the first edition of For the Crown.

From a user review of the Sportsman edition:

Players divide themselves into teams. Teams alternate turns choosing stunts and trying to complete them within the sixty-second time limit. Each completed stunt earns the player/team one point. The first entity to reach the predetermined goal, the rules suggest eleven, wins.

The Sportsman game comes with instructions for fifteen stunts and props. The Mattel Board Game version comes with equipment/instructions for 40 stunts.

boardgame: Only Fools and Horses Trotters Trading Game
description:
This game is based on the BBC TV comedy series "Only Fools and Horses".

The goal of the game is to become a millionaire buy doing some dodgy deals around Peckam and then going to the Casino to win your fortune. You need a minimum bid of a "monkey" (500 quid). Be the first to win a "million".

The Nymph was available for purchase through the Geek Store (now out of stock).

From the product page:

"The wilderness surrounding the kingdom is rumored to contain many mysterious and wondrous things, but rare is the day when one of them makes its presence known. The Nymph has strange and potent abilities linked to the cycles of nature, and motives that few men might guess, but any ally against the Necromancer is welcome in the kingdom today."

This is a special, promotional package that is being offered exclusively to BoardGameGeek for six months (as of April 15th, 2013). This package includes the Nymph along with 10 power cards, plus a new event and an artifact. This is a must-have for all Darkest Night fans!

This game requires Darkest Night

Game Components:

•    10 Power cards
•    1 Artifact card
•    1 Event card
•    1 Hero card
•    1 character tracker (minor assembly reqired)

Two games in one!

The object of Cross Factors is to be the first to cover two sets of numbers (from 1 to 12) by moving around on the board.  For example, if a player landed on the number 24 then he could cover a 6 and a 4, or a 2 and a 12.

In Cross Multiply you try to gain the most points by creating rows of three or more on a multiplication table.  This is done by first creating two numbers by rolling two dice (twice) and adding them together.  These numbers are then used as the X and Y indices (or vice versa) on the table to claim a square in the table.  On each subsequent turn the player can choose to replace one of the two numbers and thereby capture a new position on the table.

In Pactio all players build the same city. However as all of the players get different roles, they might have different agendas, and ideas for the city.

The game center point is buying buildings and developing them, as they often synergise with themselves, or your role. They also serve as your source of income to fuel your actions for the future. In addition players influence public figures and/or make deals with each other to further aid your goal.

The goal of the game is to get the most victory points by the end of 16-th turn, that is when the game ends. You gain them, again, from buildings, but also from role's missions, which might be related to buildings, but might be completely unique things. For example Pishop gets 5 Victory points if all other players' final scores are not too far apart (difference can't be bigger than 6).

Unique aspects of the game include that all deals are binding, meaning they are enforced by the game rules. Also the implementation of roles in an economical environment leads to loads of new and very interesting gameplay.

Description from the publisher:

Dr. Seuss: 3 Terrific Card Games! includes Crazy 8s, Go Fish, and a one-of-a-kind ABC Memory game with The Cat in the Hat, Horton, Yertle the Turtle, and many more favorite Dr. Seuss characters!

boardgame: Legacy: The Card Game – Core Set
description:
Legacy: The Card Game  is a fantasy based card battle game played by two opposing players in a turn-by-turn style.

Both players start with 1200 life points.

Each player builds their own deck or uses a Pre-Constructed deck from the Legacy Core Set, and tries to get the opponents life points to Zero through the use of Characters, Spells and Ttems.

Legacy TCG is VAST and has an expansive universe! There are many Heroes with Amazing powers at your disposal.  The type of decks you build are entirely up to you... Will you use a Hero? or Multiple heroes? or maybe try to over run your enemy with only monsters? Perhaps Playing only Gadgets and Gizmos to trap your opponent using technology?  Perhaps restricting your opponents movements through the use of Control cards? There are near endless combinations at your fingertips!

Geodeto Geodeto is an EASY-FAST-FUN card game with pyramids for 2-6 players (age 8+) that takes the best from Pyramideto game and increases the gaming experience for older players.

There are six Continent cards in six colors in the game representing Europe, Asia, America, Oceania, Antarctica and Africa.
Every continent is connected to two neighboring continents and to the World Ocean in the middle of the board.

Players set for how many fulfilled goals they want to play to control the length of the game. (Publisher recommands 10-15 goals per game.)

Once the last game is fulfilled, players count their points. The one with the most points wins the game.

Motto: Travelers, globetrotters, set the sails and sail the seven seas. Can you circumnavigate the world in 20 minutes?

You need one set of iGo pyramids (= 18 pyramids) and a deck of Geodeto cards to play Geodeto.

In Cinderella Royal Ball Game, the players are trying to collect as many figures from the dance floor as they can, but you never know where the music might take them...

To set up the game, place the character figures in their bases and the bases on the dance floor. Players then take turns drawing a card from the deck:


    If you draw a character card, place it in front of you.
    If you draw a pass card, pass one character card that doesn't have a figure on it to the player on your left.
    If you draw a dance card, press the button on the dance floor and watch the figures waltz around. When the dancing stops, you claim any figures in your sector of the dance floor if you have a character card matching those figures.


When all the figures have been claimed or the deck runs out, the game ends. Players score the points listed on the character cards they hold – but only if they hold this figure! The player with the highest score wins.

boardgame: Labyrinth Life: The Maze of Being You
description:
"Life is a maze—an infinite maze—that shifts with the will of its inhabitants. You are an explorer of this labyrinthine realm, trying on roles and pioneering foreign terrain, aiding friends and outsmarting rivals. To shape the world and find your place in it means drawing on powers you never knew you had in the hopes of one day reaching your goal, the one prize that can't be found around any of the labyrinth's dizzying twists and bends: the real you."


     An Awesome Game


Labyrinth Life is a tabletop strategy card game for 2-6 players, ages 12 and up. It's fast to learn, and fun to master.

In Labyrinth Life, you play an explorer who braves an ever-changing maze in search of your future self. Will you be the first to reach bliss as perhaps the Richest Woman in New Mazonia or perhaps the Hallball Team Captain? It's all up to you!

Over the course of the game, players build a maze of hexagonal cards and they move their maze explorers through it.

Every round, you move more maze explorer forward 5 spaces, draw a card, and play a card. You play a card by placing it in the maze and using one of the two powers listed on it.

The sixteen powers allow you to affect the maze and the explorers within it. It's possible to chain multiple powers and plan elaborate strategies over multiple turns.

Strategies center around moving your explorer into cards related to your goal card, and keeping other players from moving their explorers into cards related to theirs. You gain two points for moving your explorer into cards that share a power with your goal card and you lose one point for every card you move into that isn't related. Goals can change.

You win the game by reaching the target point total.


     A Work of Art


Each deck of Labyrinth Life is different. The cast of characters, constellations of maze paths, and the solitaire maze that spans the back of your 120-card deck are absolutely unique, and offer an entirely different play experience from every other deck out there.


     The Ultimate Collaboration


Early supporters of Labyrinth Life suggest and vote on the characters that will populate the maze. Each deck is a unique culture built from the the pool the fans create.

La Bataille de Leipzig 1813 - La Bataille de Dölitz 1813

The Battle of Leipzig, fought in 1813, is one of the largest conflicts ever and involved many countries / kingdoms fighting against each other. This included a coalition of Russia, Prussia and Austria committed to defeat France and its allies, forging a domination of Europe that would lead to the Nationalism of Germany. While historians have drawn a myriad of contrary opinions about who were the good guys and bad guys, the battle encircles the city of Leipzig with 500,000 man armies facing each other.

La Bataille de Dölitz 1813 occured on October 16h, when forces under Général de Division Kaminieki (Polish) and General Lefol (French) defended the line along  the Pleisse River. The river was murky and swollen from previous weeks of rainfall. Men and horses could cross the river only in a few places. These crossings were at Connewitz and Dölitz. Part of the 26e Polish division defended the intact bridge at Dölitz with its manor house guarding the entrance.

This is the area that Schwarzenberg choose to lead an assault to outflank Napoleon’s army. Therefore Schwarzenberg committed substantial Austrian troops to this assault.While outnumbering the French and Polish forces, General der Kavallerie Graf Merveldt’s 2nd Army Abteilung found forcing either crossing a major problem. Unable to deploy his artillery because of the swampy ground, his infantry fought a battle of attrition with an enemy in built-up areas or on the other side of a major river.

Merveldt has been assigned one thing however that may assist him in getting across. He has the two army pontoon and two pioneer companies. They can be used to throw a bridge across the Pleisse or repair the bridge at Connewitz. The player will note immediately the differences in the different formations and how each must be managed to achieve success.

Each 20 minute turn starts with a Command Phase for both sides followed by the French Player sequence and then by the Coalitian Player sequence. The sequence includes movement, cavalry charges, fire and melee. During each, morale plays a decisive role. This is a great one player on one player game but will also accommodate two players on a side also.

The original rules have been updated, but not expanded to any extent. The original designers of the La Bataille Series believe the Premier Rules facilitate / expedite the most accurate and competitive play. The new Premier Rules are an update of the original rules but make it easier to find specific topics and includes some user requested rules such as command control.

This game was initially offered as print and play by Marshal Enterprises; it is now removed and available as part of the commercial La Bataille de Leipzig.

The scale is standard "La Bat":

     100 meters per hex.
     100 men per infantry strength point/ 50 men per cavalry strength point/ 2 to 4 guns per strength point for artillery.

boardgame: Castle Panic: Any Color Hero Promo
description:
This card was given out at International Tabletop Day.

Its text reads:

"Hit 1 Monster in any color Archer, Knight, or Swordsman ring."
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Word-building card game. One set of cards features beginning letters or letter combinations; the other features Lickety Quick clues—part of speech, double letters, number of syllables, and more. Combine the two to make a word first and you win the round and gets the cards as a points.

Player who has collected the most points (cards) at the end of the game is the winner

The game components are the (700) pieces of a jigsaw puzzle. Each piece has one of five symbols embossed on it. Solid-color pieces may be able to fit in more than one position in the puzzle, but other pieces will only have one valid position.

The players are all working to assemble the puzzle; using the main set of rules, one player plays for a minute at a time, with the others using this time to select pieces from the box. There are also "scramble" rules in which all players can select pieces and play them as they wish.

Regardless of the rules used, players score points for playing pieces, with more points being awarded for playing more pieces and for playing rows of three pieces with the same symbol or squares of pieces with four different symbols. Points are also awarded for correcting misplaced pieces.

Players can play for a given amount of time or until someone reaches a given score (or until the puzzle is finished).

boardgame: Go Diego Go! Rescue the Animals
description:
Simple card and logic game for kids (4+). Each player receives 6 blue cards at the beginning of the game, placed face-up in front of him. There are 4 animals on each blue card. Every turn, a player draws an orange card from a common deck. There are two animals on each orange card, with or without a big red "X" on them. All players then try to discard blue cards based on the conditions of the orange card.

If the two animals on the orange card are not marked with an X, a player can discard all blue cards featuring both animals. If the two animals on the orange card are marked with an X, a player can discard all blue cards not featuring both animals. If only one of the animals is marked with an X, discarded cards must feature the unmarked animal and exclude the marked animal. If you cannot discard, you must draw another blue card. There are 6 special orange cards that allow only the drawing player to discard.

The first player to get rid of all his blue cards wins, he has saved the animals!

In Zooligans card game players take on the role of zookeepers trying to find animals to complete their exhibits. In so doing, they attract visitors to make the best zoo. The game is set up with a three exhibit goals, such as "4 or more reptiles" or "3 or more rainforest animals" that are central to all players. In addition, players have a secret continental theme, scoring them bonus visitors for animals from their continent.

When the game begins, players start with a hand of three animal cards. Each turn, players can bid on a worldwide animal in the auction. Then they play a card adding it to their zoo. When a player has all the animals to complete an exhibit goal, they can form the exhibit, attracting a number of visitors.

At the end of the game, the points are tallied, and the player with the most visitors wins.

Take me out to the ball game!

Nick's Finger Baseball is a free, two player game of strategy and outwitting. Playing the role of the captains of two baseball teams, both endeavor to lead their team to victory.

Over the course of 9 innings, there are a large number of turns. In each turn, each player simultaneously reveals their strategy for that part of the game. The player whose team is on offence reveals a number between 0 and 4, trying to reveal a high number... or are they? For they are also trying to avoid revealing one of the 2 numbers the defense revealed at that time, as revealing those spells disaster, which just gets worse the higher the number is! Do you shoot for high numbers and hope the defense isn't there, or try to sneak a lower number past them? As the defense, do you block the high numbers to stop power plays, or aim lower and stop sneaky plays?

Those are the types of choices you have to make in Nick's Finger Baseball, a battle of wits for 2 players!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Welcome to the Port of Dutch Harbor, the gateway to King Crab fishing in Alaska. Every year, hundreds of crews gather in this small port to take advantage of the very short fishing season in their attempts to get rich overnight. But beware, the Bering Sea comes with great threats, challenging even the fittest. You will face freezing temperatures, waves the size of a ten stories block, heavy lifting, and drastic weather changes in a matter of hours.

In Perfect Storm: Alaska, 2-5 players take the role of small entrepreneurs looking to make their fortune fishing in the Bering Sea. You will start small, with a basic ship and a rookie crew led by a captain with big dreams but little experience. At first, your crew will venture into shallow waters after the easiest prey, the fish. With the money you earn, you will afford a better crew, maybe even a bigger boat and you'll be able to catch shrimp or even the most valuable King Crab – but you must hurry and use your resources wisely, the fishing season is short and the weather changes in no time.

Every turn, each player will command his crew, using his 3-5 actions to move to a new untouched area or to fish. Depending on the crew experience and the depth of the water, your crew will catch fish (the least valuable), shrimp or crab (the most valuable).

The weather cards dictate the wind speed, which will increase the waves. With a small boat you will have to face the storm in shallow waters, but with a bigger boat and an experienced crew you'll be able to defy the waves and venture into the eye of the storm to catch a larger quantity of King Crab. But beware, every time the wave size is greater that your ship's stability, you will roll the danger die and see whether you ship capsizes.

Perfect Storm: Alaska is a game of risk management and every player will have to calculate his decision to fish for the most valuable prey without exposing his crew and cargo to unnecessary danger. In the end, the winner will be the player who will find the best balance between his boat, crew and captain, the place to fish and the risk to face bad weather.

In Alhambra, players are acquiring buildings to be placed within their Alhambra complex.

On a player's turn, a player may take money from the open money market, purchase a building from the building market, or engage in construction and re-construction projects with buildings that have been placed in the player's reserve. The game rewards efficiency, as when a player purchases a building from the market for the exact amount of money, the player may take another turn.

Players with the most buildings in each of the six building types score in each of the scoring phases, and points are awarded for players' longest external "wall" section within their complex. The game ends when the building market can no longer be replenished from the building tile supply, and there is a final scoring, whereupon the player with the highest score wins.

This particular box, The Family Box, comes also with Alhambra: The Vizier's Favor expansion and a Pro version of Granada game.

The Vizier's Favor expansion adds 4 new modules to Alhambra:

1. The primary module is "The Vizier's Favor", which allows players to buy a building out of turn but has to be paid with exact amount.

2. "The Builders Huts" (Die Bauhütte) introduces a number of new tiles that only count in the scoring when other tiles of the same color are built next to them. Hut tiles are free, but their number is limited to three per player.

3. With the "The Currency Exchange" (Wechselstube), players can buy building tiles by mixing different currencies.

4. The fourth module is a set of bonus cards that are dealt to the players. Each of the tiles shows a particular building. If the matching building is part of your Alhambra, then the bonus card can be revealed and will count as another building of that category.

About Granada, also called as Alhambra Advanced: it is a standalone game the players develop the city of Granada next to Sierra Nevada, where the famous Alhambra stands, by buying building tiles to construct the city which will earn the most victory points. Building tiles now have two sides and there are new building types for which the value is set during the game.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

boardgame: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Sewer Battle Game
description:
Master Splinter has sent the Turtles out to get some Pizza for dinner. While traveling in the sewers, they are attacked by the evil Shredder and his minions!

Game Objective: Be the first player to get your Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtle back to Sewer Lair space first!

Game Play: Players spin the Turtle Spinner and move the indicated amount show along the board path. Bad Guys move at the beginning of each players turn. If a Bad Guy lands on a space with a player’s Ninja Turtle a battle will take place.

Starting the Game: Get ready for some serious Turtle Action as you fight your way to getting some Pizza! It’s Cowabunga Time Dude!!

Turn Summary: The player next to Turtle player spins the Bad Guys Spinner and moves the indicated Bad Guy shown on the spinner. Example: If spinner arrow lands on 1 or 3, then the Shredder pawn will be moved the number spaces towards the player’s Turtle pawns. If a Bad Guy lands on a space with a player’s Ninja Turtle a battle will take place.

Winning the Game: The first player to their Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtle back to Sewer Lair space wins!
