Katachi is a unique geometric strategy game for 2-4 players that teaches about sequences and how to group objects by their similarities.

Roll the two dice and then find a tile that meets the criteria shown. Next move the tile back toward your "home" corner as many steps as possible by matching its color, size or shape. When you're moved as far as you can, the next player goes. The first player to get a stack of three on top of their original starting tile, plus one more on the adjacent space, is the winner.

Place balls in the center of the Upper Plate and spin the top. Balls are thrown helter-skelter causing some of the balls to be thrown off of the plate and then drop into holes on the lower plate. The balls come to rest in the little scoring areas where each player scores points dependent on where the balls land. Play continues until 1 player scores 100 points.

In Math Room, you'll do basic addition or subtraction trying to be the first to cover all the windows on your numbered board. Solve the problem on your key that corresponds to the color just rolled on the die. If anyone wants to try a different problem, they race to be first to ring the bell. This lets them flip over their key and do the problem on the back instead. This game is a great way to practice plus and minus with answers ranging from 1 to 10 - perfect for counting on small fingers or toes!

Four cards are revealed for the players to remember... One card is slid below the King in the middle of the table and the rest are shuffled into the deck.  The King has hidden his only heir to the throne (one of the 4 cards the players just saw) and it is up to you to find them.  As you draw a card into your hand and play a card to family lineage lines on the table, you begin to prove which peasants and nobles are not related to the King, the hidden one becomes more apparent to everyone, as more cards are played to the table.
Your family lineage lines on the table prove which people are not royal heirs. Players must complete family lines with a family crest card (to score extra points)  before the other player slaps the King and names the hidden heir.   You must decide between quickly exposing the King's secret before your opponent can play the right cards.. or making sure your family lines are complete so you score more points.   As the final cards are played in a round its a matter of seconds before one player slaps the king card and names the hidden heir card.

A game of strategy, deduction and timing.

Quandrum is a game that shifts under your feet like a hundred angry snakes.  Players play cards from their hands to move their tokens around a board.  Cards can also be played to change what each space on the board does, the location of the goal, the fundamental rules of the game, and a multitude of other effects.  With over a hundred unique cards in the deck each game is going to be special.  To win a player simply has to land their token on the same space as the goal.  Assuming the goal stays where it is.

A simple game in a small box which suites perfectly for trips or chat-ins with the guests. One of the players becomes a mad professor, others – his bachelors. All together they try to create a monster Frankenstein of different body parts (arms, legs, heads, horns, tails, etc.) The person who has made the monster first wins the round.
The game process consists of two main parts: making professors tasks and independent investigations. In the first part you have to four-flush a little and to make measured decisions about components pass, in the second part you have to consider the variants of the situation development for all parties. Finally you’ll have asoft and easy game with a short set where either everything may be computed beforehand, or you may just enjoy the game.

“Defensive line” is a game about severe fights which took place in Brest fortress in the beginning of WWII. Each player takes command of one or several units holding their positions in the fortress. The aim is to stand firm, restraining the enemy, as long as it’s possible.
Does everybody play for Soviet Army?
Yes, each player is in command of  Red Army unit which can accomplish different military missions. You can win if you exactly coordinate your efforts, which requires high cooperation of players. “Defensive line” teaches to understand each other at a glance, because altogether you have one goal which you must gain at any costs. Any mistake may lead to the destruction of the whole unit.
What does the move look like?
The units hold several positions when the game starts. At every move the enemy’s troops attack the position. Players choose one of the actions (for example, install mines, build fortifications, fire machine-gun etc), according to the situation and the type of enemies. At the end of the move residuary enemy troops attack the fortification.
What does the gameplay look like?
“Defensive line” is a cooperative game, where the mechanics itself takes the role of opponent. The actions of units are simulated by the cards with tactical decisions. Each unit has 10 cards of a kind. Having played one card you can take another one from the deck without looking. The point of the game is to use tactical cards which are the most effective in particular moments. The victory is possible only when you have the high level of the cooperation of actions: each player must assess the situation and deliberately and precisely use their opportunities. Even when you use an optimal strategy there is a chance of losing or winning with heavy casualties.
Who is “Defensive line” for?
•    For those who like good collective games.
•    For playing with friends or family.
•    For the journey (game board is composed of the cards).
•    For those who like extraordinary and difficult enough table games. 
•    For connoisseurs of balanced tactical games.

Embody an inventor who has to end his invention before rival  and progress through the diverse stages of manufacturings by avoiding the traps of your enemy. Be the first one to end your invention and become a Inventor Master.

It is up to you to find your winning strategy, but be careful! Any strategies have their double sharp edges. Will you opt for the attack or the defense? Will you keep your cards in hand or you will be afraid of being stolen to them?

Take part from now on in this intense full of emotion adventure or everything can change any time!

Specifically: You deploy three sources that are based on your progression through the four cards that represent the devellopement of your invention. Feel free to block the sources of your opponent so they can not play objectives or will you choose to keep on hand of cards that will protect your source material. Players must play cards that are used either to advance or regress to another.


Incarnez un inventeur qui doit terminer son invention avant son éternel rival et progressez à travers les diverses étapes de fabrications en évitant les pièges de votre ennemie. Soyez le premier à terminer votre invention et devenez Maître Inventeur.

Libre à vous de trouver votre stratégie gagnante, mais soyez prudent!  Toutes stratégies ont leurs double tranchants. Opterez-vous pour l’attaque ou la défense? Garderez-vous vos cartes en main ou aurez-vous peur de vous les faire voler?

Prenez part dès maintenant à cette intense aventure chargée d’émotions ou tout peut changer d’un instant à l’autre!

Concrètement : Vous déployez 3 sources qui sont la base de votre progression à travers les 4 cartes objectifs qui représentent le dévellopement de votre invention. Libre à vous de bloquer les sources de votre adversaire pour qu'il ne puisse pas jouer d'objectifs ou bien ferez-vous le choix de garder en main des cartes qui protègerons vos sources matérielles. Les joueurs doivent jouer des cartes qui servent soit à avancer ou a faire regresser l'autre.

“Varjazi & Basileus” is a supplement for Saga that features a 12 page full color rule booklet containing rules and lists for the Pagan Rus, the Era Of The Princes Rus and the Byzantines (as originally featured in a long sold-out issue of Wargames Illustrated.) There is also a new Hero Of The Viking Age for the Byzantines and a new Swords For Hire unit. The booklet also comes with 3 battleboards (Rus, Pagan Rus & Byzantines,) all contained in a fantastic SAGA folder. Made of sturdy cardboard, this folder is full color (inside and out) and provides an excellent way to transport rulebooks, supplements and battleboards.

This is not a stand-alone game and the Saga core rules are required to play

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

"A game that Plans for your future" Some kind of Monopoly clone that is supposed to teach you how to manage money.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
Net Results Soccer is an individual player based game that tries to incorporate everything that happens in a football/soccer game into the cards. Pretty much every result is taken off of the player cards.

Each position player is rated for goal scoring, shooting, possession, passing, defense, fouls, cards, offsides and endurance.Each keeper is rated for saves, passing and cards.

The game is triggered by results on the player cards through five dice - two 10-sided dice, one 20-sided die and two 8-sided dice. The only extra chart there is concerns special plays like corner kicks and direct kicks and those charts will be memorized within a game or two.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

From woot.com an adventure in offline commerce, a monopoly parody
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

boardgame: May Day: Dice Game – Hey Its Fred!
description:
Spread peace with May Day Baskets. Be the first player to score 99 points. Roll the 10 custom dice and keep any Peace symbols you roll. Then roll the remainder or stop with what you have. But, if you roll a reverse R Rabies Cat symbol and no Peace symbols then your turn ends and you score no points for that turn. Green Splat symbols rolled have no effect good or bad.

Each dice has 3 May Day peace symbols, 2 neutral splat symbols and 1 rabies cat R symbol.

MAMAEV KURGAN, STALINGRAD, RUSSIA, 27 September 1942: Acknowledging the damage done to his army in assaulting the southern and central sections of the city, General von Paulus had systematically reconfigured his forces for a fresh assault. Two shock groups were formed, with one consisting primarily of the 100th Jäger Division, whose task was to clear and control Mamaev Kurgan, followed by an advance into the Tennis Racket and the Bannyi Ravine and finally to occupy the Krasnyi Oktiabr workers' settlements. Paulus' counterpart, General Chuikov was well aware of these preparations and had planned appropriate spoiling attacks, though he was unaware of the fresh German reinforcements. His attack was disrupted before it could even begin, with waves of Stukas strafing his advancing forces. His strongpoint on the crest of the burial mound was destroyed. Bombs and artillery cut down the attackers, decimating their ranks and allowing the following German advance to gain ground. The jägers advanced two regiments abreast and captured the western slopes, but initially failed to take the southern slopes. The 227th Jäger Regiment's vigorous assaults, supported by intense artillery, destroyed the Russian ability to hold on. The Germans would secure the summit that evening (though some bypassed remnants would harass the Germans on the southern and western slopes for the next few days). By day's end, the 95th Division retained a foothold on the northern and eastern slopes of the burial mound, but was down to only 400 effective fighting men.

The Battle of Stalingrad has received no shortage of treatment in the wargame realm since Red BarricadesTM first focused the attention of ASLers on the factory complexes of the doomed city. No tactical-level game has ever treated the titanic and unique battle for Stalingrad's Mamayev Hill, aka Mamayev Kurgan ... until NOW. From the first thrusts of Hitler's Wehrmacht on 13 September to the last days of 1942 the hill changed hands time and again. By 27 September the Germans had again captured half of Mamayev Kurgan. The Soviets held their own positions on the slopes of the hill, as the 284th Rifle Division desperately defended key strongholds. The defenders held out until 26 January 1943, when the Soviet winter offensive known as Operation Uranus, relieved them, in the process trapping and destroying the German VI Armee in the tomb of Stalingrad.

"Get the Cheese!" is probably the cheesiest card game you ever put your hands on!

It is a non-collectible, non-customizable easy-to-pick up yet quite strategic and fun card game for 2 - 4 players.

Choose one out of four unique, whacky Characters, each of them coming with a 30 card deck filled with helpful, equally whacky and way cheesy “Pals”, strange “Stuff” and dastardly “Deeds” and go on the hunt for the Cheese! Play your Pals, Stuff and Deeds wisely and attack your opponents in order to be the first player to get a hold of 20 Cheese or win by owning the “Master Cheese” when the first deck is depleted. Every card can be played alternately for its “Master Cheese Effect” so there are plenty of ways to mess with the Master Cheese. Get it, free it, hide it, protect it, sell it or even steal it!

Each cheesy Character has its own special abilities, strengths and weaknesses and offers different strategies on the road to victory. Which one will you choose?

    Will it be the Jurassic Overlord Zombiesaurus Rex with his flaming rage and hordes of Zombie Dino Minons?
    Will you choose the shifty Galaktor, the Alien Megabrain from Dimension Dork, who likes to mess with the other players’ cards, hands and minds?
    Will Pun-Pun, the Nuclear Powered Panda Pirate be your choice, who commands a chessy crew of beastly pirates and loves to get some cheesy bootie?
    Or will you fight as General Turtozoid, the Heavy Armed Tank Turtle, with his army of aquatic critters whose orders are to free, get and protect the Master Cheese?


Find out by getting one to three friends aboard and start fighting the cheesiest card fights you’ve ever had. Can you take the cheesiness??

"Ciutat 10" is a card game where each participant has the option to build their ideal city, a city 10 which ensures the promotion and respect of human rights. The goal is to do it before the other players. To do this, each participant will have to provide your city with actions and resources to ensure the five basic principles of a city 10

The game consists of three types of boards and two decks of cards. The central Resource and Actions board, where you place three cards shown to all players and the deck to replace them, the central Cost Cards board where you place these cards in the same way as the other from the players drawn this cards to his hands, and a City Board, where players build their city and punctuate the Resource and Actions cards at the end of game

The Resource and Action cards are divided into five concepts or colors: A City for All (blue cards), Welfare City (green cards), Democratic City (yellow cards), ​​Committed City (violet cards) and Civic and Participatory City (red cards). Each cards has a cost to play

Cost Letters, with values ​​2, 4, 6, 8, and 10 with which to pay the Resource and Action cards of the same color to play in the player's City Boards.

There is a special acknowledgment cards to complete all cards of the same concept, they gave 10 extra victory points. At the end of the game the player who has the greatest total in Resource and Actions cards in his City Board wins.

Ciutat 10 is an educational game developed by Marinva, for the Red Cross in Catalonia to promote awareness of human rights of citizens


Ciutat 10 és un joc de cartes on cada participant tindrà l'opció de construir la seva ciutat ideal, una ciutat 10 que garanteixi el foment i el respecte dels drets humas. L'objectiu és aconseguir-ho abans que la resta. Per fer-ho, cada participant haurá de dotar la seva ciutat d'accions i recursos que garanteixin els cinc principis bàsics d'una ciutat 10

El joc es compon de tres tipus de tauler i dues baralles de cartes. El tauler central de cartes de Recursos i Acció, on es col·loquen tres cartes mostrades als jugadors i la baralla per reposar-les, el tauler central de Cartes de Cost on es col·loquen aquestes cartes de la mateixa manera que les altres i un Tauler de Ciutat per a cada jugador, on construeixen la seva ciutat i puntuen les cartes al final de joc

Les cartes de Recurs i Acció es divideixen en cinc conceptes o colors: Una Ciutat per a Tothom (cartes blaves), el Ciutat del Benestar (cartes verdes), Ciutat Democràtica (cartes grogues), Ciutat Compromesa (cartes violetes) i Ciutat Cívica i Participativa (cartes vermelles). Cada carta té un cost per ser jugades

Cartes de Cost; amb valors 2, 4, 6, 8, i 10 amb les quals pagar les cartes de Recursos i Acció del mateix color

Hi han unes cartes de reconeixement especial per completar totes les cartes d'un mateix concepte, que donen 10 punts de victòria addicionals. Al final del joc, el jugador que tingui el major puntuació de cartes de Recursos i Accions al seu Tauler de Ciutat guanya la partida.

Ciutat 10 és un joc educatiu desenvolupat per Marinva, per a la Creu Roja a Catalunya per promoure el coneixement dels drets humans dels ciutadans

From the PDF:
"SeaWar: 1805 is a set of wargames rules designed to recreate the naval battles that occur during the Age of Sail, from the First Anglo-Dutch War in 1652 through the Napoleonic Wars to the end of the Greek War of Independence in 1829.
Designed to be simple enough to introduce gamers new to the period (indeed even to wargaming), SeaWar: 1805 is challenging enough to provide experienced naval wargamers with hours of fun and even provide the ideal vehicle to introduce their “landlubber” friends to the joys of naval wargaming. "

A relatively simple miniatures system with rules for wind effects on movement, deterioration of ship capabilities with damage (with possible random effects), broadsides, bow & stern fire, and 7 classes of ships (with further sub-classes for finer detail). Movement is phased, 5 phases per game turn, each ship moving between 0 and 4 cm per phase. Bookkeeping fairly light, supplied record sheets gives 2 ships per sheet with generous space for writing in ship capabilities & damage. Rules include many photographs of game in play, but only a couple have rules purposes, the others being decorative only. Time scale is 5 minutes per game turn, movement scale not given - all measurements are standard regardless of scale/size of miniatures used, 1/1200 scale being recommended.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
It's 1692 and dark magic runs rampant in the New World. A covenant of magic users is plotting to kidnap the members of 4 different merchant families that have been talking a little bit too much to the magistrates in charge of the witch hunt. But this is not an easy task: the practitioners will first have to collect the ingredients for the powerful spells – Lucky Clovers ♣, Bleeding Hearts ♥, Dead Man's Spades ♠ and Powdered Diamonds ♦. Only then they will be able to kidnap the members of the merchant families and bend the authority to their will!

The Salem Conspiracy is a dice drafting game that is played with seven character cards, a standard 52 card deck and four 6-sided dice. At the beginning of the round, players roll the dice, which determines which of the seven character cards that are laid on the table can be used that round. Players take turns assigning the four dice to the different character cards and execute their abilities, which allows them to draw cards, impair their opponent or manipulate the deck or their hands of cards. If players are holding three cards of the same suit, they can choose to discard them at the end of the round and take a J, Q or K of the corresponding suit, provided they have used the specialized character on that round. The first player to get one J, Q or K of every suit under their control wins the game!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Promotional Figure for the Krosmaster: Arena game.

Eva or Evangelyne is 1 of the 42 characters from Krosmaster Arena, her availability greatly enhances the game. She is one of the Brotherhood of the Tofu figures, who can be played together as a combo.

Special Power - COVER FIRE
If one of her allies from the Brotherhood of the Tofu is in play, the max range of Evangelyne's spells is increased by 1.

Works best when teamed with Amalia, Percedal, Ruel and Yugo


    Evangelyne is originally a character from the Wakfu animated series.

One Thousand is a fast-paced battle card game for 2-4 players. Go head-to-head or play as teams and be the first to inflict 1,000 casualties and emerge victorious! Game play is similar to Mille Bornes

Use your battle cards to launch artillery strikes and unleash deadly gas attacks. Use aerial recon to scout your enemy and counterintelligence to thwart their offensive. Muster your troops and mount your attacks in a race to inflict the most damage!

Aligned as teams or playing head-to-head, players arrange their cards on the battlefield as they attempt to launch an offensive and score attack points. Opponents can use Sabotage cards to stall an offensive and disable any future attacks until the Sabotage cards have been remedied. Perk cards can be played to permanently prevent a particular Sabotage card from being used. Hand management is an important part of the game as, to win, a team or player must reach an attack score of exactly 1,000 to win the battle. Multiple battles are played and scored as part of a campaign in which teams or players attempt to be the first to reach a total of 5,000 victory points.

Build a settlement among the stars!

Mankind has finally found a planet suitable for habitation far away in a distant solar system. The race to claim its lands is on and competition is fierce! Resources are scarce and the colonists threaten to rebel if their taxes are too high! Will you be able to create the first successful colony on this alien world or will crime, natural disasters and space pirates spell doom for your fledgling settlement?

From 2-4 intrepid explorers will attempt to settle, build and administrate over a small colony on a far away planet. Players may work together or against each other in the struggle to find suitable land, sufficient resources and a happy equilibrium among the populace. Taxes, buildings and the military all play an important role in achieving a functioning and thriving settlement, while random planetary events keep you on your toes. Whoever scores the most economic, military and socio-political points is the winner!

First explore 1 of 4 types of territory, then gain income by taxing your populace. Next build factories and buildings to gain resources before training your military. Lastly, deal with rebellions and random events. When all the land is taken, add up your economic, political and military points to see who wins.

Highlighted by fantastic 1920's artwork, this is an air race between 2 to 4 players.  Some players start on North America, some in Europe.  Travelling in planes, players try to avoid perils along the way to the other side of the Atlantic Ocean in the least amount of time.

The board contains various travel points along the route, showing your planned journey.  On your turn you spin and move your plane the number shown on the spinner.  If this would take you past a landing station you must remain where you are until you spin an exact number.  You are considered on schedule as long as you don't land on any delay spots, and you may even deduct time if you land on a "time spot".  If you land on an "off course" spot, then you must take an alternate (longer) route to your journey.  You may land on a forced landing spot or run out of gasoline depending on which spot you land on.

At the end of the game you must total up the hours lost or gained to determine the fastest player and they are declared the winner.

An example of this game, given many years ago, was selected as an example of the evolution of America and is housed in the Americana collection of the Boston Childrens Museum.

JaLink! is a fast-paced party game of visual association and creative thinking. Players race to find connections between pairs of illustrated cards that match a given category—like "things that fly" or "famous duos." The game board holds 16 stacks of circular picture cards, and each round begins with a new category and a 90-second timer. As players spot matching pairs, they call them out and collect the cards, revealing new images underneath. The game continues until all cards are claimed, and the player with the most cards wins

Similar in spirit to: Perpetual Notion, FitzIt, It Fits, and Such a Thing?

What's Your Cosmo? is a set of custom playing cards that are used to play a variety of games that range from easy to difficult and from 1 to 6 players. There are seven unique card games using double-sided playing cards, two different wild cards, positive and negative signs, letters, and five title cards.

The object of each game involves making triples, straights, equations, or completions. In addition, the COSMO game system (a set of standard and customizable rules for games that use the What's Your Cosmo playing cards) provides plenty of space for ingenuity to create and design fun, new games.

The COSMO deck is one hundred cards. The dark side of the cards features one of the numbers 1 through six or -1 through -6. Each number appears six times. There also are Sun and Infinity wild cards. The light side features one of the letters C, O, S, or M, with the O appearing twice as often as the other letters.
Instructions are included for seven games, including solitaire and a children's game. Many of the games involve building sequences or sets; others use simple math. Games that use only the light side of the cards tend to be variants on spelling COSMO or finding the letters in COSMO.

Laura received a pet rabbit (named Fluffy) for her birthday ... Upon opening the rabbit cage for the first time her new pet runs under her bed.  The goal of the game is to assist Laura in luring her new friend out from under the bed.

Play begins with the puzzle turned face down on the table and the rabbit counter on the left most picture on the board. Beginning with the youngest player, the die is thrown. If a Carrot appears, the rabbit counter is moved one space towards the right (towards Laura). If a Candy is rolled a jigsaw piece is turned face up. If the Rabbit is rolled a jigsaw piece is turned face down (assuming a face up piece exists).  Play then advances to the next youngest player and continues round robin until either all of the jigsaw pieces are face up (unable to help Laura lure Fluffy out from under the bed) or the Fluffy counter advances to far right most space (Laura).

Each Monkey sports a number from 0 to 15. Players randomly choose three monkeys and, without looking at the numbers, place the monkeys number-side down in front of them. A die determines the action that a player takes on his turn: flip a monkey, trade a monkey, or take a monkey. The game ends when a player has three number-side up monkeys. The winner is the player whose monkeys' numbers add to the highest total.

56- 2 sided, hand stamped wooden tiles depict words, word parts, letters, pictograms, symbols, and/or numbers.Grab as many as you wish but at least 10 and say what ever you can say in an interconnected manner that makes sense within 2 minutes while other players hover to gain points off your demise.Players use their tiles to form interconnected sentences, words, slogans, phrases,names, countries, etc...anything that makes sense and can be explained if challenged. High points win.Players score two points per tile played but subtract points for unused tiles.Bonus Round points are scored at 3 points for and one against for all un-played pieces. Proper spelling, grammar and English are not necessary and Multi and Bi-Lingual use is allowed. Phonetic, symbolic, numeric and literal use for all pieces. Think Caveman,Shakespeare and Einstein trying to communicate.

"Tresquis: Joc de descoberta dels valors naturals de la comarca del Pla de l'Estany".

Educational game, a roll and move where you move across the board visiting 67 places around the territory (Comarca del Pla de l'Estany, inside Catalonia, Spain). Along the way, you also have to solution environmental problems.

The location cards are evenly distributed among the players. Every time a player visits a location, he/she gets the location card, draws a special card and applies the results.

The winner is the player that first gets free of every location card in his/her possession (or, if playing with a determined time limit, the one that has the less cards remaining) or also the one that solutions more environmental problems; or even the one that has visited more places.

Zahlen-Mobile consists of a set of cards showing "dice mobiles". These consist of a main bar with two additional bars attached. From each of those two bars, two dice hang down, with each of the four dice having a different color. The bars are either straight or slanted left or right.

Each player has a set of dice (one in each color) and a dice cup. Players reveal one card, then simultaneously roll their dice, trying to roll results that would fit the current mobile card. (If all three bars are straight, that would be four of the same number; if the two lower bars are straight, but the top one is slanted to the right, then the two dice hanging from the right side must be higher than the two on the left.)

The first player to roll a "solution" that fits the card gets the card (in a two-player game). With more players, the first player gets two cards and the second one gets one card. After a certain number of rounds, the player with the most cards wins.

Each player has 7 blue cubes and 3 green triangular pieces. Two cards are revealed which show an object one can build with these pieces, but from different angles. The players now try to be the first to build the building depicted on the two cards. The first player who manages correctly gains the two cards. The first player to win 14 cards wins the game.

The game consists of many pie piece shaped tiles which contain sentence parts; there are also round picture tiles on which the "round belly king" does something with someone somewhere, using some item.

The rule set contains many different game ideas, all aimed at training kids to read fast and accurately and to form correct complex sentences. Some examples: 
- A player picks a picture tile, then lays out a sentence which shows the events of the picture.
- As above, but now the player must put one wrong tile into the sentence. The first of the other players to find the mistake scores a point.
- Players compete forming as many correct sentences from the available tiles as possible.
- Each player draws an image, looks at it and turns it over. Now everyone must find the corresponding sentence parts and say their sentence; the first one to manage that is the winner.

The game consists of 16 bears in four colors and of cards numbered 1 to 9 in the same colors. 
Each player holds two cards. The active player draws a third card and has two possibilities: 
- either discard two cards which add up to ten: for that, s/he can take a bear in one of the involved colors
- or s/he plays one card on the table, next to the bears in that color. If the sum of all cards there is ten, the player can take a bear; if it exceeds ten, the last card is discarded.

The first player to collect bears of all four colors is the winner.

boardgame: BioShock Infinite: The Siege of Columbia – Enraged Songbird
description:
A special promo card that was released both with pre-orders of the board game through the Plaid Hat Games website and with the BioShock Infinite Limited Edition Strategy Guide from Brady Games.

This card adds a usable advantage to the founder player and can be used in place of the regular songbird action card included within the regular game. Card text: "You may discard this card and another card from your hand to place Elizabeth on the location Songbird is currently on. Elizabeth is now under your control."
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Issue game of Wargame Japanese History #16.

Game covering Aizu campaign of the Boshin War (1868) - (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Aizu).

With popular TV series “Yae no Sakura” in Japan, Aizu campaign has become much familiar (http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yae_no_Sakura).

This game uses card-driven mechanics and includes many related events.
It is played in 12 turns and victory is basically determined by control of important areas. The game is usually determined by the final battle at Aizu-Wakamatsu area.
family: History: Boshin War & Meiji Era Rebellions

description:
The Islands have been devastated by a huge natural disaster. It has ripped buildings apart, thrown debris far and wide, and endangered the lives of the Island’s inhabitants. As the danger subsides survivors are gradually emerging from the rubble and signalling for help.

You can choose. Will you be a team of willing rescuers on a helicopter, a UFO looking for specimens or a hungry dragon looking for easy meals? Either way players score points by collecting the survivors as quickly as they can but are restricted in their flight patterns by the local air currents.

The board consists of a grid of 9 tiles, representing the islands, each with only 2 arrows on to indicate legal moves off of the tile. As the survivors are randomly generated each turn players must try to negotiate their way to them using 2 moves, no more, no less. If they fail to pick anyone up in a turn then they can claim a chaos card that can allow them in a future turn to twist, swap, or twist and swap islands around to make their path easier and/or to foil the plans of the other player.

Don't be fooled by the apparent simplicity of only having to negotiate 9 tiles - this is a game often demanding some pretty involved thinking out.

The advanced solo option keeps the pressure on by introducing dangers to your survivors such as storms, fire, and disease.

Unashamedly based on the TV show of a very similar name, each player controls a team of 4 characters (each with their own nick-names and brief backgrounds) who have to build a fantastic competing machine out of the bits and pieces found by them in the scrapyard.

On day 1 they need to find the best components that they can in the scrapyard heaps, drag them back to base and weld them together within the time set.

On day 2 the two (hopefully completed) machines square up in competition).

There are 5 battles in the base game - a Sofa-slinger, a cabbage cannon, a powered raft, a can stacker and a hill climber that can be played individually or one after the other as a championship challenge.

An entry for the 2013 Two-Player Print-and-Play Design Contest

Dropbox Folder for pdfs ready for sharing - https://www.dropbox.com/sh/zgydx2pl2k3zg3n/0f-tfeNCNv?m
Typical spin and move board game from 1930's. The player first succeeding in moving his Mice into the Mouses' Home again in the order stated, by exact spins of the arrow, win the game.

Swords and Heroes is a unique, history-based tactical game that allows players the opportunity to battle some of the greatest armies in history. The flagship game of the independent company ZAMA Game Design, Swords and Heroes challenges players in a simple-yet-engaging combat system.

The game uses a detailed “Cards and Shards” system that uses board-based tactical “shards” to represent battlefield units of the respective armies. Basic statistics and abilities of these units are represented by beautifully-designed game cards held by each player. Units range from light-armored infantry to long-ranged archers or heavy cavalry, but vary wildly based on the historical civilization chosen by the player.

After custom-designing their armies using a simple point-based creation mechanic, players field their armies and face off for the control of the board with easy-to-learn dice-based turn mechanics. Players also make use of “General’s Orders” cards that offer players powerful and unique means of shifting the course of battle. Once the enemy has been eliminated from the board, the remaining general stands victorious!

The Swords and Heroes: Starter Set comes with all of the necessary rules and game components required, as well as two Classical Age armies: The Roman Empire and the Germanic Tribes. Future expansion packs will also offer players additional armies, such as Han China, Alexander’s Macedonia, Parthian Persia, and more!

A simple race-style game, the board is printed with instructions all along the route, in the form of lines from "Little BoPeep" and other well-known nursery rhymes. The players move wooden pieces around a spiraling track. The first player to reach the center space named "Home" by exact count wins the game.

Animal Fun is a game that aims to help children identify animals and learn their names.

The players are each given an equal of cards, on which pictures of animals are printed, along with their names.  The heads of the animals, all of which are circular cut-outs, are removed and mixed face-down in a pile.  Each player takes his or her turn to flip one of the cut-outs and fits it to their cards if it matches one of their animals' bodies.  If it matches none, the cut-out is flipped back and mixed back into the pile.

The first player to fit back all their animals' heads wins the game.  There is also the option to mix in and match the irregular shaped name cut-outs.

A fast action family word game for one or more players.

The object of the game is to form as many vertical and horizontal words as possible within 6 minutes... or whatever time limit the players wish to set.

On each turn, a player starts the timer, then randomly draws cubes from the bag one at a time. Each cube may be turned to any desired letter, and is then placed into any open square in the board. Once placed, letter cubes may not be moved or turned again. When the timer runs out, or the last letter is placed, the player's turn is over and his score is calculated for both horizontal and vertical words in the grid.

Players roll the dice and move their characters on the game board. On different fields of action, the children collect cards of each of the continents, learn animals, sights, buildings and various travel opportunities to know. The goal is to have six different continent maps collected by the World Travel. The winner is the one who first returns with six continent cards to its initial continent.

Players act simutanously and take and try to match the colored tiles from the middle of the table on their boards.

Who was able to place first all correct tiles on his board is the winner.

boardgame: The Game of Visiting Birds' Nests Without Robbing Them
description:
Game published in the Odhams Press book, The Children's Book of Games, Puzzles and Pastimes.

You will require a pack of ordinary playing cards for this game. Divide them into two separate packs, placing the Aces, Kings, Queens, Jacks, tens and nines into one pile and all the twos, threes, fours, fives, sixes, sevens and eights into another. Put them side by side face downwards on the table

Each player draws a card from each pack, turns them over and compares them with the pictures on this double page, where are depicted ten different birds with the number of their eggs and the value of the card. For example, if you draw an ace (Kestrel) you must return all the eggs you have collected except those of the Owl, Moorhen and Kestrel. If you draw a two (Rat), it steals any one egg and you must keep this card until you draw a Queen (Owl), in the meantime drawing one card only from the pack containing the picture cards. If you draw ^ King (Moorhen) you retain her egg but miss a round.
Each player keeps an account of tiie number of eggs he has collected during the game, and at the end of the round subtracts his losses from the total. The cards are then returned to the bottom of their respective packs and shuffled for the next round. Play six rounds and then again add up your total.

The first player to collect ten different eggs wins the game.

You will find there are less cards in the picture pack and the round finishes after these have all been drawn

GamBOOLee is a game for 2, 3, or 4 players. Each player tries to capture the royal families of the other players and to win all the ransom money. To determine the winner, the players have tournaments between their knights (similar to the "jousting" bouts of the knights of old) and the loser must pay ransom for the pieces he has at stake in the tournament or lose his pieces to the winner. The game continues in this manner until one player has won all the ransom money and all the pieces, or until the remaining players concede defeat with the player to the highest total -- the total of the ransom prices of all the pieces he holds and all of his ransom money.  The magnets in the knight bases decide the winner of each tournament in a completely random and unpredictable manner.

Experior is a 2-player game with minimalist rules and an emergent complexity.

The game is about a combat in a fantasy world between a Human and an Elf. A character can perform three types of attack (sword, bow or magic attack), and each attack can be strong, causing great damages, or quick, causing damages more often. The damage intensity depends on the experience in that type of attack. A successful attack increases the experience of the fighter in that type of attack.

SETUP
Players choose randomly who is the Human and who is the Elf.

In a common paper is written down the initial experiences for each type of attack and the initial number of wounds of both characters, as shown below. Another option is to print the board in the section files.

ELF 
Wounds: 0
Sword: 2
Bow: 3
Magic: 2

HUMAN
Wounds: 0
Sword: 3
Bow: 2
Magic: 2

ATTACK: TYPES AND MODALITIES
There are three types of attack: sword, bow and magic. For each of those there are two modalities: quick and strong.

So, each player has 6 cards for every possible attack: strong sword, quick sword, strong bow, quick bow, strong magic, quick magic.

You can print the custom cards in the images section. Another options is to use 6 cards, for each player, from an UNO game: two red cards with 1 and 3 values for quick sword and strong sword, two green cards with 1 and 3 values for quick bow and strong bow, two blue cards with 1 and 3 values for quick magic.

Winning order: sword beats bow, bow beats magic and magic beats swords.

TURN
Each player places a face down card in the middle of the table. When both cards are placed, they are revealed.

• With a successful quick attack, the opponent takes a number of wounds equal to the value of experience the winner has in that type of attack.
• With a successful strong attack, the opponent takes a number of wounds equal to the value of experience the winner has in that type of attack multiplied by 3.

After the opponent’s damaging, the winner increases his experience in that type of attack by one.

The winning order (sword > bow; bow > magic; magic > swords) determines who has done a successful attack. If both players chooses the same type of attack, the winner is who played a quick attack.

In case both players choose the same type of attack and the same modality, the player with less experience in that type of attack increases by one that type of attack and nobody takes any wounds. In case both players has the same amount of experience neither of them gain experience.

At the end of the turn, the two cards just played stay on the table, aside but in full view and unavailable for the next turn. At the end of the next turn, the new played cards will stay on the table, whereas the cards from the previous turn will return in the players’ hands.

END OF THE GAME
The game ends when one player reaches a number of wounds greater than or equal to 100. (The game is perfectly scalable, so players can choose any number of wounds as end condition.)

After that, players have to check who will get a bonus attack. Each player subtracts the value in his highest experience with the value his lowest experience. Who has the greater difference makes a number of wounds to the enemy equal to his highest experience multiplied by 3 (like a strong attack). If the differences are equal, whoever has less wounds gets the bonus attack..

The winner is the player with less wounds.

You are a merchant in space, traveling throughout the galaxy looking for the best cargo to sell.  The most sought-after goods come from the frontier worlds, for which the colonies back home will pay top price.  But you’re not the only merchant in the business… your competitors are after those same profitable goods!  As you race to get there first, the frontier itself is expanding, shrinking, and being settled into stable markets.  Will you be able to haul the most profit before the final frontier closes forever?

Star Cargo is a simple pick-up-and-deliver race game playable with a poker deck, a few poker chips and player pawns.

boardgame: 2038: Tycoons of the Asteroid Belt Expansion Set
description: This long lost expansion is now seeing moonlight. This Print and Play expansion includes: 2 new Corporations: Onsite Refining and Mining Robotics (with rules altering certificate holding limits), alternative private companies setup, 2 new hexes.

boardgame: Guilds of Cadwallon: 5 to 8 Player Expansion
description:
The Guilds of Cadwallon 5- to 8-player expansion was offered as a series of stretch goals for the Kickstarter campaign that funded Guilds of Cadwallon.  It includes the following:

     the figurines, capture tokens, and cards for four extra guilds
         Necromancers
         Ferrymen
         Fortune Tellers
         Thieves
    
     5 additional Militia cards
     4 additional Personality cards
         Célénia de Teren
         Asha Ruyar
         The Duke of Cadwallon
         Isabeau The Secret
    
     a duplicate set of Action cards
     a duplicate set of Contract cards, but utilizing the guilds in this expansion
     a Guild Backer card for each guild
     rules cards for games with 5–8 players, larger playfields, and the Guild Backer cards

Parrot Pile-Up is a pile of fun! Balance, nerve and a steady hand are required in this tropical-themed game of skill. Players carefully stack fruit and crates on a clumsy parrot's raft with a steady hand and give it a patient push to the next player. If the stack falls, the player doing the pushing gets more pieces. The first player to get rid of all his or her cargo wins! Difficulty can be moderated by tilting the raft or leaving it level to accommodate multiple levels of skill. Includes 1 parrot, 1 raft, 20 stacking pieces and 4 cards.

Greedy Gator Game: The toucans and the monkeys are trying to cross the bridge to take home their fruit, but in opposite directions. With a hungry alligator waiting below, they need to be careful and get across without getting knocked off to become prey. It's light strategy mixed with suspense and unpredictable destruction as players slide the alligator roller at the rotating planks of the bridge both in an attempt to knock their opponents off and to determine how many spaces they can move. The trick is to figure out how many spots to advance each of the movers. The first team across the bridge wins, but remember, you don't want to be knocked off and sent back with the hungry alligator lurking below.

3 Games in 1!

Fingertips: Animal Edition features 15 friendly and fun animal characters! Each hand has five different characters.

Can you find matches for the animals on your cards?

Play all three Fingertips matching games and have a handful of fun!

Game 1 - Mad Rush: Race other players to match the animal images as fast as you can.
Game 2 - Hot Dog: Match the animals on the cards fingertip to fingertip. If you match a dog to another dog, you get an extra turn!
Game 3 - Hide and Seek: Search the grid to see if you can find the five animals on your hand. Can you remember where you saw them last?

Mage Wars: Druid vs. Necromancer is a two-mage expansion set for Mage Wars, focusing on two schools of magic not emphasized in the core set.

The Druid is schooled in Nature magic and is the undisputed master of plants. She also favors the Water school, using it to heal, restore, and grow her plants, as well as fight her enemies with geyser like blasts of water or downpours of acid rain. She does not use traditional spawnpoints; instead, her plants can spontaneously grow quickly across the arena. She has a theme of slow, steady growth, that accelerates her power quickly. She is the perfect Mage to use against a turtler because you cannot leave her alone to grow unchecked for too long.

The Necromancer is a dark, sinister Mage who dabbles in the darkest of the black arts. His primary strengths lie in the summoning of the undead. His Graveyard spawnpoint summons armies of skeletons, zombies, and ghouls to do his bidding. His theme is a relentless assault. His skeletons are fragile, but keep reassembling themselves from a pile of bones to attack again! Zombies are slow, but very difficult to "kill". Both of these creatures just keep coming! Additionally, the Necromancer summons and controls evil spirits: ghosts and wraiths. He has an affinity with death and disease, and can quickly spread plagues across the arena.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
"You are a tiny faint whisper in the tiny mind of a tiny land hermit crab - let’s face it that’s really tiny. Still god-hood has to start somewhere and all that you can hope is that your influence will be strong enough to see your crab through its next three vital moults before your rival beats you to it with their crab."

A strategy game for 2 players (though it may work eventually for more).

The Game takes place on an area of Australian Mangrove Swamp divided up into hexagon shaped spaces. Each player takes the part of a Land Hermit Crab God guiding their chosen Land Hermit Crab through their life in order to be the first to be big enough to worship at the Crab Temple.

Each player rolls 4 d6s which determine direction of movement, distance, the nature of the god gift (usually food but sometimes a shell), and the area where the gift will drop. It is up to the player to decide which die will be carrying out which role.

The Game components (which include crab pictures in a photo frame, hermit crab playing pieces and the entire board) fit onto just 3 pages (2 if you have your own tokens). Compact rules (1 double-sided standard sheet) and an optional player's aid page are also available. The board is modular so as to enable easy storage.

1348, Europe – The Black Death ravages Europe, and your country still has no king after the last one was killed by the plague. The nobility of the land has had enough of you princes never being able to settle for an heir for the country. Therefore, the nobles have begun to interfere with your disputes. On your visits to the various buildings, it may now occur that you meet a noble with a political agenda of his own. In some cases, he welcomes you and helps you in your quest, while in other cases he tries to make life as difficult as possible for you. Will you be able to keep the nobles at your side in your struggles against the other princes so that at last a new, worthy king can be elected?

Rattus Cartus: Nobilis, the first expansion for Rattus Cartus (a card game based on the Rattus board game) includes 18 different buildings with players using all or only some of them each game to provide a wide variety of play. This first expansion also includes special cards (Books, Horses, Lances, Privilegs, Wizard Staffs and Diplomats) and extra rat tokens; a second module in this expansion includes noble tokens.

Fans of spooky games, look out! In Goliath's family game Mr. Creepy's Geisterschloss a ghost is creeping through the rooms of an old castle. Ghost hunters are getting ready to help him find final peace by closing his coffin lid.

This exciting adventure takes you through this spooky castle, in which there is danger around every corner. At the beginning of the game the castle is built up, after which the ghost hunting can begin! Who will manage to get up the tower stairs first? Of course, along the way there will be numerous tasks, of which the skull is perhaps the most difficult: once it starts to roll, who knows which trap will go off. Whoever gets past all the traps and makes it up the tower stairs to close the coffin lid will put an end to the spookiness.

The first mini-expansion to Gear & Piston, it contains 10 Engineer Assistant tiles.

At the start of the game each player is dealt one random Engineer Assistant tile. The players may look at the assistant tile they received but the tile is kept secret from other players. The tiles are placed face-down on the table, with the 1VP scoring symbol facing up.

Each Engineer Assistant has a unique special ability that may be used only once during a game. The special ability can be used by turning the assistant tile face-up thus revealing the assistant to the other players. An unrevealed Engineer Assistant is worth 1 VP at the end of the game.

Each tile is revealed either at the start of the round or at any time.  If multiple assistants are revealed simultaneously, they are resolved in turn order starting from the first player.

Each portrait on each tiles will bear the likeness of a Gear & Piston Kickstarter backer.

The second mini-expansion to Gear & Piston, it contains 2 Explosion tiles.

The two Explosion tiles are shuffled into the bottom half (roughly estimated) of the Junk Parts deck. If an Explosion tile emerges from the deck when refilling the Junk Yard the effect takes place immediately and the Explosion is resolved.

If a player draws an Explosion tile when drawing blind, then the player may decide to do one of the following:
1. Resolve the explosion immediately by showing it to all players and draw a new tile from the Junk Parts deck.
or
2. Keep the tile in hand for later use.
When a player decides to keep the tile in hand, then the player may then decide to discard it or resolve it during the “Adjust Volatility and discard a Blueprint” part of the “II. Resolve Actions” phase. If the tile is not used during the game, then it has no effect.

When an Explosion tile emerges from the Junk Yard deck or is revealed from a player’s hand, an explosion occurs. Players having the automobiles with the most Volatility must discard a part from their automobile. A player may choose the part to be removed but a junk part must be chosen if there are any in the automobile.

Regati is a sailing race game...a board game of skill, luck and strategy. Up to 10 players can all race a ship against each other around the board. Line up ready for the start, cross the starting line, sail around the buoys and Windy Point, to be the first to cross the finishing line.

But, you can only sail a maximum speed according to the wind direction, and the wind direction changes...

Each move, you either move your ship OR you decide to change the wind direction. A wind direction change affects all players equally...and it may even result in a play direction switch. Sailing moves have realistic speeds, with six points of sail to the wind. So, for example, reaching across the wind is your fastest course, as is tacking to take you upwind. Your ship may block other ships...and watch out for the wind shadows from other ships or from the land! A person at the back of the race can influence all other ships by changing the wind direction. It is seldom the case that the first person around the first buoy stays in front...tactical sailing is required.

You can also sail as teams, whereby your tactics are determined with other sailors in mind. You can also organise a regatta (multiple sail races), so the best sailor of a few races is the winner. The point scoring method for these are described in the instructions.
Rampart Expansion add 24 new cards to Island Siege! It includes 12 new forts, 6 new buildings, and 6 new ships.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring Deck-Building Game – Arwen Promos
description:
Set of 2 extra cards for Cryptozoic's The Lord of the Ring: The Fellowship of the Ring Deck-Building Game, given out by participating board game stores:

- Arwen: another playable hero, bringing the amount of heroes to select from to total of 8. Card text: "Elven Hero / You start the game with 'Ride like the Wind' in your deck."
- Ride like the Wind: 10th card for starter deck for player with Arwen as hero. Card text: "Maneuver / +1 Power / Defense: You may discard this card to avoid an Attack or Ambush. If you do, draw two cards."
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Publisher Blurb:

Lead your team to victory on the bloodstained pitches of the greatest sport ever played! DreadBall is a futuristic sports board game played with 30mm miniatures, set within Mantic's Warpath universe. You are the Coach of your team, testing your skill and luck against other Coaches in one-off matches or long-running leagues. The simple rules allow a great depth of choice and many opportunities to show your cunning. Rules are provided for four different teams, eight famous individuals, team progression and league play.

Each of 28 hex tiles displays one of three shapes in one of three colors and one of three sizes. Players take turns placing a tile from their hand next to a previously-placed tile on the board that shares at least one attribute (shape, size, or color)with the new tile. The object is to be the first player to form a row of four adjacent tiles.

The game consists of 40 Challenge cards. Each card has one Primary Challenge and three Bonus Challenges.
The challenges come in four types: 
1. Out on a Limerick --- Fill in the blank at the end of the limerick with the correct Greek or Latin prefix.
2. On the Road --- Identify the author of each imagined diary entry, and the work of fiction he or she comes from.
3. The Eschewbox --- Correct the grammatical errors. The number of such errors is in parentheses at the end of the challenge.
4. Rooted Out --- Replace the bracketed words in the challenge with one of the multiple choice answers. The bracketed words essentially are a definition of the answer.
Players take turns being the reader, with the other players, beginning in a clockwise direction from the reader, attempting to answer the Primary Challenge. The first player to answer correctly selects a bonus challenge. If he cannot answer it, then the next player gets a chance, and so on. Each correct Primary Challenge answer is worth two points, and each correct Bonus Challenge answer is worth one point. The first player to reach 10 points wins.

It's one player representing the 1% versus three players representing the 99%. Players take turns rolling dice and moving around a board that contains spaces called Wall Street, Media, Pentagon, Banking, and the like. The 99%ers work together to occupy spaces that will improve their influence in the media, Congress, and other institutions. The 1%er tries to drive the other players out of those spaces and into his or her holdings.
The 1%er wins if all the other players are broke, in jail, or in the hospital. The 99%ers win if they control seven spaces at once.

A box contains 150 double-sided cards, each in one of three colors. Each side shows three different words layered on top of one another. Two of the words are related to each other in some way.
Three cards --- one of each color --- are placed on the table. Players simultaneously try visually to separate the words on each card and then claim the card. Once all three cards are claimed, the claimer of each card states the three words and explains why one of those words does not belong. The first player to collect three cards of each color wins.

In the game Francis Drake, the players are in the Elizabethan era in the attempting to emulate the legend of Sir Francis Drake, hero to the English but a pirate to the Spanish. After the set up, each player must chart a course toward the riches of the Aztec and Incan Empires and complete 3 missions.

Spain's Revenge is an Expansion set of tiles to replace the Spanish Troops and the Spanish Frigates to give more strength to the forts and the best admirals to guard the treasure fleet on the course toward the Spain.

Promo cards for 10th anniversary of Zombiaki added to magazine Świat Gier Planszowych #28.
"Resorak" should be added to Zombies deck. If there is a card from Humans deck this zombie jumps over it.
"Ptasia grypa" should be added to Humans deck. Played on any zombie (not dog nor cat) change its strength to 1.

Mafia: Death and Deception is a game of an uninformed majority versus an informed minority. It involves two randomly selected teams of players: The Civilians and the Mafia, both of which are racing to kill one another. The catch is, the civilians don’t know who their enemies are.

Through arguing, accusations, and heated debate, players attempt to figure out who is a member of the mafia, and eventually vote to execute each other. The members of the mafia, meanwhile, try to lie and deceive their way to safety while secretly killing civilians in the meantime.

• Features 22 optional roles to add skill and complexity
• Includes non-allegiance-specific roles known as “traits”
• Thousands of possibilities, so every game is different
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Cthulhu: The Great Old One is a Lovecraft-themed quick-play card game for 2 to 10 players.

Each player is dealt cards until all cards in the deck have been dealt. Each player then makes pairs from the cards that player holds, making either Matched Pairs or Linked Pairs.  Then each player, in turn, chooses one card at random from another player, in the hope of making more pairs. Players may choose to play one Horror or Cultist card at the end of their turn, after taking a card from another player, to cause an in-game effect.

Once all cards have been paired (other than The Great Old One), then all players add up their total scores based on the pairs they created. Matched Pairs are worth two points, Linked Pairs are worth one point; and having all four cards of a Linked set is worth 4 points, no matter what order you have the two Pairs in. Horror cards are worth one point during the scoring phase. The player who ends the game holding The Great Old One loses the game, all other players compare scores to determine the winner.

The Cultist card is a separate card which may be played instead of a Horror card at the end of a player's turn, and although it subtracts 1 point from the player's final score, if a player who has played the Cultist ends the game holding The Great Old One, they win the game!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
From box bottom:
Here is a chance to test your memory!  The object of the game is to find the 15 numbers, starting with No. 1 and continuing through in sequence to No. 15.  To start: all the counters are placed face down on the board.  In turn, lift a counter and check the number on the bottom.  If it is not number 1, replace it, trying to to memorize the numbers and positions of replaced counters.  Then try to find nr. 2, and so on.  When the counters are found in the correct order, they are placed, right side up, in their proper places on the edge of the board.  You can play alone or against an opponent.  Simple rules with a score card.

The Marketplace Race is a promotional game designed to sell the virtues of Digital Equipment Corporation's new (at the time) J-11 microprocessor, introduced in 1983.  While the purpose of the game is marketing, it is theoretically a playable game.  It's a basic spin-and-move game, with the spinner built into the board, but with interrupts along the path which all speed the player with the J-11 along while slowing down the player with the "Brand X" chip.  The first player to the Market Place wins!

boardgame: The Door to Saturn (fan expansion for Arkham Horror)
description:
The struggle against the Ancient One takes the investigators out of Arkham and into the darkest corners of the globe. But the aeons-worn ruins of pre-human civilizations hold nameless horrors against which even the miracles of modern science offer little protection...

The Door to Saturn is a fan-made expansion for the Fantasy Flight Games board game Arkham Horror. It's a fan expansion, so players need to print the components themselves. The expansion doesn't really have anything to do with the planet Saturn; it's just a good name.

The expansion adds new Ancient Ones, items, spells, allies and monsters, as well as a new item type - Scientific items. The expansion also introduces the 'Expedition variant', in which Arkham's supply of Clue tokens depletes as Clues are spent, and the players have to venture on to an Expedition mini-board to replenish the supply. The four Expeditions included are "Antarctica", "Arabia", "Oceania", and "Mars"; taking inspiration from Lovecraft's At The Mountains of Madness and The Nameless City, Abraham Merritt's The Moon Pool and Clark Ashton Smith's The Dweller in the Gulf and The Vaults of Yoh-Vombis - among many others.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Conspiracy Theory is a co-operative game in which 2-6 players will work together in an attempt to thwart the nefarious schemes of a global conspiracy to dominate the world.  Each turn, players will use their actions to travel the globe, attempt to uncover pieces of the conspirator's plots, and struggle to remove the conspiracy's insidious influence.  However, the forward march of the conspiracy is relentless, and each turn another plot will be hatched.

As the game progresses, the plots will begin to build on one another, gradually growing in potency as the conspiracy races toward its final conclusion.  If the players are able to reveal enough plots in time, they will be able to expose the conspiracy to the rest of the world!  They must, however, remain vigilant, as the reach of the conspiracy is long, and they may have planted a traitor within your midst.  If the traitor is able to complete their secret objective before being exposed, then the rest of the team may lose their only chance to save the world!

Der Palast von Alhambra: Die Falkner, the sixth extension for the Alhambra, mirrors earlier boxed expansions for the game by including four separate expansion modules, each of which can be used individually or combined with any mix of modules from this or other expansions. The four modules included in this expansion are:


     The Falconers: Land falcon tiles on the intersection of four building tiles, but only so long as the colors match.
     The Building Sites: Pay for half a building tile now to reserve something you need; just be sure to pay what's due!
     The Exchange Certificates: Pay too much? Now you can store the change for a later purchase.
     The Portals: Buy a portal along with a building tile to extend your wall to new lengths.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Rise of the Runelords – Adventure Deck 2: The Skinsaw Murders
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Uncover a mystery fraught with ravenous ghouls, age-old hauntings, foul cultists, and worse with the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: The Skinsaw Murders Adventure Deck (a.k.a., Rise of the Runelords - Adventure Deck 2). This 110-card expansion to the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Rise of the Runelords Base Set includes new locations, monsters, villains, loot, and more, as well as five new scenarios that constitute the complete Skinsaw Murders adventure. Continue your character's legend with the Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: The Skinsaw Murders Adventure Deck!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Series: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game (Paizo Publishing)

description:
You are a prospector in the Old West just trying to get by doing the only thing you know and that’s digging for Gold.  Your goal of riches is only hindered by what you may find in the mountain itself.  Each prospector will take turns digging deeper into the mountain looking for gold nuggets.  When the mountain is emptied of all its available treasures, the prospector with the most Gold nuggets WINS!

This game uses a unique 2 tile set system.  The "Mountain" is built by randomly setting up 64 of the 72 possible tiles in an 8 by 8 grid.  The other set of 72 "Shaft" tiles are shuffled and set up in stacks for draw piles.  As the players dig, the "Mountain" tiles are collected and replaced with the "Shaft" tiles. This allows for more players to be added without extending the game time substantially.  And makes each game vary with the number of possible Rocks, Gold and Dirt tiles placed.

It's a family game for 2-6 players with a strong memory component.

Players compete against each other by placing apples in a basket in such a way there are as many as possible from the same player altogether. The difficulty lies in that all apples have the same appearance and only at the end of the play when all apples are turned around (apples are marked on the back side) you can see if you have placed all your apples together. To make it more difficult there are some apples with special effects that can be used to obstruct other players.

A ladybug-themed action game for children.

Theme
The ladybugs want to reach the top of the flower! But they need the players' help and lots of crawling and flying practice to do so. Can you help your ladybug reach the top first?

Goal
Be the first to complete a flower by collecting stem cards and a blossom card.

Game play
Players decide on how tall they want the flowers to be, e.g. 4 stem cards tall. Stem and blossom cards are put in separate stacks, face down.
Players take turns taking the top card from the stem card stack. The active player looks at the task described on the back of the card and tries to complete the task. If he/she is successful, he/she gets to keep the card and puts it down in front of him/herself. If he/she fails, the card is returned to the bottom of the card stack.
If a player has collected the agreed-upon number of stem cards, he/she gets to take a blossom card instead.

The first player to have built a complete flower (consisting, in this example, of 4 stem cards and a blossom card) wins the game.

Tasks require gross and fine motor skills, eye-hand-coordination, concentration and observation skills and include: flipping the wooden ladybugs with one's fingers, guessing in which hand another player is holding the bug, stacking bugs on top of each other, throwing a bug with one's foot and catching it, throwing it over one's head and catching it, balancing it on one's head, elbow or shoulders, jumping with a bug on one's hand etc.
There are 3 joker cards that allow players to add a card to their flower without completing a task, and three bad luck cards that make you lose a part of your flower.

In Boss Raid you and a friend take on the role of Great Heroes facing off against a Mighty Boss. Choose your Heroes, Choose the Boss!  Do you have what it takes to emerge victorious?

Boss Raid is set in a fantasy world where you'll face bosses like dragons, necromancers and other unspeakable beings of darkness.  It also sets aside all the "fodder" of lower level creatures and brings you to the heart of why we play games like this "the Boss Battle!"

Players throughout the game will roll dice and draft them to gain resources, but be warned, the dice you do not keep gives resources to the Boss.  Resources are needed to perform powers that each Hero and Boss have.  The more resources you have, the more powerful your attacks can be.  Players and Bosses have action decks which provide unique abilities and attributes, which can be played with the resources you gain.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Salnave's War: Civil war in Haiti 1867-1870
description:
Salnave's war is Military, Economic and Political game that represents the event that occurred in Haiti form 1867 to 1870.  The period of Haitian history called Salnave’s war by some and The War of the Cacos by others saw Haiti split into three competing states and both brilliant naval strategies and inept anti-insurgency campaigns.

The intent of the game is to provide the simple rules, short to medium playing times, indirect player interaction and abstract physical components of a Euro-game and the simulation of strategic combat of a war game.

The game uses a Euro-game approach to simulate the interplay of economics, politics, and military action. This approach is tied to the specific places, personalities, and events of the Caco War to present a compelling way to play out the flow of this intriguing part of history. There are three major modes of play that allow players to follow the events of the war in close historical order, more loosely, or only as a general backdrop.

Time Strike is a 2 player PnP card combat style game where you have to keep wondering not where you're being hit, but when!  You fight an opponent in 40 second rounds.  The player that scores the most hits, wins the round, and whomever wins the most rounds, wins the fight!

Fights take place using on the timeline, where players must add time cards before being able to play attack cards.  Time Strike is highly strategic because you can have a full-on combo planned that could be thwarted by an attack a few moments before your combo.  Keep your opponent guessing, but keep your guard up in this intense time based battle.

Awards:
Tied for 2nd place in the 2012 Mashup Game Design & Artwork Contest

Karta doppio is an abstract game from the family of 4 rows. Material is 14 translucent cards in 1st edition, 16 translucent cards in the most recent edition, divided in 2x3 square cells with 3 colored dots of 2 colors and 3 free cells. Each player chose a color.

The aim of the game is to have the maximum of lines of 4 dots at the end of the game.

Each player receives half of the cards at the beginning. Cards are distributed randomly.

Each turn, a player plays one card to try to achieve a row.

Cells of Cards can be covered but only one time.

You have 2 options : you can play with a board (11x11) where some dots already printed on or without board. In the first case, placement is free on the board. In the second case the current card must touch or cover a card already played.

boardgame: Sergeants Miniatures Game: Pontigou Farm Normandy Expansion
description:
Pontigou Farm expands play with three typical farm buildings - a two story stone farm house, a small stone barn and a stone equipment shed. Each building has special sighting rules and provide excellent cover.

The ten missions at Pontigou Farm starts with a bare knuckle pre-dawn defense of Pontigou Grange. The story continues with scouting Pontigou Ferme, destroying an observation post, overrunning a strong point at the Pontigou Shed. The six player platoon attack to secure Pontigou Farm will have you dragging your friends to the game table.

boardgame: Family Business: Blood Brothers and The Moll Expansions
description:
If you currently control the weakest mob (fewest surviving members), you may take the Blood Brothers tile and place it Blood Brothers side up in front of you.

Blood Brothers: Whenever one of your mobsters is added to the Hit List, you must move one mobster already on the Hit List to the space directly in front of your just-added mobster (effectively making them a higher priority on the Hit List).  The mobster you move can be one of yours, or an opponent's.  If there are no other mobsters on the Hit List then ignore the effect.

When you activate Blood Brothers you must make a choice: either keep the tile Blood Brothers side up, or turn it over to The Moll side.

The Moll: Whenever one of your mobsters is added to the Hit List, you may take a mobster that is controlled by the player that put you on the Hit List and place it on the Hit List in the space behind your just-added mobster.  When you activate The Moll, you must flip the tile back over to the Blood Brothers side.

When your mob is no longer the weakest in play, or you are eliminated, you must give the tile to the player with the weakest mob.  If there is ever a tie, nobody may control the tile until one player has the weakest mob.  When there are only three players left in the game, this tile is taken out of play and is returned to the box.

If you are given poison, you may choose to flip the bad gas token.  If the token lands blank side up, there is no effect, and play proceeds as usual.

If it lands bad gas side up, immediately move your belt down one notch.  Additionally, neighboring players might be affected by your gas.

The player to your left and the player to your right must check to see if they are affected!  They each flip the bad gas token.  If their flip lands gas side up, then they are forced to purge and immediately move their belt down one notch.

If both your left and right neighbors purged, you are sickened by their acts and must move your belt up by the same number of spaces as the poison you originally received.

boardgame: Road Rally USA: Dead Man's Curve Expansion
description:
Set Up: You can play with either or both tiles in any combination.  To mix things  up you may flip the tiles before playing the game to see which side you will be playing with.
