Before receiving your starting cards, place a shortcut between two unconnected squares on the same track tile, matching the layout of the pre-printed shortcuts (i.e. the shortcut skips over 3 spaces).  The spaces (that you create a shortcut to and from) may have other features on them.  You may also place and shortcut directly over another shortcut if you wish.

"Green, Green, Grass" Short Cut: The Green, Green, Grass Short Cut may only be used by spending 3 or more green cards as your standard movement.  If you do, you may use this shortcut to skip over the intervening spaces during your normal move.  You do not have to end your turn at its entrance, nor do you have to stop at its exit.

"Red Right Hand Turn" Short Cut: The Red Right Hand Turn Short Cut may only be used by spending red cards as your standard movement.  If you do, you may use this shortcut to skip over the intervening spaces during your normal move.  You do not have to end your turn at its entrance.  However, you must end your movement at the shortcut's exit space.

Set Up: Each player starts the game with a lake tile.

Gameplay: One your turn, before you draw a tile, you can place your lake tile over any number on an unmanned hex tile to heat up or cool down an area.  This can cause the hot spot to shift.  Once your lake tile is placed it cannot be removed.

Part of Mayfair Games Limited Edition Promo Expansion Set #4, the Elk Fest: Log Jam! Expansion adds 4 log jam tiles to Elk Fest.

Set Up: Set up Elk Fest normally.  Temporarily put one of the riverbank pieces roughly at the half-way point between the two starting riverbanks.  Mix up the Log Jam tiles and place them, log side up, by the corners of the riverbank.  Return the riverbank piece to its starting position, leaving the tiles in place in the middle of the play area.  You are now ready to begin normally.

Rules: When a log is hit by a stone the log remains where it lands (just like when a stone hits another stone).  Your moose can use a log to cross over the water just like a stone.  However, before moving the moose onto the log you must immediately "roll" the log over and place it roughly where it was positioned.  There are three possible results of a rolled log:

    Logs (2 tiles): If you move onto a log, you must move your front feet to a stone by the end of your turn otherwise your "elk gets wet".  You may not use the stone that you came from for your front hooves.  If you start your turn on a log, you must be off the log entirely before the end of your turn, otherwise your "elk gets wet".
    Shaky Stones (1 tile): You may treat this like any stone, but as soon as you have left the tile, the stones are washed away and the tile is removed from the game.
    Bear (1 tile): You can't enter this tile.  This tile remains in play bear side up and may be flicked instead of a stone as a blocker.  If it falls off the table the bear goes into hibernation until the next game.

The Cuban missile crisis spins out of control in this new version of Bruce's acclaimed nuclear war fighting game system.
 Using historically accurate strategic (ie Nuclear) force structures the players will be responsible for fighting the war on a global scale when the diplomats fail.

Rather than concern themselves with the operations of Army Corps and Naval Task Forces, players will be directing the B-52 bomber wings of SAC or the Strategic Rocket Forces new SS-7 “Saddler” ICBM’s.

Of course the new Soviet missile complexes on Cuba still retain their importance as a dagger aimed at the heart of the USA and will have to be factored into both sides plans. The surprise potential of these weapons are accounted for in the radar rules.

First Strike '62 is a hex-and-counter wargame. Units have movement points and move across a global map. Players select Operating Programs/Warplans to determine targets for their bomber, submarine and missile assets. Then they attempt to have those units reach their targets while avoiding opposing interceptors, ASW and anti-missiles. The "winner" is the player who scores the most victory points, based on strategic targets hit, surviving assets, and collateral damage received.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
High Heavens is a cards and board game where gods fight in the skies: the ancient Norse and Greek deities and demigods against each other in a tactic battle on a hex battlefield.

High Heavens Egyptian Expansion introduces a new pantheon deck with 24 new cards to play the ancient Egyptian gods.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Cult of the Golden Scarab (fan expansion for Arkham Horror)
description:
Cult of the Golden Scarab is a fan-made expansion for the Fantasy Flight Games boardgame Arkham Horror. This expansion deals with events surrounding the re-opening of the 'Legacy Of The Pharaohs' exhibition, one year after that exhibition closed in appalling circumstances. Once again it falls to a small band of investigators to risk life, limb and sanity by battling the minions of the despised Nephren-Ka and preventing the resurrection of the loathsome subterranean carrion-gods of the Old Kingdom...

The expansion includes 8 Investigators, 6 Ancient Ones, 144 Investigator cards, 96 Ancient One cards, 33 Monsters, 1 Guardian, 1 Herald, and more.
family: Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Your parents have given each of their children a part of the family farm in the small but growing town of Cottonwood. You have three years to show that you can make your share profitable. You may plant corn and grain, raise animals, and gather berries and herbs from the countryside. You may sell goods at each of the three Markets, and enter the income in your ledger. The sibling who has the most money in their ledger after the third year wins the contest, and will receive the remainder of the family farm. [Source: rules]

In The Cottonwood Contest, players develop their farms by plowing fields, planting corn and grain, building pastures, raising animals, weaving baskets, and gathering herbs and berries. Double-ended farm cards depict the choices for what the farmers may do with their day.

There are three scoring phases, triggered by drawing a market card. Prices for each item fluctuate, and players must determine the best time to sell their items. Corn and grain may be sold, or used to raise sheep and cows. Players may sell the wool from their sheep and the milk from their cows, or the sheep and cows themselves. Players may sell berries and herbs, or make the berries into jam and dry the herbs to sell at a later market.

The player who has the most money in their ledger after the third market is the winner.

A fruit-themed dice game for children that combines elements from Yahtzee and Old Maid

Theme
Players try to collect the biggest and ripest fruit for a fruit salad. But careful, don't get any moldy ones!

Goal
Collect as many points as possible by rolling die combinations to win fruit tiles and avoiding the mold tile.

Game play
There are 12 fruit tiles (4 different types of fruit in three sizes). They are sorted according to size and type and placed in the middle of the table.
Players take turns rolling the four dice up to three times per turn. They may set aside as many dice as they want. The dice show the different types of fruit on four sides, a joker on one, and "Moldi the mold" on the last. After a maximum of three rolls, the result is counted. Each roll of a fruit counts as two of that type, the joker can be used as one of any type of fruit. Four fruits of a type win the active player the small tile of that type, 5 the medium one, and 6 the large one. If a player rolls 7 or 8 of one fruit, he/she gets all the tiles of that type, even if another player has already won them. 
If one of the dice shows the mold side at the end of a player's turn, that player doesn't win any fruit tile and has to take the mold tile instead. The player who has the mold tile when the game ends gets two minus points.

The game ends when there are no tiles left in the pool. Players count their points and the player with the most points wins the game.

Palestina under Davidstjernen is a 1950s boardgame themed on the Jewish rights to settle in the promised land of Palestine ("Kanaans land", "Jews land"). Players move their token on a classical 50s map of Palestine, and scores points as they reach different cities and locations on the map. Winner is the player with highest score after 30 minutes.

Players move on the board from directions given by the David star; players spin the David star, mounted on a large paper clip, each turn, and move on the board accordingly, as the David star tells you both which direction to go, and how far you should go. The rule book states where this location is mentioned in the bible, and how many points you get from entering this location.

Advanced version is to point in what part of the Bible this location is mentioned. The game is in Norwegian.

'Hijack the Revolt is a quick game of calculated risk, cooperation and backstabbing for three players.

Throughout history rulers have treated their subjects badly enough for the populous to rise up against them. Though joined momentarily in their common effort to overthrow the tyrant, the rebellious forces are comprised of factions having different visions of what their new country should be. Only one of those ideals will win out, and only then if they can remain united long enough to make their rebellion a success and avoid execution.

Hijack the Revolt is a cooperative game, up to a point. In this game of urban warfare there are four objectives which the three rebels must take before the Dictator sends reinforcement. They are Communications,  Munitions,  Airport, Presidential Palace. The game proceeds by each player in their turn playing a road taking tile to move them forward. You  have farther to go than the Dictator who advances automatically at the end of each turn. The only way to take an Objective is to coordinate your assault as you advance block by hard won block  But only one of you can win each round. When is the timing right for striking out on your own?

Like the game Risk boiled down to its most excruciating 20 minutes.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: In Tangram 4, players race the timer and each other to complete the shape shown on the card.  The person who succeeds stays in place, while the others move one space closer to the dragon's mouth.  The winner is the one who is able to stay alive!

From the Back Cover:

During the period of 1600 to 1800 infamous pirates including Blackbeard, Captain Kidd, Anne Booney and Henry Morgan RAIDed ships and coastal towns in the Caribbean. Speed, cunning, surprise, determination, stalking one's prey, ambush, counter attack, fearlessness and ruthlessness characterized the RAIDs of these buccaneers.

The new Caribbean word game - RAID - recaptures the excitement, thrills, and anticaption of those bygone days.

RAID is a game for 2 - 6 players or teams. Play consists of forming words from the exposured blocks. Triple bonus points are earned by forming the name of any Caribbean country (a list is provided).

For example, Player one forms the word "cub" from the exposed blocks. Player two adds an A to form a new word "cuba". This is a RAID. Player two has formed the name of a Caribbean country Cuba, and so is awarded triple bonus points.

Included in Top Cover: Fighting Wings Journal #1.

Australia’s Pacific Air War: In a one-sided fight over Rabaul on January 20th, 1942, Japanese Zeros annihilated a small force of RAAF Wirraway armed-trainers, which were all that stood between Japanese air power and Northern Australia. Little bothered by the smattering of slow climbing American P-40 fighters subsequently rushed to the area, high flying Japanese planes raided the Northern Territories an average of four times a month during 1942.

This changed dramatically in early 1943 when the rapidly expanding RAAF deployed an entire wing of British built Spitfires to Darwin. Led by Australian ace Clive “Killer” Caldwell, the Spitfires quickly and forcibly confronted the Japanese. At this same time, the RAAF fielded another famous British fighter, the long-range, twin engine Beaufighter. These fast, ten-gun behemoths had an offensive role, ranging far and wide to attack Japanese bases and shipping as far away as Timor, Rabaul and all of New Guinea. Mid-1943 also saw the deployment of the Boomerang to New Guinea. This nimble homegrown fighter, though lacking in air combat ability, proved excellent as a ground support plane.  Thus, whether defending the north, sweeping the seas or bombing bunkers, Australian aircrews and their planes, were a decisive factor in reversing the Japanese fortunes of war in 1943.

Top Cover No.1: This is the first Fighting Wings game system expansion to be produced. This module explores the contribution of the Royal Australian Air Force to the fighting in the South Pacific in 1942 and 1943. You will need to own Whistling Death to fully access many of the scenarios contained herein.

The Module includes the following:

    Four New Allied Aircraft - CAC Wirraway, CAC Boomerang, RAAF Spitfire Mk.V and  the RAAF Beaufighter Mk.VIc. 
    Four New Japanese Aircraft – H6K4 Mavis, Ki.45 Nick, Ki.46 Dinah and a revised Ki.43 Oscar-II.
    One die cut counter sheet with 140 counters, featuring the new aircraft and additional game markers. 
    One Top-View Operational Scale map of the Darwin area of Australia.
    Thirty new air-to-air and air-to-ground scenarios for Whistling Death.


(from Clash of arms website)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Roll and move children's game with circus theme. The players should roll the dice and go around the circle, pick cards and follow the instructions on them. The winner is the first player, who reach finish line by exact count.
The paper gameboard has a great circus tent scene. There are 72 original paper cards that players pick to navigate their routes and four colored wooden place markers.

Girl Scouts & Guides game "WORLD-O, ohé! le monde". "A game on the world association of girl guides and girl scouts".
Players taking question cards and try to answer questions (answer cards shows the right answer) about scouting. The player at the end (then no more question cards left) with the most amount of answered questions win the game.

boardgame: The Robust Game of Robin Hood
description:
Simple spin and move game similar to many others of the times with Robin Hood theme. Players spin and move exact amount, take a direction card and follow the instructions. The first who cross finish line wins the game.
There are four characters: Robin Hood (green), Will Scarlet (red), Friar Tuck (black) and Sheriff of Nottingham (yellow).
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Robin Hood

description:
For centuries, the armies of the Great Houses have fought to reclaim the glory of the fallen Star League, sending regiments of their elite MechWarriors into battle across hundreds of worlds as humanity slid inexorably toward a new Dark Age. The coming of the Clans – descendants of the lost Star League army – did little to stem the slaughter.

It only raised the stakes.

BattleTech: Alpha Strike is a fast-playing form of the BattleTech game of futuristic, armored combat. Developed for the modern tabletop miniatures wargamer, this book brings players the ability to wage war on land, sea, and air using the Quick-Strike game system in BattleTech: Strategic Operations. BattleTech: Alpha Strike, like its forerunner Quick-Strike, draws upon a hybridization of the BattleForce rules also available in Strategic Operations, together with the miniature game rules presented in that same volume which allow for hexless terrain play. BattleTech: Alpha Strike includes support for both hex-based and hexless game play.

In BattleTech: Alpha Strike, themed around the Clan Invasion, each BattleMech (or other game unit) is represented using a series of Unit Cards, which are highly abstracted derivations of the respective Record Sheet for each unit in "classic" BattleTech. (While BattleForce uses the same abstraction to group 'Mechs into lances, Stars, centuries, or level IIs, BattleTech: Alpha Strike should treat each 'Mech as an individual unit.)

As with "classic" BattleTech, a player's goal is to use his force to achieve his objectives, which generally involves the defeat or destruction of opposing forces. However, the game's use of BattleForce Unit Cards and rule options allow it to play at a much faster (though less intricately detailed) level of play than "classic" BT. Complete with sample armies and a ready-to-play campaign system, all you need besides this book are dice, miniatures, and tabletop terrain to fight for control over the Inner Sphere on land, sea, and air – one world at a time!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – Lambda-class Shuttle Expansion Pack
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Noted for its tri-wing design, the Lambda-class shuttle served a critical role as a light utility craft in the Imperial Navy. Now you can use this utilitarian ship to strengthen your Imperial squad for Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game. The Lambda-class Shuttle Expansion Pack includes one large-base Lambda-class shuttle miniature at 1/270 scale, its maneuver dial, all necessary tokens, four ship cards, and a whopping twelve upgrades, which afford Imperial players an astonishing level of customization and control of their squads. The four ship cards feature three unique pilots who can each provide powerful benefits to their squad mates; for example, Captain Kagi can divert enemy target locks from your other ships and force your opponent to fire against his shuttle and its five shields.

The Lambda-class Shuttle is highly customizable, too, as each one can carry up to two crew members, one cannon, and a system upgrade. Pair Captain Kagi with a Rebel Captive, and you may discourage your opponent from trying to establish target locks and fire missiles.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

description:
During the Wiener Spielefest, "Spieleberater"  move around to   explain games to the visitors.

Simple cooperative game for kids: Move any pawn, but not the one moved by the previous player and not the one actually forbidden to the next hidden tile. Reveal it (it shows a part of a board game) and place it onto the appropriate picture. If you pass a clock symbol by moving, add 1 hour on the clock. All children win if completed all games before 9 "game hours".

advanced competitive game for families: In addition, all players start with movement points they spend during their turn.  If a  player adds a picture tile, he will get 3 or 5 victory points (by chance). Moving tiles not spent count 1 victory point at the end in addition.

Was added to a description of recommended games available in Austria

The third volume in the HorizonWars Trilogy, following MechaWar and AirFrame, BattleGroup introduces the rest of the combined arms force to the game.

BattleGroup is a hard science fiction battle game for actions from platoon to battalion level that captures the tactical dynamic of the all-arms force.  Tanks, artillery and infantry must fight as a cohesive whole under the command of a single headquarters, but every army has its own style and emphasis - how will you lead?

Players assemble their forces from a menu of options based on their choice of Command Headquarters.  Battles are scenario-based in which each player must fight for competing priorities, with each taking their turn to activate a single element - but your opponent's forces can react to the actions of your own.  As elements take damage, their ability to fight on is gradually eroded or, if they suffer a critical hit, they may be destroyed entirely!  Should you put your injured soldiers into cover where they can patch up their injured and call up reinforcements?  Or should you press on for a vital objective?

The game is compatible with the other two volumes of HorizonWars, so you can add mighty mech battlesuits to your forces for additional punch and real sci-fi flavour, and dispatch squadrons of aircraft to lay down close support alongside your advancing troops.

The design draws deeply upon the experiences of the author, a former officer in the British Army, to deliver a high-quality tactical experience with a powerfully realistic feel, whilst still giving a play experience that can be completed in under an hour.

Key features:

    Miniatures wargame of modern/science fiction combat
    Integrated turn sequence
    Action-based turns
    d12-based dice pool for "one roll" shooting actions
    Customizable statlines
    Compatible with MechaWar and AirFrame from One Pound Wargames
    Designed for use with any range of modern or science fiction miniatures in scales from 1/805 (2mm) to 1/161 (10mm)
    Includes advanced rules and three starting scenarios

boardgame: The Busy World of Richard Scarry: Busiest Shopping Day Ever Game
description:
Match the colors and be the first busy shopper to collect everything on your shopping list!

Each player selects a shopping list and a shopper playing piece.  24 cardboard grocery item tokens are placed on their matching spaces on the board.

On each turn, the player spins the spinner and moves his shopper to the next matching colored space on the gameboard path.  If the player lands on a space next to an item on his shopping list, he takes that item from the gameboard and puts it on his shopping list card.  Bonus moves include a Free Coupon space and spinning on Bananas Gorilla or Sergeant Murphy.

The first player to get to the checkout space with all the items on his list is the winner.

Theme
Players try to win most of the dwarves' treasure.

Goal
Collect as many plastic chips ("gold coins") as possible.

Game play
The board shows one central circle and 9 circles arranged around it. Each circle has a number from 2 to 11 printed at the center, and a number of small markings corresponding to that number around it. A plastic pot filled with plastic chips is placed on the central circle.
Players take turns rolling the two six-sided dice. The result of a roll is added up and a chip is placed on one of the markings on the corresponding circle (e. g. if you roll a 4 and a 5, you put a chip on one of the markings on circle 9).
If you roll a 12, you put a chip on a circle of your choice.
When all markings on one circle have been filled and that number is rolled again, the player wins the chips from that circle.

The game ends when a player has collected 20 chips or when there are no chips left in the pot. The player who has collected the most chips wins the game.

A Game of Rhyming and Poetry based on the books of Dr. Seuss.

Players roll one die and move a communal pawn around the game board.  When a player lands the mover on a "Rhyme" or "Poem" space, he draws a "Rhyme" or "Poem" card and completes the actions shown on the card.  The card actions include word play activities like: Name four words that rhyme with "cat"; Make up a four-line poem about the sea.

If a player completes an action correctly, he moves his colored Hat marker a certain number of spaces towards the multi-colored Hat in the center, then back out again towards his own colored Hat space.  The first player to return his Hat to its starting space wins.

ENGLISH:
Why can we choose between good and evil? Would you be able to explain "Christmas" without mentioning the words "white", "birth" and "Christ"? Want to try to draw the Holy Family? We propose a funny way to learn the faith, while playing mime, answer questions, draw, or you guess words.
The joy of faith is a board game inspired by the Catechism "Jesus is Lord" On a 67 squares board, you can play with your  family or church groups or school, for testing the knowledge of the first childhood catechism.

Designed for adults and children from 7 years old, "The joy of faith" is a game that can discover and teach the basics of the Christian faith.

SPANISH:
¿Por qué podemos elegir entre el bien y el mal? ¿Serías capaz de explicar la "Navidad" sin mencionar las palabras "blanco", "nacimiento" y "Jesús"? ¿Te atreves a dibujar a la Sagrada Familia? Te proponemos una forma divertida de conocer la fe, mientras juegas en equipo a hacer mímica, a responder preguntas, a dibujar, o a la palabra tabú.
La alegría de la fe es un juego de mesa, inspirado en el Catecismo "Jesús es el Señor". Sobre un tablero de 67 casillas se puede jugar en familia, en los grupos parroquiales o en la escuela para ir poniendo a prueba los conocimientos del primer catecismo de infancia.

Pensado para adultos y niños a partir de 7 años, "La alegría de la fe" es un juego con el que es posible descubrir y aprender lo esencial de la fe cristiana.

"Control Tower" is a family boardgame about the management of the planes in an airport.
The players are air controllers who must land and take off the planes and distribute those planes in the diferent areas of the airport like repair/maintenance garage or refuelling to satisfice their requeriments.

The game has three phases. Twice of them are played simultaneously the makes the game very agile and exciting. The players feel the tension and anxiety that makes them quick decisions about the suitables planes.

"Control Tower" is addictive boargame for 1 to 4 players, with easy rules but complex making decisions.


"Torre de Control" es un juego de mesa familiar sobre la gestion de aviones en un aeropuerto.
Los jugadores encarnan a controladores aereos de la gestion interna de un aeropuerto y entre sus funciones estan ordenar los aterrizajes y despegues de los aviones y las distribucion de los aviones en los diferentes servicios del aeropuerto tales como  repostaje y reparacion y mantenimiento.

El juego se divide en tres fases. Dos de ellas se juegan de manera simultanea dandole al juego vitalidad y ritmo que hace que los jugadores se involucren y sientan la tension y el agobio de la continua llegada de aviones al aeropuerto con distintas necesidades a satisfacer.

"Torre de control" es un juego adictivo de 1 a 4 jugadores, de reglas muy sencillas pero con mucha toma de decisiones.

Publisher Blurb:

Po-Rum-Bo is a Poker meets Rummy board game. Create 3 of a kind, 4 of a kind, straights & flushes and crisscross them on a game board to outscore your opponent(s). The object of the game is to outscore your opponent(s). Players criss-cross playing cards using cards Ace/1 - 10 of all four playing card suits either "across" or "down" on a game board with bonus squares, earning points with every play of the game based on the cards played, the hands created and the board bonus squares used. Jacks, Queens & Kings are wild.

description:
From Publisher's Introduction:
"Timeline 300 is a tabletop wargame, designed to be played between two or more players, that uses collectable miniatures which are intended to be built, painted, and personalised by each player so that she can construct a personal army of her (or perhaps, his) very own. Your tactical skill, and a random factor determined by the roll of dice, shall decide who wins your own “wars that never were”.

The game depicts a Martian invasion of earth during steam technology era.  Players generate resources from specific units (such as mining units) each turn that can be used to 'purchase' combat units for use in the battle portion of the game.  It simulates the type of action found in many real-time-strategy (RTS) games where resources and facilities help guide what type of forces each player can bring to bear.

The King of Gemstone Island does not have an heir to the throne. Thus, he has set up a competition to determine who is worthy of being next in line as King or Queen.

To win this competition and thus become next in line to rule the island, you must be the first player to successfully bury a complete collection of gemstones. As you attempt to complete your mission, any of the following may happen: a hole in your sack, an earthquake, someone switching sacks with you (without your permission, of course), someone bumping into you and then picking up the gems you drop, or even another player digging up all that treasure you worked so hard to bury!

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game – Fire and Ice
description:
"In ancient books of Asshai it is written that there will come a day after a long summer when the stars bleed and the cold breath of darkness falls heavy on the world. In this dread hour a warrior shall draw from the fire a burning sword."
    –Melisandre, A Clash of Kings

The second Chapter Pack in the "Kingsroad" cycle, Fire and Ice returns players to the Seven Kingdoms of Westeros, their conflicts, and their intrigues. It also marks a return to some of the prominent themes and mechanisms that have already been developed in A Game of Thrones: The Card Game.

Seasons in Westeros can last for years, Thus, it's only natural that they can drastically reshape the lives of those they impact, and the sixty new cards in Fire and Ice (three copies each of twenty different cards) draw new focus to the game's Summer and Winter mechanisms. They also add a number of intriguing new twists. Not only will you find new characters and locations to grant your deck and House a powerful boost in one season or the other, you'll find two new Seasons agendas that will lead to all-new deck types. These agendas reward decks that combine many season-based effects, and they gain support from other cards in Fire and Ice that help players pull those season-based effects to the forefront, ensuring that the clash between Summer and Winter remains as vital within the game as it is within the Seven Kingdoms.

description:
Great minds think ... alike? outside the box?  
Can you think as a group or by yourself?  Can you confound the group into choosing something different?

This is a light-hearted fun little party game.  Players are trying to guess what the majority of the group will think and choose that answer, or choose something that others will not choose.  Whomever has the most points over several rounds is the winner.

On a round the active player:
- selects one of their cards and reveals it to the group
(the cards have several options listed e.g. cat, mouse, cheese)
- forms a phrase/sentence/question about that card
- reveals the "goal" card (which indicates "alike" or "outside of the box")

Players then try to select the best answer according to the "Group Goal" card.  If "alike" is revealed, they are trying to be in the group with the majority of guesses, if "outside of the box" is revealed, they are trying to not be in the group with the majority of guesses.
  
The inspiration of this game was to make a silly little fun filler, that was portable that would provide lots of laughter, while allowing players to come up with creative statements of their own.

Super Soaker Twister is an outdoor game with the unusual requirement of a garden hose. A large, perforated vinyl mat is attached to a garden hose, which results in thin streams of water being emitted from the mat. Players place their hands and feet on colored circles on the mat, as dictated by a waterproof spinner. Example moves include "left foot green" and "right hand red."

The object is to move as directed by the spinner, while only allowing your hands and feet to touch the mat. The player who can do this for the longest duration is the winner. As the vinyl mat becomes increasingly wet, moving without falling becomes more difficult.

Players familiar with outdoor games from the 70s and 80s might recognize this as a combination of Slip 'n Slide and Twister.

boardgame: Tzolk'in: The Mayan Calendar – Mini Expansion 2
description:
Given away in the Gathering of Friends 2013 convention giftbags, this mini expansion includes one new monument and one new starting wealth tile.

From the Rules:

Bonus Monument

Unlike other monuments, the points for constructing this are scored immediately (not at the end of the game).  The number of points scored depends on when you build it:

First quarter of the game- 40 points.
Second quarter- 31 points.
Third quarter- 22 points.
Fourth quarter- 13 points.

As depicted on the tile, each Food Day is counted as the last day of its quarter.  For example, if you build this monument on the first Food Day, you get 40 points.

If a Food Day occurs one day late (because a player used the time-speeding privilege of the Starting Player Space) it still counts as the last day of its quarter for the purposes of this monument.

Bonus Starting Wealth Tile

If you take this as your Starting Wealth Tile, you get 6 corn and 4 wood, but you also move down one step on the brown temple.

In a 2- or 3-player game, this tile might be drawn when you are placing dummy workers.  If so, this tile indicates that you must place one extra dummy worker (7 in a 3-player game, or 13 in a 2-player game).

Instead of using worker figures, you can represent dummy workers with the round, flat markers in the non-player colors.  Otherwise you won't have enough.  Also, this makes it easier to distinguish players' workers from dummy workers, so we recommend using the markers even when you aren't playing with this tile.

A game of hat tricks, F.A. Enquiries, penalty appeals, and last minute goals.
Home and away matches against all the other players.
The Double is a game of chance, tactics and cunning.
Good players will win the Premiership.
Great players will win the double. The Premiership and the F.A.Cup.
Which will you be?

Players (2-4) represent football teams vying to win the double of the Premiership, and the F.A.Cup. Each player represents either Blue (City), Yellow (County) Green (Rovers) or Red (United) and takes a corresponding coloured token and six sided dice. Players start at the bottom of the league table and a fixture card is drawn. Both players roll their dice with the winner moving up the table 3 places or 1 place if the result is draw. The board is divided into 5 zones forming from the bottom The relegation zone, danger zone, mid table, and European places. Each time a player passes into a new zone a tactic card is drawn which can used and played to affect the results of matches. The home team of each match can re-roll his result dice, but has to take that result of the re throw.
The winner of the premiership has the advantage of choosing the draw for the F.A. Cup and has a one goal advantage on his throw, but tactic cards cannot be used by the players in the F.A. Cup.

A statistical replay game, loosely based on TCS Games previous Olympic Ice Hockey game, High Sticking. 
You will find the usual features of TCS team games (rating for attack, defense, killer instinct for Overtime resolution, Third die random modifier), plus advanced rules that put in play even the powerplay opportunities and the penalty killing value of each team, to make each game a little more slower (but still very fast), but much more tense.

It exists a basic version containing rulebook and data for all the WHA seasons (1972-1979) another set with the Early Leagues, from 1887 to 1926 (50 different seasons from manydifferent leagues, NHA, WCHL, PCHA, ECAHA, FAHL, CHA, and so on), a set with NHL from 1917 to 1942, and another set with NHL from 1942 to 1967.

boardgame: Conflict of Heroes: Eastern Front – Solo Expansion
description:
After more than three years of development, Conflict of Heroes: Eastern Front - Solo Expansion is a solitaire expansion for Conflict of Heroes: Awakening the Bear. Instead of AI units being individually programmed as in some past games, AI units utilize a unique emergent behavior and agent-based logic. Each situation is evaluated and the AI implements the best course of action using available resources and unit assets. Even veteran CoH players will be challenged to hone and adapt their playing styles in order to overcome the AI!

In the Conflict of Heroes Solo System, players no longer track APs, but must make a Unit Spent Check after every action. The higher the cost in APs an action was, the higher the probability that the unit will be spent. Now a player must decide if he will augment his action with APs or increase his chance of keeping the unit fresh!

Each Firefight has its own Mission Track. A Mission Track is a series of events that are triggered if the Mission Marker lands on an event's space. Certain orders advance the Mission Marker 1, 2 or 3 spaces, so the game is different each time you play. The firefight ends immediately when the Mission Marker lands on or passes the last space of the Mission Track.

Note: This is an expansion for Awakening the Bear specifically, not for other Conflict of Heroes games. It was originally released for 2nd edition Awakening the Bear.

     With a bit of adapting, this expansion can be used with 1st Edition Awakening the Bear.
     And with a different bit of adapting, this expansion can be used with 3rd Edition Awakening the Bear.


Note: The base game 2-player scenarios are NOT playable with solitaire rules "out of the box"; solitaire scenarios need additional elements (mission track etc). So additional homebrew tinkering is needed to adapt an existing 2-player scenario for use with the solitaire system. But the solitaire expansion comes with many (new) scenarios, and the Firefight Generator expansion permits creating solitaire scenarios.

boardgame: Viaje al Centro de la Tierra
description:
Game for two to four players, inspired by the novel by Jules Verne.
Players travel to the center of the earth, should get the materials needed to make progress and be the first out of the volcano Stromboli.
Its components are:
Board, 12 counters, dice, 57 cards (possibly missing a few).


Juego de dos a cuatro jugadores,inspirado en la novela de Julio Verne.
Los jugadores viajan al centro de la tierra, deben conseguir los materiales necesarios para avanzar y ser los primeros en salir por el volcan Stromboli.
Sus componentes son:
Tablero, 12 fichas,dado, 57 cartas (posiblemente faltan algunas).

Game for two players. The board represents a city where players must steal atomic energy projector the other player. The movement is done by the adjacency board, eliminating the pieces depends on the type to which they face.


Juego para dos jugadores.El tablero representa una ciudad donde los jugadores deben robar un proyector de energia atómica del otro jugador. El movimiento por el tablero se hace por adyacencia, la eliminación de las piezas depende al tipo a las que se enfrenten.

"A rich-man's game".

"The object of the game is for the player to put up as many businesses and make as much money as possible before the game is over."

A Monopoly-like game, minus the bidding and trading, manufactured in the Philippines. 2-6 players move around the track by rolling 2 dice and moves forward as the total roll allows. The player can acquire the business he lands on. If the space is already owned, then the player must pay the owner as indicated in the amount shown in the property card. Players have the option to construct or put up a one-storey or two-storey in any of the business space he own in order to increase it's value. The game ends wen all but one player has declared bankruptcy or when the bank runs out of money.

boardgame: Among the Stars: Pre-order Promo Cards
description:
Among the Stars: Preorder Promo Cards is a promotional Special Location card set given to customers who pre-ordered Among the Stars from Artipia Games.  These cards are included in the Among the Stars: Expanding the Alliance although they may have new artwork.

The card set includes two copies of each of the following:


        Ambassadorial Shuttle 
        Casino
        Defense Grid
        Galactic Expo
        Waste Processing Facility
        Xenobiology Lab
        Zero Gravity Area

Welcome to AMBIGUITY, the head-spinning letter-turning word game. The Goal: Roll the eight letter dice into the tray and turn over the two minute timer. Now the fun begins because the letters on these dice are ambiguous and represent several different letters depending on how you turn your head to view them. Quickly study the "face-up" letters, without moving them, and write down as many words as you can spell, Players earn points for word length. The first player to score 100 points wins. So what are you waiting for? Get ready for some head-spinning fun.

In this card game for 2 to 4, players assume the role of a video game mini boss vying for control of the kingdom. Each player must deftly manage their personal army of minions to kidnap the most princesses before the game ends!

This game has three different rule-sets, and while the initial setup is the same, the different gameplay rules provide vastly different experiences.
 SURLY SKIRMISH is the recommended game for new players. It keeps things fast and light and theres a lot of immediate chances spite your opponents.

FOGGY FRONT-LINES is a simultaneous action blind bidding game, with the added bonus of an embarrassing hat.

CRANKY CAMPAIGN is a slightly heavier worker placement type game about careful timing and waiting to see the whites of their beady little eyes.

All game modes involve deploying squads of minions to claim MAP cards, which in turn have various effects, such us kidnapping princesses or messing with your opponent's army. 
Also recruiting more troops into your army can give you a big advantage later.

Once the timer pile is depleted and the FINALE card is captured, players add up their final score (determined by the princesses they've captured) and find out who deserves to be crowned the next BIG BOSS.

A educational/trivia game about electrical power.

Pull a power card and answer a question determined by the color of the card. Answer correctly and move 1 to 3 spaces on the board. Follow directions on board space if any (move forward/back). Answer incorrectly and loose turn.

Winner is first person to land on or pass their starting space.

Similar to the game Authors, each card features 5 quotations from a single author. Quotations are read until someone guesses the author correctly.

From the New-York Times of August 22, 1863:
"Mr. John H. Tingley, of No. 152 Fulton-street, advertises a new game for the social circle, called the "Game of Familiar Quotations." It affords a very pleasant way of becoming acquainted with the beauties of Shakespeare, Cowper, Colley Cibber, Young, Coleridge and others, whose writings add beauty to the English classics."

Introduction
TOPOLO is a 2 player abstract game that is played on the spaces of an 8x8 grid.

The Red player and the Black player have 32 cuboid pieces in 3 sizes(16 small pieces worth - 1 points apiece, 8 mid size pieces worth - 2 points apiece and 8 large pieces worth 3 points apiece).

Objective
Assemble your pieces into the largest orthogonally adjacent group to win.

Definitions

Group – Like color 1 or more orthogonally adjacent pieces.

Play
At the start of the game the board is empty.

The player holding the Red pieces plays first.

The row on the board closest to the Red player is considered row 1 followed by rows 2,3,4,5,6,7,8.

The Red player may place any one of his pieces onto any empty space on the board, then its Black's turn. Thereafter play alternates.

Example:
If the Red player has placed his first piece onto an empty space on the board that is within an even numbered row then both players must then play onto all the empty spaces of the even numbered rows first before proceeding to fill the empty spaces of the odd numbered rows. Now if the Red player plays his first piece onto any empty space of an odd row then both players must play onto all of the remaining empty spaces of the odd rows before moving play to the even rows.

The game ends when the board is full.

Scoring
Your score is determined by counting the pieces within the largest Group. Each piece within these Groups are to be counted as 1 point apiece, irrespective of their individual heights.

The player with the highest score is the winner.

In the event the largest Groups of both players are the same size at end game, each piece within these Groups are to be counted by their individual height point values (small - 1,medium - 2 and large - 3)and added to determine a winner.

For subsequent draws look at the next largest Groups of each player and apply the same scoring rules.

From the back of the box:
"Welcher Hase ist der Schnellste? Bei der wilden Möhrenjagd gewinnt derjenige, der als erster das Möhrenbeet erreicht hat. Lustiges Würfelspiel für Klein und Groß, ideal als Mitbringspiel oder für die Reise."

Translation:
"Which rabbit is the fastest? A game of "Rabbit eating Raddish" will be won by the rabbit that reaches the raddish bed first. A funny dice game for every age. Perfect as a small gift or to play during travel."

Summary of gameplay:
"Rabbit eating Raddish" is a 2 player roll and move game. Each player takes two rabbits of the same colour and places them at the start in tandem. The raddish bed is placed on the table 50 cm away from the start. On his or her turn a player will roll the six-sided die and move the rabbit in the back ahead of the front rabbit as often as the die says. The player who reaches the raddish bed first is the winner.

“Defeat the opposing team by knocking out all of their players in the same turn or by getting to lucky 13 first on your SCORE CARD. Equip items and weapons to make your faction the strongest!” (From the rule book)

This card game recreates the pulp themed action that takes place in the Adventures of the 19XX graphic novel series. Each player receives a character card that comes with a unique set of special abilities and determines which side they’re on. Two factions battle to find a winner using one of two victory conditions:

1.    Finish a round with the entire opposing team knocked out.
2.    Be the first to score thirteen hits on the enemy faction.

Character cards are laid face up on the table with all stats showing. A marker is used to show the player’s current health out of a maximum possible five.  Each player has a Luck, Defense, and Attack value.  Every Character Card has a brief background description for those who have not read the graphic novels.

Over 100 Item and Luck cards with unique illustrations created just for this game are drawn each turn and equipped to increase your character’s attributes.  The Item deck contains weapons and other helpful cards such as the Thompson Gun, Experimental Gas Launcher, and bandages. The luck deck contains esoteric items such as curses, lucky helmets, spells, and more. Using these cards you and your faction attack opposing players each round and attempt to get to thirteen on the scorecard. Each damage you inflict on an opposing player gets them closer to being knocked out and moves your team up one point on the scorecard.

The game also comes with 21 Chapter Cards. These cards are drawn each time you score a point and allow you to play through the plot of various Adventures of the 19XX books. These cards come right from the book series using the original page art.

Monster
"Reward: Gain 5 Honour Crystals. Choose an opponent and name a card. The chosen opponent reveals their hand and you put any of the named card into your hand."

Fútbol Strategy gives you teams of 12 footballers that share the same nationality to pick the six stars you want to build a game plan around and contest a fast, strategic, and fun version of the beautiful game against your opponent.

Move your stars into different spaces in their defense, middle, or attacking thirds. Search for space on offense while cover and closing down on defense. The action cards resolve play and serve as the game clock, referencing one of the six unique ratings that each of your footballers have, making each unique and interesting to play. Pass, shoot, clear, and tackle. Futbol Strategy has realistic rules for corner kicks, free kicks, and penalty kicks while remaining sleek and easy to play.

Midway through the first game both players will be very comfortable with the system. Games then will play in just 30 minutes despite offering interesting strategic decisions in the beginning and tense tactical decisions throughout.

boardgame: Arcane Legions: Egyptian Army Pack – Infantry
description:
From the box:

"Mark Anthony commands the combined might of Greece and Egypt, but the sorceress Cleopatra VII commands him. Together they field an arcane army like none that has walked the Earth, its ranks filled with undead from Egyptian tombs, creatures of legend, the mighty Roman 13th Legion, and even the ancient gods themselves - all barely controlled by powerful sorceresses."

How high can you roll without rolling a one? The more you roll, the higher you score, the higher you score the faster you win. But, watch out that you don't roll any ones or you will lose your points and be required to pay a poker chip to the pot. The ultimate game of risk-taking and pushing the limits.

Rise of the Rock Star is a board game where players use 2 decks of combo-making cards and roll dice in a race around the unique multi-level board, rising in notoriety to become a Rock and Roll Legend. Players gain Fame Points to Level Up and to ultimately win the game. The variety of methods that players can use to gain Fame Points creates automatic momentum allowing players to focus on which strategy is best for each game.

Rise of the Rock Star is a game for 2-4 players ages 13+. Most versions of the game can be played in around 45 minutes, and several included alternate play methods allow game to increase and decrease in strategic complexity and length of play, making the game fun, customizable, and playable again and again.

Alien Frontiers: Faction Pack #2 - Sunrise Commune

Fleeing persecution on Earth, the "Sunnies" came to Maxwell in hopes of finding a place where humanity would just leave them alone.

FACTION ABILITIES

     OWNER:  Pay one less ore than usual for any colony you land on a territory where you already have at least one colony.
     FACILITY:  Pay 3 fuel to move 1 of your colonies to a territory with no other colonies on it.


COMPONENTS

     Faction Board
     1 new Alien Tech Card
     2 new Agenda Cards

description:
Plants grow, flower and wilt away as they produce seeds for the next year.

Flora is a quick and simple game played through four short seasons. During Winter, the game is set up. During Spring, players add tiles into an empty play area to form an initial group in any shape. During Summer, the group changes and grows as the players add two tiles and take one away each turn. The first gist of the game is that all these three actions must be used on different colours. Finally during Autumn, only one player takes a single tile and the game ends.

The second gist is that at the end of the game, the value of a collected tile is based on the tiles remaining in the group: multiple small, separate areas of the same colour make it valuable, but many of them might vanish or be easily connected into one large area, resulting in lots of negative points. Players also have control over the game's length and Autumn can arrive unexpectedly.

The basic two-player game has no structural hidden elements, it consists only of 11 red tiles, 13 blue tiles and 15 yellow tiles, and the rules are easily adaptable for thematic additions and personal variants.

Schachadma (from "schach", meaning chess, and "adma", short for admiral) by Klaus W. Schomberg is a chess family game played on a 12x12 board, inspired by chess and battleships.

Each piece is a ship, with between one and four masts.  Each turn, a player moves or rotates a ship, after which all of that ship's cannons fire.  Enemy masts within range of the cannons are captured; when a ship has no masts left, it is also captured.

The tallest mast on the largest ship represents the admiral; capturing an opponent's admiral wins the game.

American Revolution is a solitaire parody game that pits the left vs the right in a fight to restore the nation to it's Constitutional roots.  As a 'Patriot', you use political and military power to save the nation from a President that has usurped power to become a King-like figure.

In the game the player competes against the deck for control of strategic cities across the nation. A city card is drawn.  Four opposition cards are drawn to represent gov't presence in the city. The player keeps a hand of 6 cards but can only play 3 per city to defeat the gov't presence.  There are bonuses and penalties associated with each city, opposition, and player card.

The player must win 6 cities before the opposition (game deck) wins 6 cities.

Rob Delaney's War of Words is a party game based on the tweets of Rob Delany, who has been named "Funniest Person on Twitter" by a number of media organizations. In the game, players are competing to be the first to cross a finish line, and they advance by doing well in three types of competitions, with the type largely being determined by the space on the game board where a playing piece is located:


    "This Means War!" – On this space, the active player duels an opponent with each of them reading a Delaney tweet and the other players voting on who presented the line best.



    "Firing Blanks" – The active player reads most of a tweet, but leaves a BLANK in the text. The other players each suggest a word or phrase to fill in the blank; whoever the active player judges best moves ahead two spaces, while this player moves ahead one space for each answer that he matches with an opponent.



    "Loaded Questions" – This works similar to the previous category, but players are submitting answers to questions based on tweets.


Whoever makes it around the track first – whether through an exact die roll at the start of a turn or spaces advanced thanks to doing well on a challenge – wins!

Suspicion is a game of strategy and social maneuvering.  As wealthy tycoons from the 1800's, players compete for power to take influence away from each other while trying to not become a target themselves.

Each round of the game consists of two phases.  In the first phase, players play Arsenal cards with numbers ranging from -5 to 5 in value in front of themselves or other players one at a time.  After each player has placed three cards, the three highest non-tied values gain control of a Power Deck.

In the second phase, the players with Power Decks, which consist of cards representing all the players, vote on a player to take Influence tokens from.  If all three judges pick the same person, that player instead takes three Influence tokens from any players they choose.  If all three judges pick different people, then the judges must each give one Influence token to another player of their choice.  Otherwise, the two judges who picked the same person take one Influence token each from the player they targeted.

At this point, the Dealer passes the Arsenal deck to the player to their left who deals everyone three cards and play goes back to the first phase.  The game ends when every player has been Dealer for one round.  The player with the most Influence tokens at the end of the game wins.

Each player also has a secret Agenda card with conditions like "Have three 3's in front of you during the first phase of a round".  If the player fulfills one of the three conditions on their card, they may turn it in and take the appropriate number of Influence tokens from any player(s) of their choice.  They will get a new Agenda card at the end of that round.

Althingi is a historical game for 3-5 players.

Players compete against each other to become the icelandic lawspeaker by controlling the provinces and thus having more influence during the periodic trials which bring order as well as chaos.

Every turn new provinces are added in contest while players try to gain them through an auction. It will be with that provinces and the wise use of the few action cards you get every turn that you'll fight to win the later trials, which are won by the player with most influence points. At the end of the game, the player with most won trials will be the winner. Or at least will get more chances to be the winner.

The twelve wooden animals are placed in the center.
Question card is drawn and question is asked about one of the 12 animals. Child who first know the answer takes the animal.
If all animals is taken the player with the most animals is the winner.

Many other game-variants included.

Mind Your Step! is a press-your-luck card game where players try to earn the most money collecting flowers in the desert to sell.  This Game was designed for the 24 hour contest - May 2013

"They said it would be easy, you thought it would be simple, go and collect flowers to sell at the market - what could go wrong!?  That was until you met the Rats, no ordinary rats - these are definitely of an unusual size, but they are nothing compared to the fearsome Scorpions."

Players take turns to roll the dice and spend those points on drawing cards.  There are four decks to choose from, each deck costs a different amount to draw a card. The more expensive the cards the greater the value of the flowers in the decks, but the higher the risk of drawing a scorpion and leaving with nothing.  Players must decide whether to risk it and draw from the more valuable decks or play it safe with a cheap deck that guarantees they earn at least some money.  Rolling a double on the dice will let you choose between 2 cards so should be safer, but do you keep trying for a double or take the bonus for leaving without uncovering a Rat.  Ending your turn lets you sell your flowers at the market for coin, and at the end of the game whoever has the most coin wins.

Mystique Chain is a cardgame played with The Mystique Deck.

Players try not to "break" the Mystique Chain by finding cards that share exactly one feature with the card taken before.

The deck is shuffled and 30 randomly chosen cards are placed on the table face up. Then both players draw one of the remaining cards. The card with the lower card number is immediately put to the discard pile, and so are also all the cards not reveald. 
The player who drew the card with the higher card number chooses one of the three features of this card (Colour, Suit, Number) and puts the card on the discard pile. (During the first turn, he may not choose a card and a criteria that does not allwo the other player to find a matching card)

Then the other player must find a card that shares this feature, but none of the other two features of the card just discarded.
He chooses a card that fits this criterias, names a feature of this card and puts it to the discard pile. The game continues this way as long as players find fitting cards.

As soon as a player can´t find a fitting card, he loses the game.

FlashNics is a uniquely crafted deck of cards that can be used for serious and casual gaming, as well as educational purposes. Players will also be able to design their own "game sanctioned" cards at the FlashNics.com website.

FlashNics allows you to recruit mercenaries from across the galaxy to battle for you against other competing entities. Mercenaries will compete against one another in one of four arenas that appeal to differing skill-sets. Winning across all four arenas and the ability to secure them (or winning in extreme fashion) is necessary to defeat your opponents.

Game Play :

One of four starting arenas is chosen. Each player starts with 6 mercenary cards (not shared) drawn randomly from the game deck. The first player chooses a card combination he/she will use to attempt to defend the arena depending on the attributes of the mercenary. The second player responds with a valid card combination (depending on the first combinations, others aren't allowed). Play then resumes until one player submits the round.

The player that wins the round can then decide to defend an arena with the mercenary used in the winning combination, or can choose to run protection with the mercenary. Each player then draws up to 6 cards again.

Hand maintenance is critical, as values accumulated depend on the strength of the victorious card (the least powerful cards score higher) and cards strength in each of the 4 arenas varies.  Once the original deck is near depletion, being aware of cards still in play is a valuable tactic.  Various modifier and action cards can also modify gameplay.

40 points are necessary to win the game (the sum of the mercenary protection values; double the value of this number if all four arenas are defended).  Advanced players can create exclusive mercenaries with unique actions at http://FlashNics.com and use them directly in gameplay.

63 pictures are on the table. The clue you are given matches none of the pictres... Two pictures has a word sounding like the clue word you are looking for... And that's the answer! So think quickly and find the two matching pictures before everyone else!

The goal of the game is to name the word by pointing two pictures. To win a point you must be the first to point the good pictures.

The ultimate showdown for superior spellers, leaping linguists, and genius grammarians.

Players race against the clock to answer questions in five categories that challenge you to build words, brainstorm synonyms, and test proofreading skills - all for a lexophile's favorite reward: Letter Cards. First player to come up with a five-letter word or longer is champion of the grammar geeks!

boardgame: Sid Meier's Civilization: The Board Game – Wisdom and Warfare
description:
In Sid Meier's Civilization: The Board Game - Wisdom and Warfare, six new civilizations join in the battle for supremacy:  Aztecs, English, French, Japanese, Mongols, and Zulu – each with its own special powers. Along with the new civilizations comes new technologies, relics, wonders, and culture cards.

An alternative set of combat cards in which each unit now has separate Strength and Health values is provided for more tactical battles. With these new cards, a battle's winner is determined by comparing the total health minus the wounds of each side, plus any combat bonuses. Even if you can't completely kill a unit, damaging it will still have an effect on future turns.

Wisdom and Warfare also provides the rules for a new city action, "Starting a Building Program", which is a power-up for when that city produces something. This expansion also includes Social Policies that civilizations can adopt, such as "Rationalism" and "Organized Religion", and these policies offer options and provide bonuses for long-term planning.

description:
Elvis - The Game that Allows The Legend To Live On

Using tokens that include a blue suede shoe or a teddy bear, players roll and move around the board, hoping to land on enough spaces (or draw enough record-shaped cards) to earn $25 million - in a combination of cash and gold records (which are valued at $250,000 each).  Most cards list an accomplishment ("Variety declares Elvis "The King of Rock and Roll!") or event (Daughter Lisa Marie is born) in Elvis’ life.  Many cards let players collect money and/or gold records.  Others force them to pay money to the bank.  Some send them to specific spaces on the board.

Several of the spaces are named after places where Elvis spent his time (Graceland, Hollywood & Recording Studio).  There are also Army and Hawaii boxes, where (if you draw a card and get "drafted" or go on vacation) your token must stay for one or two turns.

One unique feature about the guitar-shaped game board is the "On Tour" row of spaces that make up the guitar’s neck.  Most of the ten spaces here are just the names of cities where Elvis toured and don’t offer anything of value.  But the ultimate goal is to reach the last space - Las Vegas - by rolling the exact number needed.  When a player makes it to Vegas, they immediately collect $500,000.  For their next 4 turns, as long as a 1 or 2 is rolled, the player can stay in Vegas for a "Return Engagement," collecting $500,000 each time!

Players get a "grace" period and can take out a loan if they lose all of their cash and are forced by a card to make a payment to the bank.  According to the rules, "In the spirit of Elvis’s well-known generosity, no player can be forced out of the game by going broke!"

"Corporate executives have launched an internal investigation of the alleged use of illicit security software, known as ice, by several reckless and disgruntled former employees…"
    –NBN Live at 5

Just how dark can a corporation become before it suffers the backlash? Opening Moves kicks off "Spin Cycle", the second cycle of Data Packs for Android: Netrunner - The Card Game, with a focus on bad publicity, good PR, and daring new gambits for players to undertake in their continued cyberstruggles. Its sixty cards (three copies each of twenty different cards) allow Corps and Runners to explore a wide range of innovative, new (and sometimes dirty) tactics as they battle for the control of highly valuable data!
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
The promo Invaders Pack includes twelve cards and twelve tokens, three cards and three tokens for each of the following Realms: Cherry Blossom, Fairy Forest, Bones' Island, Silver Kingdom.

You can mix the Invader Cards in the existed Realms' Deck but always keep the balance. Remove the number of the Invaders from the main Realms' Deck and add the equivalent number of the Promo Invaders in the same deck in order to play.

The Promo Invaders represent creatures of the game but in their petrified form. Their role in the game is pretty much the same as the normal Invaders but all of them have something common. To win such an Invader, you must exploit the double amount of the needed Talents.

DreadBall: The Futuristic Sports Game is the hyper-kinetic sci-fi sports game of unparalleled speed and ferocity – a fast, fun and tactical miniature sports board game written by Jake Thornton and created by Mantic Games.

Two coaches compete for victory with teams of beautiful miniatures on a stunning sci-fi pitch. The game is easy to learn, yet challenging, with carefully orchestrated plays and counter attacks hinging on positioning and the mercy of the dice gods.

This expansion adds an additional team made up of Robot Miniatures, the Chromium Chargers.

"Whenever another player is killed, take 1 gold from them and draw 1 card from the draw pile."

Are you thinking of a total makeover after watching the H&M swimsuit commercial? Are you so addicted to facebook that you must attend a twelve step program? Are you just as scared of the IRS as of hemorrhoids? Don't worry! You are not alone. You share these thoughts with the characters from "Solsidan" (a swedish sitcom) and probably with your friend, grandmother or neighbour.

Do you want to know more? Play the "Solsidan" game with friends and family to share issues, obsessions and megalomania. Be prepared to find out which of the "Solsidan" characters you resemble the most.

Read the statements and vote for one of the players. The winner is the player who know himself and his opponents the best.

Scary? The worst that can happen is that you gain some self knowledge or realizes that both you and your neighbour are pathologically fond of grills.

description: Two players have eight pieces each, called "Kibs."  In the first phase, they take turns placing pieces on a five by seven board (with the center node missing), trying to form a "KIBIK" (four pieces in a row).  A KIBIK allows a player to remove an opponent's piece.  After all pieces are placed, the second phase begins where players are allowed to slide pieces and make more KIBIKs.  When a player is reduced to four pieces a jumping move is allowed.  The player to first remove five of the opponent's pieces wins.

Theme and Victory Conditions:

Sabatu is a light strategy game of ancient conquest set in the plains of Sumer (Mesopotamia) over 4,000 years ago. There are two ways to win a game of Sabatu. You can employ a strategy of speed and be the first player to seize any 3 Capitals and to occupy all 3 of them at the same time. Or you can be the first player to build four Walls around one City. The first player to successfully implement either one of these strategies and is able to hold it until the beginning of their next turn is declared Conqueror of the Known World or as Sargon King of Akkad called himself Sarru-kinu, “The Rightful King”.

Gameplay:

     A player must begin their turn by drawing one Conquest card and immediately following the instructions on the card.
     The player then rolls 2 dice (3 if using all 9 regions), unless otherwise instructed by the Conquest card, or uses Spoils coins to move around the board toward their objectives.
     When landing on designated spaces such as “Spoils” or “Armory” draw one tile from the appropriate bag. If landing on a “Destiny” space draw one Destiny card and immediately follow the instructions.
     Position your troops, buy Walls, trade, do battle if you wish or build your forces and supplies. It’s up to you.
     When you are done pass the dice to the next player, signaling that your turn is done.


Battles:

When attempting to occupy the same space already held by another player on the board or when attacking an opponent’s City, the 2 Armies must fight a battle. After each player decides which armor or weapons they will use the players each roll one die and then add the value of the Armory tile to the roll of the die. The Army with the highest total amount is victorious. In the event of a tie, the attacker has the option of continuing the attack or retreat. If discretion is the better part of valor and retreat is the desired course of action then the attacker rolls 1 die and moves away from the defender the number of spaces indicated by the die. As long as a City has one or more walls any army within the walls has a 1 point advantage when fighting a battle, in addition to any Armory tile the defender may wish to play.

No player is eliminated from the game. If a player’s Armies have all been eliminated from the game, that player begins their next turn with a new Army at Home.

boardgame: Bolt Action: Armies of the Soviet Union
description: This supplement of Bolt Action provides players with all the information they need to field the military forces of the Soviet Union.  From the Winter War against Finland to the bitter urban warfare of Stalingrad and the final drive to Berlin, the detailed army lists provided in this supplement allow players to construct Soviet armies for any theatre and any year of World War II.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Picture an ancient legend....

The long ago a group of greedy villagers attempted to steal powerful magic and treasures from a long abandon temple. For their crimes, the unnamed God of the temple cursed them to transform, one by one, night after night, into a hideous beast that hunted the other Villagers and devour them. The curse was only ended when a single Villager remained to tell the cautionary tale. (Ancient legends don't have happy endings!)

Cursed is a quick, casual game for 3 to 8 player, ages 5 and up. The game focuses on heavily on Bluffing and features a unique cooperative/competitive gameplay style, in which players take turns becoming the "Monster", well the other players battle against the Monster as a group in an attempt to fend it off for the night. The next night, the next player in line becomes the Monster and the battle begins again. The game ends when the Monster eats the last 2 Villagers and the Curse is broken.

The game revolves around dice rolls and a small amount of card playing. Each "Villager" rolls a single die in the open, for all to see, well the "Monster" rolls an equal number of dice privately. The Monster knows if they can beat the Villagers at a glance, but must decide if he/she want to Bluff the Villagers into fleeing, tempt Villagers into a hopeless and costly battle, or simply flee if the Villagers are clearly too strong.

Add into this mix Ward Cards, magic powers that aid or hinder either side, or even disrupt the battle completely in some strange way, and which can be played be either side to keep the game endlessly unpredictable. These cards are costly though, and beyond the 3 you get at the start of the game, must be paid for WITH YOUR LIFE!

Warrior Heroes Legends is a set of rules for fantasy wargaming. The game can be played with any miniatures the player already owns. The system emphasizes group cohesion, leadership, and interactivity. The traditional turn sequence (move, attack, resolve attacks) is often interrupted by mini-sequences involving quick reactions from the miniatures in play, including those of the non-active player. This ensures that all players are involved in the action at all times.

Warrior Heroes Legends can be played competitively, cooperatively, or solitaire, and includes a detailed campaign system.

From the publisher's website:

LEGENDS AREN’T BORN, THEY’RE MADE.

And in Warrior Heroes – Legends here’s your chance to become one. You start life as an independent sell sword or perhaps an inexperienced caster of magic. But whichever you choose there’s nothing special about you as you start your story. But as the saying goes…

IT’S NOT HOW YOU START BUT HOW YOU FINISH!

In Warrior Heroes – Legends the goal is simple. Gain Fame through your exploits and Fortune through your deeds. Start as a Warrior but finish as a King? It can happen and it’s all up to you. But before you can be King let’s start a little smaller. Like leading a small band of adventurers.

Warrior Heroes - Legends includes:
Multiple character classes and races.
Over sixty Signatures and Skills to personalize your character.
An easy to use Economics System with little book keeping.
Quick and easy Magic rules.
Complete environment to adventure in.
A variety of scenarios and a how to for making more.

Colossus! is a free boardgame pitting giant monsters fighting each other on the tabletop. The game is presented as a generic ruleset, all players need is available using components they can build themselves or from other games and toys - most players won't need to buy anything they don’t own already.

The game lets players build giant monsters, called colossi, using three types of Power Cards: Abilities, Attacks and Specials. Abilities represent the inherent skills and characteristics of the colossus. Specials are the unexpected and unique moves which colossi can perform in battle. Attacks and Attack modifiers represent any weapons and ways of fighting that the colossus might have including claws, teeth, horns, laser beams, rockets, atomic breath, a sharp beak, fireballs, etc.

The game is played on a 1/600 tabletop with no hexes or squares. There are 6 types of terrain: cities, hazardous, wet, high, tricky or industrial parks. Given the huge size of the colossi, these are little more than nuisances to their inexorable advance.

The game is played in turns broken in 8 phases: Air Strike Planning (determine where they land - random), Orders (draw Orders Cards and assign them to colossi under your command), Movement (move colossi based on Order Card), Ranged Attack (use Ranged Attacks if any), Close Attack (use Close Attacks if any), Tinies (move the human defenders towards closes colossus and attack it), Air Strike Attack (roll for damage on colossi under air strike coordinates), End (remove markers, check if somebody won, etc). The game ends when a side's colossi have all been knocked out or when a scenario's victory conditions are met (the rules come with 6 scenarios)

Colossus! comes with its own setting called The Zillium Wars which is featured in little snippets across the rules. This story-world has been deliberately left vague and open to the player’s imagination. A range of possible colossi are suggested as starting points, but players are free to come up with their own concepts and model them using the cards available... or even invent their own cards! Hopefully there will be enough active players out there feeding back on the rules and how they use them so I can keep improving and expanding Colossus!

Players fill the snail board with the wooden rings. On a player's turn, the player quickly checks all the rings on the board to see if the colour underneath the ring is the same as the ring's colour. If this is the case, the player picks those rings up. Before the next player's turn, the rings are pushed together and new rings refilled from the cloth bag. At the end of the game, the player with the most rings wins.

Components: colourful snail board, 60 wooden rings in 4 colours, cloth bag, rules.

Solitaire card game, played with a standard 52 card deck.

Objective: Kill All 4 Kings.

Setup: Put 4 kings face up on the table; shuffle the deck.

Gameplay: Play begins when you draw the top card from the top of the deck.  You may then place that card beneath a king or place it in reserve.

You have 4 reserve spots which any card can be placed in (like freecell).  For each king you kill, you gain another additional reserve spot.

In future turns, you can either play a card from reserve, or from the top of the deck.

When a card is played below a king (or the bottom-most card below the king), it must be either opposite color and lower, same suit and lower, or same value as the card above it (aces are low).  Once a card is played below a king, it cannot be moved into Reserve.

Achievements for fun:
1) Renegade: Kill all 4 Kings
2) Revolutionary: Win 5 Games
3) Threvolution: Win 3 Games in a Row
4) Seven Up: Win 7 Games in a Row
5) Bastille My Heart: Win 14 Games
6) Triple Ninja: Kill All 4 Kings with the Same Value
7) Suited Up: Kill All 4 Kings with Same Suit
8) Rainbow Rebel: Kill All 4 Kings with Alternating Colors
9) Clean Kill: Win Game with No Cards Left in Reserve Spots
10) Patience: Use 14 or More Cards in the Killing of a King when winning the game
11) Show of Force: Killed All 4 Kings Only Using 4 Reserve Spots
12) Far, Far Better: Only Used 1 Reserve at A Time
