description:
Abstract game for two players. Best played on a Reversi board, can be extended over a 19x19 Goban.
rules:
1) players place alternately one piece on the board 
2) pieces on the board influence their cell and all the cells along horizontal, vertical and  diagonal lines, up to the first occupied cell included.
3) a player can occupy a cell if the number of pieces of him influencing that cell is equal or higher than that of the opponent; if the cell is empty, that player occupies it with one of his pieces, if it's occupied by the opponent, the player will change that piece with one of his pieces.
4) when a player has no more available moves, he must pass the turn to his opponent
5) game is over when both player have no more moves
6) the winner is the player with the higher number of pieces on the board when the game is over

A "pie rule" is recommender for the opening (first player has a clear advantage); otherwise, a compensation (like Komi in Go) should be used (TBD)

In the near future our planet has been devastated by a global war, which ended with the modern amenities and technology. Only a few ill-equipped factories, decimated armies and insufficient resources have been in the hands of four groups scattered over the face of the Earth. You lead one of them.

N*Go is a strategy game board where you must become the protector of the planet's resources for the survival of your people.

Starting from a situation where all forces and resources are in balance, luck has no place here. There is no win card or dice that do the work for you, every attack has a possible defense and nobody knows the strategy of the opponents.

The rules are easy and simple, all goals are in sight of achieving these objectives... heck you have to be prepared if you want to get it!

Make alliances, colonizes virally, passively awaiting development of the strategy of your opponents ... or a mixture of all this. There is no right or wrong way of doing things here, you have nothing to lose, yet is all at stake.

Game description from the publisher:

“For thousands of years after the collapse of the polar gates, with the immediate threat of Chaos thwarted, the Lizardmen existed in relative seclusion while the world changed around them.”
   –Warhammer: Lizardmen

The world of Warhammer: Invasion The Card Game has changed. Countless wars have scarred its lands. Torrents of blood have soaked its battlefields. No one is safe. No race can sit safely outside its ongoing battles. In the end, there is only war; there is no neutrality.

Hidden Kingdoms is the game's fifth deluxe expansion. Its 172 cards include four capital cards and the maximum legal number of fifty-five different player cards that bring the game’s four biggest neutral races to life as fully developed, playable factions! Players at last gain the opportunity to play as Lizardmen, Wood Elves, Skaven, and Undead. Hidden Kingdoms fleshes out these four new factions with their own capitals and legends, as well as a host of new units and supports, including several that are faction-specific.

Meanwhile, fans of the game’s other races will find additional support for their favorite factions, including new units, tactics, and Tributes, which allow the factions to forge temporary alliances and ignore the loyalty costs for out-of-faction cards.

description:
Agony is a solo word game.  The game components consist of tiny wooden (cherry-wood stain) blocks with embossed gold foil lettering, which fit perfectly in a maroon, plastic case that is the depth of only one block and opens so that only one face of each of the blocks can be seen at a time. There are 42 blocks in the set; each block has five letters on each block face with the sixth side blank.  (According to the description on the instructions, the distributions of letters are proportional based on common usage.) The object of the game is to arrange the blocks within the case to spell interlocking words (horizontally and vertically). The perfect game would show 13 words (seven words across, six words down) without using blanks. The number of blanks that are showing when the player is finished determines the score; each blank is worth one point.   Players attempt to score the lowest number possible.

There are two different sets of rules; one set is on a small slip of paper that can be removed from the case, while the other set of rules is glued to the inside, top lid.  Each set of rules has a different method of scoring, and a different set of game parameters, including different allowable word types and different descriptions of how the game is to be scored.    In one version, no prefixes, suffixes, or abbreviations can be used.

Though this is similar to a solvable word puzzle, the distribution of letters (and specifically the rules included with the game) suggests that there are multiple ways to get a perfect score and, therefore, multiple ways to win.
A Robot dice combat game with 2 leveling cards(Hit, Power), also a Combo card. Remove your opponents Life points to be the last PowerBot standing.

Its election time in Machi Koro and your mayorship is in peril. The citizens are no longer wowed by Cheese Factories and Coffee Shops. Winning reelection means going big.

Remember that bay that the cheese factories have been dumping their unsold Gouda into all these years? Your salvation lies in rehabbing that polluted body of water northeast of town. So get ready to roll up your sleeves and earn those votes.

A harbor with fancy boats and sushi bars and a shiny new airport will surely bring more gold to town and more gratitude! Sure, the city might not have the money in its coffers to pay for all this, but that never stopped you before.

The Machi Koro: Harbor Expansion injects further excitement into the game that has everyone talking! Want even more fun in your box?! Perhaps ten new establishments, one new starting establishment and two new landmarks will help?

Machi Koro the Harbor expansion requires the base game Machi Koro to play and is designed to add more variety, strategy and a 5th player to the smash hit Machi Koro.

Say hello to more variety, more nail biting and MORE players!

The Harbor Expansion is an absolute must for bonafide Machi- whizzes and newbies alike!

Machi Koro: Harbor Expansion includes cards that allow for up to five players to compete at the same time (82 cards total), as well as all the new cards from Machi Koro Plus.

description:
Played like regular picture Dominoes but you match rhyming words.

The major difference though is that the 'Start' domino has four sides. You can therefore match to four different dominoes each turn.

When you are the first to use up all the dominoes in your hand, you get one point for every domino still in your opponents' hands.

Play an agreed number of games and the one with the most points is the winner.

In the Dutch game of 'Tuintopia' you will be building a garden.

The game is a strategic game in which you and your opponents build together on the same garden, trying to score as many points as you can.

You will score points by making connections, i.e. a waterpump delivers water to a vegetable garden (which needs water to grow the vegetables) and then you gain one point.

Simple Goose-type game. Sooty sets out in his car to get to Sue's house. On the way, he must obey the special square instructions, good or bad.

Recommended for two or more players

boardgame: Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) – The Trollfens
description:
The Trollfens is an expansion for Descent: Journeys in the Dark (Second Edition) that introduces new heroes, monsters, classes, quests, and more. Heroes can discover and investigate secret rooms and rumors while the overlord spreads infection and brings a powerful new lieutenant into play: the massive troll Bol'Goreth!

The two new heroes are Roganna the Shade, a Scout of unparalleled cunning and valor who has considerable willpower and speed at her disposal, and Augur Grisom, a healer whose very presence provides solace to his allies; after all, it's rumored that he can see into the future.

In addition to new Shop and Relic cards, two new classes will enhance your character selection process. The Stalker, a class for the Scout archetype, is a master of the hunt who excels at laying traps of all kinds. The Healer class gains a Prophet, whose mind is his greatest tool; by sharing his insight with those around him, the Prophet bolsters the effectiveness of his fellow adventurers.

The new monsters include Plague Worms and Harpies, but most troublesome of all is Bol'Goreth, a single-minded killer devoted to the will of the overlord. This massive troll is capable of flattening farmhouses and devouring livestock whole. His uncanny resilience allows him to shrug off bodily ailments that would be lethal to lesser creatures, while his fits of unchecked rage make him a force to be reckoned with.

The new overlord class "Infector" features a wide selection of powers centered around spreading pestilence and disease.

description:
RoShamBo Rush is a competitive racing game. No story here. Just tighten your shoes, race, and be prepared for a nail biting finish. RoShamBo Rush was designed to be quick paced and to keep all players in the game with its system of catch-up logic. There is a very small learning curve, making it a great game to introduce newcomers to table top gaming.

As the name implies, Rock-Paper-Scissors is a large part of the game. Players duel each other using a set of cards that remain in each players possession throughout the game. Banter is encouraged. It is a good way to solicit a tell from your opponents. If you have people reading skills, they do come in handy.

The game features racers (pawns) that stand over 2" tall. The racers are adorned with helmets and body armor when a player lands on a "Armor Up" space. Armor serves as insurance for losing a battle and keeps that racer from taking hit points in exchange for a piece of armor.

Though linear like a race course, you have decisions to make on your turn. Who will you battle? When is the best time to play your Twist Cards? Nobody coasts to an easy victory here. Only the scrappiest competitors will earn a place on the winners podium which only has limited space reserved for the top three racers.

From the website:

Sock&Toss is a great game to be played by friends and families alike.
The main aim is to outwit your opponents and to create your own bean bag (hacky sack) before everyone else.
The way you play is by flipping the correct disc at the appropriate time. Only catch is that you’re not allowed to look at disc before flipping it!

Sock&Toss is a game of chance and strategy. The discs dictate all of the aspects, from what colour sock you get to which disc you flip and who you nominate to miss a turn or swap discs with or blocking them to fill their socks.  2-6 Players, age 6+.

﻿This game is 100% New Zealand made using local suppliers.

From website:

Board your safari truck for an adventure through Southern Africa to save Africa’s Big Five animals!

As a conservationist working in Southern Africa, you’ll travel to five major Reserves, sighting and researching famous wild animals known as the Big Five: the rhino, leopard, lion, elephant and buffalo.

Translation: The Wolf and the Seven Young Kids
players(kids) win if two of them reach target space; wolf wins after 6 kids are caught; with less players than 8 all seven kids are divided;

A 'Roll & Move' game; being a little goat throw the die and move forward, wolf only is throwing 2 dice; follow instructions glued into the lid ending on a marked space.

Game should had been published late 1920's/early 30's

Unbound:Endless War is a turn based area control strategy card game where you place cards onto the board face down to build the map and leave surprises for your opponent. You control a small fleet of ships and are trying to occupy your opponent's base of operations without letting yours get conquered as well. For example you may surround my base with tiles that are difficult to attack from, or place a hidden explosion hex in the path you think your opponent is going to take to destroy their troops and the nearby hexes.

Minions and Heroes is a trading card game that's built for 2-4 players.  Players will attempt to capture 5 region cards by strategically using the Minion or Hero cards in their hands before their opponents do.  The game also offers several different game types and variants as well so you and your friends can use the rules that fit your play style.

boardgame: The Battle of Changde: Operation Yo Go
description:
Issue game in Command Magazine Japan #111

On November 1943, the Japanese 11th Army began its attack with the goal to capture the Chinese city of Changde.

The Japanese rapidly achieved its goal and defeated the Chiense forces around Changde however one KMT Chinese division, the 57th "Tiger" division entrenched itself at Changde and fiercely resisted Japanese attempts at capturing Changde until Chinese reinforcements forced a Japanese retreat.

The game is a two player strategy game on the battle of Changde and shows the differences between the Japanese and KMT Chinese armies. Each army is different in ability and sequence and the game shows these differnces.

Issue game in Command Magazine Japan #110

This is a wargame simulation game of a future Japanese-Chinese naval battle over the Senkaku islands using a variant of the Red Dragon Rising/Falklands Showdown system.

'Send in the Clowns' is a quick-playing card management game in which you take on the role of a circus impresario. Your troupe of clowns have entered the Best Dressed Clown competition and are trying to assemble costumes (made up of four garments - hats, shirts, trousers, and shoes) that match in colour (red, yellow, green, blue, purple, or pink) and pattern (stripes, checks, spirals, or stars).

But just as fast as your three clowns gather their matching clothes, so your rival troupes swap clothes in your wardrobe with clothes from their wardrobe!

Each clown can wear accessories (red noses and blue buckets) to enhance its score, which is based on the longest run in each category (colour, pattern, and accessories): 1, 4, 9, 16 VPs. Each game, one colour and one pattern scores one step higher than the others.

You can also add balloons, unicycles, tightropes, and juggling balls to extend your scoring!

Each player turn involves 3 main actions: 1) draw a card; 2) draw another card -or- swap a card -or- play a card; 3) play a card. You can only play cards if one of their characteristics matches the adjacent card. And you can only swap a card it it matches the one you are swapping it for. An optional extra action at the end of your turn allows you to discard a card to claim an accessory (nose or bucket).

From the box...
Enchantingly different! The Magic of "Make-Believe" delights Children as they tour the Domain of the WONDROUS WIZARD on a King-size Game-board.

In a Race to reach the Castle, short cuts through the Enchanted Forest lead to a Dragon, Spectre Trees, Witches and the Giant! Moves are indicated by a Spinner. Plastic Pawns mark Progress.

Tops in Imaginative Fun for 2-3-4 Players, surprise Messages from the THE WONDROUS WIZARD help decide who wins the Game.

Trojan Cross is played on a 13 x 13 grid of red and black squares that features a “Trojan’s Cross” in the center (and adjoining four squares) of the board.  All the corner squares of the board are red.  The game’s pieces consist of 20 red and 20 black checkers, each with a pawn on one side, a crown on the other.  The game is played on the black squares of the board, with players’ pieces beginning on the black squares of the three closest rows.  Players may begin with any combination or formation of pawns and crowns, so long as they are identical to each other, though the instructions do provide three recommended ways to start: three rows of pawns, or one row of crowns and two of pawns, or two rows crowns and one of pawns.  Black moves first.

With the exception of the cross in the middle of the board, player movement is the same as an ordinary game of Checkers.  Pawns may move forward (left or right) one square only unless jumping and capturing an opponent’s piece, when they may jump however many times there are openings.  Crowns may move and jump forward and backward.  Captured pieces are removed from the board.  Crowning of pawns takes place upon reaching the opponent’s home row, or after moving over any four squares in the Trojan’s Cross.

The game’s instructions feature a number of diagrams describing what movements a pawn may make within the cross, how it may exit, what conditions it must meet to be crowned, how the opponent must be notified, and how the opponent may interrupt.  The game is won by capturing all of an opponent’s pieces, or by blocking all of an opponent’s pieces from moving, or by making seven uninterrupted moves within the cross.  Methods are provided for scoring, should players desire it for multiple matches, but the simplest method provided is one point for one victory.

Card Game

Description 
 "O'Grady's Goat: A Merry Game" card game with 89 strips of uncut playing cards and an instruction sheet in a paper covered cardboard box; box lithographed with the head of a smiling goat breaking through a globe with a banner around its center with images of goats pulling carts which you must try and pair up into appropriate related catagories,  box inscribed, "O'GRADY'S/ GOAT/ A Merry Game/ MILTON BRADLEY CO. SPRINGFIELD MASS".
 Marks on the game box
 stamped: on box cover: "O'GRADY'S/ GOAT/ A Merry Game/ MILTON BRADLEY CO. SPRINGFIELD MASS".

Argh! is a press-your-luck pirate-themed card game where players randomly draw face-down cards spread out in a center playing area. The goal of the game is to collect treasure cards and score points. A player only scores points when they decide to end their turn and the total number of points awarded to the player is based on the number of treasure cards they have in front of them. Some treasure only scores points when the player has 3 or more of the same type. Included in the cards are 8 Pirates that will steal all scorable treasure from the player if drawn before they end their turn. There are also a few cards that allow a player to steal treasure from their opponents.

The winner of the game is the first to collect a set number of points (which is based on the number of players at the table).

Say Uncle is a card game where all the players are attempting to spell the word "UNCLE" in front of their opponents.The game comes with 80 cards, which contains Letter cards (that have the letters needed to spell UNCLE) and Action cards that allow the player to move and discard Letter card in front of them. If a player has the word "UNCLE" spelled in front of them at the end of their turn, they are out of the game. The last player remaining wins.

Ever wanted to see your favorite Warriors battle each other? This is the game for you! Pick from 8 fierce and mighty Warriors for the ultimate battle to the death.

Game Objective: Pick three Warriors and Battle to the Death!
- Use energy for movement and perform special abilities unique to each character.

Starting the Game: Each player rolls a die; highest die starts the game. Game play continues with player on left.

Winning the Game: Player with the last Warrior standing is the winner!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
From the publisheräs website:
Celebrity Throw Down! is the game where trendy stars, pop culture icons and historical legends face off in the ultimate game of elimination. All players select what they think are the best celebrities for an ordinary or outlandish, “ripped from the tabloid headlines” situation. If your celebrity is the one to survive the multiple elimination rounds and is the last one standing, you win! For example, one player draws a situation card that reads “Who is the best celebrity to … play Truth or Dare?” Each player chooses and submits a Celebrity Card from their hand of four cards. Maybe Jennifer Aniston, Kanye West, Honey Boo Boo and Marilyn Monroe are competing in this round. Players now take turns eliminating the celebrity least suited for the situation until just one remains, and a winner is crowned. With almost 300 celebrities and 162 situations, the possibilities are endlessly entertaining!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The aim of the game is to think of things that other players cannot guess. Players draw cards that provide a category, and having thought of (and written down) something either related or not related to the card the player to their left and each subsequent player has 60 seconds to guess what it is. Questions may be asked, but can only be answered with a Yes, No, or Maybe. If no one is able to guess the answer the player moves forward on the game board. The first player to reach the Finish wins the game.

Contents:
6 Tokens, 60 Second Timer, 384 EyeSpy Cards, Playing Board, Magic Pad & Plastic Pencil, Instructions and Free EyeSpy tee-Shirt & Tournament Application Form.

Island Conquest is a network building game for 2 to 6 players. Players start with an equal number of islands under their control and using resource management and well planned strategy, battle their way toward building the largest collection of islands.

The larger and more connected an island, the more powerful and influential it may become, providing additional resources per turn and a stronger base from which to launch attacks.

The gameboard is designed to be played in two different formats, allowing for faster gameplay and even starting points with both the full board being utilized for 5 or 6 players, and a smaller portion of the board sectioned off for less players. So ready your troops, gather your resources and start building bridges.

Fight to the end and fight the urge to retreat. This is your quest.

A backgammon-style game where the squares on the board give bonuses.

Each player has 7 double-sided tokens (angels). The object is to initiate, move, and clear a set number tokens from the board. (The number depends on how fast you want the game to go.) The game board consists of a number of squares each depicting a Celtic motif (birds, monks, beasts, etc.) and having various bonuses. For example, if an angel lands on the Fish square, the player can move his or her other angel 4 squares forward. If an angel lands on an occupied square, the player gets no bonus. If an angel lands on the opponent's angel going in the same direction, that angel is removed from the board and has to start over.

The board is based on a wood engraving by Randy Miller and is inspired by the Book of Kells.

The angels are round wooden tokens with the angel engraving on one side and the Celtic knot on the other.

The dice used to determine the move are stone, metal,or wood d4 with 2 tops marked in green or red (depending on the edition).

Summer is here, which -- for the kingdoms of Feorhyrna -- means it's time to raid the strawberry patch! In all the world, no item is more sought after than the Feorhyrnan strawberry: wars have been waged and kingdoms have been lost all in an effort to possess its mysterious, magical fruit.

But that was last year (things are more civilized now); instead of war, the kingdoms of Feorhyrna have agreed to send eight champions to the royal strawberry patch to collect this year's harvest. It's first-come-first-serve, though, and there are no rules about how to divvy up the fruit...but they'll work on that next year. In the meantime, it's time to bust some skulls and grab some strawberries!

Royal Strawberries is a whimsical game of tactics and strategy in which teams of fabulous characters navigate a garden maze and fight for the fabled fruit of Feorhyrna. When the last strawberry is plucked (or the Royal Bazaar is exhausted), the game ends and each kingdom tallies up its victory points (from strawberries and other bonuses): the kingdom with the most points wins!

From publisher:
Long Story Short is a hit party game that mixes social media with the time-tested fun of storytelling. Each round, the Storyteller shares an experience or story with the group using the fun story starters. The storyteller tells a story that is either true or false. Players then make the long story short by creating a headline, drawing a picture, writing a text, or crafting a tweet for the story told according to the dice roll. Then story-teller scores his favorite short stories.

Player with the most points at the end of the game is the winner.

description:
Game description from the publisher:

During its original mission, the Enterprise, Captain Kirk, and his intrepid crew visited many strange new worlds and discovered many new life-forms and civilizations. Now you and your friends can boldly go and explore this galaxy, too, with the Star Trek: Catan - Federation Space Map Set, which includes two maps depicting the Known Galaxy.

Based on the "Explored Galaxy" map seen in Kirk's quarters itself, these maps allow you to settle the famous worlds of Federation Space using pieces from your Star Trek: Catan base game.

Components
2 full color maps depicting the “Explored Galaxy” known as Federation Space.
4 individual scoring tracks—1 for each player
4 sets of scoring markers—2 for each player
18 Victory Point tokens—New ways to score
1 Almanac with 1 page of rules and 20 pages of planet and episode descriptions.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: 5 Pillars: Conquer the pillars of Islam
description:
Increase your knowledge of the pillars of Islam while having loads of fun with your family and friends. Strategise and rotate your Pillar Tokens in order to build the pillars of Iman, Salah, Zakah, Sawm and Hajj. Conquer all pillars at five difficulty levels before the other team to win! Along the way, cite references from the Quran and Hadith for extra Merit Points so you learn more about Islam with every flip of the card.

Play for a couple minutes or all night long, but for the best learning experience don’t stop until you have conquered all the pillars of Islam. This is the Ultimate Islamic Board Game Experience and can be played separately or together with the 5Pillars Fun Box!

Components included: 1 game box, 1 game board, 2 punch cards with 25 pillar tiles each, 1 punch card with 5 pillar tokens, 1 vacuum tray, 750 cards, 1 instruction manual

In this snarky game of competitive philanthropy, you are a genius billionaire philanthropist who has modestly taken on the task of single-handedly saving humankind from an apocalyptic tsunami. Over the course of the game, you build a fleet of ships, collect rare artifacts of civilization, and recruit a team of skilled workers to your crew. At the end of the game, the tsunami hits and only careful preparation, your boundless genius, and a hint of luck will keep you afloat.

Tsunami! combines snark, strategy, and easy-to-learn rules, making it ideal for both introductory gamers as well as seasoned board game geeks. The game takes place over 10-14 days (turns) in which each player strives to optimize their fleet by competing for limited resources, bidding on crew members, managing cargo, and mitigating the risk of the impending apocalypse.

Do you have what it takes to become the preeminent savior of the human race?
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Do you like Trivia with an Islamic twist? Do you jiggle and wobble? Can you draw with your eyes closed? How long can you jabber without a stammer? How many teasers can your brain hammer? Could you do all of this before time runs out? If so, this fun family game was made just for you! With 500 cards full of Islamic Trivia and Challenge activities you can expect to engage in plenty of teamwork that will have you rolling in laughter! This game can be played alone or together with the 5Pillars Board Game.

Components included: 1 game box, 1 sand-timer, 1 activity coin, 1 vacuum tray, 500 cards, 1 instruction manual

In Whiskey Traders, you recreate the history of the Wild West from 1873 to 1890.

Whiskey Traders is best described as a crossover game with elements from the Euro, Wargame, Ameritrash and Historical Simulation genres.

You will play three factions simultaneously. Each side has their own agenda.

1. On the Canadian (CDN) side you will play the Northwest Mounted Police (NWMP) trying to build Forts and establish peaceful relations with the First Nations (FN) people. Canadian victory points score double if the Canadian Pacific Railway (CPR) track completes.

2. On the United States (US) side, you will play the US Army who are trying to build Forts and move the FN tribes to Reservations.  You will also play the American Whiskey Traders who are building Whiskey Posts on both sides of the border in an attempt to swindle the FN tribes out of precious Bison Hides. US victory points score double if the Bison become extinct.

3. You will also play a First Nation or Métis Tribe. They are trying to survive, maintain tradition, and adjust to the growing European settlement. FN victory points score double if the 10 card Event Deck completes before the CPR reaches its final destination or before the Bison become extinct.

A fourth end game condition exists where you score an immediate individual victory if you manage to be the only FN tribe who hasn’t been forced onto a Reservation.

Which faction will shape history and win the Western frontier?
family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada

description:
Junior version of the Silhouette

60 tiles with with various child-oriented silhouette images on one side. These are placed on the table, face down. The active player flips over two tiles and tries to make a compound word out of the two images. If successful, the player keeps the pair and goes again. If not, the tiles are returned and it's the next player's turn.

When all tiles are claimed (or there are no other possibilities), the player with most tiles is the winner.

Al Djemma has a two-step objective to win the game.  The first objective is to move around the outer court until the player has an opportunity to move his token in safety through the treasure maze of any of his opponents with the object of landing on any one of  the opponent's 5  treasure circles, thus capturing an opponent's treasure.
  The final objective of the game is TO MAKE AL DJEMMA.  This occurs when a player, having secured treasure pieces for his tokens, and having entered the inner court, succeeds in landing by an even throw of the dice on one of the three circles of his own color within the inner court .

The Tactical Bomber is a new set of boards, cards and tokens to further expand the Gunship galaxy.  Intended for 4-Player battles, the Bomber will offer massive firepower options at the expanse of being slower and less repairable than a Gunship.

Tactical Bomber Boards are landscape in nature and feature 10 Hit Locations, including Wings and a customizable Rear Turret.  With 3 Bomb Bays, a multitude of payloads will be available, including high-explosive Nova Bombs and Plasma Bombs, which include a new Fire Mechanic.

This set will also provide a new Attacker/Defender system to the game.  One team will be defending a Planet with their Carrier, Gunship and Fighters.  The Planet will be the site of a powerful Fortress, which must be assaulted by the Attackers.

The Attackers will have a Carrier, Gunship, Tactical Bomber and their own Fighter Squadrons.  The mission objective is the destruction of the Fortress below and team tactics and strategy will be paramount.

Bombing Objectives will be a major part of the upcoming Storyline Mode.  An abandoned Fortress can be seen in the Gunship: Afterburners expansion.

New Crewmates relative to the Bomber and Fortress will be announced during the July Kickstarter and available for purchase.

Be the fastest to connect your brain to your hand. Slap the correct colored card in this lightning fast game as you twist and test your ability to connect what you hear with how quickly you can react! The first player to collect 8 Rapid Reflex cards wins!

The Stranger
The Stranger is a new hero for "Legends of Andor". His special feature is, that you can design him by yourself. You decide which name, special ability and colour he has.

Guardians Chronicles is a superhero-themed miniatures game in which you play as one of the members of the Liberty Patrol or as the group's arch-nemesis, Professor Skarov.

With the Guardians Chronicles Kick-starter, a number of Kick-starter exclusive content items were added to the game:

The following Heroes And Villains were produced in limited quantities and given out at Essen or other avenues. They are shown above in the Product Image.
New Heroes (each has a miniature, an ID card, and cards to play rookie, standard, and veteran versions of the character)

     Antaean
     Miss de Green
     Killeroo


New Villain (has a miniature and an ID card)

     Lady Montagu

Folium is a minimalist abstract game for 2 players, inspired by Babylon.

You only need a blank sheet, a pen and scissors to play.

Cut the sheet in nine fragments. Place them on the table, three rows of three fragments each. Each fragment is called a pile of fragments. On each turn a player may:
-mark a fragment with a symbol (cross or circle; players are not limited to only one symbol during a game), or
-move a pile of fragments onto an adjacent pile of fragments already marked with different symbol.

The game ends when one of the players can’t make an action. The other player wins the game.

boardgame: Ascension: Miles, Golden Prodigy Promo Card
description:
Miles, Golden Prodigy was created by Jacob McCarthy, immortalazing his son Miles, for the Ascension Immortal tournament winner on June 2013.

Text on card:
Miles, Golden Prodigy - 5 Runes
Enlightened Hero
"Draw a card. When you play a card of another faction this turn, draw a card. This happens only once per faction."

A Campaign for Warhammer Fantasy produced by Forge World featuring the chaos lord Tamurkhan, Nurgle's most favoured son, and his army as they march against the Empire. There are 6 scenarios that are unique to this book. The entire campaign can consist of 6 seasons of randomly rolled scenarios pulled from the 6 in the book, from the main Rulebook, or from Storm of Magic. The campaign can be played by two players, or can be played by a huge group of players, such as a gaming community.

There is also additional rules for units for "Warriors of chaos" and "the Empire", and a new list for "Chaos Dwarfs".

Gives rules for new monsters and creatures for Warhammer Fantasy Battle. The rules are based on the "Storm of Magic: binding scrolls" rules.

There is also rules for more scenarios and a campaign based on monsters.

Monopoly with a Hewlett-Packard theme.

As you go around the board, you encouter the 22 steps in the Supply Chain. You'll build Pallets and Containers (houses and hotels). Tokens represent various industries, such as Automotive and Pharmaceutical.

The most famous and powerful warrior of Mythic Greece is now ready to join your army!

Heracles is a Hero card with following effect: "This card can be played in a combat, immediately after players have declared the use of Talents by their Units. Until the end of the turn, the targeted Infantry Unit gains +3 to CC Attack, and Titans which are attacked or counter-attacked by thisUnit suffer a -1 penalty to Defense."

boardgame: Panzer Grenadier (Modern): 1967 – Sword of Israel: The Six Day War
description:
From the Avalanche Press website:

In June 1967, Israeli forces struck first against the Egyptian, Syrian and Jordanian forces massing on their borders for a final invasion of the Jewish state. For six days, battle raged along all fronts with the Israelis inflicting massive defeats on their Arab foes before the United Nations brokered a cease-fire agreement.

1967: Sword of Israel is a military history board game based on the battles of the 1967 Six-Day War, in which Israel held off and then defeated the combined armies of Egypt, Syria, Jordan and the Palestine Liberation Organization. There are fifty scenarios, portraying each of the important battles that took place in this war that set the course of modern history.

The game system is based on the very popular Panzer Grenadier system, and will be instantly familiar to anyone who’s played any of the games in that series. Units represent platoons of tanks and infantry, batteries of artillery and anti-tank or anti-aircraft guns, and flights of three to five helicopters or aircraft. Designer John Stafford has provided fifty scenarios from the Six Day War. There are eight new maps by Guy Riessen, and 909 playing pieces.

description: Not every score in the Gunship universe is settled on the battlefield.  Each season, the best of the best Gunner crews are selected for the Gunship Death Race.  This gladiator-style tournament features a deadly obstacle course that must be navigated as the combatants do their very best to destroy each other.  Weaving through Asteroids, sprinting through open space and then diving into a Trench on the moon below, each Gunship must find a way to not only be the first to complete laps, but also to survive loads of enemy fire!  There can be only one Winner – the victorious crew earns glory and instant fame among warriors and civilians alike.  The losing teams get sent back into the war, IF they survive!

description:
The primary focus of the Dark Horse: Rebels & Rogues expansion is the addition of a Rebel & Rogue character card deck, a Progress card deck, as well as a Hidden Agenda card deck. The overall goal of the expansion is to inject a heavy dose of historical theme into the Dark Horse game as well as offer additional game play options.

The expansion introduces the concept of players having a specific Reputation. This is done through various game mechanics and cards by offering two distinct options based on Reputation tokens, i.e., White Hat or Black Hat. The Wild West was characterized by famous heroes, villains, lawmen and outlaws. Players will now be able to choose a side and hire either classic White Hat or Black Hat type characters. The good guys, called Rebels, have abilities that assist players in accomplishing goals. The bad guys, called Rogues, can harass opponents and even rob from them. Players can opt to exclude Rogues or add in a variant that allows characters to initiate gunfights and capture Rogues.

Reputation adds in a thematic decision that can affect player’s options in many of the expansion variants. For example, additional Action Cards will be introduced that will have two options on them. Players will only be able to select the option that corresponds to their Reputation. So, if a player chooses to side with Outlaws then they would only have access to “Black Hat” abilities.

The expansion will also offer players additional Action Cards, Player Characters, and Special Roles.

boardgame: Raiding Parties: Brethren Of The Coast
description:
Raiding Parties: Brethren Of The Coast is an expansion for Raiding Parties (AKA Raiding Parties: The Golden Age of Piracy). It includes 51 game cards, 3 Party List cards and a rule book.  The expansion introduces two new card types :"Land" cards, which work similarly to fort cards; and One Timers, which may be used at any time.

The cards included are:

Pirate* (5)
Brethren* (5)
Swashbuckler* (5)
Privateer* (3)
Pirateer* (3)
Lucky Parrot (3)
Lookout (3)
Master Gunner (3)
Pirate Chief (3)
Coastlands (2)
Dueling grounds (1)

Lieutenant (2)
Skipper (2)
Raiders (2)
Flotilla (2)
Quicksilver (1)
Liberty (1)
Schooner (1)
Pirate Ship (1)
Captaine Redd (1)
Parly List (3)


    Cards marked with an asterisk are duplicates of cards from the base game.

Everything your Granny shouldn't know about grapes or hops or sherry parties or bananas and other totally obscure alcoholic trivia that nobody really gives an Aussie XXXX about unless they're more than schlightly sqwiffy.

This is a game of totally obscure and useless trivia. Nobody knows the answers and nobody probably cares. The idea is to bluff and counter-bluff to avoid collecting the green bottles. Collect nine green bottles and you're out!

Players attempt to correctly answer all ten trivia questions from one card before their time runs out (There is a DVD timer included in the game). The first nine correct answers are worth $10 each. If all questions are answered correctly, the player pockets a total of $1,000 from the Bank. The player with the most money at the end of the game wins!

This classic game of skill can be learned in seconds, but it offers a fun test of hand-eye coordination that is challenging to people of all ages and skill levels. 10 metal balls must be skillfully maneuvered to the correct hole (only the central hole counts). You are rated for your efforts, from 1 ("You're Not Trying") to 10 ("You're Champ").

A roll and move game where the first player to get all four of their jetliners to their destinations wins the game.  Along the way players may be impeded in their journey by various hazard cards that represent the real and perceived perils of the times in which this game was designed such as hijackings, bomb threats, UFO encounters, and the devil's triangle.

Elemental Strife is a chess style semi abstract game for two players that take turns playing on a board of 10 by 10 squares, 32 of which are randomly covered up with one of the elements, being 8 of each type, which configures a different scenario every encounter.

Prior to start each player chooses one of the elements to play with: earth, water, fire and air. Having each faction its own characteristics. For example: air elementals are faster, and can pass through other element spaces, while earth elementals are very hard to capture.

Each team has 15 pieces in four levels, higher level (4) are stronger and dangerous but have less movement capability. Each player starts with 5 of those elementals, but can create new ones during the course of the game.

boardgame: Mage Wars: Meditation Amulet Promo Card
description:
Promo card given away at Origins 2013.
Add 3 mana to your mana supply.

Non foil stamped version was released in the Druid vs Necromancer Expansion (4 copies)

description:
No one denies that Kaosball is a deadly game.  Teams are expensive to build, train and maintain.  The Malltown Zombies have a rather ... unique solution to the problem.  As long as they're well paid in brains, everything should be ok?

This is a Kickstarter and convention exclusive team!

"Hah! You like that? Don't pretend you're not impressed."

Zorra’s not happy unless she’s got her team standing beside her and the bodies of their opponents heaped around them.

Kickstarter and convention exclusive Ringer.

Keyflower: The Farmers is an expansion for the game Keyflower. In that game, each player develops his own unique village over four seasons by successfully bidding on a variety of village tiles (specialized buildings and boats), skills, resources, and workers ("keyples").

In Keyflower: The Farmers, players develop the agricultural side of their economy by acquiring new farm buildings, growing wheat, and collecting and breeding farm animals (cows, pigs, and sheep). Animals are kept in the fields created by the layout of the roads, adding a new dimension and considerations to the base game. Points can now be scored through the acquisition and breeding of the animals, by harvesting wheat, and from the layout of the fields.

Players may choose to use all of the tiles from Keyflower: The Farmers and add tiles at random from Keyflower to make up the required number of tiles or simply mix the two sets of tiles.

Ex-pirate scum turned major league heroes? The Greenmoon Smackers bring a wealth of violent tricks and brute force to the arena floor in a spectacular demonstration of brawn over brain!

The Greenmoon Smackers are an Orx and Goblin Marauder expansion team for DreadBall.  This boxed set provides 3 Orx Guards and 5 Goblin 'Jacks for your DreadBall teams.

ClaudeSoucie is a variant on Lines of Action, where the object is to be the first player to get all their pieces into one group.  ClaudeSoucie differs from Lines of Action several different ways:
1. The game is played is playable on a 10x10 board, or larger or smaller size.
2. One player sets up all the pieces around the outside of the board, and the other player then picks what color they will play.
3. Pieces that would normally be removed from play are put at the bottom of the piece that is capturing the piece in the stack.  By doing this, a capture creates a stack of pieces containing 2 or more pieces.  A single piece can move on top of a stack of pieces to control the entire stack.
4. The player who is the first player to have all their pieces in one group at the start of their turn wins, as opposed to immediately, as is the case with Lines of Action.

ClaudeSoucie was invented by Donald Silberger.

This is an educational card game for teaching materials science to people.  This game can be played by 2 or more players.  Shuffle the cards and deal them face down.  Each player turn the top card face up but in a way that others don't see the card.  Each card present a material with 6 attributes.  The player to the left of the dealer picks one attribute from the top card and reads its number, eg. density 2320.  Other players read out their respective attribute.  The highest value on the attribute collects all other top cards and place them in their pile.  Then it's the winner's turn to turn the top card and to pick an attribute.  In the case of a tie in the attribute value, these cards are placed in the centre of the table and the main player gets to pick another card and to choose an attribute.  The winner gets to take the cards from both rounds.

Quarq is a card game system. You can play an unlimited number of games with a Deck of Quarqs.

Quarq games are simple.  The rules for each fit on a single card.

Fundamentally, Quarq is a deck of 72 cards, consisting of

     60 Quarq cards, each a unique "3-D" combination of 3 of the 12 suits
     12 suit cards, which are used in some games


Quarq comes packaged with 14 games, including

     5 adaptations of top traditional card games
     4 brand new strategy card games
     3 novel stand-up games
     2 poker games


...and many more Quarq games added by YOU, the community, at quarq.org.

Quarq games range from 
twists on traditional games like Bridge, Hearts, Concentration, to
radically new games that could not be played with a traditional deck of cards, like Psychics, Trade Routes, Conquistaspores.  Some are designed for 2 young kids; others are for 15 abstract thinkers.

Quarq games are more radically diverse than traditional card games.  They use almost any other mechanic.

As it is a game system, Quarq is also a creative medium.  
At quarq.org, players can create new games, or post reviews of existing Quarq games.  They earn points for their participation.

Munchkin Tricky Treats is a 15-card booster that contains Munchkin cards with a Halloween theme.

It's Halloween, and you know what that means – it's time to dress up, kill monsters, and take their sweet, sweet CANDY!!!! Put on your Zombie Costume, grab your Favorite Machete, and show that Sparkly Vampire who's boss! But be careful . . . you may fall prey to The Prim Reaper or the worst Halloween fate of all . . . Healthy Snacks!

description:
From the publisher's website:
""The Romans knew not…how deeply the greatness of their own prosperity and the fate of the whole Western world were involved in the destruction of the fleet of Athens in the harbor of Syracuse.” Arnold

Say what? The ‘fate of the whole Western world?’ Syracuse? How?

Simple. If the Athenians had taken Syracuse, during their invasion of 415 BCE, they would have captured Sicily and become a major superpower. They would dominate the Italian boot as well, and make it much harder for a city-state named Rome to expand.

Maybe one of the oddest things about the lengthy Peloponnesian War is that, after decades of balance, the Athenians actually lost it with a campaign well away from the Greek mainland.

The Athenians have a mighty host of fleets and men, filled with confidence. The Syracusans have a long history of clinging to their independence (and powerful allies coming to foil Athens).

Combat units include cavalry, light troops, peltasts, and the heavily armed hoplites—the backbone of both sides’ armies. You the players can decide your best approaches for attack and defense, selecting from pitched battles or skirmishes…or building miles of siege walls.

The Siege of Syracuse includes:
One full color, 11” x 17” mounted mapboard
One 8.5” x 11” battle board
176 large, full color, die-cut counters
12 page rulebook

Can you extend the Golden Age of Athens or maintain the freedom of the most powerful city in the mid Mediterranean?

Part of the TPS "Decisive Battles" series

That means straightforward rules, key insights into the history behind the game, and designs aimed at one-session conclusions and high replay value. "
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: The Battle of The Metaurus Northern Italy 207 BC
description:
From the publisher's website:
"As people think over the actions of Hannibal in the 2nd Punic War, many great battles come to mind…Cannae, Zama, Trebia, Lake Trasimene…

Yet, Creasy chose The Metaurus as the decisive battle for the entire war. How can that be? Hannibal was not even there!

But his brother was there, in command of an army sent to reinforce Hannibal and give him the strength and stamina to finish off the Romans. By this time (207 BCE), the Romans were mostly avoiding head-to-head battles with Hannibal, and he mostly lacked the cavalry strength to force them to fight where he wanted them to. When the Romans intercepted messengers Hasdrubal sent to alert Hannibal to his arrival, they realized how critical it was to keep the brothers from joining together, and rushed an overwhelming force to surprise and destroy the new army. And won.

Now, see if you can change or maintain the history.

The Romans have numbers and overall troop quality on their side. Hasdrubal has a wide range of troops, from crack heavy infantry and light cavalry from Africa to poor quality Celtic levies. And, they have the superior position. The game calls on both players to “do better” than their historical counterparts. Richard Berg’s design emphasizes morale and the importance of getting troops to execute the right commands.
The Battle of the Metaurus includes:
One 11” x 17” mounted game-map
200 die-cut mounted counters
8 page Rules Booklet

Part of the TPS "Decisive Battles" series

That means straightforward rules, key insights into the history behind the game, and designs aimed at one-session conclusions and high replay value. "

boardgame: Quest: Zeit der Helden – Abenteuerband 4: Die Augen der Echse
description:
This is an expansion for Quest: Angriff der Orks. To play this adventure you will need the base game.

Quest is a RPG-like boardgame and this booklet provides an additional introductory adventure for two players, featuring the lizard shaman Kr'zzzt.

Chung Ling Soo was a very talented stage magician who was shot and killed during last nights performance.  You play a police detective who will try to bring in more evidence than the other players.  The game board is made up of 12 hex tiles which make up a map of London.  You may move your pawns around a maze of streets, back alleys and even make use of the Underground train system. The three interior tiles have police stations, the nine outside tiles have evidence locations.  The object of this game is to score the most victory points.  Victory points are earned by moving your "police" pawn around the game board to various named evidence locations, acquiring the evidence and returning the evidence to one of three police stations.  When you attempt to find the piece of evidence you decide how valuable it will be, the more valuable the harder it is to find.  During your game turn you also move one of nine "bad" guy pawns.  When moved adjacent to another players pawn you may pay the "bad" guy to attempt to steal various items from the player (money, tools, or evidence).  The game ends when evidence from all differently named nine evidence tiles have been turned in by any combination of players.

NooPolitik is a non-collectable table- top card game designed by Ben Drain and published through the print-on-demand site thegamecrafter.com . 
Players take on the role of one of 12 countries attempting to make a mark on history. As such, the theme of the game is historical and political. Players interact with each other through the spheres of political, military, clandestine, and economic pools of resources, using 3 different bidding systems. When a player accomplishes a feat such as bringing a new achievement into the world, or expunging someone else’s, they score a card towards victory, playing to 21 points.
The game contains 12 unique country cards, 96 unique action cards, 104 unique achievements, 18 multicolored non-standard dice, and a large collection of resource tokens  representing the resources of the game (labor, steel, plastics, unrest, etc), and 50 glass beads used for bidding.

description:
You are a cunning and dastardly spy.  Deny it, you scoundrel! Use your skills of wit and observation to discover the other spies’ identities and expose them before they expose you. The winner is the last remaining spy.

Logical Spies unfolds like a social logic puzzle, in which the players play the role of a spy.  Your identity is represented by 6 cards: Nationality, Codename, Cover, Vulnerability, Secret Weapon, and special Agent ability.

Each round begins with the Spying phase and ends with the Accusations phase.  Spying is fast and simultaneous.  Recover your cards as quickly as you can while observing what other players do.  Accusations is calculating and methodical.  Consult your Logic Sheet.  Accuse other players until you make a false accusation.  As you make correct accusations, players must reveal that card.
When your entire identity is revealed, you are out of the game.

The 12 Agent cards add a special ability.  The Enigma may lie about his Codename.  The Assassin may wink at players to make them reveal a card.  The Die Hard gets 1 final round of accusations upon elimination...

(From the publisher's web site)  Of all the battles in the American Revolution, Saratoga was the one that really gave the Patriots a belief that they could win—and convinced the outside world (starting with France) that the rebels were worth supporting. Take away the victory at Saratoga, and we can only wonder how much more time would have passed before the French alliance happened…if ever.

Now you can explore the two main battles of Saratoga, both Freeman’s Farm and Bemis Heights, as a growing storm of colonial militia and regulars, commanded by Gates but really led by Arnold, turned “Gentleman Johnny’s” adventure into a disaster.

Rob Markham’s design gives life to the combatants, as players wrestle with command-control, fog-of-war, and troop quality ratings that mean a larger force may actually be weaker.
The Battle of Saratoga contains:
One 11” by 17” mounted map sheet
One set of 200 die cut mounted counters
One 8 page rulebook
Two Six-Sided Dice

description:
Available for free on the IronWall Games website.

Viking Funeral makes use of decks of standard playing cards that would otherwise be disposed of.  Like "Risk: Legacy" by the same designer, permanent changes are made to the cards throughout the course of play, and these changes will persist into future plays of the game using the same set of cards.

This game is played with a 52-card deck (French-suited) that you supply yourself, and a marker or pen to write on the cards, and optionally, a pair of scissors or matches or something to destroy cards with, and if available, a trash can to serve as "Valhalla".

A single card is drawn randomly at the start of a game and destroyed, but not to the point of its number and suit being unrecognizable.  This becomes the "recently deceased".

Players start with a hand of cards, with the lead player playing a single card to the center of the table ("the funeral") that are either in agreement (same suit color) or start a fight (differing suit colors).

Tricks are won by playing the high card when in agreement, or by winning fights (being form the same family as the recently deceased provides a bonus in a fight).  The winner takes the trick and puts the cards into that player's victory point pile ("mead hall").  Cards are drawn by the player who did not win cards for their mead hall from the deck ("the village").

Throughout play, players are encouraged to either sing the praises of the "recently deceased" or trash-talk the card, treating each suit as it were an viking family line.  The game ends when no more cards are left in the village to replenish players' hands, and the game is won by the player with the most cards in their mead hall, with a tie breaker going to the player whose mead hall cards have the most wounds (as indicated by marks made to cards during play).

In true viking fashion, the game strongly emphasis that there are no ties, either during play or in calculating victory.

A Fantasy Island-Building Game for 2 - 4 Tribal Leaders by Andreas Propst & Tobias Strunz.

“In the middle of a medieval fantasy archipelago, rival tribes are building their settlements on neighbouring islands. You are the clan-chief of one of these tribes and responsible for your village. Build and expand your settlement, care for your followers and nurture your clan. Finally you will be able to build a massive army or achieve cultural superiority. Whatever path to victory you choose - the fantasy islands await you!”

The goal of the game is to be the first player to collect 20 Victory Points. Each player starts with a tiny Starting Island made out of 7 hex tiles. Players will expand their islands in order to mine stone, ore and gather wood needed to build the more advanced buildings. Players will grow food and wine to keep their soldiers and wizards loyal and collect mana to cast powerful Spells.

In Fantasy Islands, many roads lead to victory: Pure Military force, Naval supremacy, securing the Trade monopoly, keeping your population happy, Cultural dominance or Spellcasting power. Excelling at one or more of these aspects will net you the VP needed to win and furthermore, players will be able to engage in Conflicts (Military, Trade, Sorcery, Cultural and Naval) which, in case they emerge victorious, will get them certain benefits.

This game is a cooperative effort by Andreas Propst (Austria) and Tobias Strunz (Germany).

From Publisher's Site:

Lead your team to victory on the bloodstained pitches of the greatest sport ever played!

With a host of new models, new mechanics and exciting abilities, the Season 2 Rulebook takes DreadBall to new heights and expands your gameplay experience. Featuring brand new photography as well as a dedicated FAQ section, the Season 2 Rulebook is a must-have for any DreadBall fan.

Season 2 Expands your DreadBall Experience with:

Hatfield vs. McCoy - "Gold Rush" is a dice / strategy table top game of hillbilly prospecting.  Each family is seeking new gold mine claims. Roll your dice to pick up new claims, or become a claim jumper and try to take one from the other family. Roll your 5 rock, paper, scissors dice and bid them against a family member that holds that mine. The family with the most claims that hit gold at the end of the game wins!

This Box Contains: 
5 Custom RPS Blue Dice
5 Custom RPS Red Dice
2 Custom Black Dice
8 Red Marker Pawns
8 Blue Marker Pawns
12 Prospecting Cards
3 Hatfield Family Cards
3 McCoy Family Cards
2 Game Guide Cards
And 1 Rule Book

User Description

A set of simple fast play wargame rules designed for fighting large scale battles using 6mm and 2mm scale miniatures.  They cover the period from 1935 to the present day.

They also have WWII army lists, supplement rules for post 1945 and air to ground aircraft rules.

They have been designed to use a D6 dice and minitures which are multiable based in to units (dependent on scale).
A spin-and-move game where you are trying to shoot the most valuable big game animals using a spinner shaped like a gun.

Retro Rampage Trivia Quest is a table top trivia game focusing on classic games and consoles, ranging from the Nintendo Entertainment System and Sega Master System, up until the Super Nintendo, Sega Genesis, and even the Turbografx-16 and the PC Engine.

description:
Using American Football fundamentals, players simulate an Iowa Hawkeye football game with charts, dice, and an exact replica Kinnick Field game board.

Game-Play:  The defensive player secretly selects one of three defensive formations (running, blitzing, and passing) and marks it with his d6. Then, the offensive player verbally selects one of tweleve running or passing plays. The defensive player exposes his formation, and the offensive player rolls 2d10 to reveal the outcome of the play from the play chart. Play results include, but are not limited to: incomplete pass, interception, sack, penalty, touchdown, no gain, or a yardage gain of another dice roll. Game time and score are tracked on a separate charts. Special team results are also covered on a field goal, kick-off, and punt chart.

Contents:  2 play charts, 1 exact replica of Kinnick Field game board, 1 field goal and penalty chart, 1 official time chart, scorepad, 1 line-of-scrimmage marker, 1 ten-yard marker, 3 colored markers, 2 offensive dice (d10), 1 defensive die (d6), 1 rules folder, and 1 Hawkeye button (1st issue Collectors Edition only).

The game rules do not cover the sport of American Football; therefore, basic knowledge of the sport should be known or referenced with a NCAA college football handbook.

“There ain’t no AYE in team, but there is MEAT. And that’s you when we’re done.”

-Coach Dru

The Goblins approach Kaosball with the same attitude they approach life: We'll do things our own way.  Worstshire's reputation as a rough and rowdy town with little to recommend it is well deserved. The Goblins are Kaosball’s anarchs, the team who spit on authority and defy convention. Their team power turns the crossfield into a giant mosh pit, and if your runners or bruisers are surrounded by Goblins, prepare to be crowd-surfed right out of the action!

This is a Kickstarter and convention exclusive team!

Boardgame "U Koryta" is a satire of political and social issues, the realities of the political fictional world, localized somewhere between the fall of the Fourth Republic and the birth of Fifth Republic. 
Players take the role of gray eminences. They leads political parties and their choices affect both the shape the political scene, but also the condition of the state.

http://www.bellringer.pl/u-koryta,3.html

"It's just a game man... Chill out!"

Kongbad is one of the most memorable ringers in the game today, and not simply because he's a huge ape.  His laidback and easygoing approach to life, love and Kaosball make him a fan favorite!

He's made a long career manipulating play to cost opponents points in unusual ways, and he's very good at it.

Kickstarter and convention exclusive Ringer.

Your goal in Galtoni is to place four balls of your color in a straight line, whether diagonal or orthogonal.

Unlike in other games with this concept, this game has a randomizing element in that the board resembles a Galton Board. Players take turns inserting a ball in one of the openings at the top of the slanted board. As the ball rolls down the board, it hits a series of pegs that deflect it and bounce it around before it lands in one of seven slots at the bottom of the board.

You can play with each player dropping only balls of their own color, or you can have players draw a ball at random from a bag, thereby possibly forcing them to figure out where best to drop a ball of an opponent's color. Two neutral balls are included, and these count toward each player's line.

This edition of the Trivial Pursuit game is dedicated to the history of F.C. Barcelona (Barça).

The mechanics of the game aer similar to the other Trivial Pursuit games, with questions about players, games , history and other sports sections of F.C. Barcelona.

"The game may have changed, but our principles have not" - Coach Z

The Paragons are a beacon of truth and righteousness in the bloody sport of Kaosball. By focusing their team’s collective soul, they can temporarily sacrifice themselves to boost their teammates to superhuman feats and score like you wouldn’t believe!

This is a math based card game.   The object of the game is to play eight cards, each with a larger number, in a sequence.  There are eight different sequences each starting from a number between one and eight.  The sequence is basically the multiplication table.  For example, the second number in the sequence that starts with 5 is 10 and the third number in that sequence is 15.  You do not have to stay in the same sequence. If you have played a 10, you can proceed with a 12 or 15.  The cards helpfully show which cards they can be played on and which cards can be played on them.

Each of up to four players is dealt three cards.  On each turn they can draw a card from the draw pile or the discard pile.  They then either place a card in their sequence or discard.  There are eight cards when played cause player to exchange hands.

Scoring is done by adding up all the points played by the player who completes his sequence of eight cards.  All the other players add up their cards played and subtract the points for cards still held in their hand.

boardgame: Polite Pigs Playing Cards: A Game of Good Manners
description:
From the game itself:

"Polite Pigs will familiarize children with the etiquette of responding appropriately in a variety of social situations. It is also designed to reinforce and habituate these responses through verbal repetition. Parents should enjoy hearing their children repeatedly ask and give permission to proceed as the game is played. The object of the game is to capture the most situation cards with the appropriate polite responses."

Gameplay description:

Cards are dealt to each player (child). The remaining cards form a draw pile. The dealer turns cards over from the draw pile until s/he reaches a "situation" card. (Others are mixed back into the pile.)

The dealer announces the situation and the youngest player (presumably to help children learn politeness and pragmatics starting at a young age) figures out whether s/he has the appropriate response card. If so, s/he says so and takes the situation card as a matched pair with his/her response card.

Else, the player takes a draw card and further, the game's rules require the child state, "Now you try, please" and the next player or opponent to respond, "Thank you" as an additional method of reinforcing politeness. If the next player has the corresponding response card, s/he may claim the pair. Otherwise, s/he draws a card and play continues so on and so forth.

If a player is able to match a response card to a situation card, s/he become dealer. A series of formalistic polite exchanges must be had between the players and if any player forgets the appropriate phrase, such as, "May I give you a situation, please?" then the offending player must hand his opponent a response card or draw until he gets one to hand over.

The winner is the one with the most pairs.

Ultimately, this game focuses not only on physical game mechanics but social dynamics to reinforce its educational affect. The rules take into account that the players may fail to say required phrases, something akin to—but more complex than—the requirement in UNO to declare "¡Uno!" with one card left in hand.

Based on the Classic & Original Jenga Game

Keep your tower from toppling! Set up your tower of multicolored blocks—each color counts for a different point. Then, aim your blaster at the tower to try to knock a block off. Just aim carefully, because you don’t want the tower to fall! See if you can even manage the ultimate trick shot: blast out the last block in a level to drop the top blocks down!
Includes JENGA blaster, 39 foam blocks, pedestal, 13 crash chips and rules.

boardgame: Seasons of War:  Days of the Conqueror
description:
Days of the Conqueror is a mass-battle miniatures game for the period of William the Conqueror (11th century).  Units are composed of a number of stands of miniatures.  Units are rated by a die (D4, D6, D8, or D10) in each attribute:  Discipline, Morale, Attack, and Armor.

Each unit must make a Discipline Test (with results of either Rash, Simple, or Strategic) each turn to determine what action it can or must perform.  Rash actions require units to make disadvantageous actions, while Strategic actions allow more complex actions such as reforming, force marching, and forming shieldwall.

The combat system is very "Risk-like" where the Armor dice of the defender are compared to the Attack (or missile) dice of the attacker, with each Attacking die that exceeds an Armor die causing a hit.  Units use their Morale attribute whenever they take damage to determine their reaction.

Army lists for Normans, Anglo-Saxons, Vikings, Franks, Bretons are included.  Six typical scenarios are included as well as a campaign system that links the scenarios.

A set of bonus cards provided to backers of the Kickstart project.  Nine cards provide new actions/events to include in the game, and twelve cards are blank to add your own ideas.

New Cards:
- 5 new Surprise cards
- 1 optional rule card
- 3 new Courtier cards

Blank Cards:
- 2 Courtier blanks
- 2 Vassal Courtier blanks
- 2 Surprise blanks
- 3 Debt blanks
- 3 Triumph blanks

boardgame: Rally Point Volume 7: Schwerpunkt's Greatest Hits
description:
Rally Point Volume 7: Schwerpunkt's Greatest Hits is a collection of 10 of the best scenarios from the pages of Schwerpunkt Volumes 1 through 10.

These are highly-regarded, ASL-compatible scenarios, focussing on late-war actions.

This is an educational game. You are rolling dices to move around the board - depending on the field you stay on - you earn some cards (you can use them against your oponent or to defense yourself) or get questions (correct answer give you positive points). The goal is to score as many points.
family: TV Shows: Once Upon a Time (Animated, French)

description:
Objective: The winning player is the first to connect 5 of their stones in a row.

1. Player 1 places one stone on the first turn.
2. After turn one, each player places 2 stones per turn.
3. Two stones can not be placed adjacent to each other (including touching diagonally) on the same turn unless they are placed to squares of the same color.
4. Two stones can not be played to squares of the same color during a single turn unless they are adjacent to each other.
5. Whenever a player fills in a single shape with their stones, they gain another stone to place during that turn. This stone placement must follow normal placement rules.

Galaxy Trucker: Latest Models  consists of new game boards for 2-5 players with model C spaceships. Class 1C and 2C come on the same game board with color denoting spaces that can be used in 2C but not in 1C. Asteroids that approach the spaceship from the front or from behind sometimes swoop around to hit the ship on the side, and asteroids that approach from the side sometimes split in half and hit the ship twice! A structural rift down the center of the spaceship requires universal connectors to connect only with other universal connectors when bridging this rift. So much for their universality!

Class 3C is a toroidal spaceship with the top of the 6x6 tile grid wrapping around to the bottom and the left side wrapping around to the right. Since lasers require an empty space in front of them – and engines an empty space behind them – your ship must have holes in it to accomodate those building requirements, and the larger and more numerous the holes, the more you must pay in insurance costs prior to launching your spaceship.

Class 4C has space to build four separate spaceships, but when the sand timer is flipped the first time, someone rolls a die and players can now build in additional areas of the game board in order to connect some of the ships. When the sand timer is flipped a second time, the die is rolled once more, possibly providing additional building sites.

Conquer the largest number of islands of the archipelago of the Azores.
The player who answers correctly two questions about the Azores, can choose which island is conquered.

Bilingual: English/Portuguese

Roll the dice and move player pawns through the archipelago the required number of spaces in any direction and answer the appropriate question based on where you land. If correct take over an island and roll again.

Object: take over as much of the Azores as you can.

Swytch, the second in the BGO series by designer Nathan Hansen, is a symmetrically balanced abstract strategy game for 2 players. Each player has two types of Counters: Hexagonal and Circular. Hexagonal counters can move in 3 directions, while Circular counters can move in all 6 directions.

Each player tries to outmaneuver and eliminate all of the opposing player's pieces on the board. To do this, they can do one of three things on their turn. They can either: Move/Attack with a counter or stack of counters, prepare a stack of Hexagonal counters for an advanced move with changing directions, or "Swytch" (i.e., change the orientation of a Stack of Hexagonal counters, and thus what directions they can move). Every time that a player attacks with a stack of counters, they MUST also Swytch with that stack immediately after.

The number of spaces a stack of counters can move/attack is determined by its height. When a player attacks by moving into an opponents stack, they reduce the size of the target's stack of counters by the size of their own. If this is enough to eliminate the target, they do so. If not, they are forced to stop one space short and also reduce the size of their stack by 1.

Deeply strategic and full of possibilities, get the upper hand in Swytch!

Game Data:

Complexity: 5 on a 9 scale
Solitaire Suitability: 2 on a 9 scale

Polybag Game Components:

•    One 8-page full-color Rules booklet 
•    One 8.5” x 11” game map
•    52 two-sided, multi-shaped game pieces

This expansion to the The Duke core game adds a little personalization. It introduces a new tiles for each side based on the designs of the person creating them.

Includes:
2 x Blank Light Stained Tile
2 x Blank Dark Stained Tile
2 x Sticker Sheets with Movement/Ability Icons

From the Packaging:
Build your own Troop Tiles and conquer new lands for The Duke!

Customization tiles allow players to create their own, unique tiles. Using the blank tiles and the sticker sheets, each player can design and field a tile of their very own.

Snacks and Bladders teaches anatomy, biology, diet & nutrition by exploring the human digestive system through roll & move and trivia question mechanics.

Correctly identify body parts and make wise food choices to stay ahead of your opponents.

A jig-saw puzzle game which can be played in four different ways.

Basic play: Each player gets a one of four different color/picture puzzle frames and a box of 36 numbered puzzle pieces of the same color. Then there is a die/dice/chit pull and the players take/loose pieces depending on version played. This continues till a player completes a puzzle and yells Eureko!

The four ways to play are:

1.  Color Eureko! - roll color die (d6 yellow, brown, green, blue, yellow/blue, green/brown) and number die (d6 1, 2, 3, 4, -1, -2), get/loose that many pieces of the color(s) rolled.

2.  Number Eureko! - Put your color puzzle pieces number side up (pieces are numbered from 1-72, not all numbers are in each puzzle), a number disc is drawn and any one with that number gets that piece, if the number was red players with that number also get a credit chit, chit may be turned in for more pieces.

3.  Picture Eureko! - each player rolls the number die and gets/looses that many pieces.

4.  Solitaire Eureko! - play however you want or just put the puzzles together.

The central computer's codes have been hacked and are circulating freely in the city. Batman is trying to recover them, but he is not alone... Who will take control of the city? As they move about the board, players must take different kinds of action. They must also face and outsmart villains like Clayface, Firefly and Mr. Freeze and attempt to avoid surprise explosions...

Aim of the Game: To recovers codes to obtain two coded keys and gain access to the Gotham City central computer to take control of it. The first player to do so wins.

Box contains: 
- 40 playing cards 
- 32 tokens(16 codes, 8 bombs, 8 coded keys) 
- 4 playing pieces (characters) 
- 2 dice (characters) 
- 1 six-figure die 
- Game rules 
- 20-sticker sheet

Pairadice Hockey is a hockey board game simulation that uses a pair of dice to activate results from a master game chart to recreate the game of hockey.  Individual players are rated based on their actual season performance to generate reasonably accurate results over a full season worth of play.  Many NHL and Minor League seasons are rated for play.
