Ein Schaf (also titled ひつじがいっぴき, or Hitsuji ga Ippiki in Japanese) is a simple children’s card game.

The deck consists of cards with anywhere from one to seven sheep on them. Play proceeds with one player playing the “one”, the next player playing the “two”, and so on in order of rank. Players may at any time take a card from the deck and reset the count to one. Players must take a card if they do not have the correct card to play. The first player to get rid of his or her entire hand is the winner.

A funny game about telling a good story. But you have to fulfill a couple of requirements. First of all your story has a certain theme and then has to end with a specific punchline.
You have a stock of handcards where you choose your cartoon and punchline from, so it matches the theme.
When everyone told there story, you have to choose what story you liked best, second and third. 
And then there will be points for all the Players.

In ancient times long gone by, when the veil between the world of the humans and the realm of magic was thinner, almighty wizards battled each other in epic duels to determine who was the master of sorcery and arcane wisdom. Armed with a Spellbook and with the aid of the enchanted Element-Stones, the sorcerers summoned fantastic Creatures and magical Spells. A wizard who had no more pages left in his Spellbook would eventually have lost the magical battle of wits.

The aim of the game is to reduce the cards in your opponent's card-deck (or Spellbook) to zero by using the Spells and Creatures in your Spellbook. Players take turns that always follow the same structure. For each point of damage a Player receives through Creatures or Spells, he puts the top card of his or her deck on his Discard Pile (or Archive). If a player is unable to draw a card, he loses immediately.

Elemental Clash: Legendary Legacy will be the first of hopefully many Expansion to Elemental Clash: The Master Set, featuring state-of-the-art illustrations done by professional artists (as opposed to the pre-Master Set versions of the game). This exciting new expansion will contain over 90 different Creatures, Spells and Element Stones as well as 10 brand-new, powerful Spellcaster cards and introduce Neutral cards (cards that belong to no particular Element) and a new sub-type of Spells: Equip-Spells as well as the Familiar Spirit Creature Subtype among other things. Furthermore, the set will feature lots of powerful "Unique" cards, Dragon Lords and might Kings and Queens, making it literally a "royal" expansion to Elemental Clash: The Master Set!

The expansion will not contain Basic Element Stones which are included in the base game and needed to build decks and play the game.

A Card-Driven Action Strategy Game!

The planet Antaeras was a thriving world, full of majestic beauty and alive with a natural flow of magic that extended to all its inhabitants. This magic was its most precious commodity…but would also prove to be its downfall.

The ages-long civil war between the Brotherhood of Steam and the Reptilisaur Empire has now become a three-way battle with the appearance of the Corrupted Legion.

ARCANITE WARS is a strategy card game for two players where you must harvest resources, construct a base, train units, explore the terrain around you, and battle the enemy army. The player who destroys his opponent's Headquarters is victorious!

If you are familiar with the concepts behind the Real-Time Strategy game genre (think Command and Conquer, StarCraft, Age of Empires, etc), then you will easily pick up the flow of this game.

The Core Set comes complete with a set of Terrain cards and three races:

> The Brotherhood of Steam
> The Reptilisaur Empire
> The Corrupted Legion

Each of these races uses two decks: its Race deck (Units, buildings, and Workers) and Event deck (consisting of special abilities and ways to add to Units' strength on the battlefield). Each race has different strengths and plays differently from the others.

Everything you need to play is included - just grab another player and let the battle begin!

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Game Night Kit 2013 Season Two
description:
The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game - Game Night Kit 2013 Season Two includes 3 nightmare decks:
The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Nightmare Deck: The Hunt for Gollum
The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Nightmare Deck: Conflict at the Carrock
The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – Nightmare Deck: A Journey to Rhosgobel

Game Night Kits are technically only available to retailers and not for general purchase. Game Night Kits contain 3 Nightmare Decks, Tournament rules, a Poster and a Goodie (Playmat, Deck box). It is a limited release, which is meant to support FFG's Game Night program. Later on, only the Nightmare Decks are released as Print on Demand to the general public. Those decks are sold individually.

description:
Erie Canal is a 2-4 player Print-and-Play game that focuses on transportation in the canal.  The canal board pieces are modular so you can change the canal each time you play the game, offering many possible scenarios.  As a part of the 2012 Historical Themed Board Game Contest, Erie Canal comes with 4 pre-defined scenarios to play.

In Erie canal each player on their turn will be given an allotment of action points, which they will use to perform various actions.  Some of these actions will allow the player to control the locks, which is a new element introduced for this game.  Each player will have to transport goods and people up and down the canal while trying to make effective use of these locks.  The player gains the most victory points by transporting the most people and goods will be declared the winner.

Jak and Poi is a two-player card game based on the hand game Rock, Paper, Scissors.

Everybody knows that Rock beats Scissors which beats Paper which then beats Rock. But when we were kids, we all tried to go around this rule by inventing our own hand symbols like Lightning, Bomb or Tank. Jak and Poi builds on this “anything goes” concept and the result is an accessible one-card-per-turn game that has surprising depth and strategy.

The objective of the game is to reduce your opponent’s 20 life points to zero. To do this, each player uses the 2 main card types in the game: Jaks and Pois. The main mechanic of the game revolves around how these two card types interact with each other. Since Jaks (representing Rock, Paper, Scissors) can counter against or synergize with Pois (representing your lighting, bomb, tank, etc.) correctly anticipating your opponent’s moves or leading your opponent to play in your favor is key to victory.

While unique, the gameplay of Jak and Poi is only half the reason the game is so special. Leveraging on the fact that every one of us, at some point in our childhood, invented our own hand symbols to try and win at Rock, Paper, Scissors, we crowdsourced the creation of all the cards in the game from players and artists who wanted to let their imaginations run wild.

The two decks in Jak and Poi’s first release are the result of the efforts of 40+ designers and 20+ artists. This collaboration between passionate creators is no doubt the heart and driving force of the game.

BigforkOpoly is a promotional board game released by Pride Distributors, Inc. as a fundraiser for local community organizations in the town of Bigfork, Montana. It is a derivative of Monopoly and is virtually identical to it in most respects.

Запретные слова (No Words) is a game of (not-)creating words from the letters of the Russian alphabet. Each player has a hand of 3 cards (1 letter on each card). On his/her turn, the player should play one card from hand into the common pool. If after his/her turn any of the opponents is able to create a complete Russian word from the letters in the pool, the player has to take all the letters of this word as his penalty points (1 letter equals 1 penalty point). The winner is the player with lesser number of penalty points at the end of the game.

Contents:
84 cards with Cyrillic letters
rules

1914, the world is on the brink of a major war. The major powers are preparing for the contest, but its resources for such an undertaking are very limited due to the social changes of recent times.
That's why the big companies charge each nation to find and acopien resources. Where where more then abound.
On Mars.
Who has the power in these resources will have much of the war won.



Descripción del Juego:
“Extraordinarios aparatos y máquinas maravillosas han permitido a los poderosos imperios europeos  de principios del s. XX  viajar hasta Marte. El descubrimiento del Unobtanium, metal de dureza imposible, en las  profundidades del rocoso suelo marciano, han  acrecentado las políticas militaristas entre las grandes potencias en los primeros meses de 1914.Representas a unas de las Compañías Mineras de los imperios, que agrupadas alrededor del Emporio Terrestre y compelidas por la guerra en ciernes, intentan establecer su dominio en el suelo marciano. 
Al final del juego triunfará aquella compañía que haya conseguido extraer más minerales y colonizar la mayoría de regiones marcianas.”

Mars Mineral War (1914) es un juego de mayorías, gestión de recursos y colocación de trabajadores,   en el que los jugadores utilizan un mazo de cartas propio para gestionar las acciones. 
Las cartas representan  los distintos “Contratos de Explotación” que el Emporio Terrestre ha concedido a las Compañías que  intentan extraer el preciado Unobtanium. 
Cada jugador posee un mazo de 20 cartas (Contratos). Cada mazo tiene el mismo tipo de Contratos pero diferenciados por los Logos y el color de cada una de las Compañías. Así los jugadores gozan de  las mismas oportunidades de jugar cada carta   pero, dado que los mazos están mezclados  y boca abajo, esas opciones se presentaran para cada uno de ellos  en momentos distintos de la partida. Además, bajo ciertas condiciones, también podrán usar los Contratos de Explotación  de las otras  Compañías
Al principio de la partida los jugadores barajan su mazo y lo dejan boca abajo. Al finalizar la primera ronda deberán volver a mezclar su mazo de descartes y colocarlo, de nuevo, boca abajo. Los mazos que en la primera ronda eran idénticos,  en la segunda ronda  contienen cartas de  los otros jugadores o descartes previos. 
Concretamente en su turno cada jugador toma  cuatro cartas (Contratos de Explotación) de su mazo, elige una de ellas y la descarta boca arriba en un mazo de descartes propio, después escoge otra de las cartas de su mano y la pasa al jugador de su izquierda. 
Posteriormente  los jugadores  juegan o descartan, una a una,  las tres cartas que quedan en su mano. 
 Los Contratos de Explotación (cartas)   permiten explorar la superficie marciana o bien realizar distintas acciones:
i.- Realizar una o dos  acciones  sobre la Superficie del Planeta (iniciar explotaciones, ubicar trenes-oruga, reubicar minas, etc). 
ii.- Realizar una o dos acciones de Gestión y/o Explotación en el Tablero de Emporio (formar mineros en los barracones, extraer Unobtanium, vender el mineral, reasignar recursos, conseguir contenedores de energía de Oort).
iii. Descartase de dos cartas  y obtener, a cambio,  contenedores de energía o nuevos mineros formados de los barracones.
iv.-Descartarse de una carta y obtener, cada jugador, una unidad de energía.
Cada Contrato de Explotación ejecutado  conlleva un gasto en energía. Además si el contrato originalmente pertenece a otra Compañía supondrá  un gasto extra de energía. El mantenimiento de las instalaciones sobre la Superficie de Marte también genera un coste de energía.
El jugador con mayor número de puntos de victoria después del recuento de las dos rondas del juego será el ganador.
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
Gladiators is a thematic combat game set in the sand arenas of the classic roman sport. Players each control 2 (in 2 player, 1 in 3-4 player) gladiators and simultaneously plot their actions upon their gladiator cards. Once three moves have been decided they are revealed and gladiators move around the arena attacking each other, hurling nets and trying to out manoeuvre their opponents.

Plays in 30-45 minutes, there is a mixture of luck and strategy in the outcome of the game.

Expert players are given further scenarios to try out which require adaptation of their existing tactics.

This is currently a print and play game that can be played with hex tiles and miniatures.

Kosmiczny Kupiec (Space Trader) is a economical board game where you get your own frigate handfull of credits and first transport assingment to complete. 
During the game you get random encounters like pirate attack,space police control or exploring some alien artifact. You can also upgrade your ship with either science, weapons or transport modules.
Goal of the game is to have the largest sum of money when the last assingment is completed.

Contents of the box:

Board
   23 trading missions cards
   23 space routes cards
   23 exploration cards
   36 goods tokens
   4  spaceship boards
   8  science module upgrades
   8  weapon module upgrades
   24 transport module upgrades
      Space cash supply
      Dice
   4 pawns to represent ships
    Rules of play

From directions:

The box represents the mining fields of the Klondike.
Put the nuggets into the lower part of the box through any of the mine openings and place the mine covers on at random.
The players decide which shall commence the game and also the number of ounces of gold to be found to constitute the game as 50 ounces, 100 ounces, 200 ounces, etc.
Shake the nuggets in the box.
The first player takes a "pick axe" and lifts up any mine cover he chooses. If a nugget appears underneath lying wholly or partly within the circle covered by the mine cover, the player has to his credit the number of ounces stamped on the underside of the mine cover and he
also has another chance to lift a cover and so continue until he finds a mine with no nugget, at which time he looses his play. 
The second player to the left follows and so on throughout the game.
If more than one nugget is found at one time under a mine cover the player places to his credit as many ounces of gold as there are nuggets times the mark on the cover.
Leave all the nuggets in the box and shake them up between every play. Any player can shake when another player is not actually lifting up a mine cover.
The player which first gets the number of ounces of gold agreed upon at the beginning of the game, wins.
Implements:
6 mine covers, 6 nuggets, 1 pick axe.

An unknown virus spreads from a small town to a city,  frenzied humans and zombies are rampant, even the military has failed, the survivors must put aside their prejudices to cooperate with each other in order to have a chance of survival ...

This time players will  play characters with a special ability; use brand new items, weapons and locations to survive. The difference is that the players must go hand in hand, earn points by achieving various achievements and the end, the key to deciding victory is no longer just to survive, but to have a wonderful life while doing so.

description: Panini are proud to announce the launch of their exciting new trading card game based on the legendary cartoon from Nickelodeon; Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles. The franchise is back with a brand new look and awesome new content and Panini's all-new trading card game will draw on this and is sure to be a huge hit with Turtles fans. Featuring all of the lead characters, the collection will follow the Turtles as they fight to clean up the streets of New York and defend innocent citizens from Shredder and The Kraang. boardgame: Hand in Hand: The Card Game
description:
Hand in Hand is a deck of 60 cards, each with two different brightly coloured halves that depict one half of a child that needs your help to be made whole. Similar to a deck of playing cards, there’s no limit to how many ways you can play with these cards.

However, the base game is suitable for 2-6 players aged 6+ and can be played in 15-20 minutes. By matching colours, players create a chain of children holding hands that can be “taken home” to score points. This is a great family game that offers positive, encouraging turns leaving everyone feeling like they’ve contributed to something good.

Race to collect and successfully sacrifice the most princesses to the wise Dryer Dragon in order to prevent the asocalypse and be named king or queen of Lint. Your sock puppet adventurer has been chosen to overthrow the evil princess cult in their effort to raise the destroyer of Lint existence, TOES. The only way to prevent the end of days is to successfully sacrifice the required amount of the evil princesses so the Dryer Dragon can craft the great TOE SOCK OF BINDING to defeat TOES, but if too many princesses are rejected then TOES will arise and the evil princesses will win.

Players win by being the first to fill their "throne meter" - accomplished by sacrificing princesses. (description from Kickstarter)

The purpose of this game is to dispel commonly held myths related to teen pregnancy and sexuality and to show the consequences for one's quality of life. In the process of playing, teenagers will be challenged to face the real consequences of sex.

While moving around the board, players will draw cards dealing with the subjects of Sex Facts, Health and Disease, Contraceptives, Sex and Values, and Sex and Success. Correctly answering the questions on these cards gives the player points.

Certain spaces on the board will force the player to spin the Sex Wheel of Fortune.  Of the several results on the spinner, one is pregnancy. Some spaces also will cause a pregnancy. Failure to maintain your score above a certain point will also result in pregnancy.

Pregnancy forces players into a Dilemma. Female players will have to decide on which path to take: Adoption, Motherhood, or Abortion. Male players will have to decide on which path to take: Support or Marriage. Each choice may have different consequences affecting a player's score.

After a predetermined period of gameplay, players will tally their scores.  The player(s) with the higher scores have increased their chances of success and happiness while those with lower scores have diminished their chances.

(Publisher description)

AS DARKNESS DESCENDS...

In 3132, eighty percent of the hyperpulse generators in the Inner Sphere shut down, silencing virtually all interstellar communications between the thousands of worlds humankind called home. Remembering the horrors of the Jihad that ended just half a century before, paranoid citizens and opportunistic leaders across the Sphere instinctively girded for the invasions that were sure to come. As ancient rivals once again turned against each other, the Republic of the Sphere—once a beacon of hope for billions—found itself nearly torn asunder by hostile neighbors on every side.

A new Dark Age had begun.

Era Report: 3145 describes the state of the Inner Sphere at critical age in BattleTech history: the years of the Dark Age era. Presented as a general overview of the period, rather than a focused report on a single conflict, this book includes descriptions of every major Inner Sphere faction active in the wake of the catastrophic failure of the interstellar HPG grid. Notable personalities, military forces, and an overview of the technological capabilities of the Dark Age era are also provided, along with special advanced-level rules for campaigns set during this time, whether played using the Total Warfare core rules or those found in A Time of War: The BattleTech RPG.

(Publisher information)

ENEMIES AT THE GATES

It is the year 3145. Only thirteen years have passed since Gray Monday—the day that most of humanity’s interstellar communications grid suddenly and mysteriously collapsed. Plunged into darkness and fearing the worst, the leaders and armies of the Inner Sphere scrambled to act, some fearing the approach of invaders, others seeking to exploit the chaos. Now, armies are on the march, war has erupted on every front, and the Republic of the Sphere—once a sign of mankind’s hope for a brighter future—has retreated behind its fortress walls to prepare for the inevitable.

Field Manual: 3145 updates the military and political state of the Inner Sphere as it stands in the year 3145. This report includes a brief history and overview of recent developments in the BattleTech universe, as well as current TO&Es for the major ’Mech forces and mercenary commands employed by the realms and Clans of the Dark Age Inner Sphere. Special era-specific rules are also included, enabling players to create characters and forces for use in campaigns set in and after this critical point in BattleTech history.

Dr. Boom is a 2-5 player variant of Two Rooms and a Boom, which requires at least six players. Dr. Boom is a wallet-sized, take-with-you-anywhere game of social deduction.  One player is the President who needs to let the real Doctor into his/her office.  Careful!  If the President chooses the nefarious Dr. Boom, the end result is explosive failure.

description:
From the Publisher's Website:

'QBits' is a new innovative mathematical board game designed by young people for young people. It incorporates the four basic principles of maths (addition, subtraction, multiplication and division) in a format that is quick and easy for players of all ages and abilities to learn. Without even realising it, players are continually doing maths in a fun way throughout the game. Younger or less able players can play using smaller numbers whilst older or more able players can play using higher numbers, once familiar with the rules this becomes a tactical game which even teaches prime numbers.

Game Includes:
Game Board
50 Numbered Plastic "Qubes"
Cloth Holding Bag
Score Pad
Pencil

Supernova is a 36 card space battle game published in six installments of the 2000 AD comic. Players take turns dealing a card from the top of their and selecting one of the parameters listed on the card, such as Attack Armament, Crew, etc. The opposing player then plays the top card from their deck and compares the ratings of the chosen parameter. The highest rating wins that contest. The Supernova ship beats all others. The player to collect all the cards wins.

Battle through space to match up the THUNDERCATS characters!
Match and collect complete characters to win the game.

Spin the wheel in center of the board and place a characters body part (either their head, body or legs) in corresponding section of the board. 
Player with most correctly matched complete characters is the winner!

A game for 6 players. 4 years and over.

Alchemy dice is a strategic dice allocation/worker placement game in which the players will use their dice to collect precious stones as resources for their alchemical studies, increase their influence by buying new cards and dice and finally earn victory points to win the game.


            
        
    


"The Patriarch of Rishadan was an impressive man who had used his secret knowledge to prolong his life well beyond his natural span. But in the end even he could not escape the last voyage and now the alchemist guild is without a leader. 
A council of 111 alchemists has chosen the few who are deemed worthy enough to become the new patriarch and the most powerful man in the whole city. But which one is the best? Which one knows the secrets of the transmutation like no other? Which one can use his influence in the city best to strengthen his position? And which one will be triumphant in the secret rituals known as “chucking the dice”?
Let the competition begin and the council will elect the most successful alchemist as the new patriarch."


            
        
    


In Alchemy Dice, the player start out with three different cards, three dice, an unique Character and an unique assortment of resources. During each round the players are rolling their dice and allocate them to their cards where they, based on the dice-roll will be able to use different actions - like collecting resources, modifying another die, tranforming one kind of resource into another one and so on.
During the later phases of the game each player can use the collected resources to buy a new card and/or die which will improve his abilities during future rounds.

The game can end in several ways (e.g. if all dice from 2 of the 4 dice markets have been bought, the last card from the deck has been drawn or one player has bought 8 cards) and the player with the most victory points (granted by the cards and through bonus conditions) wins the game.


            
        
    


Currently there are 2 versions of the game which will both be available through the Gamecrafter later this year:

The Deluxe version:
This version includes all the cards and all the other components needed to play the game. The resource tokens are glass beads and the game comes with cloth bags to store all the dice and gems for a high production quality version of the game.

Included in the deluxe version are:
- 56 cards
- 48 dice
- 60 gems
- 40 markers
- 4 cloth bags
- 1 start player marker
- 1 rulebook

The Redux version:
This is the version for the gamer on a small budget but with plenty of spare parts at home. The redux version comes in a small tuck-box and contains only the 56 cards and the rules. In order to play with this version who will have to provide your own dice, gems and markers. But as most people will have these things at hand already the redux version offers a very affordable way to get Alchemy Dice.

Octonauts: Fische retten is an easy roll and move game for children.

According to the result of a die roll, you can move a fish on the bord. 
The first player to reach his boarder areas with four fishes is the winner.

IntroductionKopano is a drawless connection game for two players: Black and White. It’s played on the intersections (points) of a square board, which is initially empty. The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white. "Kopano" is the Tswana word for "connection".

				
				
					DefinitionsTwo like-colored stones are strongly connected if they are orthogonally adjacent to each other, and weakly connected if they are diagonally adjacent to each other without sharing any strongly connected neighbor. Note that two stones of different colors are never considered to be connected.

A chain is a set of (weakly or strongly) connected stones.

				
				
					PlayStarting with Black, the players take turns placing stones of their own color on empty points of the board, one stone per turn.

It's illegal to place a stone on a point where it makes a weak connection unless placing an enemy stone on that same point would also make a weak connection. It's also illegal to form a crosscut, i.e. a 2x2 pattern of stones consisting of two diagonally adjacent Black stones and two diagonally adjacent White stones.

If a player can't make a move on his turn, he must pass. Passing is otherwise not allowed. There will always be a move available to at least one of the players.

The game is won by the player who completes a chain of his color touching the two opposite board edges of his color. Draws are not possible.

				
				
					Pie ruleThe pie rule is used in order to make the game fair. This means that White will have the option, on his first turn only, to change sides instead of making a regular move.

A card/dice game relying heavily on chance as players try to steal money away from Dicken's Ebenezer Scrooge.  Players take the role of a famous character, Mr Pickwick, Artful Dodger, Fagin, Mr. Micawber or Oliver Twist.  By rolling a lettered die and numbered die, you determine if you collect or deposit money with Scrooge, and sometimes you draw one of the lavishly illustrated cards (with artwork from Habiot Browne, George Cattermole, Robert Seymore, George Cruikshank and John Leech) which forces you to pay extra fees or gather extra gains.  The game was designed with the aid of the curator of the Dickens House Museum.

Players command a squadron of tanks, German or Soviet, battling in Eastern Europe.  Unit cards display the capabilities of each tank unit, and players mark their orders on their unit cards simultaneously and in secret.

Game components include six 1/100 tank models, four double-sided gameboards, terrain tiles, game cards, player aids, ten dice and two water-based markers.

description: Two new and growing kingdoms have recently been discovered in the land of the Fables, and now with Upon a Fable: New Kingdoms up to 7-8 players can explore the fairy tale Realms in a single game of Upon a Fable. In addition to the two extra Story Boards, two Realm Boards, and dozens of Fable and Wonder tokens in this expansion, you’ll also find over 100 new game cards, including two new card types: Enchantment cards that allow you to disrupt your opponents’ strategies while defending your own, and Ever After cards that mimic Story Board spaces and earn you Magic Wonders each round.

Races of the Expanse is a game of creating an alien race, or human variant race by shuffling multiple decks of cards together, and then using your race to explore and colonize the Expanse of space between the Orion Spur and the Sagitarius arm. In Races of the Expanse each player takes turns picking a deck for their base creature, such as Crystaline, Humanoid, Crustacians, or Octopoids, Then in reverse order players choose a special ability Deck from clairvoyant, aquatic, subterranean, or creative. Your random homeworld will then add a regional events deck. These three decks will be Shuffled together, to form your races play deck. Extra races and abilities are planned as stretch goals. finally a communal planets deck is built based on the number of players. Play proceeds clockwise from a random start player.

All players start with a hand of five cards. Player 1 plays up to two cards, and then draws two cards, unless affected by cards or special abilities. Players may have no more than 10 cards in their hand. If, after drawing, you have more than ten cards, discard until you have only ten. If a player runs out of cards in their deck they shuffle their discarded cards and start the deck fresh. Play moves clockwise until the last planet is drawn. (Planets can only be drawn when Explorers are played.) When the last planet is drawn, each player gets one last turn, and then the game is ended and a winner is decided.

The player with the most resource at the end of the game is the winner. You get resource by having the most population on a planet. If two or more players have an equal amount of population on a planet, nobody gets the resource for that planet. The max population of a planet is in the upper lefthand corner of the planet card, the resource of that planet is in the lower righthand corner of the planet card.

Four new savage gunfighters ride into battle, lured by Mapache’s ill-gotten wealth and promises of power. For a fistful of Pesos, these lawbreakers join the self-styled general and wait to do his sinful bidding. Stood firmly against these desperadoes are a gang of cowpunchers, card sharks, and one-time outlaws – marshalled to help the cause by the commendable Padre.

Combine this expansion with the base game to explore new tactics and strategies and define your own play style.

This expansion develops a new unique set of guardians to flesh out the crew that the Padre player recruits to combat 'General Mapache' - giving your games more replayability and adventure. No longer can the devious Mexican rely as heavily on his gatling gun for victory - his world is about to be shaken up!

Revolver 2: The Savage Guns also includes four cutthroat lieutenants for the 'General' to muster - who are tougher and nastier than anything the Padre has ever had to confront before. Paid in combinations of Pesos and Mexican Army tokens, these gunslingers add variety to the experience - each also 'carrying' a clutch of saddlebag drafting cards.

Determined to fight for the township of Malpaso like a cornered Kilkenny cat, the Padre has a few other tricks up his sleeve: he can arm and train the Malpaso menfolk into a rudimentary fighting force; and set up a bastion at the town's chapel.

A very simple game that you could play with Xs and Os. It's certainly related to Go but has much simpler rules, and while definitely not as "deep", it can still produce some interesting competition.

The best board for beginners is probably 7x7x7, maybe 6x6x6.

The rules are explained here: http://www.scribd.com/doc/287823949/Loops

Here are a bunch of interesting variations: http://boardgamegeek.com/thread/1286815/variants

You can also change things up by playing with two moves per turn and/or the ability to place either side's color each move. The easiest way for beginners to account for pie while playing with two moves per turn is by playing only a single move on the very first turn.

This pack contains survivors Eagle Chaz (The American Hero), Troy (The Traveller), and The Cardboard Tube Samurai. This pack also contains the Zomb'ivors of those survivors as well as Zomb'ivors of Dave, El Cholo, and Nick.

boardgame: Heart Attack or Escape from the Old Folks Home
description:
You are an “Old Folk” whose evil children have placed into a retirement home.  One night after an average day you drift off to sleep as usual.  In the morning you are awakened by the loudspeaker system, this is very much not usual.  It appears to be a recording.  A young insulting voice is outlining the victory, which his group achieved overnight.  You realize he is part of the youth movement, which has been threatening the establishment, as usual.  The voice declares that they have taken over the government and the first new law has been set into motion is nobody over the age of 30 is worthy of life.  However, the law had been quickly modified to allow for one “Old Folk”, as they are to be called, to be allowed to live per retirement home.  The first Old Folk to make his or her way out of the home will receive the pardon for life.  Be aware however, the entire home has been booby trapped and there will be no medical teams sent in to help the fallen.  The recording starts again.

Victory Conditions:
Players should agree to a victory point total before play begins, 20 points is a short game, 30 average and 40 long.  Points are scored

How to score points:
After playing some room cards you may play a Passkey.  This will do two things, 1) will allow you to move to another Quad if you wish to.  Once the points are calculated the scorekeeper should note the player's new score and announce to all players what the score is.  There are four scoring positions, position one, two, three, and four.  The first room you play is in “Position One”, the next card your play will be in “Position Two”, then next “Position Three” and finally the fourth in “Position Four”.

Nexus (working title) is an abstract strategy game for two players in which you want to be the first to occupy the opponent's home blocks. The playfield is comprised of a series of three-dimensional blocks that can be re-configured to create paths.

To start, the three red and three blue home blocks are placed on opposite sides of the game board with the corresponding pawns on top. Each player rolls a die to determine how many action points (APs) he can spend on his turn; the points can be divided between moving pawns and modifying the board. The possible actions are:


     Move (1 AP) — Move a pawn to any orthogonal, unoccupied adjacent face if the path is unbroken. If a player lands on an opposing pawn, that pawn returns to one of its home cubes.



     Add Block (1 AP) — Add a purple block to the board adjacent to one of your pawns. This block defines a path that all players must follow. Blocks can be stacked vertically.



     Rotate Block (2 AP) — Rotate any unoccupied adjacent block; blocks below other blocks cannot be rotated.



     Remove Block (3 AP) — Remove any unoccupied adjacent block from the board; blocks below other blocks may not be removed.


Red and blue home blocks cannot be rotated, removed or stacked upon. The first player to move all three of his pawns to his opponent's home blocks wins.

Meander is a mechanized board game with a game board that rotates on its own while players try to reach their home base on the opposite side of the board.

To start, each player has five colored spheres in a starting area of the corresponding color. Players take turns moving their spheres one space per turn — but a turn can occur only when the board has paused and isn't currently moving. If a player has a sphere in hand when the board starts to rotate, he must return the sphere to the start position. A player cannot move into a colored space other than his own. If a player moves a sphere to the center of the game board, he can warp that sphere to the home position. Two spheres cannot occupy the same space. The first player to move all of his spheres to his home spaces wins.

For a different gameplay experience, during set-up, each player takes turns placing neutral white spheres on the game board, one sphere per player. The neutral spheres can be moved by any player and serve as blockers.

"Conan the Barbarian" is an adventure wargame for 2 to 4 players with each of the players representing one of the major factions of Hyboria. Each faction has agents at their disposal with which they attempt to thwart their enemies plans while enlisting the aid of Conan. There are five types of agents: Wizards, Warriors, Courtesans, Assassins, and Thieves. Each type of agent has different capabilities and different strategies for enlisting the aid of our hero.

SPI's planned "Conan the Barbarian" adventure wargame featured a full-color map of Hyboria, character cards, event cards, and counters for every major character. A pre-publication play test kit was released.  There was comprehensive background material written by L. Sprague de Camp.

Mealtime Sabotage is a strategic card game where food is your weapon. The goal of the game is to create a complete meal with the fewest calories possible. In addition to the food cards you need to build your meal (entree, 2 sides, dessert and drink), there are sabotage cards which change the value of each food card.

Each player starts the game with 5 cards. On each turn, the active player draws one card and plays one card. Sabotage cards can be played on any place setting, but they cannot stack (one sabotage card per food item). Once your meal is complete, you can play food items on other place settings. The game is finished when all meals are complete. The player with the lowest calorie meal, wins.

boardgame: Les Maréchaux II: Dupont 1808 – Victor 1811 – Suchet 1813
description:
Les Maréchaux (The Marshals) is an operational wargame designated to simulate campaigns led by French marshals and generals of French Revolution and 1st Empire, when Napoleon was not directly acting on the theater of operations.

The game includes three scenarios:
- "Dupont 1808" related to general Dupont's campaign in south of Spain  against spanish general Castaños, during summer 1808.
- "Victor 1811" related to marshal Victor's campaign near Cadix against general La Peña trying to relieve the cit'y siege with help of an english division, late winter 1811.
- "Suchet 1813" related to marshal Suchet's campaign against general Murray for the last big battle in Aragona, spring 1813.

Players are moving divisions and brigades on a "point to point" map. They have to deal with supply, forced march and to prepare battles. The leaders of each army have a key role in coordinating actions.

The game includes:
- one A4 map for Dupont's campaign; 
- one A4 map for Victor's campaign;
- one A4 map for Suchet's campaign;
- 108 die-cut counters;
- independent rules and scenarios booklet.

Available with French rules, but English rules can be downloaded from Vae Victis or Designer's website.


The first game in the series is Les Maréchaux: Junot 1808 –  Soult 1809 with two campaigns in Portugal.

description:
Some cute little people have invaded the land, and when they get together in groups of four they trigger strange symptoms in humans. Apparently, this game is so much fun that you may lose control of your faculties, causing others to chant "Zig! Zag!"

ZigZag is a partnership game (with variants for two and three players) that is played with a deck of 93 cards. Of those cards, 64 show the aforementioned cute little people, and each one of these cards has three characteristics: a person representing one of the four elements, a landscape, and an accessory (sunglasses, a suitcase, etc.)

The goal of the game is to get sets of four cards with a single characteristic (Zig) or two characteristics (Zag) in common. To finish a round, it is necessary to complete a zig, a zag, a double zig, or a zig and a zag. Each combination is allowed only once, and the first partnership to complete all four wins. When a player has completed a set, he must give a pre-arranged signal to his partner, who must then announce what type of set has been completed out loud. If a signal is mistaken, both partners must discard their cards, draw new hands, and play on.

Everyone plays at the same time, exchanging cards with those cards in the center of the table. Beware as the opposing team will do all they can to prevent you from achieving your goal, including using some of the 17 special cards that do things like stop you from collecting a set with a certain characteristic or force an exchange of hands.

In the two- to three-player variant of ZigZag, each player receives one of twelve constraint cards, which limits the types of sets they can collect.

A simulation of Baseball. Bottle caps represent players, one color for each team. The ball is a white bottle cap.  By way of flicking a player into the ball with one finger, the ball is; pitched, hit, fielded, thrown to the bases.  The playing field is a mat of slick plastic coated paper and has areas marked that indicate different actions of baseball.  For instance, if the ball comes to rest in the rectangular area in front of the home plate then a strike is counted against the batter.

From one of the creators of Trivial Pursuit.
Object: move along the coloured rope convincing your opponents that you have BEEN THERE AND DONE THAT. To win players must move in the inner drum and convincing opponents that you have truly BEEN THERE AND DONE THAT.

Roll the die, move your pawn the required number of spaces along your colour of rope (picture on game board), Opponent asks a question from the cards. If you answer no, the next player goes. If you answer yes you must convince they you have been there etc. Players may grill you to see if you are lying. After a vote, if the vote is no, the next player goes, if yes roll again and continue until a no vote or no response.

description:
"Convention: konwentowa gra karciana" is collective card game. Each player tries to organize manga/anime convention. Players use their card to expand school buildings, prepare events, hire event makers and try to prevent other players to lunch their own conventions.

Released in 2008 at polish MAGNIFIcon convention by manga publishing company Studio JG, game was a parody of polish manga/anime fandom. Many of the cards are based upon real people or situations famous in the polish fandom.

ABOUT THE GAME

TagOn® is a Bible-based, family-friendly crossword board game that provides instant fun for everyone! You've played crossword games before, but never like this! It enhances your Scriptural knowledge and promotes learning of the Bible. It provides an excellent way for families and friends to have meaningful fun while strengthening their knowledge of God's Word.

TagOn® is a game in which two to four players score points by playing and tagging Bible principal words from individual letter tiles. Words are played across or down in crossword fashion and must appear in a Bible dictionary, Bible Concordance or Bible Research Guide adopted by the players as the game dictionary. The game dictionary may be a hard copy print edition, online edition or mobile phone app. It may also be a searchable online Bible or its corresponding mobile phone app. With your game dictionary, you have set up the stage for fun!

What makes TagOn® unique and interesting? Through suspenseful action and thoughtful play, Bible books, characters, utterances, lands and genealogies of Bible characters as well as events and scenes of Bible times are dramatically revealed. What a game!

OBJECT OF THE GAME

To have fun, learn and to become the person with the most point for playing and tagging Bible words.

THE PLAY

The play of the game involves finding, playing, connecting and tagging related Bible words. Played words may have a direct connection with existing words on the game board. This game is about remembering what Bible words were said, who said them, who were related to those characters, where the words were spoken, and in which Bible book can the words be found.

Players will have opportunities to obtain extra points by playing Bible words that have a direct scriptural relationship with other words [tagging words] or/and by playing words on specific colored squares marked with Bible books, characters, utterances, lands and genealogies on the game board. The revelation of Bible characters, places, events, utterances and genealogies, combined with the tag word challenge makes playing TagOn® a fun and interesting game for the entire family.

What about playing new words by replacing existing letters on the game board? You have to see the game rules and actually play the game. Different Bible stories or events appear in every play of the game. This game is so much fun you’ll want to play until God’s Kingdom comes!

Rumble Pie is the blazingly fast, incredibly fun card game that keeps everyone in the middle of the action from start to finish. Players slap down cards quickly to build piles, land a pie and ... KaPow!... earn their team a point!

Piece of cake, right? Not when cards are flying in from every direction — any player can attack or defend on any other player's board at any time. Teams that work together, act fast and watch each other's back will surely have an edge when the pies start to fly.

A 2 players abstract game that uses a bunch of d4. 
The goal is to capture the opponent's 4 target dice.
The dice can move as many times as shown on their current position but one can chose to use one turn to change the value on one die instead of moving.
Any captured non-target die is rerolled and replaced on the board, so placement and adjusting values is often a better option than attacking the opponent's dice.

Ĝaberŭoko (Jabberwock) is a trick taking card game with an Alice in Wonderlands theme where players must fulfill different contracts (20 available), sometimes trying to take some specific cards, sometimes avoiding them.
The game contains standard Ace->10 cards and three different Jabberwocks in each color. The Jabberwock is a trump card that comes back in the hand of a player and immediately evoluate to a less powerful version of himself. Only another Jabberwock can beat him.
Because it's not good to have Jabberwocks in your hand at the end of the game, you'll have to find the right balance between taking cards with the trump and trying to weaken it to give it to someone else.

Eight Year Resistance War is a solitaire strategy game covering the Second Sino-Japanese War (July 7, 1937 – September 2, 1945), which was a military conflict fought primarily between the Republic of China and the Empire of Japan from 1937 to 1941. After the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor in 1941, the war merged into the greater conflict of World War II as a major front of what is broadly known as the Pacific War. The Second Sino-Japanese War was the largest Asian war in the 20th century. It also made up more than 50% of the casualties in the Pacific War if the 1937–1941 period is taken into account. Although the two countries had fought intermittently since 1931, total war started in earnest in 1937 and ended only with the surrender of Japan in 1945.

In this game, you, the player, lead China’s Army (KMT Central Army) to fight against Japanese invasion. Major battles and historical events are represented by event cards which may be executed chronologically as the history (see the Historical Game option in 17.0), or randomly so as to make youself unprepared for what will happen in the next turn. You have to use your limited resources (limited number of actions, and event cards beneficial to you) to avoid Japanese capture of your Capital, Chungking. To do so, you need to gain support from Western Allies and the help from your unreliable temporary ally – Chinese Communist Party’s Red Army, which will get the final victory (China) from you if you allow them to expand and gain sufficient political support among generals, soldiers and civilians.

boardgame: Le Vol de l'Aigle: Volume 3 – Le systeme complet
description:
Le Vol de l'Aigle III (The Flight of the Eagle III) is a wargame that recreates some of the most famous Napleonic campaigns.

The system is a double-blinded system that uses an Umpire. Players can communicate only through written messages. It uses maps of that time and a simple system to manage units. Combats are solved using a face to face system, simple but precise, with the morale as the main endpoint. Combats can also be solved using any existing operational or tactical system including miniatures.

The present volume 3 does not need the volume 1 or 2 to be played. It includes four booklets (three are over 60 pages) including all the rules of volume 1 and 2, a new edition of the tactical rules The Eagle Fights, 50 pages of data to transfer all the armies of the time between both systems, the special rules of the ten campaigns included: Bavaria and Austria 1809, France 1814 and the eight Peninsula campaigns in Spain and Portugal (around one a year between 1808 and 1814). Seven maps and 52 units sheets are included.

Cherry on the cake, an experimental recruitment module is also presented, with a strategic map, two pages of units, and rules managing every parameter involved in the Administration of War.

The game is available in French and in English.

Welcome to the Hexa Sea, General. This land is vital to our Nation. We must hold it at any cost, and remove the enemy's presence. Set a hold in the land of the Hexa Sea, bring forth our Army, and strike deep at the enemy headquarters. Win for us.

Hexa Sea is an abstract, terrain control wargame played on a modular map that changes every new game. You set hold on the map with your Headquarters, build new units and fight your opponent off the map.

The board is comprised of sea tiles and land pieces called Hexas. These Hexas are arranged over the board forming islands with land tiles. Controlling different numbers of Hexas allow the player to build different units, that interact between themselves in different ways. The goal of the game is to capture the Soldier controlling your opponent's Headquarters.

description:
Since the time before the plague swept Europe to the final days of the Napoleonic Campaign, bands of soldiers were contracted to protect the interests of the small city states of Europe.These soldiers for hire came to be known as Condottieri. But these soldiers were not just Italian arms for hire, soldiers flooded into the northern Italian city states from all over Europe. German Landsknechte fought alongside Welsh Longbows; Moorish Cavalry rode alongside Albanian Stratioti; Swiss Reisläufer and Scottish Highlander Kerns shared in their profits; knights of many noble backgrounds and nationalities toured alongside each other for wealth and glory. Most Famously “Sir” John Hawkwood, Albertico da Barbiano, Cesare Borgia and Cosimo de’ Medici all fought as condottiere in various Italian Wars. Conflicts ranged from skirmishes to large scale battles of thousands of heavily armored soldiers fighting to fulfill their contracted duties. This was the dawn of the modern era, the birth of contractual agreement released men from serfdom, and the rise of the first full time professional armies along with their experienced leaders since the fall of Rome.

Condottieri Conflicts is a game which lets players explore the nature of uneasy alliances, allows players to wage battle and negotiate victory conditions in search of riches, glory, and honor. Challenge your friends and family to conflict at its rawest and most cutthroat level. Command your army, form alliances, and backstab your rivals to vanquish the strongest enemies and wage war unlike any other war game you have played before.

This is a game that plays just as well with 2 players as it does with 8. You recieve fully customizable armies ready for medieval warfare, and you also receive Sterritt Strategy’s unique terrain system. Great for any gamer who thinks chess could use more realism, Diplomacy© and Risk© lack tactical skill, or might not have the time or space for classic War Games with miniatures. This is for gamers who want a strategic platform that they can tailor to their an hour of leisure time, the rainy afternoon or their multiple weekend long campaigns.

This is a free Print and Play Demo of a continuing project. This Demo is complete and playable.

Theme
The theme of the game revolves around Deities, and their involvement in the epic conflict amongst civilizations over the course of centuries.  The theme of the provided Demo Map is fantastical, but any map or setting that players wish to design may be played.  A generic-universal rules system invites you to create or recreate the Theme as your game group likes, be it historical, mythological, or fantastical, or any mix thereof.

Gameplay
Turn-based strategy on region-based map.  Actions use a point allocation system and are resolved by dice rolls using d4/d6/d8/d10/d12/d20-type dice.

Players take the roles of Deities who can use Powers to influence gameplay while also directing their Follower Units and more powerful Summoned Units on the World Map in a variety of possible actions such as battling, converting or assassinating enemy Units.  Each player's Units have unique stats which are derived from those of their Deity and which control what type of dice to roll in each situation.

This Demo version includes 5 pre-designed Deities for playtesting; the full game permits players to design Deities of any kind, again, historical, mythological or fantastical, or any mix thereof.

Goal
To have more friendly Follower Units on the board at the end of the game than any other player.  This goal can be pursued through a variety of means, primarily those of warfare and subterfuge (reducing enemy Followers), expansion and development (increasing friendly Followers) or politics and conversion (affecting the allegiance of Followers.

In this card game, players will have to duck, dodge, and morph to avoid combat and to attack their enemies. The classic hand game Rock Paper Scissors is taken to new heights of intensity and competitiveness. Clever timing divides the winner from the loser.

It all starts with having 3 "Soldiers" to use during the game. Each Soldier represents a blunt Rock, a piece of Paper, or sharp pair Scissors. The standard rules of combat engagement are enforced that only allow Rock to attack Scissors, Scissors to attack Paper, and Paper to attack Rock. But player can change their form to attack otherwise unreachable opponents or avoid combat altogether. And all the rules can be bent or broken.

The game is played in turns with no set number of turns in a game. After a player loses all their Soldiers, they are out of the game. The last player with at least 1 Soldier left wins!

What is Rogue Chess? In five words: Advanced Strategy Cards for Chess. In more than five words...
Rogue Chess uses cards to modify the move and capture of chess pieces. Each card is specific to a certain chess piece and when played will modify that piece's move, capture, and/or add a special effect. Every round a player may use a card or move as normal. Once the card is played, the player discards it and draws a new one.
These cards are placed face up and everyone knows exactly what each other can do. In this way, you're still basically playing chess. Strategy is still king. However, you now have to play on your toes and by the seat of your pants. Rogue Chess will challenge your ability to adjust strategy much like actual battle. Because no plan survives contact with the enemy.

In Graduation, each player is a stereotypical high school student trying to gain status while taking courses and eventually graduating. Along the way, each player goes through trials and tribulations, sometimes brought upon by the other players, who are also trying to gain status. You can change your student type throughout the game, so players may start out a jock in freshman year and end up a nerd by senior year.

During the game, you start as one type of student and have a hand of cards, either action cards (such as "Detention" or "Friendly with Foreign Exchange Student") or course cards (such as "Algebra" or "Biology"). By playing these cards, players gain status, and if they play enough course cards, they'll eventually progress to the next grade. After finishing their senior year, the students graduate and the player with the most status wins.

Cube of Death is a role playing game that uses geek trivia to resolve actions. This is played with two teams generally of two or three players each. Every round the host draws a card and asks each team a question. If they get it right they get to roll 1d6 damage to the opposing team.

Each team has 30 hit points. The first to run out of points loses the match and is eliminated. Each player can chose a class (thief, wizard, barbarian, etc.) to play and each class has a special ability they can use once per game. There are also special questions that generate special rules (Volley Round, Double Damage, Wandering Monster).

Matchstix challenges players to assemble matching patterns using "matchstixs". On a players turn they can Turn, move, add, or remove Matchstix trying to match the patterns on their cards. Choose your moves wisely as you only get two per turn -and some of the moves cost a card to do. Score points for each completed card.

There is also a solo puzzle version of the game that can be played.

48 cards

description:
The Battle of Raszyn was fought on 19 April 1809 between armies of the Austrian Empire under Archduke Ferdinand Karl Joseph and the Duchy of Warsaw under Prince Józef Antoni Poniatowski.

Austrian armies, part of the Fifth Coalition, invaded Duchy of Warsaw in April 1809. In the battle of Raszyn polish army managed to stop them. It was major victory for a newborn polish army and one of the most important battles for polish people in XIX century.

Game is typica hex and counter wargame. It uses the rule of "Napoleon" system, made for the XIX century battles. "Raszyn 1809" was attachted to the 9th issue of the "Dragon Hobby" magazine.

Explore a mansion full of traps and save the world from the invasion of monsters. You can arm yourself with anything, anything goes, a denture, a nunchuck of sausage... anything before falling to the threat.
Explore each of the rooms of the mansion, no game is equal. 
One of the players will play the role of king of the monsters, others will have to join forces to defeat him. 
If you choose be the king of the monsters, use the suspect Cuco, the flying Hippocampus and other monsters against your opponents; everything is full of uncertainty.

Anything goes in...
The bane of my grandfather.


Recorre una mansión llena de trampas y salva al mundo de la invasión de los monstruos. Puedes armarte con lo primero que pilles, todo vale, una dentadura, un nunchaku de chorizo... cualquier cosa antes que sucumbir a la amenaza.
Explora cada una de las habitaciones de la mansión, ninguna partida es igual.
Uno de los jugadores desempeñará el papel del Rey de los monstruos, los demás tendrán que aunar sus fuerzas para derrotarle.
Si eliges ser el Rey de los los monstruos, utiliza contra tus adversarios al Cuco sospechoso, al Hipocampo volador y al resto de monstruos, todo está lleno de incertidumbre.
Cualquier cosa vale en...   
La pesadilla de mi abuelo.

Promotional card for Give Me Five with question about Paris.

Your teammates have five seconds to guess a word – but first you need to figure out the word to be guessed and how to clue them in to that same word.

The object of the game is to be the first to complete the puzzle.

There are two similar puzzles for both players but beware although the puzzles seems to be identical all pieces fit only one of the puzzles.

Boss Fight is a hybrid of slower-paced collectible card games and quick, highly accessible social card games.  Magic and Uno had a baby.

In Boss Fight, players take on the role of bosses in a fantasy RPG dungeon competing to land the killing blow on an adventuring Hero.  Doing the most damage has no bearing on the victor -- whoever ends the Hero's life wins.  It will require careful use of devious Traps, disruptive Spells, and convenient Loot drops to ensure you will become the Final Boss!

Boss Fight is NOT a collectible card game.  Every pack contains the same cards in the same quantities.  No need to break the bank hoping to find the one card that will make your game "work."

The game is designed for two to five players.  Most games take 10 to 20 minutes to complete, making it the perfect game for those looking for a quick game that doesn't sacrifice strategic challenge.

Accessible and friendly play are central concepts of Boss Fight's design.  Simple core rules combine with descriptive cards to create a smooth and short learning experience, while careful design limits the tense, confrontational feeling that can emerge in many strategic card games.

In short, it's fun, fast!

boardgame: Existenz: On the Ruins of Chaos – Energy Sublimation Apparatus Promo
description: This promo can be redeemed by submitting the voucher found in each purchased copy of Existenz: On the Ruins of Chaos.

In Flag Frenzy USA, players try to match flags on cards in their hands with flags on the table, emptying their personal card deck before anyone else can.

Flag Frenzy USA consists of 49 flag cards and 10 region cards (two each of West, South, Northeast, Midwest and Islands); each flag card shows eight state flags, and any two flag cards have exactly one flag in common, similar to the cards in Catch the Match and Spot It!

Game play differs from those titles, however, as two cards are placed face-up on the table at the start of play while the remainder of the deck is distributed evenly among the players. All players take two cards in hand, then everyone simultaneously tries to play onto the face-up cards by spotting the matching flag and giving its state's name when playing the card. With a region card, the player has to name a flag in the region being played.

After playing one or two cards, the player refills her hand. The first player to play all of the cards in her deck wins.

Gojira has chased Mr Awesome all the way from distant shores! It doesn't help to be speedy if there's no where to go.... time to face the terrible might of Gojira!

Gojira's ability reads:
If Gojira wins a contest, he may crush his space (putting a damage token on it) and move adjacent. Figures can't enter a space with a damage token on it.

language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The Combat Deck is a stand-alone 2-player card game in which each player controls one of four unique Kaiju (giant monsters) and battles for supremacy! The game is very quick & strategic - most games take only 10 minutes or less. Bet you can't play just one!

The mechanics are based off the upcoming Kaiju Combat video game production, and this Combat Deck can be used with any of the Monster Trading Cards available through www.sunstone.co. These monsters are all original creations submitted by our fans - join our community and help us decide which will become playable characters in Kaiju Combat!

The universe is dying.  You and the other surviving remnants of various alien species have managed to remain alive these past months by pooling all of your resources into a communal Worldship, on which you all live.  The Worldship has arrived in a new system - and each species has sent out a team to scout for information & resources.  If you can work together and repair & refuel the Worldship before it leaves then everyone can continue to survive.  If some tasks prove too difficult, then one or more species will have to be left behind.

The goal of the game is to not be left behind - but whether that means cooperation or competition is up to each individual player.

boardgame: The National Hunt Sport of Kings: The Hurdlers
description: An expansion of Sport of Kings. This one includes many new horses that you can train and race. Basic and campaign game rules are provided. The basic rules expand the basic game to include hurdles.

An expansion for Sport of Kings which converts the original game into a replay simulation.

The game deals with the Cheltenham Gold Cup and contains 24 winning horses from various years (1932 - 1990) as well as a few charts and rules.
This expansion set for Sport of Kings contains cards for 100 different historical race horses, to be used with the base game.

Britannia Bridge (Pont Britannia in Welsh) is a bridge across the Menai Strait between the island of Anglesey and the mainland of Wales. It was originally designed and built (1846-50) by Robert Stephenson as a tubular bridge of wrought iron rectangular box-section spans for carrying rail traffic.

Snowdonia: Britannia Bridge is a scenario for Snowdonia created by designer Tony Boydell and publisher Indie Boards and Cards to commemorate the second edition of the game; this scenario is available directly from IBC at conventions. Snowdonia: Britannia Bridge includes a trio of tweaked contract cards and a new engine – The Dawn Raider – that allows its owner to build track instead of the game (if he wants) whenever the game would build track due to an event.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Bayern Reise is a version of Deutschlandreise (http://www.boardgamegeek.com/boardgame/6787/deutschlandreise) and is similar to Hessen Reise (http://www.boardgamegeek.com/boardgame/34470/hessen-reise).

In Bayern Reise you travel around Bavaria/Germany. 
You get 9 destination cards (11 in the 2 and 3 player version). Now you start traveling around the board (which shows a map of Bavaria) to reach the citys showing on your cards. Therefor you have to roll a die. 
When rolling a 6, you get an action card. The action cards give you some tasks, e.g. going back to your starting position or move to a specific city.
The first player who has visited all his destinations and traveled to the state capital Munich wins the game.

Jewel Counterfeiters

From the publisher's webside:
Thanks to smugglers, green emeralds have found their way to the jewelry box.
But the finest cut diamond is counterfeit. Meanwhile, the jeweler protects their drawers filled with signet rings, which prove to be particularly valuable. And although the crooks all act extremely cautiously, this time everyone leaves its mark ...

Includes 8 fingerprints, 8 green emeralds in a diamond shape, 3 high-quality elastic jewelry rings made ​​of cast metal in an organza bag. Instructions in German and English.

Juwelenfälscher is a high quality expansion with metal rings in a special gift box.

Limited to only 500 pieces, this expansion will complete Master Thieves and call on all crooks to exercise extreme caution, creating an even more exciting gaming experience.

This expansion includes several game content only with the basic game "Master Thief" playable.

"Narvik 1940" simulates the battle fought in april/may 1940 between German forces that inavded Norway and Norwegian army supported by ally expeditionary corps, formed with polish, english and french units.

Game was attachted to the 3rd issue of "Dragon Strateg" magazine and published in 1997.

"Narvik 1940" uses "Bitwy Drugiej Wojny Światowej" system rules.
family: Series: Bitwy II wojny światowej 1943-45 (Dragon / Taktyka i Strategia)

description:
"You know what they say: Kaosball ain't easy"

Gangsta Z brings his unique brand of high living to our beloved sport! His amazing ability can bend the game's very rules, and allow his team to stay on the offense all game if needed!

Remember, the uncanny resemblance to a certain games designer is completely coincidental....

"Your foe's spirit is your strength."

Rumblefist is Kaosball's most celebrated martial artist. Having not only mastered every traditional form, he has invented at least a dozen of his own, usually based on focus and reaction. In his own words, awareness is victory. And the scoreboard proves it.

Game comes with 2 balls, 10 pins, a pin setter, a shooter and a magic slate scorecard. The game claims that "IT's A SNAP TO STRIKE OR SPARE". 


Perhaps someone can by looking at the design or the logo determine who created the game in the first place. Also game looks like a game from the 70's or 80's.

"Do you want to play again – double or nothing?"
    –Reina Roja

The cyberstruggles of Android: Netrunner have always been a risky affair for all involved, but Mala Tempora, the third Data Pack in the "Spin Cycle", raises the stakes to all-new heights. Will you be able to plug its upgrades into your deck and find success? Or are you the one who will fall behind?

Whatever the outcome, it won't be dull. The runners and corporate executives who direct the struggles of Android: Netrunner lead frantic lives full of fantastic highs and staggering lows, and the pace doesn’t slow a beat in Mala Tempora. Many of its sixty new cards (three copies each of twenty different cards) encourage daring gambles, staking health and fortunes against opportunities to rapidly accelerate wealth and success. Of course, in an age of technological one-upmanship, why play fair? Mala Tempora also introduces a number of programs and ice that allow runners and corps to "cheat" these wagers by stealing information about their opponents’ cards.

Meanwhile, leave it to the Anarchs to do their own thing; while the other factions bid for greater efficiencies and more devious tricks, a new Anarch runner arrives to play the game by another entirely different set of rules!
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Bohemian Schneider is a trick-taking game in the Ace-Ten-family for two players played with a 32-card pack. Players are dealt six cards initially and after each trick, players draw another card.

The goal of the game is collect valuable point cards. There's no trump and no need to follow suit, and the only way the second player can win the trick is to play the next higher card of the suit led.

The game is over after the deck runs out, and the player with the most points wins the game. The cards have the typical point values for Ace-Ten games (Ace 11, Ten 10, King 4, Queen 3 and Jack 2 points), but the card ranking is the more usual AKQJT987, instead of ATKQJ987 that is used in Ace-Ten games. To win, player needs to collect 61 points.

Bohemian Schneider is similar to a German game Elfern, or Figurenspiel, in which the cards don't have the usual point values, but instead the honour cards are all worth one point and the goal is to collect 11 of the 20 honours, hence the name (Elfern means "making elevens").

(Source: [i]The Penguin Book of Card Games[/i] by David Parlett)

A fixed set Deckbuilding Game based on the upcoming iOS Card Game of the same name. War Command blends traditional elements of collectible card games with the gameplay of a tabletop strategy game. The game plays out similar to chess, with each player maneuvering their six character cards to attempt to attack and kill the enemy commander character. 
Players can use three actions each turn to move characters, attack with characters, or play support cards from their hand, like a spell or upgrade card. When attacking or being attacked, players can also play attack or defense cards which only affect that instance.
Gameplay and Deckbuilding are both quick and easy to learn, but deep and open for exploration. The game storyline revolves around three races (the reptilian G'harrana, the technologically advanced Delylians, and the war-hardened Humans) who have fled to the same remote planet to avoid a wave of radiation that is wiping out all life in the galaxy. Their fragile civilizations, crumbling from the expense of the migration effort, are unable to maintain communications due to the planetwide frequency of magnetic storms. The units on the ground are soon isolated, and must make new allies to stay alive. This is reflected in game in that you can mix races in your decks ;)
The storyline is brought to life on each and every card through the spectacular artwork of established artists like Keith Thompson and Shaun Mitchell, as well as younger talents like Austen Mengler, Ven Locklear, and more!

First puzzling - and then play! First, the children get used to weekdays, months and seasons by playing. On the track assembled from puzzle pieces then they also can play the dice game "The Great Calendar Rally"!

The game consists of two parts: in the first part you put together the playfield from puzzle parts. It consists of a circle with the twelve months (each a single part of the puzzle) and kind of a "bridge" of the seven days of the week (again each weekday a single part of the puzzle) - there you can describe the months, the four seasons and the days.

In the second part the winner has to collect the most chips during a race through the months. A dice determines the number of months to go, you have to name them as you pass by. The game stops when the two chips of every month have been collected.

From Diffraction Entertainment:
."The world will hold its breath" claimed der Fuhrer as German and Axis troops crossed the borders of the Soviet Union. Barbarossa is the TSWW game covering the entire Eastern Front from June 22nd, 1941 until June 30th, 1943.

From the back of the box:

    Fully researched game modules covering the war-changing campaign(s) in the USSR from June 1941 through June 1943
    20 state-of-the-art maps covering east-central Europe from Poland to beyond the Ural Mountains.
    7840 world-class die-cut counters covering Axis, Soviet, and Western Allied forces.
    Extensive rules and charts included in this game, thus continuing the TSWW Game Series covering World War II in its savage entirety.
    Two D10 Dice.


Scenario and Campaign list:
In addition to the entire campaign, the following scenarios and campaigns are included in the game:


    Learning Scenarios:
         Target Berlin.
         Stuka.
         Panzers Los!
         The Battle of the Barents Sea.
    



    Crimea Scenarios (Discovery Group):
         The 'Neck' Taken.
         The Crimea Invaded.
         The First Assault on Sevastopol.
         The Soviet Amphibious Attack.
         Trappenjägd.
         The Final Assault on Sevastopol.
    



     From the Danube to the Don.
     The Crimean Campaign.
     Kalinin--The Northern Gateway to Moscow.
     Operation Mars.
     Operation Uranus.
     November II 1942.
     Northern Finland 1941-1942.
     Fall Blau: The 1942 Campaign.
     Rommel Goes East.
     Hitler Goes East.
     Barbarossa Extreme.
     The Beginning of the End.
     Enemies of the Blood Royal.

description:
From Diffraction Entertainment:

"Artillery is the God of War" claimed Josef Stalin... Prove him right with the next title in the acclaimed TSWW game series... Vengeance is nigh!

Vengeance is the final instalment of the Great Patriotic War front of the TSWW game series. In the box you will find at least 5000 counters and up to 16 maps covering the scope and sweep of the Soviet front of World War II. Additionally the game will include multiple campaigns and several smaller scenarios covering the war in the Steppes of Ukraine and the forests of Byelorussia from July 1943 to the surrender of the German Reich in 1945.
