description:
"I'm lost."

Straight out of legend, the one and only Minotaur from the Final Labyrinth has joined the ranks of Kaosball. His ability to make the field more like his native dungeon will devastate any opponent unlucky enough to face him.

boardgame: A Test of Mettle: Three Battles of the Lorraine Campaign, September 1944
description:
A Test of Mettle is three games in one package, one of two Professional Series Games published by High Flying Dice Games. Components include three 11 by 17 inch full-bleed maps, one sheet of 280 die cut counters, one full color Players' Aide Chart, and three 8 page rule books. The game is available in either boxed (plastic counter tray with clear lid and a six sided-die) or zip bagged.

Patton’s Finest: the Battle of Arracourt is a simulation of the armored clash between the U.S. Third Army’s 4th Armored Division and counterattacking German forces led by the 111th and 113th Panzer Brigades. After breaking out of the Normandy Bridgehead in July 1944, the 4th Armored had led the advance across France to the German Westwall fortifications, earning the nickname “Patton’s Finest.” Desperate to stop the American blitzkrieg, and recapture the vital railroad center at Nancy, Hitler placed General Von Rundstedt in command. To support the counterattack, emergency reinforcements were ordered up to the Western Front that included new and improved models of Panther tanks. The armored clash east of Arracourt would confirm that the Americans had learned well the lessons of war, and would foreshadow the desperate fighting in the Ardennes a few months later.

Revanche! The Battle of Dompaire is a simulation of the armored clash between the Free French 2nd Armored Division and counter attacking German forces of the 112th Panzer Brigade. After breaking out of the Normandy Bridgehead in July, the Free French 2nd Armored, led by General Jacques Leclerc, triumphantly entered Paris and advanced across the French countryside intent on liberating the province of Lorraine. Desperate to stop the Allied blitzkrieg, and recapture the vital railroad center at Nancy, Hitler placed General Von Runstedt in command. To support the counter attack, emergency reinforcements were ordered up to the Western Front that included new and improved models of Panther tanks. The armored clash at Dompaire on the 12th and 13th of September confirmed that the French had learned well the lessons of war, and that Allied victory in the West now was now at hand.

Tough Hombres: The Battle of Mairy is a wargame simulation of the clash between the U.S. Third Army’s 90th Infantry Division (nicknamed the “Tough Hombres”) and counterattacking German forces led by the 106th Panzer Brigade and elements of the newly formed 59th Volksgrenadier Regiment. After breaking out of the Normandy Bridgehead in July, Patton’s Third Army led the advance across France to Germany’s Westwall fortifications. Desperate to stop the American blitzkrieg, and forestall the American’s drive on the vital railroad center at Nancy, General Otto von Knobelsdorf ordered an attack by the newly formed 106th Panzer Brigade, equipped with new Panther tanks and commanded by the legendary commander Colonel Franz Bake, against the 90th Infantry Division. Although nicknamed “Tough Hombres” for their divisional patch letters “TO” (for Texas and Oklahoma, the two states whose National Guard units comprised the division), its record was far from distinguished. The division had run-up ghastly casualty rates during the Normandy campaign (some battalions suffering a staggering 400% rate of loss!) and the sacking of its commander. The Germans expected this American formation, charged with holding Patton’s open left flank, to quickly fold in their early morning night attack of September 8th…

description:
Mare Nostrum is part of "The Second World War Series" from Diffraction Entertainment.

MN depicts the struggle for North Africa, East Africa and the Middle East. It starts with the ill-prepared Italian offensive into Egypt and ends with the surrender of all Axis units in Tunisia. Unlike most other games about the African Campaign does Mare Nostrum include the battles for British and Italian Somaliland, the Iraqi coup d'etat from 1941 with the subsequent Anglo-Iraqi War and the Anglo-Soviet invasion of Iran.

The mapped Area covers Northern Africa from the Western Desert to Egypt, East Africa, the Iberian peninsula, most of the Middle East including Iran and parts of Italy.

description:
Players roll the dice and move around the board, using the spaces and cards available to try to outdrink their opponents and collect 10 Bottoms Up tokens.

Intended for those who are more free spirited and enjoy doing risque things, some of the tasks required in the game are to remove pieces of clothing, massage or kiss other players, and of course take a drink!  Either the first player to collect 10 tokens or the last player conscious wins!

Players take turns rolling the die and moving around the board trying to become the most chic person in Los Angeles.  All the major attractions are featured on cards players use to collect cash.  The player with the most money wins.

From the box: "Be seen in all the right places, wear all the right clothes, have all the right friends, or lose...The ultimate status-seeking, power-tripping, money-spending game, babe."

The Name: "Devil's Backbone" is a colloquial term for the Natchez Trace.  "Breaking" means successfully completing a trip up or down the Trace.

The Game: By 1769, frontiersmen loaded their flatboats with furs, tobacco, whiskey, coal, copper, livestock, etc. and floated down the Mississippi River to sell their goods in Natchez.  Along the way they have to endure many hazards on the Mississippi River.  After selling their goods in Natchez, they could not go back up the river (no steam boats yet), so they walked back to Nashville, on ancient Indian Trails.  Travel the Natchez Trace as those frontiersmen did; endure the same hardships and privations they faced: the fevers, the thieves, the Indians, etc.  Meet the famous men of the past: Andrew Jackson, Lincoln, Audubon and many, many others who also traveled the historic Indian trail between Natchez and Nashville.

The Purpose: To endure the hazards of the Trace and get to Nashville, if you do, you will have Broken the Devil's Backbone.

(taken from the game insert)

Designed by Joe and Jean McConnell (Times Daily, Florence AL, dated 19 Jul 1990)

In Inventing the Future, players race to invent the flying car, teleportation and time travel. Whoever can best manage the bottlenecks of research, development and production stands to earn everlasting glory, fame and power.

To accomplish this, players must assemble teams to perform research, testing, and manufacturing. Specialist teams provide more powerful abilities. To support all of this, players must raise both public and private funding.

Each round six dice are rolled and players take turns drafting them, marking the corresponding intersection of number and color on their boards. In each row only one choice is possible. When all rows are filled, players execute the action phases, their type and order having been determined via the same dice.

You are the head coach of soccer team either World Star Team or Europe Star Team. You are under pressure of the competition with your opponent. Situation in the field is going on by simple simulation mechanic, fast move game and very dynamic in its situation.

Lets proves yourselves as the Great head coach of the team. Foresee the match situation, watch out the dynamic of game and manage team by relocate kicker at the right place - right time. That is the way you can dominate the game in dynamic situation.

You will surprise game can be changed dramatically if you plan well tactically and strategically and put into action at the right place - right time.

"Wanna know how many times Ive been crossed? Count the kills on the scoreboard."

She's a unique brand of fighter, with a powerfully vengeful spirit. They say on the field, if she's got her eye on you, nothing else matters. Make peace with your gods, and say good night.

Duudsonit Lautapeli is a Finnish board game were you are one of the Dudesons' and try to gather as much publicity as you can. Duudsonit (The Dudesons) are Finnish Ostrobotnian equivalent to Jackass.

Basically this is a card game with the point scoring board. The player who gets through the board first is the winner.

In the game you play several episodes, which consist of player turns. In your turn you have four choices: Play A card, change a card, heal one body part or pass. When you pass, you are out until the end of the current episode. At the end of the episode player/players with the most publicity gets to go forward two spaces in the board. The player/players with second most publicity gets to go forward one space. The episode ends when every player passes or is unable to play cards.

There are three types of cards in the deck: prank, stunt and hassle cards. Hassle cards cause some kind of events to happen. You get publicity either by making pranks or stunts. Making pranks causes injuries to chosen player and gives you publicity. Making Stunts gives you both the publicity and the injury.

There is three types of injuries (two each): Leg, hand and bodily injuries. Getting injuries might unable you from playing certain cards. Injuries are taken randomly from the bag and positioned in the players personal player board. If you have all six injuries, you are sent to hospital and you are out until the end of the episode.

Did you feel you were the monkey king? Remember that in the jungle everthing changes like in a kaleidoscope...

"Prawo Dżungli - dodatek" is translated to "Jungle Law - expansion". Unlike the base game, this is not an unauthorized production. The graphics and the mechanics are unique.

Prawo Dżungli - dodatek adds 80 new cards which provide one new color (pink), 8 new symbols and 3 new special cards. The special cards are:
a) "different colors, different symbols" - when this card shows up each player who has a card which has unique color and shape has to grab the sceptre
b) "second hand" - when this card is face up every player has to play with his second hand
c) "cards switch" - the player who grab the sceptre first may switch his face up cards with any other player

Shipping Forecast is a roll and move, push-your-luck, dice-rolling action game with strategy, based on the shipping forecast.

The variable weather and sea conditions given on the Shipping Forecast Cards will help and hinder you as you move your trawler to and try to land hauls of fish.

Should you stick with that much needed fuel can, or roll the dice just once more to bring in another haul of fish? Battle the conditions at sea and try to reach calmer waters, or take your chances where you are, preserve your fuel and weather the storm?

Shipping Forecast is an easy to learn dice rolling game with strategy that will force you to make tough decisions every move!

This is a psychology-themed card game for 2 to 6 players.

At the start of the game, each player is dealt two Need cards visible to everyone, a Motivation card visible only to the player, and a hand of six cards containing a mixture of Fulfilment and Action cards. The Need cards specify quantities of Fulfilment points you have to collect in order to satisfy your Needs. However, the Motivation card gives your objective for the game. You could be a Dominator, seeking to fulfil your own Needs whilst ensuring other players' Needs are not met. You could be an Idealist, trying to make sure everyone's Needs are fulfilled. You could be a Victim, making sure that everyone's needs are satisfied except yours.

There are 12 different Motivations, each with a game goal with different levels of difficulty and worth different scores. If you achieve your Motivation and you have an "End game" card, you can claim the points. But beware! It's possible that more than one Motivation can be achieved simultaneously, so another player may claim points too.

The Fulfilment cards allow you to satisfy your Needs or the Needs of other players. The Action cards allow players to manipulate the distribution of Fulfilment cards, moving or discarding Fulfilment or even forcing players to change their Motivation.

boardgame: Robinson Crusoe: Adventures on the Cursed Island – Voyage of the Beagle (Vol. 1)
description: The adventure is based on the real events that took place between 1831 and 1836, which led to the creation of the Theory of Evolution. During this very expedition, Charles Darwin laid the foundations for one of the most breakthrough scientific papers in the history of mankind– the paper on the Origin of Species. Players take on the roles of members of the crew of HMS Beagle with Darwin, at the start of a great adventure. In the following scenarios, they will have to challenge adversity and support the young scientist in his research. With five new scenarios, you are ready to dive into new adventures!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Game: Robinson Crusoe – Adventures on the Cursed Island

description:
City Center is a map-building / tile-laying game about planning a city. City planners (the players) take turns placing new buildings into the grid. After each round of placement, the new buildings are scored and the next round commences. When the whole city grid is filled in, the game is over and the planner with the most points wins.

As an additional twist, each player has full knowledge of the tiles she'll choose from right at the beginning. Once a player draws her hand of buildings, there are no more draws. She'll have to play all but two of the cards.

The game scales from 2 to 7 players by increasing the size of the city grid and eventually decreasing the number of cards in the hand. For instance, at 2 players, the city will grow to 4x4 and each player will have a hand of 10. At 7, the city is 6x6 and the players have a hand of only 7. Each player gets an equal number of chances to score; sometimes an initial card is played (unscored) to start off the city.

boardgame: Pixel Tactics: HD Leader Card – Baenvier Marlgrove
description: Pixel Tactics: Baenvier Marlgrove is a single leader card for use in either Pixel Tactics or Pixel Tactics 2. Marlgrove receives a curse counter at the end of each round, then can spend this counter during a wave as a free action to give the player's heroes +2 Attack Strength and skip casualty checks.

boardgame: Little Red Riding Hood: Full Moon Rising
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Little Red Riding Hood

description:
Can't find a tabletop wargame opponent?

This easily-readable guide provides the tools needed to undertake tabletop wargame campaigns without the need for other players. The guidelines presented within are designed to supplement your favorite wargaming rules set (not included). They can be bent or twisted, even broken and reformed, to suit a wargamer's needs. Novices and veterans alike can employ these ideas and innovations to further the thrill of pitting army against army without the need for another player. Features methodologies for campaigns (mobilization, weather, logistics, morale, alliances, revolts, sieges, and mutinies), sea campaigns, integrating fantasy, tactical actions (terrain, weather, commander's competency, concealment, ambushes, and minefields), and air warfare.

The universe is teetering out of control and the aliens that inhabit it are getting restless. Rumors about total annihilation have spread through every corner of each galaxy. The rumor is that if one particular object can be obtained, then that alien's race and home planet will be saved. Otherwise, their race will be exterminated and their home planet will be destroyed. One alien whispers to another, "What are we looking for?" The other alien snaps back, "The Grail, of course."

Extraterrestrial Grail is a turn-based strategy board game for 2-4 players. Extraterrestrial Grail takes place in outer space in which you are an alien searching for the Grail. Only one player gets the Grail at the beginning of the game, so the other aliens must find out where it is by fighting each other and exploring planets for it.

Each player receives attribute cards at the beginning of the game. A player can only hold a maximum of 6 attribute cards in their hand at a time. All of the attribute cards that the player has chosen to not place in their hand must go into their planet boxes. Some attribute cards are weapons which remove life points from an enemy alien (bomb, missile, laser rifle, and etc.). Other attribute cards are designed to steal cards from other aliens (mug gloves, rope, and etc.). There are also attribute cards that let you safely search and give you the ability to search foreign planets (respiration headgear, flashlight, thermal garment, and etc.). There are many more kinds of attribute cards as well!

Turn Options:

     Roll die and move your alien (only once per turn)
     Search a planet for attribute cards (if your alien token is on a planet)
     Start a battle with an enemy alien located on the same space as your alien
     Use attribute card(s) in your hand


Battle Turn Options (choose one per battle turn):

     Use natural attack
     Flee from battle
     Use an attribute card in your hand


The object of the game is to have the Grail in your hand when the alarm goes off or have the Grail in your hand when all the other aliens have been eliminated. If no one has the Grail in their hand when the timer alarm goes off, then everyone loses. For example: If there is time still left on the timer and only one alien is still alive, then that alien must find the Grail before time is up to win the game (the game will end when the last player standing finds the Grail).

description:
A cross between Rummy and Miles Borne - family fun for anyone who loves the beach and card games.

Collect Sandcastles and Seashells, but watch out for Sunburn!

Objective:
Make 3 sets to win!

Setup:
Deal 4 cards to each player (their hand). 
Deal 4 cards face-up as "The Ocean". 
The discard pile is face-up next to the Deck (reuse if you finish the deck)

Gameplay:
Play goes clockwise.  Each turn either:

    Swap 1 card from your hand with the Ocean; OR
    Draw 2 cards from the Deck.  1st one in the Ocean, 2nd in your hand; OR
    Play a Yellow Action Card from your hand


Action Card Notes

    Discard means to the discard pile.  
    Pick which player to play Sunburn, Wipeout and Shark Attack on 


The first game, the person most recently at the beach goes first.  After that, the winner goes last.

And most importantly: please enjoy A Day at the Beach!!!

Full official rules at http://www.standardgames.com

This game of strategy is a welcome break from gin rummy and can be played with a standard deck of cards.

The main dynamic is deciding what to hang on to, and what to discard (which can be taken by your opponent).

If you like Lost Cities, you'll love this!

Rules at:

In Abeja Reina ("Queen Bee"), two players take turns moving their bee pieces on the hexagonal cells of the game board. Whoever eliminates the opponent's queen bee first wins.

The game board depicts a shared hive of sorts, with players starting on opposite sides of the board with a trench between them. The hive spaces hold only a single piece, while the trench can hold two layers of pieces. The queen bee and three workers move only one space to an adjacent hex space; the six drones move "diagonally" along a line leading away from the hex to the next hex. If you move  a token onto an enemy piece in the hive, you eliminate that piece from the game.

If you move a token onto a piece already in the trench, you pin that piece; if you move a token onto an enemy that's pinning another piece, you eliminate that enemy token. When moving from the trench, workers and drones can move any number of spaces, allowing for long-range attacks. A queen can never step onto the lower level of the trench. After all, she's the queen!

Expansion cards for Avalon Hill's statistical replay tennis game.

One set represents one season and contains the top 50 men and the top 50 women players for a specific year, one card each.

Expansion cards for Avalon Hill's statistical replay golf game.

Each set comes with 50 different player cards and a Second Putt card. The cards are useable with all three Avalon Hill editions of the base game.

Introduction
Frey is an abstract game similar to Go played on the line segments of a base 4 hex board.

Object
The object is to surround the largest total area with your color wooden segments.

Player with the highest score wins.

Play
Two players Red and Blue take turns placing wooden segments in their color onto the empty line segments of a base 4 Hex board.

Red plays first then play alternates.

On your turn you have one of two choices,

1) Place a wooden segment in your color onto an empty line segment of the board or
2) Move a wooden segment in your color currently on the board to another empty line segment by pivoting off of one of said segments endpoints.

Pivots and Breaks
Pivoting off an endpoint is only allowed if

1) The endpoints that your pivoting off of are free, not connected to your opponents pieces or
2) The endpoints that your pivoting off of are connected to your own pieces.

When pivoting, a segment may not cross over either a friendly nor enemy piece.

A Break is when you pivot one of your like color connected segments breaking them into two segments.

Capturing
Capturing your opponents segments is done by surrounding them with your color segments from endpoint to endpoint. 
You may not play into any area that has been closed off by your opponents segments.

The game ends when the board is full or no valid moves can be made.

Scoring
Count all of your captured enemy pieces and all of the spaces surrounded by your colored pieces. Each captured piece is worth .5 points apiece and the spaces are worth 1 point apiece.

Highest score wins.

boardgame: Mage Knight Board Game: Krang Character Expansion
description:
Krang is here! Let Chaos reign!

The Mage Knight Board Game: Krang Character Expansion includes all that players need to add Krang, the mysterious and dangerous Orc Chaos Shaman, as a new playable character to the Mage Knight Board Game, including his prepainted miniature, unique starting cards, and skill tokens.

description:
Legends of Rock is a game about acquiring as much Fame as possible before meeting your untimely and tragic demise. Rockers begin the game by creating an album in one of the 8 genres represented on the genre wheel. Throughout the game rockers will create new albums, go on tour, and create hit songs.

Legends of Rock is primarily a set collection game with two resources: Music Tokens and Tour Chits. Turn order for selecting these resources can be manipulated throughout the game during the Free Time phase, where rockers can choose to become more Talented, practice their on-stage Diva skills, impress a better Producer, or hire a competent Managers.

Album sales rely on the genre track, only what is popular sells. Hit songs rely on the quality of the song and can score the most points but don't offer lasting success. Touring can score a lot of Fame if you have the Fan base that wants to attend.

"Die Schwebebahn" is typical roll and move board game about Wuppertaler Schwebebahn - The Wuppertal Suspension Railway. It is the oldest electric elevated railway with hanging cars in the world and is a unique system.
The first player who defeats the whole track (71 stations) wins the game.

Murder in the Dark is a parlor game played with 6 or more people.

Played in the dark, a randomly selected "murderer" attempts to commit at least one murder without being noticed.  For, if he's noticed, the (also randomly selected) detective may have enough clues to lead to a proper accusation.

GAME PLAY:
First, establish the play area. Usually this is played in a house, but it can be played anywhere that is dark enough.  You will want to establish areas that are off limits if any.

Prepare a deck of cards, one per player.  The player who secretly, and randomly, selects the ace of spades is the murderer.  The one that selects the King is the detective. All others are civilians.

Once these cards are secretly looked at, and replaced, a countdown is done, and the lights are turned out.

At this point all players may wonder about freely. Some may hide, some may go out looking for evidence.  As the murderer, you may walk up to someone and say "You're dead".  Once they've heard this they're dead, but they may also scream before they've been killed.  A "dead body" can be lead wherever the murderer would like to place them, and they must stay there until the end of this phase.

One may also, regardless of the role, touch someone and ask "are you dead?".  This is the only time a dead person may speak, "yes" they would say.  At this point "body body body!" is yelled.  Someone turns on the lights, and now we try to discover who the murderer was.

All gather in a common area. Those who are dead indicate as such, and where they were found is established.  It is best for the dead to sit away from the main area.  It is VERY IMPORTANT that the dead not speak at all, or give any indication who killed them.

If the detective has been killed, then the murderer has won.  Otherwise, the detective will start asking questions of the living.  Some play that the detective may only ask "yes" or "no" questions.  The murderer may lie about anything, and also make false accusations.  Once the detective has an idea who the murderer is, they formally accuse them, the murderer may not lie at this point.

If they're wrong, the lights go back out, and play continues.  The dead are still dead. But, of course, now the murderer knows the detective's identity.

If they're right, the murderer has lost.

ALTERNATE PLAY: Some play with more than one detective, which is a good idea if the group gets above 8.

Planetoid is a deceptively simple game played on a spherical board. Players must capture all of his her opponent's pieces. Strategic challenges emerge as players navigate a borderless play surface.

Players: 2

Setup: Each player places their 6 pawns on the designated home spaces (Red or Blue). Players roll 2 dice to determine who plays first. The highest number plays first.

Gameplay: On each turn players rolls 2 dice to determine how many spaces they can move. Moves may be split between two pawns.

Only one pawn may occupy a space at a time. If a player lands on his or her opponent's pawn, that pawn is removed from the game board.

Goal: The first Player to capture all of his or her opponent's pawns, wins the game.

Playing time: 20-25 minutes

Erwischt vermischt! is a party game which is played while doing other activities. It is based on the original game Erwischt! (aka J'te Gage Que... or I betcha...).

Each player receives a card with certain tasks which have to be fulfilled within a time frame upon which the players agree at the beginning.

In this version of the game the tasks are quirks which they have to adopt. The players have to identify the quirks of the other players. In order to prevent the other players from finding out the quirks from the card, the players have to adopt as many quirks as possible.

Players score points for identifying the right quirks of the other players and for their own quirks which haven't been identified. The player with the most points is the winner.

boardgame: Darkling Plain: an Augmented Reality Miniature Board Game
description:
Darkling Plain is a miniatures board game that is powered by augmented reality (AR). Darkling Plain combines all the best aspects of a traditional board game such as Settlers of Catan or Ticket to Ride and a video game such as Final Fantasy or World of Warcraft. Board games offer a social experience and depth of play, while video games offer immersion, glorious eye-candy, and computational wizardry.

There are no actual miniatures in Darkling Plain. You place cards that represent your units and other features, like a ring of standing stones or a village, upon a full-color game board. When the cards or board are viewed through the camera of a smart device or computer, the game comes to life like a video game. Spectacular special effects abound as a fantasy battle bursting with the magical and the medieval consumes your tabletop.

Dai Sai Sou! includes 36 unique robo part cards with different characteristics, providing diversity to create up to 6 robos at once. Part cards have a maintenance gauge, defense, melee and range attacks, and a special ability. Players will use these parts to attack their opponent's robo. In addition, players will be able to use action cards to modify attacks and defend against oncoming bombardment. A dice roll plus your modifiers from the robo part you use to attack and action card will have to beat your opponent's armor for the robo part you attack. A successful hit lowers the opponents maintenance gauge, usually by one level.
Parts will take damage when hit. If a part takes too much damage, they can become inoperable and are at risk of blowing up, leaving you more vulnerable. If a player disables too many parts or destroys the chest part of a robo, then that robo is destroyed. Some may be fortunate enough to collect the necessary cards to perform an ultimate attack that will turn their opponents’ robo parts into scrap metal. Make sure you're the last one standing in this robo-royale.

boardgame: Mice and Mystics: Heart of Glorm
description:
Beautiful glow-worm, shimmer, shimmer!
Shine your light brightly through pain so glum,
Follow love's arrow along the thum,
Ne'er mind life's sorrows, lest you succumb
For dear friend would you become?
Oh, glow-worm getting dimmer, dimmer.

Prince Collin and his friends defeated Vanestra and thought evil had been banished from the castle forever. But deep within those scorched and shadow-haunted halls, something is moving that should not be. A malicious presence has taken root, and it has hatched a scheme that will ensnare the Prince and his friends and will forever change the course of history for the mouse-town of Barksburg.

The storybook in Mice and Mystics: Heart of Glorm picks up right after the events of Sorrow and Remembrance, letting you and your friends continue the story of Prince Collin, Maginos, Tilda, Nez, Filch and Lily. Included in this expansion are new plastic figures, cards, and counters, as well as a new series of chapters sure to test your mettle! And joining the heroes is Nere, a wild mystic who brings her powerful magic and knowledge of alchemy to the battle.

description:
In the worker movement game Alchemy Bazaar, 2-5 players are rival alchemists who jointly own a growing bazaar of alchemical shops. Each round, new shop tiles are added to the bazaar, and the players then take turns moving their apprentices around the various shops to collect coins, alchemical ingredients, formulas and special actions.

Each apprentice can move to one adjacent shop for free, after which the player can pay a coin to have the apprentice move to another adjacent shop, then two coins for another shop, and so on. Once an apprentice is finished moving, the shop where the apprentice ends it turn is unavailable for other apprentices to use, but other apprentices may move through that shop freely.

Formulas can be completed with certain combinations of ingredients, resulting in the alchemist gaining wisdom (by drawing wisdom cards). Each player also has a secret goal card, which will give bonus points at the end of the game. The player with the most wisdom and bonus points at the end of the game is the winner!

Promotional game for the makers of Fry's Chocolate.

A simple roll and move game where you must get your ship from London to the publisher's factory in Somerdale, Bristol. Obey instructions on the pink squares. First to reach the factory, wins.

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game – The Horn That Wakes
description:
“I am the fire that burns against the cold, the light that brings the dawn, the horn that wakes the sleepers, the shield that guards the realms of men.”
    –George R.R. Martin, A Game of Thrones

In the north, winter is coming. The dead walk, and the fate of all the Seven Kingdoms, perhaps the fate of the whole world, depends upon the vigilance and valor of a few sworn brothers of the Night’s Watch.

The Horn That Wakes is the fourth Chapter Pack in the "Kingsroad" cycle for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game. Its sixty new cards (three copies each of twenty different cards) revisit the Night’s Watch, the Wildlings, and the intrigues and threats of the North. Eleven of the pack’s twenty new cards (three copies of each) support the Night’s Watch and Wildling themes, as well as a host of other dynamics build around The North agendas. You’ll find new copies of such notable characters as Jon Snow, Janos Slynt, and Coldhands, as well as the LCG’s first version of Ygritte. Additionally, twelve new characters, three new events, and two new plots ensure that each House finds plenty of reason to rejoice, even those furthest removed from the Wall.

description:
Game of the 70s spy, where players have to play some missions before you get to the airport and escape to win the game. The game board is a city with various places that players must attain, they can also take advantage of the rail, ferries and helicopters to move faster and take advantage of the other spies.
A turn based game, with dices for movement, Top Secret goal card and event card.

Game simulates fictional battles at western Pacific, fought between Japanese and American forces at the island Yafra. It was first part of the mini series for "Bitwy drugiej wojny światowej" system about battles at Pacific during II World War.

"Yafra 1942/1945" was publish in the 2nd issue of "Dragon Strateg" magazine and contained two scenarios - Japanese landing at August 1942 and Americana attack at February 1945.

Island itself and battles fought on it are fictional, but were based upn real fights at Philipines or Iwo Jima. There were also some new rules to the "Bitwy drugiej wojny światowej" system, made for battles at Pacific, including Kamikaze units, guerilla and caves.

Sligo is a territory game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). There is also an off-board location called prison.

Sligo is based on Corey Clark's Slither and the ancient game of Go.

				
				
					DefinitionsA group is a stone along with all stones one can reach from it through a series of steps onto orthogonally adjacent stones of its color. A liberty of a group is an empty point orthogonally adjacent to it. A group without liberties is said to be dead.

A naked diagonal is a pair of diagonally adjacent stones of the same color with no other stone of their color adjacent to both.

				
				
					PlayBlack plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, perform exactly one of these actions:


     Remove an enemy stone from the prison.
     Place a friendly stone on an empty point, then move to the prison all dead enemy groups. If the prison now contains stones of both colors, remove pairs of opposite-colored stones from it until at most one color remains. Finally, if there are naked diagonals on the board, move a stone of your color to a diagonally adjacent empty point.


At the end of your turn, there must be no naked diagonals and no dead friendly groups on the board.

The last player to perform an action wins. If a play recreates any previous end-of-turn board position with the same player to move, the game ends in a draw. This is possible in theory, but probably requires colluding players.

Before the game starts, to make it fair, the first player places a number of black stones in the prison, and then the second player chooses sides.

Quattro is board game variant of the classic game Connect Four.

The game is played on a 5x5 game board and the goal is to get 4 of your pawns in a row.

Each player has 12 pawns. On their turn, a player places one of their pawns on any edge of the board. Already placed pawns are pushed inwards. If the player manages to get one or more rows with 4 of their pawns, they score points.

After the last pawn entered the playing field, the game ends and the player with the most points wins.

Originally published in spielbox magazine.

Everwind - Jeksey

Galwyndor's Oldest Strategy Game
Match wits with your opponent as you strategically maneuver your Messenger to the other side of the board.

A forgotten game from a forgotten time, Jeksey was invented as a cover for a secret cipher. The ancients would use the gameboard and a key to transmit encrypted messages to one another, which is why the object is to get your Messenger deep into enemy territory.

It plays like chess, but there are only four types of pieces, so the rules are simple. Using five Wards, four Cavals, three Litus pieces, and one Messenger, you must outwit and outmaneuver your opponent.

Based on the game appearing in the Everwind Series of books by Janden Hale. Game designer: Janden Daniel Hale.

https://www.thegamecrafter.com/games/everwind-jeksey
